business_glossary_term: Analysis Data Model (ADaM) Dataset Specification ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each ADaM dataset specification record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: adam_dataset_spec has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which analysis population the dataset serves. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integ
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: ADaM dataset specs are programmed against source data structures defined in a specific EDC build. When the EDC build changes, ADaM specs must be reviewed for impact. Domain experts expect this link fo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: ADaM ADLB datasets are sourced from specific lab panels. ADaM programmers and validators must trace which lab panel definition (analyte list, SDTM domain, CDISC codes) feeds a given dataset spec. This
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan that governs the derivation rules and analysis objectives for this ADaM dataset. Ensures traceability from dataset design to the approved SAP.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: ADaM dataset specs are derived from specific SDTM datasets. Biostatisticians and programmers reference the source SDTM dataset record for version, QC status, and data cut date when building ADaM specs
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this ADaM dataset specification is authored. Links the specification to its parent protocol and study context.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: ADaM datasets are submitted to FDA/EMA as CDISC-required components of NDA/BLA/MAA submissions. The submission_flag attribute on adam_dataset_spec confirms this business reality. Direct FK supports su
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Version of the CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide to which this dataset specification conforms (e.g., 'ADaM IG v1.3', 'ADSL v1.1', 'BDS v1.1'). Required for regulatory submission conformance validation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this variable is a primary analysis variable (AVAL, AVALC, or equivalent) used directly in statistical analyses and TLF production. True = analysis variable; False = supporting/descriptive variable. Guides programmer prioritization.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Date on which this ADaM dataset specification was formally approved by the lead biostatistician and/or statistical programming lead. Marks the specification as ready for SAS programming implementation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Name or user identifier of the biostatistician who authored this ADaM dataset specification. Establishes accountability and is referenced in the audit trail for regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether this ADaM dataset contains unblinded treatment assignment data. Blinded = treatment codes masked; Unblinded = actual treatment assignments visible (post-DBL or for open-label studies); Open-Label = study design is inherently unblinded. Governs access control and data release procedures.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Narrative justification for changes introduced in this version of the specification relative to the prior version (e.g., 'SAP amendment v2.0 added new secondary endpoint', 'Protocol amendment PA-003 added new visit'). Required for change control documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Name of the specific CDISC or sponsor-defined codelist associated with this variable (e.g., 'NY', 'SEX', 'RACE', 'DTYPE'). Referenced in the define.xml ItemDef and used for PINNACLE 21 conformance validation.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; CDISC Define-XML v2.1; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Reference to the controlled terminology codelist applicable to this variable (e.g., 'CDISC SDTM CT RACE', 'NCI Thesaurus C66742', 'MedDRA v26.0 PT', 'WHO-DD'). Ensures standardized values for regulatory submission and cross-study comparability.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; NCI Thesaurus; MedDRA; WHO-DD; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: CDISC ADaM core designation indicating the requirement level for this variable. Required = must be present in all datasets of this class; Expected = should be present when applicable; Permissible = may be included at sponsor discretion. Drives conformance validation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Timestamp when this ADaM dataset specification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and record lineage per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: CDISC ADaM structural class of the dataset. ADSL = Subject-Level Analysis Dataset (one record per subject); BDS = Basic Data Structure (one or more records per subject per analysis parameter per analysis timepoint); OCCDS = Occurrence Data Structure (one or more records per subject per occurrence of an event); OTHER = datasets not conforming to a standard class.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: Human-readable descriptive label for the dataset (e.g., 'Subject-Level Analysis Dataset', 'Adverse Events Analysis Dataset'). Used in SAS PROC CONTENTS output and regulatory submission metadata.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Short, uppercase dataset name conforming to CDISC ADaM naming conventions (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADTTE, ADLB, ADCM, ADEX, ADRS). Maximum 8 characters per CDISC standard. This is the externally-known identifier used by SAS programmers and regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Narrative description of the analytical purpose of this dataset, including the primary analysis populations it supports, key endpoints addressed, and its role in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). Authored by the biostatistician.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; ICH E3 Structure of CSR

description: Version of the CDISC Define-XML standard used to generate the define.xml metadata file for this dataset (e.g., 'Define-XML v2.0', 'Define-XML v2.1'). Required for FDA and EMA electronic submission packages.
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Detailed narrative or pseudocode describing the derivation logic for this variable, including source variables, transformation rules, imputation methods (e.g., LOCF), and conditional logic. The primary programming instruction for SAS programmers. Must be unambiguous and traceable to the SAP.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM Traceability

description: Date from which this version of the ADaM dataset specification is effective and authoritative for programming use. For superseded versions, this marks the start of the version's validity period.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this dataset specification is intended for use in an interim analysis (True) or the final analysis (False). Interim analysis datasets may have restricted access and require DSMB/DMC review procedures.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance

description: Comma-separated list of variable names that form the unique record key for this dataset (e.g., 'USUBJID' for ADSL; 'USUBJID, PARAMCD, ADT' for BDS datasets). Defines the grain of the dataset and is critical for merge/join operations in SAS programming.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: Result of the Pinnacle 21 (formerly OpenCDISC) conformance validation run against this ADaM dataset. Pass = no errors; Pass with Warnings = minor issues noted; Fail = conformance errors requiring remediation; Not Run = validation pending. Required for FDA submission readiness.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; Pinnacle 21 Enterprise

description: Statistical programming language used to implement this ADaM dataset specification. SAS is the regulatory standard; R and Python may be used for exploratory or supplemental analyses. Governs QC and validation approach.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; SAS Drug Development

description: Quality control methodology applied to validate this ADaM dataset. Double Programming = independent programmer recreates dataset and compares; Peer Review = senior programmer reviews code and output; Independent QC = separate QC team validates; Not Required = exploratory/non-submission dataset.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current QC completion status for this ADaM dataset. Not Started = QC not yet initiated; In Progress = QC underway; QC Complete = dataset passed QC validation; Failed QC = discrepancies found requiring programmer remediation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; SAS Drug Development

description: SAS display format assigned to this variable (e.g., 'DATE9.', 'DATETIME20.', '$CHAR200.', '8.2'). Governs how values are displayed in SAS output and define.xml DisplayFormat attribute. Required for SAS Drug Development programming specifications.
reference: SAS Drug Development Standards; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: SAS informat used to read raw values into this variable (e.g., 'YYMMDD10.', 'E8601DT.', '$CHAR200.'). Required for date/datetime variables derived from ISO 8601 SDTM character dates. Specified in SAS Drug Development programming specs.
reference: SAS Drug Development Standards; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: File path or program name of the SAS program in SAS Drug Development that produces this ADaM dataset (e.g., '/studies/STUDY001/adam/programs/adsl.sas'). Links the specification to its implementing program for traceability and QC.
reference: SAS Drug Development; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Name of the source SDTM or predecessor ADaM variable(s) from which this variable is derived (e.g., 'AE.AEDECOD', 'DM.RFSTDTC', 'ADSL.TRT01P'). Supports CDISC traceability requirements and audit trail for regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC ADaM Traceability; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current lifecycle status of the ADaM dataset specification. Draft = initial authoring; Under Review = submitted for biostatistics/programming review; Approved = formally signed off for programming use; Superseded = replaced by a newer version; Retired = no longer applicable.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Version number of this ADaM dataset specification document following major.minor versioning (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0). Incremented upon each approved revision to support change control and audit traceability.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this ADaM dataset is included in the regulatory submission package (NDA, BLA, MAA, or IND). True = included in eCTD submission; False = internal analysis only. Governs define.xml inclusion and FDA/EMA conformance validation requirements.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards; ICH M4 CTD; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Date on which this version of the specification was superseded by a newer version. Null for the current active version. Supports version history and audit trail for regulatory inspection.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Indicates whether this variable is a CDISC ADaM timing variable (e.g., ADT, ADTM, ADY, AVISIT, AVISITN). True = timing variable subject to ADaM timing variable conventions. Used for conformance checking and define.xml classification.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this ADaM dataset specification record. Supports change tracking and audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Descriptive SAS variable label (max 256 characters) providing a human-readable description of the variable (e.g., 'Unique Subject Identifier', 'Parameter Code', 'Analysis Value'). Appears in SAS PROC CONTENTS and submission define.xml.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: SAS variable length in bytes. For character variables, specifies maximum string length (e.g., 200 for USUBJID). For numeric variables, typically 8 (SAS default double precision). Required for SAS dataset creation and define.xml generation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; SAS Drug Development Standards

description: SAS-compliant variable name for this specification record (uppercase, max 8 characters per SAS/CDISC convention, e.g., USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVAL, DTYPE). Each row in this table represents one variable within the dataset specification.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; SAS Drug Development Standards

description: SAS data type of the variable. Char = character/string variable; Num = numeric variable. Governs SAS dataset structure and informat/format assignments in programming specifications.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; SAS Drug Development Standards

business_glossary_term: Analysis Data Model (ADaM) Variable Specification ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each ADaM variable specification record within the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.estimand. Business justification: adam_variable_spec currently has estimand_reference: STRING — a free-text reference to an estimand. This should be normalized to a proper FK to biostatistics.estimand. ADaM variables that implement es
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent ADaM dataset to which this variable specification belongs (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADLB). Links variable-level spec to its dataset container.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan that governs the derivation rules and analytical intent for this variable specification.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_factor. Business justification: ADaM ADSL stratification variables (STRAT1, STRAT2) must be derived from the randomization stratification factors. CDISC ADaM programming requires the variable spec to reference the exact stratificati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this ADaM variable specification is defined. Scopes the variable spec to a specific protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice

description: CDISC ADaM structural classification of the parent dataset: ADSL (Subject-Level Analysis Dataset), BDS (Basic Data Structure — one record per subject per parameter per analysis timepoint), OCCDS (Occurrence Data Structure — for events like AEs and CMs), or OTHER.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3

description: Date on which the variable specification was formally approved by the responsible biostatistician or lead programmer. Marks the transition from draft to approved status in the specification lifecycle.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Name of the associated baseline flag variable (e.g., ABLFL) used to identify the baseline record for this analysis variable. Supports change-from-baseline derivations and is required for BDS datasets.
reference: CDISC ADaM BDS Implementation Guide

description: Name of the corresponding change-from-baseline variable (CHG) or percent change-from-baseline variable (PCHG) derived from this analysis variable. Establishes the analytical linkage between AVAL and its change metrics.
reference: CDISC ADaM BDS Implementation Guide

description: Name of the specific CDISC controlled terminology codelist associated with this variable (e.g., NY, DTYPE, PARAMCD, AVISIT). Used in DEFINE-XML generation and submission package validation.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Free-text field for additional notes, clarifications, or reviewer comments regarding the variable specification. May include sponsor-specific guidance, open questions, or cross-references to SAP sections.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Reference to the applicable CDISC controlled terminology codelist, MedDRA dictionary, WHO-DD, or CTCAE version governing the permissible values for this variable. Example: 'CDISC CT DTYPE 2023-09-29', 'MedDRA v26.0 PT', 'CTCAE v5.0'.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; MedDRA; WHO-DD; CTCAE; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: CDISC ADaM core classification indicating the mandatory status of the variable: Required (must be present), Expected (should be present unless not applicable), Permissible (optional, may be included), or Conditionally Required (required when specific conditions are met). Drives compliance validation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Timestamp when this variable specification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Display format specification for this variable as it will appear in the DEFINE-XML submission metadata file (define.xml). Includes display format string and any associated decode mapping for coded values. Required for FDA/EMA electronic submission packages.
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Detailed textual description of the derivation logic used to compute or assign the variable's value. Includes source variable references, transformation rules, imputation methods (e.g., LOCF), and conditional logic. Enables automated SAS program generation and independent QC programmer validation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: SAS format or informat name applied to this variable for display or input purposes (e.g., DATE9., DATETIME20., $CHAR200.). Used in SAS program generation and dataset creation.
reference: SAS Drug Development; SAS Language Reference

description: Description of the missing data imputation method applied to this variable as specified in the SAP. Examples: LOCF (Last Observation Carried Forward), BOCF (Baseline Observation Carried Forward), multiple imputation, worst-case, or none. Critical for regulatory submission transparency.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Missing Data; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Indicates whether this variable is designated as a primary or secondary analysis variable (AVAL, AVALC, or equivalent) used directly in statistical analyses as defined in the SAP.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Indicates whether this variable is part of the dataset's sort key (BY variables) as defined in the ADaM dataset specification. Key variables determine dataset sort order and are required for PROC SORT and merge operations in SAS.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Indicates whether this variable is a PARAM/PARAMCD/PARAMN grouping variable used in BDS (Basic Data Structure) or OCCDS datasets to identify the analysis parameter. Drives parameter-level filtering in TLF production.
reference: CDISC ADaM BDS Implementation Guide

description: CDISC-defined origin classification indicating how the variable value is obtained: Derived (computed from other variables), Assigned (set by sponsor/programmer per protocol), Collected (directly from eCRF/EDC), Protocol (defined in study protocol), or Not Available.
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Name of the analysis population flag variable this specification defines (e.g., ITTFL for Intent-to-Treat, SAFFL for Safety, PPROTFL for Per Protocol, FASFL for Full Analysis Set). Null if the variable is not a population flag.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Name of the predecessor variable from which this variable is directly derived, as required in DEFINE-XML metadata. Establishes the data lineage chain from SDTM collected data through ADaM derived variables for regulatory traceability.
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Date on which the independent QC programmer completed verification of this variable's derivation. Recorded as part of the audit trail for regulatory submission readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Name or identifier of the independent QC programmer responsible for verifying the derivation of this variable through independent programming. Required for dual-programming QC methodology compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current QC lifecycle status of this variable specification. Tracks progression from initial draft through independent QC programmer review to final approval. QC Passed indicates the derivation has been independently verified by a second programmer. Locked indicates the spec is frozen for DBL.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Name or path of the SAS program in SAS Drug Development that implements the derivation for this variable. Enables direct linkage between the variable specification and its executable SAS code for QC and audit purposes.
reference: SAS Drug Development; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Number of significant digits to retain for numeric variables in analysis outputs and TLF displays. Defined in the SAP and applied during SAS program generation to ensure consistent rounding across tables, listings, and figures.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Primary SDTM or ADaM source dataset from which this variable is derived or mapped (e.g., AE, LB, VS, ADSL). Supports traceability from analysis results back to raw clinical data.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Comma-separated list of source variable names (from SDTM datasets or other ADaM datasets) used in the derivation of this variable. Supports data lineage tracing and ALCOA+ compliance. Example: 'SDTM.AE.AESTDTC, SDTM.DM.RFSTDTC'.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Lifecycle status of this variable specification document. Draft indicates work in progress; In Review indicates under biostatistician or sponsor review; Approved indicates finalized and signed off; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Retired indicates no longer applicable.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Version identifier for this variable specification record, following semantic versioning (e.g., v1.0, v1.1, v2.0). Incremented when derivation algorithm, source variables, or controlled terminology references are updated. Supports audit trail and change management.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: Therapeutic area or disease indication for which this variable specification is designed (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Relevant for CDISC Therapeutic Area User Guides (TAUGs) compliance and cross-study reuse.
reference: CDISC Therapeutic Area User Guides (TAUGs); FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this variable specification record. Required for change tracking and audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Human-readable SAS variable label (up to 40 characters) describing the variable's business meaning. Used in SAS output headers, TLF footnotes, and reviewer documentation. Example: 'Analysis Value', 'Parameter Code'.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development

description: SAS storage length for the variable. For Character variables, specifies maximum character width (e.g., 200). For Numeric variables, typically 8 (SAS default double-precision float). Used in SAS dataset creation and DEFINE-XML.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Language Reference

description: The SAS-compliant variable name as defined in the ADaM dataset specification. Must conform to CDISC ADaM naming conventions: uppercase, up to 8 characters, starting with a letter. Examples: USUBJID, AVAL, DTYPE, PARAMCD.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development naming conventions

description: Sequential position of this variable within the ADaM dataset column order as defined in the specification. Determines the column sequence in the SAS dataset and DEFINE-XML variable listing. Required for consistent dataset structure across programming and QC.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development

description: CDISC-defined role classification describing the variable's functional purpose within the dataset structure. Drives DEFINE-XML metadata generation and reviewer interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Identifier|Topic|Timing|Grouping Qualifier|Result Qualifier|Record Qualifier|Synonym Qualifier|Variable Qualifier|Rule — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: SAS data type classification for the variable: Character (text/categorical values) or Numeric (continuous, date, or flag values). Drives SAS program generation and DEFINE-XML metadata.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Language Reference

description: Primary key for database_lock_event

Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: DBL SOPs require documenting which blinding configuration was in effect at database lock — confirming whether lock occurred under blinded or unblinded conditions. Regulatory inspectors verify this for
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: The DMP version in effect at time of lock is a regulatory traceability requirement (ICH E6 R2, 21 CFR Part 11). The DBL event must reference the governing DMP to confirm data collection and review pro
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: A DBL event locks a specific EDC study build version; this is operationally critical for audit trails and unlock procedures. edc_system_lock_reference is a denormalized string reference to the build,
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: Pre-lock QC checklists require reconciliation of all randomized subjects in the locked dataset against the active rand_list version. The subjects_locked_count must match the rand_list's allocated_slot
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_milestone. Business justification: Database lock is a critical regulatory milestone tracked in regulatory timelines for NDA/BLA submission planning. Direct FK from database_lock_event to regulatory_milestone supports critical path anal
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: A database lock event is executed against a specific version of the Statistical Analysis Plan. The SAP version in effect at the time of DBL governs which analyses are performed post-lock. Linking data
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this database lock event applies. Links the DBL event to the parent study master record.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Database lock events directly trigger and gate regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA filing, interim analysis submissions). The DBL date is a prerequisite for submission package finalization. Direct FK enab
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The database lock memo/certificate is a required TMF essential document (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4). Replacing the denormalized lock_memo_reference string with a proper FK to tmf_document enables
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The calendar date on which the database was formally locked. This is the principal business event date for the DBL milestone and is recorded in the eTMF, regulatory submissions, and CSR statistical sections.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM/ADaM

description: Sequential amendment number for post-lock data corrections. Zero for the original lock; increments with each formal unlock and re-lock cycle. Tracks the amendment history of the locked database.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Formal description of the reason for unlocking and re-locking the database after the initial lock. Documents the nature of the data correction or update requiring the amendment. Null for the original lock (amendment_number = 0).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The type of statistical analysis for which this database lock is being performed. Determines the downstream workflow: interim analyses feed DSMB/DMC reviews; final analyses feed CSR and regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA/MAA).
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity

description: Date and time at which the Biostatistics representative provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Indicates whether all medical coding assignments (MedDRA for adverse events/medical history, WHO-DD for concomitant medications) have been completed and verified in the EDC system prior to database lock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MedDRA Guidelines; WHO-DD Guidelines; CDISC SDTM

description: Date and time at which this database lock event record was first created in the lakehouse (Silver Layer). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ standards.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: The clinical data cut-off date defining which subject data and observations are included in this lock. All data with a collection date on or before this date is included in the locked dataset. Critical for interim and partial locks.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E9(R1) Addendum; CDISC ADaM Guidelines

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for this database lock event (e.g., DBL-2024-001). Used in regulatory submissions, TMF filings, and cross-system references in Medidata Rave and Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Date and time at which the Data Management representative provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Notification Date
description: The date on which the DSMB/DMC was formally notified of the database lock and provided access to the locked dataset for their review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DSMB; ICH E9(R1)

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Notification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) must be notified of this database lock event, typically for interim analysis locks where the DSMB reviews unblinded data.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DSMB; ICH E9(R1)

description: Defines the scope of data included in this lock event. Full study locks the entire study database; interim locks a defined data cut for interim analysis; partial locks a subset of data (e.g., specific sites or forms); cohort locks a defined patient cohort; site-specific locks data from one or more named sites.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC Data Management Guidelines; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Current lifecycle status of the database lock event. Tracks progression from planning through execution and any subsequent unlock/re-lock cycles required for data corrections or amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the database lock was executed in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and ALCOA+ data integrity standards.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the lock is a hard lock (no further data changes permitted without formal unlock and re-lock procedure) or a soft lock (data is locked for analysis but minor corrections may still be applied under controlled conditions). Hard locks are required prior to final DBL and regulatory submission.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Date and time at which the Medical Monitor provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Free-text field for additional context, observations, or operational notes related to this database lock event that are not captured in structured fields. Includes deviations from planned timelines or special circumstances.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Number of open data queries (discrepancies) remaining unresolved in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) at the time of database lock. A non-zero count must be formally justified and documented per GCP and ALCOA+ requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; CDISC Data Management Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Formal written justification for proceeding with database lock when outstanding queries remain unresolved. Required by GCP and ALCOA+ when outstanding_query_count is greater than zero. Documented in the DMP and lock memo.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: The originally scheduled calendar date for the database lock event as defined in the Data Management Plan (DMP) and study timeline. Used for milestone tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS and project planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC Data Management Guidelines; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Conduct

business_glossary_term: Pre-Lock Quality Control (QC) Checklist Completion Status
description: Indicates whether all items on the pre-database lock Quality Control (QC) checklist have been completed and signed off prior to lock execution. The checklist covers data cleaning, query resolution, coding completion, and SAE reconciliation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC Data Management Guidelines; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Conduct

business_glossary_term: Pre-Lock Quality Control (QC) Checklist Completion Date
description: The date on which the pre-database lock QC checklist was formally completed and all items signed off. Must precede the actual lock date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Serious Adverse Event (SAE) Reconciliation Completion Status
description: Indicates whether the reconciliation between the EDC system (Medidata Rave) and the safety database (Oracle Argus Safety) for Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) has been completed and confirmed prior to database lock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) has been completed to the required percentage or scope across all sites prior to database lock. SDV confirms EDC data matches source documents per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: Total number of investigative sites whose data is included in this database lock event. Relevant for partial and site-specific lock scopes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: Identifies the specific study part, period, or epoch covered by this lock event (e.g., Part A, Part B, Period 1, Run-in, Treatment, Follow-up). Applicable for multi-part or adaptive study designs.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Total number of study subjects whose data is included in this database lock event. Used for completeness verification and comparison against enrollment targets defined in the SAP.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM Guidelines

description: The date on which treatment assignment unblinding was performed following the database lock. Null if unblinding_required is false or unblinding has not yet occurred.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Indicates whether treatment unblinding is required as part of or immediately following this database lock event. Applicable for blinded studies where the lock precedes the formal unblinding procedure for final or interim analysis.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Date and time at which the database was formally unlocked to permit post-lock data corrections. Null if the database has not been unlocked. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Date and time at which this database lock event record was last modified in the lakehouse (Silver Layer). Tracks record-level changes for audit trail and data lineage compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each interim analysis record within the clinical trial biostatistics domain.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; CDISC ADaM

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: interim_analysis has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which population was analyzed at the interim. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: Interim analyses must operate under a defined blinding configuration governing firewall maintenance, unblinding scope, and independent statistician access. ICH E9(R1) and DSMB charters require that in
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.commitment. Business justification: Post-approval interim analyses are often mandated by regulatory commitments (post-marketing study conditions of approval). Direct FK from interim_analysis to commitment supports commitment fulfillment
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: An interim analysis is performed on data that has been locked via a database lock event. The FK links the interim analysis to the specific DBL event that defined the data cut, enabling audit traceabil
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: Interim analyses are planned at epoch boundaries (end of treatment epoch, end of follow-up). This FK enables DSMB meeting scheduling aligned to epoch completion, alpha-spending function application at
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: Interim analyses are conducted on subjects randomized under a specific rand_list version active at the data cutoff date. DSMB reporting requires the interim analysis to cite the exact randomization li
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Adaptive trial interim analyses (sample size re-estimation, population enrichment) directly trigger protocol amendments submitted to health authorities. The interim_analysis has 'adaptation_descriptio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.safety_report_submission. Business justification: Interim analyses generating safety signals trigger expedited IND safety reports (DSURs, SUSARs). The interim_analysis has 'regulatory_submission_required' flag. This FK links the interim analysis data
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) version that governs the methodology and pre-specified rules for this interim analysis.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Each interim analysis uses a specific locked SDTM dataset version at the data cutoff date. Data managers and statisticians must trace which SDTM dataset was used for each interim analysis for audit, r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: An interim analysis evaluates one or more statistical endpoints defined in the SAP. Linking interim_analysis to statistical_endpoint provides direct traceability of which endpoint was assessed at each
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent clinical study for which this interim analysis is conducted.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_milestone. Business justification: Interim analyses are tracked as critical-path milestones in CTMS. Linking interim_analysis to study_milestone enables integrated timeline management, contractual milestone reporting to sponsors, and r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Interim efficacy/safety data from adaptive trials may be submitted to health authorities. The interim_analysis has 'regulatory_submission_required' flag; linking to submission tracks which submission
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Interim analysis statistical reports and data packages are essential TMF documents filed per GCP requirements. Linking interim_analysis to tmf_document enables TMF completeness tracking and traceabili
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.signal. Business justification: Interim analyses may formally confirm or trigger a safety signal that meets stopping criteria. Role-prefix 'triggered_' distinguishes this from a general signal reference; DSMB packages and regulatory
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual calendar date on which the interim analysis was executed and results were produced. May differ from planned date due to enrollment pace or operational factors.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Version identifier of the CDISC ADaM datasets used as input to this interim analysis, ensuring traceability between the analysis results and the underlying data snapshot.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Data Standards Catalog; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Narrative description of any pre-specified design adaptation implemented as a result of this interim analysis (e.g., sample size re-estimation, dose selection, population enrichment). Populated only when analysis_outcome is 'adapt'.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: The portion of the overall one-sided or two-sided alpha (Type I error rate) allocated to this specific interim look by the alpha spending function. Used to derive the efficacy stopping boundary.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: The pre-specified alpha spending function used to allocate the overall Type I error rate across interim looks, as defined in the SAP. Common functions include O'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming approximation, Lan-DeMets Pocock approximation, Hwang-Shih-DeCani, or a custom function.
reference: ICH E9(R1); Lan & DeMets (1983); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Numeric parameter (gamma) for the selected alpha spending function, where applicable (e.g., the gamma parameter for Hwang-Shih-DeCani function). Null for functions that do not require a parameter.
reference: ICH E9(R1); Hwang, Shih & DeCani (1990)

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this interim analysis, used in regulatory submissions, DSMB/DMC charters, and TMF documentation (e.g., IA-STUDY001-001).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; CDISC ADaM

description: Sequential ordinal number identifying this interim analysis within the study (e.g., 1 for first interim, 2 for second interim). Used to track the series of planned looks at accumulating data.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: The formal decision or recommendation resulting from the interim analysis: continue (study proceeds as planned), stop_efficacy (early termination for demonstrated benefit), stop_futility (early termination for lack of benefit), stop_safety (early termination for safety concerns), adapt (pre-specified design adaptation implemented), or pending (DSMB/DMC recommendation not yet issued).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: Current lifecycle state of the interim analysis: planned (scheduled but not yet initiated), in_progress (analysis underway), completed (results finalized), cancelled (analysis removed from plan), or on_hold (temporarily suspended).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Classification of the interim analysis by its primary statistical purpose: efficacy (test for early stopping due to benefit), futility (test for early stopping due to lack of benefit), safety (review of accumulating safety data), adaptive (pre-specified adaptation of design parameters), combined_efficacy_futility, or administrative (data review without hypothesis testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|futility|safety|adaptive|combined_efficacy_futility|administrative — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: Probability of achieving a statistically significant result at the final analysis, conditional on the data observed at this interim look and assuming the originally hypothesized treatment effect. Used in futility assessments.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; Lan & Wittes (1988)

description: Data classification level assigned to the interim analysis results and associated outputs, controlling access and distribution. Interim analysis results are typically restricted to prevent unblinding of the broader study team.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; GDPR; HIPAA; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp when this interim analysis record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: The date through which subject data was included in the interim analysis dataset. All data collected after this date is excluded from the analysis. Critical for regulatory traceability and blinding integrity.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Data

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) / Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) Involvement Flag
description: Indicates whether a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) reviewed the unblinded interim analysis results (True = DSMB/DMC reviewed; False = internal review only).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DMCs 2006; EMA Guideline on DMCs

description: Date on which the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) convened to review the interim analysis results and issue a recommendation.
reference: FDA Guidance on DMCs 2006; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Pre-specified critical value (e.g., z-statistic or p-value threshold) for the efficacy stopping boundary at this interim look. If the observed test statistic exceeds this boundary, early stopping for efficacy is triggered.
reference: ICH E9(R1); O'Brien & Fleming (1979); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Number of primary endpoint events (e.g., deaths, disease progression events) observed as of the data cutoff date. Applicable for event-driven trials; used to compute the actual information fraction.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM ADTTE; FDA Oncology Guidance

description: Total number of primary endpoint events required for the final analysis as specified in the SAP and sample size calculation. Used as the denominator for the information fraction in event-driven trials.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Indicates whether a blinding firewall was maintained between the unblinded interim analysis team and the blinded study team (True = firewall in place; False = firewall not maintained or not applicable). Critical for regulatory compliance in adaptive trials.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: Indicates whether the futility stopping boundary is binding (True = mandatory stop if crossed; False = non-binding/advisory only). Binding futility boundaries affect the overall Type I error calculation.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Pre-specified critical value for the futility stopping boundary at this interim look. If the observed test statistic falls below this boundary, early stopping for futility (lack of efficacy) may be triggered. May be binding or non-binding per the SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: Observed proportion of total planned statistical information actually accumulated at the time of this interim analysis. May differ from planned fraction due to enrollment variability or event rate changes.
reference: ICH E9(R1); Lan-DeMets alpha spending framework

description: Pre-specified proportion of the total planned statistical information (e.g., events, subjects) accumulated at the time of this interim analysis, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.5000 = 50% of planned events). Drives alpha spending calculations.
reference: ICH E9(R1); Lan-DeMets alpha spending framework; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, operational notes, deviations from the pre-specified plan, or DSMB/DMC commentary relevant to this interim analysis that is not captured in structured fields.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: The nominal p-value from the primary hypothesis test at this interim analysis. Must be interpreted in the context of the alpha spending boundary, not as a standalone significance threshold.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The actual computed test statistic (e.g., z-score, chi-square, log-rank statistic) from the interim analysis dataset. Compared against efficacy and futility boundaries to determine the outcome.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM; SAS Drug Development

description: Pre-specified calendar date on which the interim analysis is planned to be conducted, as documented in the SAP and DSMB/DMC charter.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the interim analysis results or the resulting design adaptation must be submitted to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.) as part of an IND, NDA, BLA, or MAA amendment (True = submission required; False = not required).
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; FDA IND Regulations; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E9(R1)

description: Indicates whether the interim analysis results have been disclosed externally (e.g., to regulators, in a public registry, or in a publication) prior to study completion (True = disclosed; False = not disclosed). Tracks blinding integrity at the study level.
reference: ICH E9(R1); WHO ICTRP; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA Transparency Guidance

description: Updated total sample size determined through the pre-specified sample size re-estimation procedure at this interim analysis. Null if sample_size_reestimated is False.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Indicates whether a pre-specified sample size re-estimation was performed as part of this interim analysis (True = sample size re-estimated; False = no re-estimation performed).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Version identifier of the SAS Drug Development program or analysis package used to execute this interim analysis, enabling reproducibility and audit traceability.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC ADaM; SAS Drug Development platform

description: Narrative description of the specific stopping rule(s) triggered at this interim analysis, including the criterion crossed and the observed vs. boundary values. Populated only when stopping_rule_triggered is True.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Indicates whether any pre-specified stopping rule (efficacy, futility, or safety) was triggered by the results of this interim analysis (True = stopping rule crossed; False = no stopping rule triggered).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019

description: Total number of subjects enrolled in the study as of the data cutoff date for this interim analysis. Provides context for the information fraction and statistical power.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC SDTM DM domain

description: Date on which formal authorization was granted to unblind the interim analysis data, as documented in the unblinding log. Required for audit trail and blinding integrity compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles

description: Defines the extent of treatment assignment unblinding performed for this interim analysis: full (all treatment assignments revealed to the analysis team), partial (only aggregate or summary-level unblinding), or none (blinded analysis only). Critical for blinding integrity management and regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance 2019; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp when this interim analysis record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each produced TLF output instance. Serves as the primary key for the tlf_output data product within the biostatistics domain.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports

description: Reference to the specific ADaM dataset version used as input for generating this TLF output, ensuring full traceability from output back to source analysis data.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: tlf_output has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which population the output covers. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integrity and enables
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.ctd_dossier. Business justification: TLF outputs are packaged into CTD Module 5 of the regulatory dossier. Regulatory publishing teams must track which TLF outputs are included in each dossier version. Direct FK supports dossier complete
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) version that governs the analysis methodology, population definitions, and output specifications for this TLF.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: A TLF output instance is produced to present results for a specific statistical endpoint. One statistical endpoint can generate multiple TLF outputs across production runs, versions, and interim/final
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this TLF output was produced. Links the output to the governing study protocol and sponsor context.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Finalized TLF outputs (tables, listings, figures) are included in CTD Module 5 of NDA/BLA/MAA submissions. The tlf_output has 'submission_package_ref' (plain text denormalization). This FK enables sub
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the TLF shell specification that defines the structure, layout, and metadata template from which this output was produced.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development TLF Shell Specification

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Final TLF outputs (tables, listings, figures) are filed as TMF documents within the CSR package in the eTMF. This link enables filing status tracking and inspection-readiness verification confirming e
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Classification of the analysis type this TLF output supports: primary (confirmatory), secondary, exploratory, interim (pre-specified), or sensitivity analysis. Determines the regulatory weight and reporting requirements for the output.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance

description: Final approval lifecycle status of this TLF output. Approved outputs are eligible for inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and regulatory submissions. Superseded outputs have been replaced by a newer version.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date and time when the TLF output received formal approval, captured as an electronic signature event per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. Null if not yet approved.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether this TLF output was produced from blinded or unblinded data. Unblinded outputs are restricted to authorized personnel only and are subject to strict access controls to preserve trial integrity prior to database lock.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; DSMB/DMC charter requirements

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, production notes, known limitations, or reviewer comments associated with this TLF output instance. Supports communication between programmers, biostatisticians, and medical writers.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Date and time when this TLF output record was first created in the system, capturing the initial registration of the production run. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reference to the specific section of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where this TLF output is cited or embedded (e.g., Section 11.4.1, Appendix 16.1.9). Supports CSR authoring traceability per ICH E3 structure.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; FDA NDA/BLA Submission Requirements

description: Indicates whether this TLF output was produced from a pre-database lock (DBL), post-database lock, or interim analysis dataset. Critical for distinguishing final regulatory submission outputs from exploratory or interim analyses.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; CDISC ADaM

description: Version label of the ADaM dataset used as input for this TLF production run (e.g., v1.0, v2.3-interim). Ensures traceability between the output and the exact data snapshot used, critical for database lock (DBL) compliance.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which this TLF output was formally delivered to the sponsor, regulatory team, or CSR authoring team. Used for milestone tracking and contractual deliverable compliance.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; Oracle Siebel CTMS milestone tracking

description: Total number of discrepancies identified between the primary programmer output and the QC programmer output during validation. A count of zero indicates a clean validation pass. Non-zero counts require resolution before final approval.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Statistical Review Guidance

description: Narrative description of how identified discrepancies between primary and QC programmer outputs were investigated and resolved. Required when discrepancy_count is greater than zero to support audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this TLF output instance is the final approved version intended for inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) or regulatory submission. Only one version per output_number should have this flag set to True.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Functional category of the TLF output indicating the statistical domain it covers (e.g., demographics, efficacy, safety, pharmacokinetics, laboratory). Used for organizing outputs within the CSR and regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demographics|efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetics|laboratory|vital_signs|exposure|disposition|concomitant_medications|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; CDISC SDTM/ADaM

description: File format of the produced TLF output (e.g., RTF for Word-compatible tables, PDF for final regulatory submissions, SAS7BDAT for dataset outputs). Determines compatibility with CSR authoring tools and eCTD submission requirements.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; eCTD Submission Guidance; ICH M8 eCTD

description: Name of the output file generated by the SAS program (e.g., t_14_1_1_demog.rtf, l_16_2_3_ae.pdf). Used for file management, eTMF archival, and regulatory submission packaging.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; eCTD Submission Guidance; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Full repository or file system path to the stored TLF output file in SAS Drug Development or the eTMF system. Enables direct retrieval for CSR authoring, regulatory review, and inspection readiness.
reference: SAS Drug Development; Veeva Vault eTMF; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this TLF output as defined in the SAP or TLF shell specification (e.g., T-14.1.1, L-16.2.3, F-11.4). Used for cross-referencing in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Full descriptive title of the TLF output as it appears in the header of the produced document (e.g., 'Summary of Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — Safety Population'). Used for CSR cross-referencing and regulatory submission.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; CDISC ADaM

description: Classification of the statistical output as a Table (T), Listing (L), or Figure (F), corresponding to the three standard output categories in clinical trial reporting per ICH E3 and CDISC conventions.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

description: Version label of this TLF output instance (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, vFinal). Tracks the production history of the output across multiple runs, enabling identification of the current approved version versus superseded drafts.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Environment in which this TLF output was generated: development (exploratory/testing), validation (UAT), or production (regulatory-grade). Only production-environment outputs are eligible for regulatory submission.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; FDA Guidance on Computer System Validation; GAMP 5

description: Date and time when the SAS program was executed to produce this TLF output instance. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the production lifecycle, supporting ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ALCOA+; SAS Drug Development

description: Date and time when the QC validation process for this TLF output was completed and the qc_status was set to a terminal state (passed, failed, or waived). Supports milestone tracking and SLA compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Current QC validation status of this TLF output. Indicates whether the output has passed independent validation, is pending review, is under active review, has failed validation, or has been formally waived per documented justification.

description: Reference to the section of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where results for this estimand are reported (e.g., 'Section 11.4.1'). Supports ICH E3 CSR structure compliance and regulatory submission traceability.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; ICH E9(R1); FDA/EMA eCTD submission requirements

description: The inferential objective of the estimand — whether the analysis is designed to demonstrate superiority, non-inferiority, or equivalence of the treatment condition relative to the comparator.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E10 Choice of Control Group; FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials

description: Date from which this version of the estimand definition is operationally effective for analysis execution. Used for version control when estimands are amended mid-study.
reference: ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR Part 11; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Statistical nature of the endpoint variable. Determines the appropriate population-level summary measure and analysis methodology (e.g., continuous endpoints use mean difference; time-to-event use hazard ratio).
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the estimand, used in SAP documents, regulatory submissions, and cross-functional communications. Follows the pattern EST-{STUDYCODE}{SEQ}.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA/EMA regulatory submission conventions

description: Current lifecycle state of the estimand definition record within the SAP authoring and regulatory submission workflow. Drives version control and approval gating in SAS Drug Development.
reference: ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the estimand within the study's endpoint hierarchy. Determines regulatory weight and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|key_secondary|secondary|exploratory|sensitivity — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance for Industry: Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials

description: Narrative description of the specific intercurrent event(s) addressed by this estimand (e.g., 'Discontinuation of study treatment due to adverse event', 'Initiation of rescue medication'). Provides regulatory-submission-ready documentation.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.2; FDA Estimand Framework Guidance

description: The primary strategy applied to handle intercurrent events (ICEs) for this estimand, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.2. Defines how post-ICE data are treated in the analysis (e.g., treatment policy ignores ICE occurrence; hypothetical posits ICE did not occur).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.2 — Strategies for Handling Intercurrent Events; EMA Reflection Paper on Estimands

description: Flag indicating whether this estimand is evaluated at one or more pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., for DSMB/DMC review, adaptive design decision points, or early stopping rules).
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; DSMB Charter; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Flag indicating whether this estimand is part of the confirmatory (inferential) testing hierarchy, as opposed to exploratory or descriptive analyses. Confirmatory estimands are subject to multiplicity control procedures defined in the SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E9 Statistical Principles; FDA Multiple Endpoints Guidance

description: Flag indicating whether this estimand corresponds to the study's primary endpoint. Primary endpoint estimands carry the highest regulatory weight and drive sample size calculations and confirmatory testing procedures.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance for Industry

description: Short human-readable label or title for the estimand (e.g., 'Primary Efficacy Estimand', 'Key Secondary Estimand 1'). Used in TLF headers, CSR statistical sections, and regulatory submission narratives.
reference: ICH E9(R1); EMA Guideline on the Investigation of Subgroups in Confirmatory Clinical Trials

description: The statistical strategy applied to handle missing outcome data for this estimand (e.g., Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), multiple imputation, mixed model for repeated measures). Distinct from the ICE strategy — addresses data gaps rather than post-ICE observations.
reference: ICH E9(R1); NRC Panel on Handling Missing Data in Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance on Missing Data

description: The statistical method used to control Type I error across multiple estimands or endpoints in the confirmatory testing hierarchy (e.g., 'Bonferroni', 'Hochberg', 'Hierarchical testing', 'Gatekeeping procedure'). Null if estimand is exploratory.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints in Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: The pre-specified non-inferiority margin (delta) for this estimand, applicable when the effect direction is non-inferiority. Expressed in the units of the summary measure. Null for superiority or equivalence estimands.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on the Choice of the Non-Inferiority Margin

description: Free-text field for additional biostatistician commentary, regulatory discussion points, or cross-references not captured in structured fields. Supports regulatory submission narrative preparation and internal review discussions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E3 CSR Structure

description: The 'Summary Measure' component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework — the statistical measure used to summarize the endpoint at the population level (e.g., 'Difference in means', 'Odds ratio', 'Hazard ratio', 'Difference in proportions').
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.1 — Summary Measure; CDISC ADaM; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: The primary regulatory framework or agency guideline under which this estimand is defined and will be submitted. Determines the specific regulatory expectations for estimand documentation in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICH_E9R1|FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance for Industry; EMA Scientific Advice; PMDA Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this estimand definition is currently aligned with the approved SAP version. Misalignment triggers a SAP amendment workflow. Critical for regulatory inspection readiness and DBL (Database Lock) governance.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Statistical Guidance; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Specific section number or identifier within the SAP document where this estimand is formally defined and described (e.g., 'Section 4.2.1'). Enables direct cross-referencing between the estimand record and the governing SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Statistical Guidance; CDISC SAP Template

description: Description of the associated sensitivity analyses planned to assess the robustness of the primary estimand result to deviations from assumptions, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.8. References specific SAP sections where sensitivity analyses are detailed.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.8 — Sensitivity Analysis; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Date on which this estimand version was superseded by a newer version due to a SAP amendment or protocol change. Null for the current active version. Supports historical audit trail.
reference: ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles

description: Description of supplementary analyses associated with this estimand that provide additional context or explore alternative assumptions beyond the primary and sensitivity analyses, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.9.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.9 — Supplementary Analysis

description: Narrative description of the 'Population' component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework — the set of subjects to whom the question of interest applies (e.g., 'All randomized patients with Type 2 Diabetes and baseline HbA1c ≥ 7.5%').
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.1 — Population

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this estimand record. Used for change tracking, SAP version management, and regulatory inspection readiness in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Version identifier of the estimand definition (e.g., '1.0', '2.1'). Incremented upon each approved amendment to the SAP or protocol that affects the estimand specification.
reference: ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
