reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this sample size calculation was performed.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM

description: The assumed patient accrual (enrollment) period in months, used in time-to-event sample size calculations to determine the number of events required.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The randomization allocation ratio between treatment arms (e.g., '1:1', '2:1', '1:1:1'), specifying the relative number of subjects assigned to each arm.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM Randomization Standards

description: The pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level, alpha) used in the sample size calculation, typically 0.05 for two-sided or 0.025 for one-sided tests.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity

description: The alpha-spending function applied to control Type I error across planned interim analyses (e.g., O'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets). Null or 'none' if no interim analysis is planned.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: Narrative justification for revising a prior version of the sample size calculation, such as updated assumptions from interim data, protocol amendment, or regulatory feedback.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Protocol Amendment Guidance; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The date on which the sample size calculation was formally approved by the responsible biostatistician, marking readiness for inclusion in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles

description: The date on which this sample size calculation was performed or finalized, serving as the principal business event date for regulatory traceability.
reference: ICH E9; ALCOA+ principles

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code or reference number assigned to this sample size calculation, used in regulatory submissions and protocol documentation (e.g., SSC-2024-001).
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Sample Size Calculations

description: Current lifecycle status of the sample size calculation record, from initial draft through regulatory approval to archival.
reference: ICH E9; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Version number of this sample size calculation (e.g., v1.0, v2.1), enabling tracking of revisions driven by protocol amendments or updated assumptions.
reference: ICH E9; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date and time when this sample size calculation record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2)

description: The assumed proportion of enrolled subjects expected to discontinue or be lost to follow-up, used to inflate the calculated sample size to ensure adequate evaluable subjects.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Missing Data in Clinical Trials

description: The clinically meaningful difference or effect size assumed between treatment arms (e.g., mean difference, odds ratio, hazard ratio) used as the basis for the sample size calculation.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Sample Size Calculations

description: Unit of measurement for the assumed effect size (e.g., mmHg, %, hazard ratio, odds ratio, mean difference), providing dimensional context for regulatory interpretation.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM Unit Standards

description: The assumed event rate or response rate in the control arm, applicable for binary or time-to-event endpoints. Null for continuous endpoints.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The assumed event rate or response rate in the treatment arm, applicable for binary or time-to-event endpoints. Null for continuous endpoints.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The assumed minimum follow-up period in months after the last patient enrolled, used in time-to-event calculations to ensure sufficient events are observed.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Oncology Endpoint Guidance

description: The statistical hypothesis framework applied (superiority, non-inferiority, equivalence), which determines the directionality and margin used in the sample size formula.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E10 Choice of Control Group; FDA Non-Inferiority Guidance

description: Indicates whether one or more interim analyses are planned for this study, which may require alpha-spending adjustments to the sample size calculation.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Adaptive Design Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs

description: The assumed median survival time in the control arm for time-to-event endpoints, used in log-rank or exponential model-based sample size calculations. Null for non-survival endpoints.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Oncology Endpoint Guidance

description: The pre-specified non-inferiority or equivalence margin (delta) used in the hypothesis test, applicable when hypothesis_type is non_inferiority or equivalence. Null for superiority designs.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E10; FDA Non-Inferiority Guidance 2016

description: Total number of treatment arms (including control) in the study design, used to determine the allocation structure and multiplicity adjustments.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E10

description: The minimum number of primary endpoint events (e.g., deaths, progressions) required to achieve the specified power for time-to-event analyses. Null for non-event-driven endpoints.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Oncology Endpoint Guidance; EMA Guideline on Survival Analysis

description: The pre-specified statistical power (1 minus Type II error rate, beta) used in the calculation, representing the probability of detecting the assumed effect size. Typically 0.80 or 0.90.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Statistical nature of the primary efficacy endpoint (e.g., continuous, binary, time-to-event, ordinal, count) that drives the choice of sample size formula.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E9(R1) Estimands Addendum; CDISC ADaM

description: Citation or reference for the data source used to derive the assumed effect size, standard deviation, or event rate (e.g., published literature citation, prior Phase II study identifier, internal pilot data).
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; ALCOA+ principles

description: The calculated number of subjects required per treatment arm to achieve the specified power at the given alpha level, accounting for the assumed effect size and standard deviation.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Name of the validated statistical software used to perform the sample size calculation (e.g., PASS, nQuery Advisor, SAS PROC POWER, R), required for regulatory audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E9; CDISC Validation Standards

description: Version number of the statistical software used (e.g., PASS 2023, nQuery 9.1), required for reproducibility and regulatory validation documentation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 820 (software validation)

description: The assumed population standard deviation for continuous endpoints, derived from prior studies or literature, used in the sample size formula. Null for non-continuous endpoints.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The specific statistical formula or method used for the calculation (e.g., two-sample t-test, log-rank test, chi-square test, Kaplan-Meier), providing methodological traceability.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; CDISC ADaM

description: The clinical trial design type (e.g., parallel group, crossover, factorial, adaptive) that determines the statistical methodology applied in the sample size calculation.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance

description: The total number of subjects required across all treatment arms before applying the dropout inflation, representing the minimum evaluable sample.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The date and time when this sample size calculation record was most recently modified, supporting version control and audit trail requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2)

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Statistical alpha spending function used to allocate the overall significance level across planned interim and final analyses (e.g., O'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets). Applicable only when interim analyses are planned.

description: Classifies the type of statistical analysis governed by this SAP. 'interim' for pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., DSMB/DMC reviews); 'final' for the primary confirmatory analysis; 'post_hoc' for unplanned analyses; 'exploratory' for hypothesis-generating analyses.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; EMA Guideline on Data Monitoring Committees

description: Date on which this SAP version received final approval from all required signatories (biostatistics lead, sponsor, and/or medical monitor). Must precede database lock (DBL) for confirmatory trials. Critical regulatory milestone.

description: Name of the system used to author and manage this SAP document (e.g., SAS Drug Development, Veeva Vault). Supports system-of-record traceability and integration lineage.

description: Indicates the blinding state of the SAP at the time of authoring or approval. 'blinded' means the SAP was finalized before unblinding; 'unblinded' means finalized post-unblinding (e.g., for final CSR); 'partially_unblinded' applies to interim analyses with limited unblinding. Critical for regulatory integrity.
reference: ICH E9 Section 4.1; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; EMA Reflection Paper on Methodological Issues

description: Timestamp when this SAP record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which the clinical database was locked (DBL) for the analysis covered by this SAP. The SAP must be approved prior to or on this date for confirmatory analyses. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Database Lock; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Review Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this SAP includes or supports interim analyses to be reviewed by a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). Drives interim analysis planning and blinding procedures.
reference: ICH E9 Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees; EMA Guideline on Data Monitoring Committees

description: Date from which this SAP version is considered effective and binding for statistical analyses. Typically the approval date. Supports version history and regulatory audit trail.
reference: ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which this SAP version was superseded by a newer version or retired. Null for the currently active version. Supports version history management and regulatory audit trail.
reference: ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Description of the treatment effect estimand(s) as defined per ICH E9(R1), including the population, variable (endpoint), intercurrent events handling strategy, and summary measure. Mandatory for SAPs submitted to FDA and EMA under ICH E9(R1) framework.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands and Sensitivity Analysis; EMA Reflection Paper on Estimands

description: Number of pre-specified interim analyses planned for this study as defined in the SAP. Zero if no interim analyses are planned. Drives alpha spending function selection and DSMB/DMC meeting scheduling.
reference: ICH E9 Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; EMA Guideline on Data Monitoring Committees

description: Indicates whether this record represents the currently active and approved version of the SAP for the study. Only one version per study should have this flag set to True at any given time.
reference: ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records

description: Formal definition of the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as specified in the SAP, including inclusion/exclusion criteria for subjects in this population. The ITT population is the primary analysis population for most confirmatory trials per ICH E9.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.2.2; FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; EMA Guideline on Missing Data

description: Pre-specified strategy for handling missing data in the primary and key secondary analyses (e.g., multiple imputation, LOCF (Last Observation Carried Forward), MMRM, tipping point analysis). Regulatory agencies require explicit pre-specification of missing data approaches.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; EMA Guideline on Missing Data; FDA Guidance on Missing Data

description: Statistical method used to control the family-wise error rate (FWER) or false discovery rate (FDR) when testing multiple hypotheses (e.g., Bonferroni, Holm, Hochberg, Dunnett, hierarchical testing, Benjamini-Hochberg). Required for confirmatory trials with multiple endpoints or doses.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.6; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues in Clinical Trials; FDA Multiple Endpoints Guidance

description: Pre-specified statistical power (1 - beta) expressed as a percentage (e.g., 80.00, 90.00) used in the sample size calculation. Represents the probability of detecting a true treatment effect of the assumed magnitude.
reference: ICH E9 Section 3.5; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Formal definition of the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as specified in the SAP, including criteria for protocol deviations that would exclude subjects. Used as a sensitivity analysis population alongside ITT.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.2.3; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Populations

description: Full narrative definition of the primary efficacy or safety endpoint as specified in the SAP, including the outcome measure, timepoint, and measurement scale. Aligned with the study protocol and regulatory submission.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.2; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Description of the primary statistical methodology applied to the primary endpoint analysis (e.g., ANCOVA, mixed-effects model for repeated measures (MMRM), Cox proportional hazards, logistic regression). Sourced from SAS Drug Development SAP authoring.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.3; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Missing Data

description: Classification of the regulatory impact of this SAP version or amendment. 'none' for administrative changes; 'minor' for non-material updates; 'major' for changes affecting primary analysis; 'requires_submission' for changes requiring regulatory notification or submission.
reference: ICH E9; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Identifies the regulatory submission type(s) this SAP is aligned to support (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD). Ensures the SAP meets the statistical requirements of the target regulatory agency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|CTD|eCTD|none — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA); FDA 21 CFR Part 601 (BLA); EMA MAA Guidelines; ICH CTD M4S

description: Formal definition of the safety analysis population as specified in the SAP, typically all randomized subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment. Used for all safety and adverse event analyses.

description: Total number of subjects planned for enrollment as specified in the SAP sample size calculation section. Derived from power calculations and protocol-specified assumptions. Used for enrollment tracking and regulatory review.

description: Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the SAP document, typically assigned by the sponsor or CRO (e.g., SAP-STUDY001-001). Used for regulatory submissions and cross-system referencing in Veeva Vault and SAS Drug Development.

description: Comma-separated list or narrative description of SAP sections that were modified in this amendment (e.g., 'Section 3.1 Primary Endpoint, Section 5.2 Multiplicity Adjustment'). Supports change impact assessment and regulatory review.

description: Description of pre-specified sensitivity analyses designed to assess the robustness of primary analysis conclusions to key assumptions (e.g., missing data assumptions, outlier handling, covariate selection). Required per ICH E9(R1).

description: Full descriptive title of the Statistical Analysis Plan document, typically including the study name, indication, and phase (e.g., 'Statistical Analysis Plan for a Phase III Randomized Controlled Trial of Drug X in Indication Y').

description: Date on which the study blind was broken for the analysis covered by this SAP version. Null if the SAP was finalized while the study remained blinded. Supports regulatory audit trail for pre-specification of analyses.
reference: ICH E9 Section 4.1; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this SAP record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this amendment modifies the interim analysis plan, including timing, stopping rules, alpha spending, or DSMB/DMC charter alignment.

description: Indicates whether this amendment includes a change to the primary efficacy endpoint definition or analysis. Primary endpoint changes post-DBL are subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny.

description: Indicates whether this amendment modifies the randomization scheme or stratification factors used in the statistical analysis. Changes to randomization factors require protocol-level review.

description: Indicates whether this amendment requires formal notification to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.) as part of a protocol amendment or IND safety report.

reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Comma-separated list of pre-specified covariates and stratification factors included in the statistical model for this endpoint (e.g., 'baseline_value, region, age_group'). Defined in the SAP per EMA guideline on adjustment for baseline covariates.

description: The statistical measure used to quantify the treatment effect for this endpoint (e.g., 'Mean Difference', 'Hazard Ratio', 'Odds Ratio', 'Risk Difference', 'Least Squares Mean Difference'). Specified in the SAP and reported in TLFs and the CSR.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC ADaM; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Hierarchical classification of the endpoint within the study's inferential framework: primary (confirmatory, controls Type I error), secondary (supportive), exploratory (hypothesis-generating), or safety (adverse event-related). Drives multiplicity adjustment strategy.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the endpoint, used in SAP documents, TLF shells, and regulatory submissions. Follows the pattern EP-{STUDY_CODE}-{SEQ}.
reference: CDISC ADaM; ICH E9; FDA Statistical Analysis Plan Guidance

description: Full narrative description of the statistical endpoint as stated in the SAP, including the precise definition of the outcome measure, population, and timeframe.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Short, human-readable name of the statistical endpoint as defined in the SAP (e.g., 'Overall Survival', 'Change from Baseline in HbA1c at Week 24'). Used in TLF titles and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC ADaM; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: Current lifecycle status of the statistical endpoint definition within the SAP authoring and versioning workflow: draft (being authored), under_review (in statistical/medical review), approved (finalized in SAP), locked (post-database lock, immutable), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or retired (no longer active).
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Analysis Plan Guidance; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Scientific domain classification of the endpoint: efficacy (clinical benefit), pharmacokinetic (PK - drug exposure), pharmacodynamic (PD - drug effect), biomarker (biological marker), patient_reported_outcome (PRO - patient self-report), or safety. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic|biomarker|patient_reported_outcome|safety — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9; CDISC ADaM; FDA Guidance on Biomarker Qualification; EMA PRO Guidance

description: Type of statistical hypothesis being tested for this endpoint: superiority (treatment better than control), non-inferiority (treatment not worse by a defined margin), equivalence (treatment within bounds of control), or exploratory (no formal hypothesis). Drives regulatory interpretation of results.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on Non-Inferiority

description: Indicates whether this endpoint is included in one or more pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., for Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) review or adaptive design decision rules). True if the endpoint is evaluated at interim; False if final analysis only.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs; DSMB Charter

description: Date on which this endpoint definition was locked following database lock (DBL), rendering it immutable for regulatory submission purposes. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Analysis Plan Guidance; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Statistical measurement scale of the endpoint variable: continuous (numeric), binary (two outcomes), categorical (nominal groups), ordinal (ranked categories), or time_to_event (survival/event-time). Determines the appropriate statistical test method.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC ADaM; FDA Statistical Guidance on Reporting for Clinical Trials

description: Pre-specified method for handling missing data for this endpoint (e.g., 'Multiple Imputation', 'Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF)', 'Baseline Observation Carried Forward (BOCF)', 'Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM)', 'Complete Case Analysis'). Defined in the SAP per ICH E9(R1). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple_imputation|locf|bocf|mmrm|complete_case|worst_case — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Missing Data; EMA Guideline on Missing Data

description: Statistical method applied to control the family-wise error rate (FWER) or false discovery rate (FDR) when testing multiple endpoints: Bonferroni, Hochberg, Holm, Benjamini-Hochberg, fixed-sequence, gatekeeping, or none. Required for regulatory submissions with multiple primary or secondary endpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bonferroni|hochberg|holm|benjamini_hochberg|fixed_sequence|gatekeeping|none — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Name or label of the multiplicity testing family or hierarchy to which this endpoint belongs (e.g., 'Primary Family', 'Secondary Family 1'). Used to group endpoints within a gatekeeping or hierarchical testing procedure defined in the SAP.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Pre-specified non-inferiority margin (delta) for non-inferiority or equivalence endpoints, representing the largest clinically acceptable difference between treatment and control. Required by FDA and EMA for non-inferiority trial designs.
reference: FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on Non-Inferiority; ICH E9

description: Formal statement of the null hypothesis (H0) for this endpoint as specified in the SAP (e.g., 'There is no difference in overall survival between treatment and control arms'). Required for confirmatory endpoints per ICH E9.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; FDA Statistical Analysis Plan Guidance

description: Regulatory authority-specific designation of this endpoint indicating its role in submissions to FDA, EMA, or both. Enables endpoint-level traceability for multi-regional regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fda_primary|ema_primary|fda_secondary|ema_secondary|fda_exploratory|ema_exploratory|global — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA IND/NDA/BLA Guidance; EMA MAA Guidance; ICH E9; ICH CTD

description: Version number of the SAP document in which this endpoint was defined or last modified (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.1'). Supports version-controlled traceability of endpoint definitions across SAP amendments prior to database lock.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Analysis Plan Guidance; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether one or more pre-specified sensitivity analyses are defined for this endpoint in the SAP to assess the robustness of the primary analysis results. True if sensitivity analyses are planned; False otherwise.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Missing Data; EMA Guideline on Missing Data

description: Pre-specified Type I error rate (alpha) for this endpoint, typically 0.05 for primary endpoints or adjusted values for multiplicity-controlled secondary endpoints. Defined in the SAP prior to database lock.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues in Clinical Trials

description: Operational system of record from which this endpoint definition was sourced or managed (e.g., SAS Drug Development for SAP authoring, Veeva Vault for document management). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; CDISC ADaM

description: The primary statistical test or model used to analyze this endpoint (e.g., 'ANCOVA', 'Log-rank test', 'Cox proportional hazards model', 'Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM)', 'Wilcoxon rank-sum test'). Specified in the SAP and implemented in SAS Drug Development. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ancova|log_rank|cox_ph|mmrm|wilcoxon|logistic_regression|cmh — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Adjustment for Baseline Covariates

description: Indicates whether pre-specified subgroup analyses are defined for this endpoint in the SAP (e.g., by age, sex, region, disease severity). True if subgroup analyses are planned; False otherwise. Relevant for FDA and EMA subgroup reporting requirements.
reference: FDA Guidance on Subgroup Analyses; EMA Guideline on Subgroup Analyses; ICH E9

description: Numeric position of this endpoint within the pre-specified hierarchical or fixed-sequence testing procedure. Determines the order in which endpoints are tested to control Type I error. Lower values are tested first.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Timestamp when this statistical endpoint record was last modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance for SAP version control.

description: Calendar date on which this SDTM dataset was generated from source EDC data. Used for audit trail and submission package dating per ALCOA+ contemporaneous principle.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Precise date and time at which the SDTM dataset was generated, including timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Indicates whether this dataset has been split into multiple physical files due to size constraints (e.g., LB domain split by lab panel). True = dataset is one part of a split; False = single complete dataset file.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Indicates whether this SDTM dataset has passed all QC checks, Pinnacle 21 validation, and internal review gates and is approved for inclusion in a regulatory submission package. True = ready for submission; False = not yet approved.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; CDISC SDTM IG; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Free-text field for capturing dataset-specific annotations, known issues, deviation justifications, or reviewer instructions that do not fit into structured fields. Supports ALCOA+ documentation completeness.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Number of errors (severity: Error) identified during the Pinnacle 21 validation run. Errors must be resolved or formally justified before regulatory submission.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; Pinnacle 21 Validation Rules

description: Date on which the most recent Pinnacle 21 validation was executed against this dataset. Supports audit trail and ensures validation currency relative to dataset generation.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Outcome of the Pinnacle 21 Community or Enterprise conformance validation run against this SDTM dataset. Regulatory agencies (FDA, PMDA) require Pinnacle 21 validation reports as part of the submission package.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; Pinnacle 21 Validation Rules; PMDA Data Standards

description: Number of warnings identified during the Pinnacle 21 validation run. Warnings require documented justification in the Reviewer's Guide or ADRG if not resolved.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; Pinnacle 21 Validation Rules

description: Date on which the QC review of this SDTM dataset was completed and signed off. Required for audit trail documentation per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Current quality control review status for this SDTM dataset. Tracks progression through the double-programming or independent QC review process required before regulatory submission.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Total number of observation records contained in this SDTM dataset at the time of generation or last update. Used for reconciliation, QC verification, and submission package validation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: File path or document reference to the Analysis Data Reviewer's Guide (ADRG) or Study Data Reviewer's Guide (SDRG) section that documents dataset-specific conventions, deviations, and reviewer instructions for this SDTM domain.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; CDISC SDRG Template

description: File system path or storage location of the SAS transport file (XPT format) for this SDTM dataset. FDA requires submission datasets in SAS Version 5 transport format (.xpt).
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; SAS Transport File Format Specification

description: Version of the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide used to generate this dataset (e.g., '3.3', '3.4'). Critical for regulatory submission conformance validation and Pinnacle 21 checks.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

value_regex: Medidata Rave|Oracle Clinical One|SAS Drug Development|Oracle Argus Safety|Manual|Other
description: Operational system of record from which the source data for this SDTM dataset was extracted (e.g., Medidata Rave for EDC data, Oracle Argus Safety for safety data, SAS Drug Development for transformed outputs).
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Sequential number identifying the order of this file within a split dataset series (e.g., 1 for LB1, 2 for LB2). Null when is_split_dataset is False.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Sponsor-specific internal name or alias for this dataset, which may differ from the standard CDISC domain abbreviation in cases of custom or extended domains. Used for internal tracking and sponsor communication.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Number of unique study subjects (USUBJID) represented in this SDTM dataset. Supports population-level QC checks and reconciliation against the DM domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Version identifier for the regulatory submission package in which this dataset is included (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.1'). Tracks dataset evolution across multiple submission cycles for the same study.
reference: FDA eCTD Guidance; ICH M8 eCTD

description: Indicates whether a supplemental qualifier dataset (SUPP--) accompanies this SDTM domain dataset to capture non-standard variables. True = a corresponding SUPP-- dataset exists; False = no supplemental dataset.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.4 Section 8.4; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SDTM dataset record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5

description: Total number of variables (columns) in this SDTM dataset. Used for define.xml completeness verification and to ensure compliance with CDISC domain variable requirements.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Size of the SAS transport (.xpt) file in kilobytes. Used for submission package size management and to flag datasets approaching the 5GB FDA file size limit.
reference: FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Mapping ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for a single source-to-target SDTM variable mapping rule record within the Clinical Trials data management system. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — this record represents one variable-level mapping line within a parent SDTM dataset specification.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: The DMP specifies sdtm_mapping_version and sdtm_ig_version, which directly govern the SDTM mapping rules. sdtm_mapping already captures sdtm_ig_version and mapping_version as attributes, but lacks a d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are created and maintained per a data standards SOP. sdtm_mapping has compliance_sop_id (compliance domain) but needs document_sop_id for the document-level SDTM mapping pr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: SDTM EX domain mappings reference dosing regimens — regimen_code and sdtm_ex_mapping_notes on dosing_regimen confirm this. Linking sdtm_mapping to dosing_regimen enables EX domain traceability and exp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: SDTM IE domain mappings directly reference eligibility criteria — each IE record maps to a specific criterion. Linking sdtm_mapping to eligibility_criterion is required for IE domain define.xml genera
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: SDTM mappings are directly tied to endpoints — adam_parameter_code and sdtm_domain on endpoint align with sdtm_variable_name on sdtm_mapping. Endpoint-level SDTM traceability is required for SAP align
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: SDTM LB domain mappings are panel-specific. SDTM programmers must know which lab panel a mapping applies to for define.xml generation and LB dataset construction. A direct FK avoids traversal through
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are governed by the SAP, which defines required SDTM domains, variables, and submission versions. Programmers reference the SAP when authoring and validating SDTM mappings
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent SDTM dataset specification record that owns this mapping rule. Establishes the header/detail relationship between the dataset-level specification and this individual variable mapping line.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4

description: Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: sdtm_mapping defines transformation rules from raw EDC eCRF fields to SDTM variables. source_field_name and source_field_label are denormalized references to the source eCRF field. Adding source_ecrf_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: sdtm_mapping defines transformation rules from raw EDC eCRF fields to SDTM variables. source_form_name is a denormalized reference to the source eCRF form. Adding source_ecrf_form_id normalizes this r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this SDTM mapping rule is defined. Enables study-scoped filtering and traceability of all mapping specifications back to the originating protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are submission-specific — NDA, MAA, and JNDA submissions may require different mapping versions and controlled terminology. Regulatory data managers need to know which subm
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are filed in the TMF as data management plan supporting documents. TMF completeness assessments require mapping specifications to be traceable to TMF filings. Supports insp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: SDTM ARM/ACTARM variable mappings (TA, ADSL domains) are directly sourced from treatment_arm definitions. Regulatory submission requires SDTM mappings to reference the exact treatment arm record to ge
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: SDTM mappings trace source data to SDTM variables at the procedure level. Visit procedures define what data is collected and in which SDTM domain. Procedure-level SDTM traceability is a core requireme
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date and time when this mapping rule was formally approved and promoted to 'approved' status by the authorized reviewer. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this mapping lifecycle transition. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Free-text justification for the most recent change to this mapping rule (e.g., 'Protocol Amendment 3 — new AE severity scale', 'Corrected derivation logic per QC review'). Required when mapping_status transitions or derivation_algorithm is modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Medical coding dictionary used for this mapping when derivation_type is 'coded'. MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) for adverse events and medical history; WHO-DD (WHO Drug Dictionary) for concomitant medications; CTCAE (Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events) for severity grading. 'none' if no coding dictionary applies.
reference: MedDRA; WHO-DD; CTCAE; ICH E2A; FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: Version of the medical coding dictionary applied for this mapping (e.g., MedDRA v26.1, WHO-DD March 2023). Ensures reproducibility of coded terms across transformation runs and supports regulatory submission traceability.
reference: MedDRA; WHO-DD; ICH E2A; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

business_glossary_term: CDISC Controlled Terminology Code List Name
description: Name of the CDISC Controlled Terminology code list applied to this SDTM variable (e.g., 'AESEV', 'NCOMPLT', 'SEX', 'RACE'). Null if no controlled terminology applies. Governs permissible values for the target variable in regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Date and time when this SDTM mapping rule record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Immutable after initial record creation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Version of the CDISC Controlled Terminology package applied for this mapping (e.g., '2023-09-29'). Ensures reproducibility of coded values across transformation runs and regulatory submissions. Critical for FDA/EMA submission compliance.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Supplementary comment text to be included in the CDISC Define-XML v2.1 submission package for this variable. Provides additional context for FDA/EMA reviewers about the derivation logic, data source, or special handling. Appears in the define.xml ItemDef Comment attribute.
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Origin attribute value for this variable as it will appear in the CDISC Define-XML v2.1 submission metadata. Indicates the source of the data: 'CRF' (collected on eCRF), 'Derived' (algorithmically derived), 'Assigned' (assigned by sponsor/CRO), 'Protocol' (defined in protocol), 'eDT' (electronic data transfer), 'Predecessor' (derived from prior SDTM variable).
reference: CDISC Define-XML v2.1; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Full specification of the transformation logic or derivation rule applied to produce the target SDTM variable value from the source field(s). Includes SAS code snippets, conditional logic, date calculations, concatenation rules, and multi-source merge logic. For direct mappings, states 'Direct mapping from {source_field_name}'. Supports reproducible and auditable CDISC data transformation per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11; SAS Drug Development

description: Classification of the mapping derivation method: 'direct' (verbatim copy from source), 'derived' (computed from one or more source fields), 'assigned' (constant or protocol-defined value), 'predecessor' (derived from a previously mapped SDTM variable), 'computed' (algorithmic calculation), 'coded' (medical coding via MedDRA or WHO-DD lookup).
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Date after which this version of the mapping rule is no longer active and has been superseded or deprecated. Null for currently active mapping rules. Supports temporal versioning of mapping specifications across protocol amendments.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Date from which this version of the mapping rule is effective and applicable to SDTM transformation runs. Supports version-controlled mapping specifications where different versions apply to different data collection periods or protocol amendments.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether this SDTM variable mapping requires data from more than one source field or form (True) or derives from a single source field (False). When True, the derivation_algorithm field documents all contributing source fields and the combination logic.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether the predecessor variable dependency documented in predecessor_variable is a hard requirement (True) that must be resolved before this mapping executes, or an informational dependency (False). Governs transformation program execution sequencing.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development

description: Indicates whether the target SDTM variable contains sensitive subject data requiring special handling under GDPR, HIPAA, or sponsor data privacy agreements (True). Examples include subject identifiers, dates of birth, and geographic data. Governs data masking and de-identification procedures in submission datasets.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date and time when this SDTM mapping rule record was most recently updated. Updated on every change to the mapping rule, derivation logic, or status. Supports change tracking and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this mapping rule within the study's SDTM mapping specification document. Follows the pattern MAP-{DOMAIN}-{VARIABLE}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in Data Management Plans (DMP) and CDISC submission packages.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current lifecycle state of this SDTM mapping rule. Controls which version is active for production CDISC transformation runs. 'approved' rules are eligible for DBL and regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|deprecated|superseded|on_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Version number of this mapping rule in major.minor format (e.g., 1.0, 2.3). Incremented when the transformation logic, source field, or controlled terminology mapping changes. Supports version-controlled, auditable CDISC data transformation per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Free-text field for additional programmer or reviewer notes about this mapping rule, including special handling instructions, known data quality issues, sponsor-specific requirements, or cross-references to related mapping rules. Not included in regulatory submission artifacts.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Name of the SDTM variable (within the same or a different domain) that must be derived before this mapping can be executed. Documents derivation dependencies for sequencing transformation programs. Format: '{DOMAIN}.{VARIABLE}' (e.g., 'AE.AEDECOD'). Null if no predecessor dependency exists.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development

description: Name or employee identifier of the Data Management or Biostatistics reviewer who reviewed and approved this mapping rule. Supports dual-review traceability and ALCOA+ compliance for the SDTM mapping specification document.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code identifying the target dataset domain (e.g., DM, AE, LB, VS, CM, EX, MH, DS, SV, TU). Determines the SDTM class and required/expected variable set for this mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DM|AE|LB|VS|CM|EX|MH|DS|SV|TU|EC|FA|RS|TR|TU|PR|CE|DD|HO|ML|PC|PP|RE|RP|SR|SS|TI|TS|TV — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Version of the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide that governs this mapping rule (e.g., 'SDTMIG v3.4', 'SDTMIG-AP v1.0'). Ensures regulatory submission compliance with the correct CDISC standard version as required by FDA and EMA data submission guidelines.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: CDISC core designation indicating whether the target SDTM variable is Required (Req), Expected (Exp), or Permissible (Perm) for the domain. Required variables must be populated; Expected variables should be populated when data is collected; Permissible variables are optional.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Human-readable descriptive label for the target SDTM variable as defined in the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide (e.g., 'Dictionary-Derived Term', 'Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units'). Used in SAS dataset metadata and regulatory submission define.xml.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; CDISC Define-XML v2.1

description: Name of the target CDISC SDTM variable within the specified domain (e.g., AEDECOD, LBSTRESN, VSORRES). Must conform to CDISC SDTM naming conventions: uppercase, 2–8 characters, domain prefix for most variables. This is the column name in the final SDTM dataset submitted to regulatory agencies.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: CDISC-defined role of the target SDTM variable within its domain class (Identifier, Topic, Qualifier, Timing, Rule). Determines how the variable functions within the SDTM dataset structure and governs its position and usage in regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4

description: CDISC-defined data type of the target SDTM variable. 'Char' for character/text variables and 'Num' for numeric variables, as specified in the SDTM Implementation Guide and define.xml metadata. Governs SAS dataset column type in regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; CDISC Define-XML v2.1

description: Ordinal sequence number of this mapping rule within the parent SDTM dataset specification. Governs the order in which variables appear in the SDTM dataset and define.xml, and the execution order of transformation programs in SAS Drug Development.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; CDISC Define-XML v2.1

description: Name of the raw/staging dataset (SAS dataset or EDC extract table) that contains the source field prior to SDTM transformation (e.g., 'RAW_AE', 'RAW_VS'). Used in SAS Drug Development transformation programs to identify the input dataset for each mapping rule.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; SAS Drug Development; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Indicates whether this variable is mapped to a CDISC SDTM Supplemental Qualifier (SUPP--) dataset rather than the parent domain dataset (True). SUPPQUAL datasets hold non-standard variables that cannot be accommodated in the standard domain structure.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; CDISC Define-XML v2.1

description: Short variable name (QNAM) used in the SUPPQUAL dataset for this non-standard variable. Populated only when suppqual_flag is True. Must be uppercase, 1–8 characters, and unique within the SUPPQUAL dataset for the domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4

description: Pinnacle 21 / CDISC conformance rule error or warning code associated with the most recent validation failure for this mapping (e.g., 'SD0083', 'SD0006'). Null when validation_status is 'passed' or 'not_validated'. Supports targeted remediation by the CDISC programmer.
reference: CDISC Conformance Rules; Pinnacle 21; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current status of the CDISC Pinnacle 21 / OpenCDISC validation check result for this mapping rule. 'passed' indicates no conformance errors; 'failed' indicates outstanding validation errors requiring remediation before DBL; 'waived' indicates a documented exception approved by the Data Management team.
reference: CDISC Conformance Rules; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; Pinnacle 21

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the bioanalytical assay record in the lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a validated analytical method owned and managed by the bioanalytical laboratory.
reference: CDISC SDTM; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018)

description: Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: GLP/GCP bioanalytical assays must reference their validated method SOP. Regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA bioanalytical section) and FDA/EMA inspections require direct traceability from the assay record
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: FDA/EMA bioanalytical method validation guidance requires each validated assay to be linked to the specific IMP it measures. Regulatory submissions (NDA/MAA) require assay-to-drug traceability. DMPK t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Bioanalytical assays are validated to measure specific investigational products. Regulatory submissions (IND/NDA bioanalytical method validation sections) require tracing which validated assay measure
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: A bioanalytical assay is validated and performed at a specific laboratory facility. The existing STRING columns responsible_lab_name and lab_accreditation_number are denormalized references to lab_fac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: The SAP governs bioanalytical methods, LLOQ/ULOQ thresholds, and acceptance criteria used in PK statistical analyses. Regulatory submissions require the SAP to reference the validated bioanalytical_as
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Bioanalytical validation reports are TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3, Section 3.2). Regulatory submissions require direct navigation from the assay record to the filed validation report.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Mean accuracy of the assay expressed as percent of nominal concentration (%Nominal) at QC levels during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically 85–115% (80–120% at LLOQ). Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Broad classification of the analyte type measured by the assay. Distinguishes small molecule drugs (PK), large molecule biologics, biomarkers (PD), immunogenicity (anti-drug antibodies), metabolites, and endogenous compounds. Drives regulatory submission strategy and validation requirements.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Name of the substance being measured by the assay (e.g., drug compound, metabolite, biomarker, anti-drug antibody). Directly links the assay to the pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), or immunogenicity endpoint it supports.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the assay by the bioanalytical laboratory or sponsor (e.g., 'BA-LC-001', 'ELISA-TNF-003'). Used as the business identifier across CTMS, EDC, and regulatory submissions. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011)

description: Full descriptive name of the bioanalytical assay as used in study protocols, validation reports, and regulatory submissions (e.g., 'LC-MS/MS Assay for Compound XYZ in Human Plasma'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Primary scientific purpose the assay serves within the clinical or nonclinical program. PK (Pharmacokinetics) assays measure drug concentration over time; PD (Pharmacodynamics) assays measure biological effect; Immunogenicity assays detect anti-drug antibodies; DMPK (Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics) assays support metabolite profiling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PK|PD|Immunogenicity|Safety Biomarker|Efficacy Biomarker|DMPK|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM PC/PP Domains; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10

description: Analytical technology platform used to perform the assay. Common types include LC-MS/MS (Liquid Chromatography-Tandem Mass Spectrometry), ELISA (Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay), ECL (Electrochemiluminescence), PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction), Flow Cytometry, and LBA (Ligand Binding Assay). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LC-MS/MS|ELISA|ECL|PCR|Flow Cytometry|LBA|HPLC|GC-MS|Western Blot|IHC|CLIA|RIA — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Mathematical regression model used to fit the calibration curve for back-calculation of analyte concentrations from instrument response. Common models include Linear (1/x or 1/x² weighting), Quadratic, 4-Parameter Logistic (4PL), and 5-Parameter Logistic (5PL) for LBA methods.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Standardized test code from the CDISC Controlled Terminology for the SDTM PC (Pharmacokinetics Concentrations) or LB (Laboratory Test Results) domain. Enables direct mapping of assay results to CDISC SDTM datasets for regulatory submission.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA SDTM Submission Requirements

description: Timestamp when the bioanalytical assay record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

description: Statistical method used to establish the screening and confirmatory cut points for immunogenicity (anti-drug antibody) assays (e.g., 'Parametric 5th percentile', 'Non-parametric 5th percentile'). Applicable only to immunogenicity assays; null for PK/PD assays.
reference: FDA Immunogenicity Testing Guidance (2019); EMA Guideline on Immunogenicity Assessment; ICH M10

description: Maximum validated dilution factor that can be applied to samples exceeding the ULOQ while maintaining assay accuracy and precision within acceptance criteria. Samples requiring dilution beyond this factor must be flagged.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10

description: Date after which the assay validation is no longer considered current and re-validation or re-qualification is required. Relevant for long-running studies where assay reagents, reference standards, or critical reagents may expire.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Indicates whether the assay is performed under Good Clinical Practice (GCP) standards as required for clinical trial sample analysis. True = GCP-compliant; False = non-GCP. Distinct from GLP compliance — clinical samples require GCP; nonclinical samples require GLP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC

description: Indicates whether the assay was validated and is performed under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP compliance is required for nonclinical safety studies submitted to regulatory agencies. True = GLP-compliant; False = non-GLP.
reference: GLP (21 CFR Part 58); OECD Principles of GLP; EMA GLP Directive 2004/10/EC

description: Between-run precision expressed as percent coefficient of variation (%CV) across multiple analytical runs during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically ≤15% (%CV). Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Name or identifier of the internal standard (IS) used in the assay for quantification normalization (e.g., stable isotope-labeled analog, structural analog). Critical for LC-MS/MS and other chromatographic methods to correct for matrix effects and extraction variability.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10

description: Within-run precision expressed as percent coefficient of variation (%CV) at the QC concentration levels, as determined during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically ≤15% (%CV), or ≤20% at LLOQ. Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10

description: Lower Limit of Quantification — the lowest analyte concentration that can be reliably quantified with acceptable accuracy and precision per the validated method. Expressed in the unit defined by lloq_uloq_unit. Critical for PK/PD data interpretation and regulatory submission. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2011); ICH M10; CDISC SDTM PC Domain
