reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018)

description: The date and time the PK sample was received and logged at the bioanalytical or central laboratory. Used to calculate total sample transit time and to verify cold-chain compliance. Part of the chain-of-custody audit trail.
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Numeric representation of the nominal time point in hours relative to dose administration (e.g., -0.0833 for pre-dose, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0). Negative values indicate pre-dose collections. Used for PK modeling, NCA (non-compartmental analysis), and SDTM/ADaM dataset generation in SAS Drug Development.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCTPTNUM; CDISC ADaM ADPC; FDA PK Guidance; ICH E3

description: The protocol-specified nominal time point for sample collection relative to dose administration (e.g., 'Pre-dose', '0.5h', '1h', '2h', '4h', '8h', '24h'). Used as the planned time point label in PK analysis and SDTM PC domain mapping. Stored as a string to accommodate mixed formats (pre-dose, fractional hours, day-relative).
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCTPTNOM; ICH E3 Section 11.4; FDA PK Guidance for Industry

description: Indicates whether this sample is a pre-dose (trough/baseline) collection. True = pre-dose sample; False = post-dose sample. Pre-dose samples are used to assess trough concentrations, baseline drug levels, and accumulation ratios in multiple-dose PK studies.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCTPT; CDISC ADaM ADPC; FDA PK Guidance for Industry

description: Current processing stage of the PK sample from collection through shipment to the bioanalytical laboratory. Distinct from sample_status (overall lifecycle); this tracks the physical processing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|processed|centrifuged|aliquoted|frozen|shipped|received_at_lab|failed|destroyed — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; ICH M10; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ALCOA+

description: The date and time the sample was processed (e.g., centrifuged and aliquoted) after collection. Time from collection to processing is a critical pre-analytical variable that can affect analyte stability and bioanalytical results.
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether a protocol deviation was associated with this PK sample collection event (e.g., collection outside the acceptable sampling window, incorrect tube used, missed collection). True = deviation present; False = no deviation. Triggers deviation reporting workflow.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations; 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM DV Domain

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the PK sample, typically printed on the sample tube label and used across CTMS, LIMS, and bioanalytical systems for chain-of-custody tracking. Corresponds to the specimen barcode or accession number.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCREFID; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ALCOA+ principles

description: Assessment of the physical condition of the PK sample upon receipt at the bioanalytical laboratory. Conditions such as haemolysis, lipaemia, or temperature excursion may affect analyte measurement and trigger sample rejection or result flagging. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acceptable|haemolysed|lipemic|insufficient_volume|temperature_excursion|damaged|missing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; CDISC SDTM PC Domain

description: Indicates whether this PK sample has been excluded from the primary PK analysis dataset. True = excluded; False = included. Exclusions may be due to protocol deviations, sample integrity issues, or bioanalytical failures. Exclusion reason is captured in exclusion_reason.
reference: CDISC ADaM ADPC ANL01FL; FDA PK Guidance for Industry; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Current lifecycle status of the PK sample from scheduled collection through bioanalytical reporting. Drives workflow routing in LIMS and CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|collected|received|in_transit|received_at_lab|analyzed|reported|rejected|missing|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; Medidata Rave specimen workflow; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The biological matrix type of the PK sample. Determines the bioanalytical method applied and the reference range for analyte concentration. Plasma and serum are most common for small molecule PK; urine for renal clearance studies; CSF for CNS penetration studies.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCSPEC; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); ICH M10

description: The protocol-defined acceptable latest collection time in hours relative to dose for this nominal time point. Together with sampling_window_start_hours, defines the acceptable window for protocol compliance assessment.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; Study Protocol PK Sampling Schedule; FDA PK Guidance for Industry

description: The protocol-defined acceptable earliest collection time in hours relative to dose for this nominal time point. Used to assess whether the actual collection time falls within the acceptable PK sampling window for protocol compliance determination.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; Study Protocol PK Sampling Schedule; FDA PK Guidance for Industry

business_glossary_term: CDISC Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Domain
description: The CDISC SDTM domain to which this PK sample record maps for regulatory submission. PC = Pharmacokinetics Concentrations; PP = Pharmacokinetics Parameters. Enables automated SDTM dataset generation in SAS Drug Development.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA eCTD Technical Specifications; ICH M2

description: The temperature in degrees Celsius at which the PK sample is stored prior to shipment or analysis. Critical for analyte stability monitoring; deviations from protocol-specified storage conditions (e.g., -70°C, -20°C) must be documented and assessed for impact on sample integrity.
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ICH Q1A Stability

description: The protocol-assigned subject number (e.g., site-subject identifier such as 001-001) used to identify the participant in regulatory submissions and PK analysis datasets. Classified as restricted PII as it can be used to trace back to an individual participant.
reference: CDISC SDTM SUBJID; ICH E6(R2); HIPAA 45 CFR §164; GDPR Article 4

description: The date and time this PK sample record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail and data integrity monitoring under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Used to detect unauthorized or unexplained data changes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: The actual volume of biological sample collected from the subject, expressed in millilitres. Used for sample sufficiency assessment, bioanalytical aliquoting planning, and protocol compliance verification against the required volume specified in the PK sampling schedule.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC.PCVOL; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ICH M10

description: The protocol-specified minimum volume of sample required for the PK assay at this time point, expressed in millilitres. Used to assess whether the collected volume meets protocol requirements and to flag insufficient sample events.
reference: CDISC SDTM PC Domain; Study Protocol PK Sampling Schedule; ICH M10

description: Indicates whether the actual sample collection time falls within the protocol-defined acceptable PK sampling window for this nominal time point. True = within window (compliant); False = outside window (potential protocol deviation). Used in PK analysis inclusion/exclusion decisions and protocol deviation reporting.
reference: CDISC ADaM ADPC; FDA PK Guidance for Industry; ICH E3 Section 11.4; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory accreditation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ISO 15189; CAP; CLIA; UKAS; DAkkS

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.capa. Business justification: When a lab accreditation inspection identifies deficiencies, a formal CAPA is initiated. lab_accreditation has capa_status, capa_due_date, capa_closure_date as denormalized text fields — these are own
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Lab accreditation activities (qualification assessments, renewal processes) must follow documented SOPs per GCP/GLP requirements. Linking accreditation records to the governing compliance SOP enables
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Lab accreditations are recognized by specific health authorities (FDA recognizes CAP/CLIA, EMA requires ISO 15189/GLP). Sponsors must demonstrate that lab accreditation meets the specific health autho
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: GCP requires sponsor to qualify labs for specific IMP bioanalytical analysis. lab_accreditation.scope_includes_pk_pd and scope_includes_dmpk indicate IMP-specific scope. Regulatory submissions and ven
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the laboratory facility for which this accreditation record applies. Links to the laboratory master record.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Site files track lab_certification_on_file status. Linking lab_accreditation to site_file enables automated completeness checks — confirming which accreditation record satisfies the site file's lab ce
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to laboratory.lab_facility

Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Lab accreditation certificates are essential TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3). Regulatory inspectors require filed accreditation certificates in the eTMF. This FK links the accreditation
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name of the accrediting or certifying organization that issued the accreditation (e.g., CAP - College of American Pathologists, CLIA - Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments, ISO 15189, UKAS - UK Accreditation Service, DAkkS - Deutsche Akkreditierungsstelle). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAP|CLIA|ISO 15189|UKAS|DAkkS|A2LA|COFRAC|INAB|NABH|NABL — promote to reference product]
reference: CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189:2022; UKAS; DAkkS; A2LA; FDA 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the primary laboratory accreditation (CLIA, CAP, or ISO 15189) expires. Used to trigger renewal workflows and to validate facility eligibility for ongoing and new study assignments. Critical for regulatory compliance monitoring. [Moved from lab_facility: This scalar field on lab_facility collapses all authority-specific expiry dates into a single value, which is incorrect when a facility holds certifications from multiple health authorities each with independent expiry timelines. The per-authority expiry_date on facility_accreditation replaces this field with full fidelity.]
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Narrative or structured description of the test menu and analytical disciplines covered by the accreditation (e.g., clinical chemistry, hematology, microbiology, PK/PD bioanalysis, DMPK assays, flow cytometry). Defines which tests the laboratory is qualified to perform under the accreditation.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM LB domain

description: Version or edition of the accreditation standard under which the certificate was issued (e.g., ISO 15189:2022, ISO 15189:2012, CAP 2023 Checklist). Relevant for regulatory submissions and gap assessments when standards are updated.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; ISO 15189:2012; CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Current lifecycle status of the accreditation certificate. Drives eligibility of the laboratory for use in regulated clinical trials. Active status is required for regulatory submission documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 58; ISO 15189:2022; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Classification of the accreditation grant indicating the scope and conditions of the award (e.g., full accreditation, provisional, conditional, limited scope, renewal).
reference: CAP Accreditation Program; ISO 15189:2022; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Externally-issued certificate or accreditation number assigned by the accrediting body (e.g., CAP number, CLIA ID, ISO certificate number). This is the primary business identifier used in regulatory submissions and audit documentation.
reference: CAP Accreditation Program; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189:2022; UKAS; DAkkS

description: Current lifecycle state of the accreditation as maintained by the issuing health authority. Drives facility eligibility for use in trials subject to that authority's jurisdiction. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Timestamp when this accreditation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR

description: Date on which the health authority's certification or recognition of this facility became valid. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Date on which the accreditation certificate expires and the laboratory must have completed renewal to remain qualified for use in regulated clinical trials. Critical for trial eligibility monitoring.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 58

description: Indicates whether the laboratory has been assessed and confirmed as compliant with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) requirements for clinical laboratory operations supporting regulated clinical trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP; MHRA GCP

description: Indicates whether the laboratory operates under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) principles as required for non-clinical safety studies submitted to regulatory agencies. GLP compliance is distinct from ISO/CAP accreditation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); OECD GLP Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EMA GLP

description: Result of the most recent inspection conducted by this health authority at this facility. Authority-specific — the same facility may receive different outcomes from different authorities. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Date on which the accreditation certificate was formally issued or granted by the accrediting body. Represents the effective start of the accreditation period.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Full name of the qualified laboratory director responsible for the accredited laboratory facility. Required for regulatory submissions and accreditation documentation. Classified as confidential business data.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 Subpart M; CAP; ISO 15189:2022; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Professional qualifications, credentials, or certifications held by the laboratory director (e.g., MD, PhD, FACB, FRCPA). Required to verify director eligibility under CLIA and accrediting body requirements.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 Subpart M; CAP; ISO 15189:2022

description: Classification of the laboratory by operational role in the clinical trial (central laboratory, local/site laboratory, reference laboratory, bioanalytical laboratory, specialty laboratory). Determines applicable accreditation requirements and regulatory expectations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM LB domain; FDA Guidance for Industry: Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Date of the most recent regulatory or accreditation body inspection of the laboratory facility. Used to assess compliance currency and to schedule re-inspections per regulatory requirements. [Moved from lab_facility: A single last_inspection_date on lab_facility cannot represent the fact that FDA, EMA, and PMDA each conduct independent inspections on different schedules. The inspection history per authority belongs on the association record.]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 58; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Result of the most recent regulatory or accreditation inspection. Drives risk-based monitoring decisions, sponsor qualification status, and CAPA initiation for the facility. [Moved from lab_facility: Same rationale as last_inspection_date — a single outcome field cannot capture authority-specific inspection results. The inspection_outcome attribute on facility_accreditation provides per-authority granularity.]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 58; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022; EMA GCP Inspection Procedures

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, context, or clarifications related to the accreditation record that do not fit structured fields (e.g., special conditions, interim arrangements, liaison contact details).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022

description: Certificate number from the immediately preceding accreditation cycle. Supports continuity tracking and audit trail for regulatory submissions requiring historical accreditation evidence.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Name of the external proficiency testing or external quality assessment (EQA) program in which the laboratory participates (e.g., CAP PT, UKNEQAS, EQAS, RCPA QAP). Supports accreditation body verification.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 Subpart H; CAP Proficiency Testing; ISO 15189:2022; UKAS

description: Current status of the laboratory's external proficiency testing (PT) or external quality assessment (EQA) program participation. Proficiency testing is a mandatory component of CLIA and CAP accreditation and demonstrates ongoing analytical competency.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 Subpart H; CAP Proficiency Testing; ISO 15189:2022 Section 7.7; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the CRO or sponsor conducted an internal laboratory qualification assessment (distinct from the accrediting body inspection). Supports vendor qualification and oversight requirements under GCP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP; ISO 15189:2022

description: Outcome of the internal CRO or sponsor laboratory qualification assessment. Determines whether the laboratory may be activated for use in clinical trials managed by the organization.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP; ISO 15189:2022

description: The specific type of certification or regulatory recognition granted by this health authority to the facility (e.g., CLIA for FDA, GLP for EMA, GCP for PMDA). Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.
reference: ICH E6(R2), 21 CFR Part 58, OECD GLP Principles

description: Indicates whether this laboratory's accreditation record is currently eligible to be cited in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTD/eCTD). Derived from accreditation status, expiry, and CAPA closure but stored as an explicit business flag for audit and reporting purposes.
reference: FDA IND 21 CFR Part 312; FDA NDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA MAA; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH M4 CTD; eCTD

description: Target date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the accrediting body to avoid a lapse in accreditation status. May differ from expiry_date based on accrediting body lead-time requirements.
reference: CAP Accreditation Program; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189:2022

description: Current status of the accreditation renewal process. Tracks whether the laboratory has initiated, submitted, or completed renewal prior to certificate expiry to ensure uninterrupted trial eligibility.
reference: CAP Accreditation Program; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189:2022

business_glossary_term: Scope Includes Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics (DMPK)
description: Indicates whether the accreditation scope explicitly covers drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics (DMPK) assays, relevant for early-phase and ADME studies.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guideline; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the accreditation scope explicitly covers pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) bioanalytical testing, which is required for studies with PK/PD endpoints.
reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance (2018); EMA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guideline; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the clinical trial sponsor formally approved the laboratory for use in their studies. Required for audit trail and regulatory submission documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP

description: Indicates whether the laboratory has been formally approved by the clinical trial sponsor for use in their studies. Sponsor approval is a prerequisite for laboratory qualification in addition to regulatory accreditation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP; Sponsor SOPs

description: Timestamp when this accreditation record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory facility record. Primary key for the lab_facility data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA (42 CFR Part 493); CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Primary street address line of the laboratory facility. Used for specimen shipment labels, regulatory correspondence, and audit documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Secondary address line (suite, floor, building) for the laboratory facility. Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing and shipment address.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility has certified long-term specimen storage (biobanking) capabilities, including controlled-temperature archival storage. Relevant for studies requiring retained samples for future exploratory analyses.
reference: ISBER Best Practices for Repositories; ISO 20387:2018; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Number
description: Accreditation number issued by the College of American Pathologists (CAP) confirming the laboratory meets rigorous quality standards. Widely recognized by sponsors and regulatory agencies as evidence of laboratory quality. Null if not CAP-accredited.
reference: CAP Accreditation Program Standards; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance for Industry

description: City in which the laboratory facility is physically located. Used for geographic reporting, logistics planning, and specimen shipment routing.
reference: ISO 3166-2; GDPR Article 4; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) Certificate Number
description: Federal CLIA certificate number issued by CMS to U.S. clinical laboratories authorizing them to perform testing on human specimens. Required for regulatory compliance and sponsor qualification audits. Null for non-U.S. facilities.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CMS CLIA Program; FDA 21 CFR Part 58

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the laboratory facility is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives regulatory jurisdiction determination and applicable accreditation standards.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 44; HIPAA

description: Timestamp when the laboratory facility record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics (DMPK) Analysis Capability Flag
description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped and validated to perform Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics (DMPK) analyses, including metabolite profiling and enzyme characterization studies.
reference: FDA Guidance on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2018); ICH M10; EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Standardized laboratory code used in electronic data interchange (EDI) transmissions between the laboratory and EDC/CTMS systems. Aligns with CDISC LAB data standards for lab results transfer.
reference: CDISC LAB Data Standard; HL7 FHIR; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the laboratory facility (e.g., 'LAB-CENT-001'). Used in study protocols, eCRFs, and regulatory submissions to reference the facility. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 58

description: Official legal or operating name of the laboratory facility as registered with accreditation bodies and regulatory authorities. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Classification of the laboratory facility by its operational role in clinical trials. Central labs process specimens from multiple sites; local labs are site-affiliated; bioanalytical labs perform assay development and sample analysis; PK/DMPK labs focus on pharmacokinetics and drug metabolism. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM LB Domain; FDA Guidance on Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility has validated flow cytometry capabilities for immunophenotyping and cell-based assays, commonly required in oncology and immunology trials.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility holds a current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) certification or has been qualified under GCP guidelines by the sponsor or CRO. Required for laboratories participating in regulated clinical trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped to perform genomic analyses (e.g., PCR, NGS, genotyping) relevant to pharmacogenomics endpoints in clinical trials.
reference: ICH E15 Pharmacogenomics; FDA Guidance on Pharmacogenomics; ISO 15189:2022

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility is certified under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations, required for non-clinical safety studies and certain bioanalytical work supporting regulatory submissions.
reference: OECD GLP Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 58; EMA GLP Directive 2004/10/EC

description: Certificate number issued by a national accreditation body (e.g., UKAS, DAkkS, COFRAC) confirming the laboratory conforms to ISO 15189 requirements for medical laboratories. Recognized internationally for cross-border clinical trial operations.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; ILAC P15; EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Unique identifier for this facility within its Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Used for electronic data exchange, specimen tracking, and results transmission to EDC systems such as Medidata Rave.
reference: CDISC LAB Data Standard; 21 CFR Part 11; GAMP 5; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Name of the LIMS software vendor used by the facility (e.g., LabVantage, STARLIMS, LabWare). Relevant for EDC integration planning and data transfer agreement scoping.
reference: CDISC LAB Data Standard; 21 CFR Part 11; GAMP 5

description: Full name of the licensed physician or doctoral scientist serving as the laboratory's Medical Director, as required by CLIA and CAP accreditation. Accountable for overall laboratory quality and regulatory compliance.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1441; CAP Accreditation Standards; ISO 15189:2022 Section 4.1

description: Name of the central laboratory network or parent organization to which this facility belongs (e.g., 'Covance', 'Q2 Solutions', 'BioReference', 'PPD'). Used for contract management, network-level performance reporting, and escalation routing.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; ISO 9001:2015

description: Description of the laboratory facility's standard operating hours (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 07:00-19:00 EST'). Used for specimen shipment scheduling, turnaround time SLA definition, and site coordinator guidance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022 Section 4.1

description: Current lifecycle state of the laboratory facility indicating whether it is actively accepting specimens for clinical trial studies. Drives eligibility for new study assignments and site qualification workflows. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped and validated to perform pharmacokinetic (PK) sample analysis, including bioanalytical assays for drug concentration measurement.
reference: FDA Guidance on Bioanalytical Method Validation (2018); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation; ICH M10

description: Postal or ZIP code of the laboratory facility's physical address. Used for specimen shipment logistics and geographic analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA

description: Name of the preferred or contracted courier service for specimen shipment to this facility (e.g., 'World Courier', 'Marken', 'FedEx Custom Critical'). Used for logistics planning and chain-of-custody documentation.
reference: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022 Section 5.4

description: Email address of the primary operational contact at the laboratory facility. Used for study coordination communications, specimen shipment notifications, and results transmission alerts.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Full name of the primary operational contact person at the laboratory facility (e.g., Laboratory Director or Operations Manager). Used for study coordination, issue escalation, and regulatory correspondence.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Primary telephone number for the laboratory facility's operational contact. Used for urgent specimen handling queries, critical value notifications, and logistics coordination.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA

description: Date on which the sponsor or CRO qualification of the laboratory facility expires, requiring re-qualification before the facility can be assigned to new studies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; FDA Guidance on Vendor Qualification

description: Sponsor or CRO qualification status of the laboratory facility, reflecting the outcome of vendor qualification audits and ongoing performance reviews. Determines whether the facility may be assigned to new studies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; FDA Guidance on Vendor Qualification; ISO 9001:2015

description: Comma-separated list of additional regulatory certifications held by the facility beyond primary accreditation (e.g., 'FDA-registered, EMA-compliant, PMDA-approved, NY State DOH'). Supports multi-jurisdictional study eligibility assessment.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 493; EMA GCP Directive; PMDA Guidelines; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Maximum number of specimens the facility can process per business day under normal operating conditions. Used for capacity planning, site feasibility assessments, and study logistics. Serves as MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 15189:2022 Section 4.1

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format, local facility time) by which specimens must be received to be processed on the same business day. Critical for study protocol logistics and turnaround time commitments.
reference: ISO 15189:2022; CAP Accreditation Standards; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: State, province, or region in which the laboratory facility is physically located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping and logistics.
reference: ISO 3166-2; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA

description: IANA time zone identifier for the laboratory facility's local time (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Used for specimen receipt timestamp normalization, turnaround time calculations, and critical value notification SLA tracking.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM

description: Standard turnaround time in hours from specimen receipt to results availability for routine panels. Used in study feasibility assessments and service level agreement (SLA) monitoring.
reference: ISO 15189:2022 Section 5.8; CAP Accreditation Standards; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date after which this version of the lab panel definition is no longer valid for new protocol inclusions. Null for currently active panel versions. Supports panel retirement and version management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Date from which this version of the lab panel definition is valid and available for protocol inclusion. Supports temporal validity management of the panel catalog.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Minimum number of hours the subject must fast before specimen collection when fasting is required. Null when fasting_required is False.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Protocol Schedule of Assessments

description: Indicates whether the subject must be in a fasted state (typically ≥8 hours) prior to specimen collection for this panel. Relevant for lipid panels, glucose, and insulin assays. Drives patient instructions and visit scheduling.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Protocol Schedule of Assessments

description: Indicates whether this panel includes exploratory or validated biomarker assessments (e.g., genomic, proteomic, or metabolomic markers). Biomarker panels may require additional consent and specialized sample handling.
reference: FDA Biomarker Qualification Program; ICH E16; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3

description: Indicates whether this panel is a Pharmacodynamics (PD) panel measuring the biological effect of the drug. PD panels may map to CDISC SDTM LB or FA domains depending on the analyte type.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LB/FA Domains; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether this panel is a Pharmacokinetics (PK) panel used to measure drug concentration over time. PK panels map to CDISC SDTM PC domain and require specialized bioanalytical assay management.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - PC Domain; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance; ICH M10

description: Indicates whether this panel is designated as a safety monitoring panel (e.g., liver function, renal function, hematology) required for adverse event assessment and DSMB/DMC review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; CTCAE v5.0

description: Validation status of the analytical method used for this panel per ICH M10 and FDA/EMA guidance. 'validated' methods have full regulatory-grade validation; 'qualified' methods have partial validation; 'exploratory' methods are research-grade only.
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance 2018; EMA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guideline

description: High-level classification of the panel by clinical discipline or analyte group (e.g., Hematology, Clinical Chemistry, Coagulation, Urinalysis, Immunology, Pharmacokinetics, Biomarker). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hematology|clinical_chemistry|coagulation|urinalysis|immunology|pharmacokinetics|biomarker|microbiology|serology|endocrinology — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBCAT; NCI Thesaurus

description: Externally-known, sponsor- or CRO-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lab panel within the study catalog (e.g., CHEM20, CBC, LFT). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and CDISC SDTM LBTESTCD mapping.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBTESTCD; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Full human-readable name of the laboratory test panel or battery (e.g., 'Complete Blood Count', 'Comprehensive Metabolic Panel', 'Liver Function Tests'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBTEST.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBTEST; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Abbreviated display name for the panel used in eCRF headers, reports, and dashboards where space is constrained (e.g., 'CBC', 'CMP', 'LFT').
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBTESTCD; Medidata Rave EDC field conventions

description: Current lifecycle status of the lab panel definition in the catalog. 'active' panels are available for protocol inclusion; 'retired' panels are no longer used in new studies; 'draft' panels are pending validation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3

description: Secondary classification within the panel category providing finer granularity (e.g., within Clinical Chemistry: 'Liver Function', 'Renal Function', 'Lipid Panel'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSCAT.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBSCAT

description: Source of the normal reference ranges applied to results from this panel. Central lab-derived ranges are preferred for regulatory submissions; sponsor-defined ranges may be used for novel biomarkers.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBORNRLO/LBORNRHI; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation; ISO 15189

description: Indicates whether results from this panel must be included in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Drives data management and CDISC SDTM/ADaM transformation requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation 536/2014; ICH E3 CSR Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Domain
description: CDISC SDTM domain code to which this panel's results are mapped for regulatory submission (e.g., LB for Laboratory Test Results, PC for Pharmacokinetics Concentrations, PP for Pharmacokinetics Parameters). Required for FDA/EMA eCTD submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Required temperature condition for shipping specimens from the clinical site to the central laboratory. Governs cold chain logistics, packaging requirements, and IATA compliance for biological specimens.
reference: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; CLSI GP44-A4; ISO 15189

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this lab panel definition was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave, Oracle Clinical One, LIMS). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer provenance tracking.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Free-text field capturing any special pre-analytical or handling requirements for the specimen (e.g., 'Protect from light', 'Process within 30 minutes', 'Do not freeze', 'Centrifuge at 2000g for 10 minutes'). Communicated to site staff via lab manual.
reference: CLSI GP44-A4; ISO 15189; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Standardized method used to collect the specimen (e.g., Venipuncture, Finger Stick, Midstream Catch, Lumbar Puncture). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBMETHOD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|finger_stick|midstream_catch|lumbar_puncture|biopsy|swab|catheterization — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBMETHOD; CLSI H3-A6

description: Primary biological specimen type required for this panel (e.g., Whole Blood, Serum, Plasma, Urine, CSF, Tissue Biopsy). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSPEC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|serum|plasma|urine|csf|tissue|saliva|stool|bone_marrow|synovial_fluid — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - LBSPEC; CLSI GP44-A4

description: Minimum volume of specimen required in millilitres to perform all analytes in the panel. Used for phlebotomy planning, patient burden assessment, and paediatric study feasibility.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CLSI GP44-A4; Protocol Feasibility Assessment

business_glossary_term: Specimen Stability Hours at Ambient Temperature
description: Maximum number of hours the collected specimen remains analytically stable at ambient temperature (15–25°C) before processing or freezing is required. Critical for chain of custody and pre-analytical quality management.
reference: CLSI EP25-A; ISO 15189; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3

description: Maximum number of hours the collected specimen remains analytically stable when stored refrigerated (2–8°C). Used to define shipping and processing windows for central lab specimens.
reference: CLSI EP25-A; ISO 15189; CLSI GP44-A4

description: Expedited turnaround time in hours for urgent (STAT) requests, typically triggered by safety events or critical value alerts. Distinct from standard SLA turnaround time.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CAP Laboratory Accreditation; ISO 15189

description: Required storage temperature condition for the specimen prior to analysis (e.g., ambient, refrigerated 2–8°C, frozen –20°C, frozen –80°C). Governs cold chain logistics and sample chain of custody.
reference: CLSI GP44-A4; ISO 15189; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations

business_glossary_term: Turnaround Time Service Level Agreement (SLA) Hours
description: Contracted or target turnaround time in hours from specimen receipt at the laboratory to result reporting. Used for SLA monitoring, site management, and safety decision timelines. Critical for SAE-related panels.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; CAP Laboratory Accreditation; ISO 15189

description: Timestamp when this lab panel definition record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Version number of the lab panel definition (e.g., '1.0', '2.1'). Tracks changes to panel specifications over time to support audit trails and protocol amendment management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Comma-separated list of protocol visit names or codes at which this panel is scheduled (e.g., 'Screening, Day 1, Week 4, Week 8, End of Treatment, Follow-Up'). Supports protocol schedule of assessments design and EDC form configuration.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 - VISITNUM; Oracle Clinical One Study Design

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory data query record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CDISC CDASH; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study under which this laboratory query was raised. Links the query to the parent protocol and sponsor context.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the investigational site associated with the subject whose laboratory result triggered this query. Used for site-level query metrics and monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected and the lab result was generated, providing temporal context for the query.
reference: CDISC SDTM TV/SE domains; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose laboratory result is the subject of this query. Supports subject-level data quality tracking.

description: Type of organizational party to whom the query is assigned for response — investigational site, central laboratory, local laboratory, sponsor, or CRO (Contract Research Organization). Determines routing and accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+ principles

description: Free-text explanation of why the query was cancelled, if applicable (e.g., query raised in error, duplicate query, data issue resolved through another mechanism). Required for cancelled queries to maintain ALCOA+ audit trail integrity.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: The laboratory result value after correction, if a data change was made as a result of this query. Null if no correction was applied. Provides after-state for audit trail per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Date and time when this lab_query record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Audit trail field for data lineage and ALCOA+ compliance. Distinct from raised_datetime which captures the business event time.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the resolution of this query resulted in a data value change in the source EDC or LIMS record. True = a data correction was made; False = original data confirmed or query cancelled without change. Key metric for data quality reporting.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this open query is blocking the Database Lock (DBL) for the study or subject. True = must be resolved before DBL can proceed; False = non-blocking. Critical for study close-out planning and timeline management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; DBL SOP

business_glossary_term: Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) Field Name
description: Specific field name within the eCRF form that contains the queried laboratory data value (e.g., 'LBORRES', 'LBORRESU'). Enables precise identification of the data point in question for site response and EDC correction.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18

business_glossary_term: Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) Form Name
description: Name of the eCRF form in the EDC (Medidata Rave) on which the queried laboratory data field resides (e.g., 'Laboratory Results', 'Hematology'). Supports EDC navigation and query routing to the correct data entry form.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18

description: Native query identifier assigned by the source EDC system (Medidata Rave) or LIMS. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC Query Management; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18.4

description: Date and time when the query was formally escalated. Null if no escalation has occurred. Used for escalation cycle time analytics and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; ALCOA+ principles

description: Indicates whether this query has been formally escalated to a higher authority (e.g., from CRA to Clinical Lead, or from site to sponsor medical monitor) due to non-response, SLA breach, or data criticality. Triggers escalation workflow in CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Sponsor-CRO escalation SOPs

description: The laboratory result value as it existed at the time the query was raised, captured as a string to accommodate numeric, text, and coded values. Provides before-state for audit trail and data lineage per ALCOA+ requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Functional category indicating which business process or team initiated the query — data management programmatic check, medical monitor review, safety review, statistical review, or site-initiated clarification. Supports workload analytics and process improvement.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Number of times this query has been re-opened or re-issued against the same laboratory result field, indicating the number of response-review cycles. A high iteration count signals persistent data quality issues at the site or lab.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; Medidata Rave EDC Query Management

description: Human-readable, externally visible query identifier assigned by the EDC (Medidata Rave) or LIMS system at the time of query creation. Used for cross-system tracking and communication with sites and labs (e.g., QRY-2024-00123).
reference: Medidata Rave EDC Query Management; CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18

description: Business priority assigned to the query indicating urgency of resolution. Critical queries (e.g., those linked to SAE data or DBL-blocking issues) require immediate attention. Drives escalation and SLA enforcement.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Identifies the operational system of record in which this query originated — EDC (Medidata Rave), a Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), Oracle Clinical One, or a manually entered query. Supports multi-system data quality governance.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Current lifecycle state of the laboratory data query. Drives workflow routing, SLA monitoring, and DBL readiness assessment. Open = awaiting response; Answered = response received, pending DM review; Closed = resolved and accepted; Cancelled = withdrawn.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC Query Lifecycle; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Full narrative text of the query as raised by the data manager or medical monitor, describing the data issue, the specific concern, and the action requested from the site or laboratory. Core ALCOA+ documentation field.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Classification of the reason the query was raised against the laboratory result. Drives triage, routing, and resolution workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missing_value|implausible_result|unit_mismatch|collection_time_deviation|reference_range_deviation|transcription_error|duplicate_entry|method_deviation — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Functional role of the person or process that raised the query (e.g., Data Manager, Medical Monitor, Clinical Research Associate (CRA), Statistician, or automated system check). Denormalized for reporting without requiring a user-role join.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; ALCOA+ principles

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the query was formally raised and entered into the EDC or LIMS. Principal business event timestamp for the query lifecycle. Required for SLA calculation and ALCOA+ contemporaneity compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC CDASH

description: Outcome action taken to resolve the query: data value was corrected in the EDC/LIMS, original data was confirmed as correct, query was withdrawn by the raiser, or no action was required. Critical for data audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the query was formally closed or cancelled by the data manager after reviewing the site/lab response. Marks the end of the query lifecycle. Used for cycle time analytics and DBL readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the site or laboratory submitted their response to the query. Used for SLA compliance measurement and ALCOA+ contemporaneity tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC CDASH

description: Full narrative response provided by the site or laboratory in reply to the query, explaining the data discrepancy, confirming the value, or documenting a correction. Core ALCOA+ documentation field for audit trail.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018

description: Indicates whether the queried laboratory result is associated with a safety event (SAE, AE, or SUSAR) or a critical value, requiring expedited resolution. True = safety-relevant; False = routine data quality query. Drives prioritization and safety reporting workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; CTCAE

description: Indicates whether the query response or resolution exceeded the defined SLA target days. True = SLA was breached; False = responded within SLA. Drives escalation workflows and site performance KPI reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Sponsor-CRO Agreement SLA terms

description: Date and time when this lab_query record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.

description: CDISC SDTM-compliant unique subject identifier (sponsor-study-subject concatenation) denormalized onto the query record for direct regulatory submission traceability and ALCOA+ audit compliance without requiring a join.

description: Indicator of whether the result falls outside the normal reference range. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBNRIND. Values: NORMAL (within range), LOW (below lower bound), HIGH (above upper bound), LOW LOW (critically low), HIGH HIGH (critically high).
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBNRIND; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Indicates whether there is at least one open data query (discrepancy) associated with this laboratory result in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports data management workflow and DBL readiness tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; CDISC CDASH; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Free-text comment from the laboratory or data manager regarding this result (e.g., hemolyzed sample, lipemic specimen, instrument flag, repeat analysis note). Supports data review and query resolution.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB Domain LBCOMM; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Date and time when the laboratory result was received by the sponsor/CRO data management system (EDC or CTMS). Used for turnaround time monitoring and critical value notification tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; CDISC SDTM LBENDTC; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date and time when the laboratory formally reported and released the result. Distinct from collection and receipt timestamps. Used for lab turnaround time KPI calculation and audit trail.
reference: ISO 15189 Medical Laboratories; CAP Accreditation Standards; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Sequential number identifying the order of this result record within the subject's laboratory data. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSEQ variable for dataset generation.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBSEQ

description: Standardized unit of measure for the numeric result value after conversion. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESU. Enables cross-laboratory and cross-study comparisons in statistical analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBSTRESU; UCUM; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Current lifecycle status of the laboratory result indicating its data quality and review state. PRELIMINARY: initial unverified result; FINAL: verified and released; CORRECTED: amended after final release; CANCELLED: voided result; AMENDED: modified with audit trail.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport.status; CDISC SDTM LBSTAT; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Unit of measure for the original reported result value as received from the laboratory. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBORRESU (e.g., g/dL, mmol/L, U/L, 10^9/L).
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBORRESU; UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure)

description: Original reported result value as received from the laboratory, stored as STRING to accommodate numeric, text, and operator-prefixed values (e.g., '<0.5', '>1000', 'POSITIVE', 'NEG'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBORRES.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBORRES; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Standardized numeric result value after unit conversion to the standard unit. NULL for non-numeric results. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESN. Used for statistical analysis, shift tables, and CTCAE grading.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBSTRESN; ICH E3 Clinical Study Report

description: Status of source data verification (SDV) for this laboratory result, performed by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) against source documents. Supports risk-based monitoring and GCP compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; CDISC CDASH

description: Relative study day of specimen collection, calculated from the subject's reference start date (Day 1 = first dose). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBDY. Negative values indicate pre-treatment assessments. Used for time-course analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3 - LB Domain LBDY; ICH E3

description: Date and time when this laboratory result record was last modified. Audit trail field required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Tracks corrections, amendments, and status changes throughout the data lifecycle.

description: Globally unique subject identifier in the format STUDYID-SITEID-SUBJID, as defined by CDISC SDTM. Required for regulatory submission datasets and cross-study subject traceability.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Maximum age in years of the subject population for which this reference range applies. Null indicates no upper age bound.

description: Free-text narrative providing additional detail on the reason for the reference range update. Supplements the coded change_reason field for regulatory audit trail documentation.

description: Date and time when this reference range record was first created in the system. Part of the mandatory GCP audit trail for laboratory data per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The critical (panic) high threshold for the analyte. Results at or above this value require immediate clinical notification per laboratory SOP and GCP safety reporting requirements.

description: The critical (panic) low threshold for the analyte. Results at or below this value require immediate clinical notification per laboratory SOP and GCP safety reporting requirements.

reference: CTCAE v5.0 (NCI); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 2 Upper Threshold
description: Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 2 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.
reference: CTCAE v5.0 (NCI); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 3 Upper Threshold
description: Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 3 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.
reference: CTCAE v5.0 (NCI); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade 4 Upper Threshold
description: Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 4 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.
reference: CTCAE v5.0 (NCI); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

description: Date on which this reference range version ceased to be effective. Null indicates the range is currently active. Populated when a new version supersedes this record.

description: Date from which this reference range version became or becomes effective for use in clinical trial lab result evaluation and out-of-range flagging.

description: Ethnic or racial population for which this reference range is specifically calibrated (e.g., ALL for universal, or specific population codes per CDISC). Null or ALL indicates the range applies universally regardless of ethnicity.

description: Identifier of the specific laboratory instrument or analyzer for which this reference range was established. Instrument-specific ranges are required when different analyzers produce systematically different results.
reference: ISO 15189 Medical Laboratories; CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA (Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments)

description: Indicates whether this record represents the currently active version of the reference range for the given analyte, lab, and stratification combination. True = current; False = historical version.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this reference range was explicitly defined or mandated by the clinical trial sponsor, as opposed to being the laboratory's standard range. Sponsor-defined ranges take precedence for trial-specific out-of-range flagging.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Lab Data in Clinical Trials; CDISC SDTM

description: Accreditation standard under which the laboratory facility that established this reference range is certified (e.g., CAP, CLIA, ISO 15189). Relevant for regulatory submissions and sponsor qualification of central labs.
reference: CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA (42 CFR Part 493); ISO 15189; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Central Lab Qualification

reference: CDISC SDTM LB Domain (LBORNRLO); ICH E3; FDA Guidance on Lab Data

description: Code identifying the analytical method or instrument platform used to generate results evaluated against this reference range (e.g., colorimetric, immunoassay, HPLC). Reference ranges may differ by method.

description: Date and time when this reference range record was last modified. Part of the mandatory GCP audit trail for laboratory data per ALCOA+ principles.

description: The lower normal limit value from the immediately preceding version of this reference range. Retained for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The upper normal limit value from the immediately preceding version of this reference range. Retained for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether this reference range is sponsor-defined (provided by the clinical trial sponsor), lab-standard (established by the laboratory facility), derived from a regulatory guideline, or from published literature.

description: Lot number of the reagent kit or calibrator associated with this reference range version. Captured when a reagent lot change triggers a reference range update per laboratory SOPs.

description: Biological sex for which this reference range is applicable. Use M for male, F for female, BOTH for sex-independent ranges, or UNKNOWN when sex stratification is not defined.

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this reference range was sourced or ingested (e.g., Medidata Rave, Oracle Clinical One, central lab LIMS). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.

description: Standard unit of measurement for the reference range limits and analyte result (e.g., g/dL, mmol/L, U/L, mg/dL). Corresponds to LBORRESU in CDISC SDTM. Should align with UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure).
reference: CDISC SDTM LB Domain (LBORRESU); UCUM Standard; ISO 11240

description: The upper boundary of the normal reference range for the analyte. Values above this threshold are flagged as abnormally high. Corresponds to LBORNRHI in CDISC SDTM.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB Domain (LBORNRHI); ICH E3; FDA Guidance on Lab Data

description: Sequential integer version number of this reference range definition. Incremented each time the range is updated. Supports full version history and audit trail per GCP requirements.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Specimens are collected at protocol-defined visits; linking to the visit schedule enables deviation detection (collection outside visit window), SDTM domain mapping, and protocol compliance reporting.

description: Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which this specimen was collected. Enables visit-level specimen tracking.

description: Current status of the specimen's chain of custody documentation. 'Intact' indicates unbroken custody from collection to current location; 'broken' indicates a gap requiring investigation. Critical for regulatory and legal defensibility of specimen data.

description: Assessment of the specimen's physical condition upon receipt at the laboratory (e.g., acceptable, hemolyzed, lipemic, temperature excursion). Determines whether the specimen is suitable for analysis or requires rejection/re-collection. description: Date and time at which this specimen record was first created in the system. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, and data lineage tracking.

description: Date on which the specimen was destroyed or disposed of, in accordance with the protocol-defined retention period and applicable regulations. Required for regulatory accountability and chain-of-custody closure.

description: Current lifecycle status of the specimen within the laboratory workflow. Drives operational actions and regulatory accountability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_receipt|received|in_analysis|analyzed|stored|shipped|destroyed|rejected|lost — promote to reference product]

description: Date after which the specimen is no longer considered analytically valid for its intended assay(s). Determined by specimen type, storage conditions, and analyte stability data. Used to flag specimens requiring re-collection or exclusion.

description: Fasting status of the subject at the time of specimen collection (fasted, non-fasted, unknown). Required for metabolic panels, lipid profiles, and glucose assays where fasting state affects reference ranges.

description: Number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen has undergone. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles can degrade analyte integrity; this field is used to assess specimen quality and flag specimens that may yield unreliable results.
reference: FDA Guidance on Bioanalytical Method Validation; ISO 15189; CAP Laboratory Accreditation

description: Indicates whether this specimen record represents the primary (original) specimen (True) or an aliquot derived from a parent specimen (False). Drives chain-of-custody logic and parent-child specimen relationships.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Accession Number

reference: ISO 15189; CAP Laboratory Accreditation; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 LBREFID

description: Description of pre-analytical processing steps performed on the specimen prior to analysis or storage (e.g., centrifugation speed/duration, temperature, time to processing). Critical for result interpretation and protocol compliance.

description: Date and time at which pre-analytical processing of the specimen was completed. Used to calculate time-from-collection-to-processing, a key pre-analytical quality metric per laboratory manual requirements.

description: Date and time at which the specimen was received at the central or local laboratory. Used to calculate transit time, assess cold-chain compliance, and trigger LIMS accession workflows.

description: Reason for specimen rejection when disposition_status is 'rejected' (e.g., insufficient volume, wrong tube type, temperature excursion, unlabelled, clotted). Required for deviation tracking and re-collection decisions.

description: Biological matrix type of the specimen collected from the subject (e.g., blood, urine, plasma, serum, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), tissue). reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 LBSPEC; NCI Thesaurus; ISO 15189

description: Physical storage location of the specimen within the laboratory or biorepository (e.g., freezer ID, rack, box, position). Required for specimen retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody documentation.

description: Required storage temperature for this specimen in degrees Celsius (e.g., -80, -20, 2-8, ambient). Used to verify cold-chain compliance and specimen integrity throughout the chain of custody.

description: Date and time at which this specimen record was most recently modified. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.

description: Volume of biological material collected from the subject, expressed in millilitres (mL). Used to verify adequacy for planned assays, assess protocol compliance, and support chain-of-custody documentation.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen aliquot record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.

description: Broad business domain category of the alert, used for routing, reporting, and escalation prioritization. DATA_QUALITY: data entry or consistency issues. SAFETY: adverse event or safety reporting concerns. ENROLLMENT: subject recruitment or screen failure anomalies. PROTOCOL_COMPLIANCE: protocol deviation patterns. REGULATORY: regulatory document or submission concerns. OPERATIONAL: site operational performance issues.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; CDISC RBM Reference Model

description: Date and time when the alert was generated by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system or manually created by a central monitor. This is the principal real-world business event time. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the alert, formatted as CMA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in communications, reports, and audit trails to uniquely reference an alert across systems. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC RBM Reference Model

description: Risk-stratified severity level of the alert, driving escalation urgency and response timelines. CRITICAL: immediate action required, potential patient safety or data integrity risk. HIGH: significant risk requiring prompt review within defined SLA. MEDIUM: moderate risk requiring scheduled review. LOW: informational, monitor for trend.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Current lifecycle state of the alert within the review and disposition workflow. OPEN: generated and awaiting assignment. UNDER_REVIEW: assigned reviewer is actively assessing. ESCALATED: elevated to on-site visit or senior oversight. CLOSED: reviewed and dispositioned. CANCELLED: determined to be erroneous or duplicate. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Classification of the alert by the nature of the triggering condition. KRI_THRESHOLD_BREACH: a Key Risk Indicator exceeded its amber or red threshold. DATA_ANOMALY: a data pattern inconsistency detected by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system. STATISTICAL_OUTLIER: site-level metric is a statistical outlier relative to the study population. MANUAL: alert raised by a central monitor without an automated trigger. ENROLLMENT_SIGNAL: enrollment rate deviation. SAFETY_SIGNAL: potential safety concern flagged centrally. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: TransCelerate RBM Initiative; CDISC RBM Reference Model; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the alert was assigned to a reviewer for investigation. Used to measure assignment SLA compliance from alert generation to assignment.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) must be initiated as a result of this alert. Set to True when disposition is CAPA_INITIATED or when a confirmed critical finding requires systematic remediation.
reference: ICH E6(R2); MasterControl CAPA; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; ISO 14155

description: Number of consecutive computation cycles in which the triggering KRI has breached its threshold at this site prior to and including this alert. A higher count indicates a persistent risk pattern and informs escalation urgency.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Timestamp when this alert record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Distinct from alert_generated_timestamp which captures the business event time. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2)

description: Action decision made following review of the alert. ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT: alert severity warrants an unscheduled or triggered on-site monitoring visit. REMOTE_FOLLOW_UP: issue to be addressed via remote monitoring or site communication. NO_ACTION_REQUIRED: alert reviewed and no further action needed. CAPA_INITIATED: a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been opened. PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_RAISED: a protocol deviation record has been created. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT|REMOTE_FOLLOW_UP|NO_ACTION_REQUIRED|CAPA_INITIATED|PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_RAISED — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Date on which the alert was formally escalated to a higher oversight level. Null when escalation_required is False. Used to track escalation timeliness and compliance with monitoring plan escalation SLAs.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Indicates whether this alert has been escalated to a higher level of oversight (e.g., sponsor, Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB), or senior management). True when disposition is ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT or when severity is CRITICAL.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Indicates whether this alert is a recurrence of a previously closed alert for the same KRI and site within the current study. True when a prior alert for the same KRI-site combination was closed and the KRI has breached again, signaling unresolved systemic issues.
reference: TransCelerate RBM Initiative; ICH E6(R2)

description: Standardized code identifying the specific Key Risk Indicator (KRI) that breached its threshold and triggered this alert (e.g., KRI-AE-001 for adverse event reporting timeliness). Denormalized from the risk_indicator product for alert-level reporting without requiring a join. Null for manually triggered alerts.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Human-readable name of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) that triggered the alert (e.g., 'Adverse Event Reporting Timeliness', 'Query Resolution Rate'). Denormalized for alert reporting and dashboard display without requiring a join to the risk_indicator product.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: The actual computed value of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) or data signal at the time the alert was generated. Compared against threshold_value to confirm the breach. QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category per TRANSACTION_HEADER non-monetary role.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: End date of the data analysis window used by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system to compute the KRI value that triggered this alert. Together with reference_period_start, defines the exact data window under review.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; ICH E6(R2)

description: Start date of the data analysis window used by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system to compute the KRI value that triggered this alert. Defines the temporal scope of the anomaly.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; ICH E6(R2)

description: Actual date on which the reviewer completed the assessment and recorded a disposition for the alert. Used to calculate review cycle time and SLA compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Target date by which the assigned reviewer must complete the review and disposition of the alert, as defined by the monitoring plan SLA for the given alert severity level.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: Free-text narrative entered by the reviewer documenting the findings, rationale for the review outcome, and justification for the chosen disposition. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Conclusion reached by the reviewer after investigating the alert. CONFIRMED: the anomaly or KRI breach is genuine and requires action. FALSE_POSITIVE: the alert was triggered in error or the signal is not clinically meaningful. INCONCLUSIVE: insufficient data to determine; requires further monitoring. PENDING: review not yet completed.
reference: ICH E6(R2); TransCelerate RBM Initiative; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Native record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Siebel CTMS or CSM engine) for this alert. Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the sponsor was formally notified of this alert. Null when sponsor_notified is False. Used to verify compliance with sponsor notification SLAs defined in the monitoring plan.
