description: Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this alert, as required for critical or high-severity alerts under the monitoring plan and sponsor oversight obligations per ICH E6(R2).

description: Indicates whether the breached threshold was the amber (warning) or red (action-required) threshold level as defined in the monitoring plan. Drives escalation logic and response SLA.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; TransCelerate RBM Initiative; ICH E6(R2)

description: The amber or red threshold value that was breached at the time the alert was generated. Captured at alert generation time to preserve the threshold configuration in effect, as thresholds may be revised over the study lifecycle.
reference: CDISC RBM Reference Model; ICH E6(R2); TransCelerate RBM Initiative

description: System or process that generated the alert. AUTOMATED_CSM: centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) engine. MANUAL_ENTRY: entered directly by a central monitor. SAFETY_SYSTEM: triggered from Oracle Argus Safety integration.

description: Unit in which the observed_value and threshold_value are expressed (e.g., 'percent', 'days', 'count', 'ratio'). Required for correct interpretation of the KRI metric values in alert review and reporting.

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Resolution Window (Days)
description: Maximum number of calendar days within which a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) identified during monitoring must be resolved and closed by the site or sponsor. Defined in the monitoring plan to ensure timely remediation.

description: Indicates whether centralized statistical monitoring (e.g., Key Risk Indicator (KRI) dashboards, data trend analysis) is included as a component of the monitoring strategy for this study.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; TransCelerate RBM Framework

reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Description of minimum qualifications, certifications, and experience required for Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) assigned to monitor sites under this plan (e.g., therapeutic area experience, GCP certification, sponsor-specific training).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.2; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Workday HCM

description: Timestamp when this monitoring plan record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Comma-separated list or narrative description of data fields designated as critical per ICH E6(R2), such as primary efficacy endpoints, informed consent dates, eligibility criteria, and safety data. These elements receive mandatory SDV regardless of overall SDV scope.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; CDISC SDTM; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Description of study processes designated as critical per ICH E6(R2), such as randomization, informed consent, Investigational Product (IP) accountability, and Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting. These processes receive heightened monitoring focus.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Description of applicable data privacy and patient data protection requirements governing monitoring activities under this plan, including GDPR, HIPAA, and local data protection laws relevant to the study regions.
reference: GDPR (EU) 2016/679; HIPAA 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which this version of the monitoring plan ceases to be operative, typically upon supersession by a new version or study close-out. Nullable for the currently active version.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date from which this version of the monitoring plan becomes operative and binding for study monitoring activities. Aligns with study initiation or amendment approval date.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Defined conditions or thresholds that trigger escalation of monitoring intensity, additional site visits, or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation. Examples include KRI breaches, repeated protocol deviations, or SAE under-reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; Oracle Siebel CTMS

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Training Required Flag
description: Indicates whether current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training certification is a prerequisite for all Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) executing monitoring activities under this plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guidelines

description: Monitoring visit interval in calendar days applicable to sites classified as High risk tier. Typically shorter than the standard interval to ensure heightened oversight.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; TransCelerate RBM Framework

business_glossary_term: Informed Consent Form (ICF) Review Required Flag
description: Indicates whether review of signed Informed Consent Forms (ICFs) for all enrolled subjects is a mandatory component of each monitoring visit under this plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; OHRP Regulations; GDPR Article 7

description: Standard interval in calendar days between scheduled interim monitoring visits for an average-risk site. Actual frequency may be adjusted based on site risk tier and Key Risk Indicator (KRI) triggers.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Indicates whether Investigational Product (IP) accountability checks (dispensing, return, destruction reconciliation) are mandated as part of on-site monitoring visits under this plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GMP Guidelines

description: Narrative or structured description of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) thresholds and tolerance limits defined in this monitoring plan. Specifies the upper/lower bounds for metrics such as query rate, protocol deviation rate, and enrollment rate that trigger escalation actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; TransCelerate RBM Framework; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Timestamp when this monitoring plan record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and version control requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Monitoring visit interval in calendar days applicable to sites classified as Low risk tier. Typically longer than the standard interval, reflecting reduced oversight requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; TransCelerate RBM Framework

description: Maximum acceptable percentage of missing or incomplete data fields in the Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) before a site is flagged for corrective action under the centralized monitoring program.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM; Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Primary monitoring strategy defined for the study. On-Site involves Clinical Research Associate (CRA) visits to investigational sites; Remote involves off-site review of electronic data; Centralized involves statistical and data-driven oversight; Hybrid combines multiple approaches per Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) principles.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring (2013); EMA Reflection Paper on RBM

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the monitoring plan, typically formatted as MP-[StudyCode]-[SequenceNumber]. Used in Trial Master File (TMF) references and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the monitoring plan. Draft indicates work-in-progress; Under Review indicates pending approval; Approved is the active operative version; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Retired indicates no longer applicable.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; MasterControl

description: Maximum acceptable protocol deviation rate (expressed as a percentage of total subject visits or data points) before escalation actions are triggered at a site. Defined per the RBM strategy.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; TransCelerate RBM Framework

description: Maximum acceptable data query rate (expressed as a percentage of total data points entered) before centralized monitoring flags a site for additional review or on-site visit. Supports Electronic Data Capture (EDC) quality oversight.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; CDISC SDTM; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Overarching Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy classification applied to the study. Determines the balance between on-site, remote, and centralized monitoring activities based on study risk profile per ICH E6(R2) and FDA/EMA guidance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring (Aug 2013); EMA Reflection Paper on RBM (2011)

description: Indicates whether remote (off-site) monitoring activities are permitted under this monitoring plan as a complement or alternative to on-site visits, per the approved RBM strategy.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Remote Monitoring; EMA Reflection Paper on RBM

description: Minimum percentage of source data records or data points that must be verified per site visit or monitoring cycle, as defined in the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Defines the breadth of Source Data Verification (SDV) to be performed across the study. 100% SDV requires verification of all data points; Risk-Based SDV targets high-risk data fields; Critical Data Only restricts SDV to protocol-defined critical data elements; No SDV applies to centralized monitoring-only studies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; CDISC SDTM

description: Indicates whether a formal Site Initiation Visit (SIV) is mandated for all sites under this monitoring plan prior to first patient enrollment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Methodology used to assign risk tiers (e.g., High, Medium, Low) to investigational sites. Determines how site-level risk scores are calculated to drive adaptive monitoring frequency and intensity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; TransCelerate RBM Framework; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Version identifier of the monitoring plan (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Incremented with each approved amendment to support audit trail and regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Maximum number of calendar days after a monitoring visit within which the CRA must submit the completed monitoring visit report to the sponsor or CRO. Typically 5–10 business days per GCP standards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.6; Oracle Siebel CTMS; MasterControl

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the centralized monitoring risk indicator (Key Risk Indicator / KRI) record within the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) framework.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance (2013); EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether the finding underlying this action item compromises the integrity of clinical trial data (e.g., eCRF entries, source data, audit trail). When True, may require data query issuance in Medidata Rave EDC and notification to data management.

description: Number of calendar days the action item has been open past its due date (or extended due date if applicable). description: Specific GCP guideline section, regulatory citation, or SOP reference that the finding violates or that governs the required corrective action (e.g., ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.0, FDA 21 CFR 312.62). Supports regulatory inspection documentation.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this action item record in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.

description: Business classification of the action item by the type of finding or compliance gap it addresses. description: Full narrative description of the monitoring finding, protocol deviation, or compliance gap that necessitated this action item. Includes observed facts, regulatory reference, and expected corrective action per GCP documentation standards (ALCOA+).

description: Current workflow state of the action item lifecycle in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Overdue status is set automatically when the due date passes without closure. Closed status requires documented evidence of resolution.

description: Priority level assigned to the action item indicating urgency and potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. Critical items trigger immediate escalation workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.

description: The calendar date on which the action item was formally raised and entered into Oracle Siebel CTMS. Serves as the principal business event date for SLA calculation, aging analysis, and regulatory inspection timelines.

description: Assessment of the potential regulatory impact of the finding underlying this action item. Drives prioritization for regulatory submission readiness, inspection preparation, and escalation to regulatory affairs. Aligned with FDA and EMA inspection classification frameworks.

description: Date on which the site formally submitted their response to the action item. Used to measure site responsiveness KPIs and to determine whether the response was received within the required timeframe.

reference: Oracle Siebel CTMS; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance 2013; EMA Reflection Paper on RBM

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflow must be initiated for this deviation. Typically True for major deviations and recurring minor deviations. Drives CAPA creation in MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.20; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether the deviation was identified and approved prospectively (before the deviation occurred, as a planned exception) or retrospectively (after the deviation occurred). Prospective deviations may be pre-approved by the sponsor or IRB/IEC; retrospective deviations require post-hoc review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations; IRB/IEC Reporting Requirements

description: Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or contextual notes related to the deviation finding that do not fit into structured fields. Captured by the CRA or data manager in Oracle Siebel CTMS.

description: Indicates whether the protocol deviation compromised the integrity or reliability of the clinical data collected for the affected subject or visit. Impacts subject's inclusion in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) or Per Protocol (PP) analysis populations.

description: The method by which the protocol deviation was identified. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) effectiveness analysis and helps identify whether centralized monitoring or on-site monitoring is more effective at detecting specific deviation types.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Addendum Section 5.18.3; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: Detailed narrative description of the protocol deviation, including what occurred, which protocol section was deviated from, and the specific circumstances. Captured by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) in Oracle Siebel CTMS during monitoring visit documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.4; ALCOA+ Principles; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the deviation finding record within the monitoring workflow. Drives CAPA initiation, sponsor notification, and regulatory reporting workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: The calendar date on which the protocol deviation was first identified, either during an on-site monitoring visit, remote monitoring review, or data management query. Used to calculate detection lag and reporting deadline compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.4; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Name or role identifier of the person who identified the protocol deviation (e.g., CRA name, data manager, site staff). Supports accountability tracking and ALCOA+ attributability requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.4; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Narrative assessment of the actual or potential impact of the deviation on subject safety, data integrity, and study validity. Informs the severity classification and determines whether regulatory reporting is required.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations; EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: The date on which the protocol deviation was formally reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Null if irb_iec_reportable is False or reporting is pending.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3.7; FDA 21 CFR Part 56.108; OHRP Guidance

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) per applicable regulations and the IRB/IEC-approved protocol. Drives automated notification workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3.7; FDA 21 CFR Part 56.108; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 54; OHRP Guidance

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to the deviation finding record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The calendar date on which the protocol deviation actually occurred at the site. This is the real-world event date, distinct from the identification date. Critical for regulatory reporting timelines and IRB/IEC notification windows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 54

description: The specific section number or clause of the study protocol that was deviated from (e.g., 'Section 6.3.2 - Visit Window'). Enables protocol-level deviation frequency analysis and supports regulatory submission documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6; CDISC SDTM DV Domain (DVTERM); FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: Indicates whether this deviation represents a recurrence of a previously identified deviation of the same category at this site. Recurring deviations trigger escalated CAPA requirements and may indicate systemic site quality issues.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.20; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA) as an important protocol deviation (IPD) or substantial protocol amendment trigger. Applies primarily to major deviations affecting subject safety.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 54; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.17; EMA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: The date on which the protocol deviation was formally resolved and closed in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Null if the deviation is still open or under review. Used to calculate deviation resolution cycle time KPIs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.4; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Sponsor SOP

description: Narrative description of the actions taken to resolve the protocol deviation, including immediate corrective actions implemented at the site. Distinct from the formal CAPA plan, this captures the immediate remediation steps.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.4; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Sponsor SOP

description: Narrative description of the identified root cause of the protocol deviation, as determined through investigation by the site or sponsor. Used to drive CAPA development and systemic issue identification across sites.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.20; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Standardized categorical classification of the deviation root cause to enable cross-site and cross-study trending. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) signal detection and CAPA effectiveness analysis.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.20; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance; ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Protocol Deviation Category

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Protocol Deviation Term

description: Regulatory severity classification of the protocol deviation. 'major' deviations may affect subject safety or data integrity and typically require IRB/IEC and sponsor reporting. 'important_protocol_deviation' (IPD) is the EMA/EU CTR classification for deviations requiring regulatory notification. 'minor' deviations have no significant impact on subject safety or data integrity.

description: Indicates whether this randomization scheme version has been included in a regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or eCTD package). Triggers additional change-control requirements.

description: Date on which the randomization scheme was retired or superseded. Null if the scheme is still active. Used for version lifecycle management and TMF archival.

description: Version identifier of the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that specifies this randomization scheme. Ensures traceability between the scheme implementation and the approved statistical methodology.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this randomization scheme within the RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave RTSM). Used as the business key for cross-system reference.

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the randomization scheme (e.g., 'Phase III Stratified Block Randomization v2'). Used in reports, TMF documentation, and biostatistics deliverables.

description: Current lifecycle status of the randomization scheme. 'draft' = under development; 'approved' = biostatistics/QA sign-off obtained; 'active' = in use for subject allocation; 'suspended' = temporarily halted (e.g., DSMB hold); 'retired' = superseded or study closed.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Clinical One

description: Version identifier of the randomization scheme (e.g., '1.0', '2.1'). Incremented when the scheme is amended post-approval. Supports audit trail and regulatory submission traceability.

description: Total number of distinct strata defined by the combination of stratification factors. Used to validate that the randomization list covers all strata and to monitor enrollment balance.

description: Semicolon-separated list of stratification factors used to define strata for stratified randomization (e.g., 'site;gender;disease_severity'). Defined in the protocol and SAP. Null for non-stratified schemes.

description: Total number of randomization positions (slots) generated in the scheme list. Represents the maximum number of subjects that can be randomized under this scheme version.

description: Number of treatment arms (including placebo/control) defined in this randomization scheme. Must align with the protocol and allocation ratio.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM TA Domain; Protocol

description: Defines the authorized unblinding procedure for this scheme. 'emergency_only' = individual subject emergency unblinding; 'dsmb_only' = unblinding restricted to DSMB/DMC; 'dbk_full' = full unblinding at database lock; 'partial_sponsor' = sponsor statistician partial unblinding for interim analysis.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Blinding; DSMB Charter

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this randomization scheme record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and version control in the silver layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a stratification cell record within the randomization scheme. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials

description: Reference to the parent randomization scheme under which this stratification cell is defined. Links the cell to its governing allocation algorithm.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM RAND domain

business_glossary_term: Cell Factor Level - Stratification Factor Id
description: Foreign key linking to the stratification factor that contributes a level to this cell definition.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this stratification cell is defined. Enables cross-study reporting and enrollment balance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM

description: Reference to the treatment arm associated with this stratification cell. Enables arm-level enrollment balance monitoring within each stratum.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM ARM domain; FDA Guidance on Randomization

description: Current count of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled into this stratification cell. Updated in real time from the IRT/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM / Oracle Clinical One) upon each randomization event.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM RAND domain; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Ratio of subject allocation across treatment arms within this stratification cell (e.g., '1:1', '2:1', '1:1:1'). Defined in the protocol and SAP and configured in the RTSM system.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Randomization; CDISC SDTM RAND domain

description: Acceptable percentage deviation from the target enrollment count before the cell is flagged as imbalanced. Defined in the SAP and used by the RTSM system to trigger balance alerts.
reference: ICH E9; SAP; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Size of the permuted block used for randomization within this stratification cell. A value of zero or null indicates variable block size or non-block-based methods. Stored as confidential to protect blinding integrity.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Randomization; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Current enrollment balance status of this stratification cell relative to its target. 'balanced' indicates enrollment is within acceptable tolerance; 'under_enrolled' or 'over_enrolled' indicate deviation from target; 'closed' indicates the cell is no longer accepting subjects; 'suspended' indicates temporary halt.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Stratified Randomization; CDISC SDTM

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this stratification cell within the study randomization scheme. Used in IRT/IVRS/RTSM systems such as Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One for cell-level kit assignment and allocation tracking.
reference: CDISC SDTM RAND domain; ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Detailed narrative description of the stratification cell, including the clinical rationale for the factor level combination and any protocol-specific notes.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Human-readable composite label describing the full combination of stratification factor levels that define this cell (e.g., 'Age>=65 / Male / ECOG 0-1'). Used in study reports, CSR tables, and enrollment dashboards.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Stratified Randomization

description: Ordinal sequence number of this cell within the randomization scheme, used for consistent ordering in reports, listings, and IRT/RTSM system configuration.
reference: CDISC SDTM RAND domain; ICH E9

description: Operational lifecycle status of the stratification cell within the randomization scheme. 'active' means the cell is open for randomization; 'inactive' means it has been deactivated; 'closed' means enrollment is complete; 'suspended' means temporarily halted; 'draft' means not yet activated.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Oracle Clinical One

description: Timestamp when this stratification cell record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.

description: Date on which this stratification cell was closed to further subject randomization, either due to reaching the enrollment cap or protocol-driven closure.

description: Date on which this stratification cell was opened for subject randomization and enrollment, as recorded in the RTSM system.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Oracle Clinical One

description: The system-level alphanumeric code for the specific factor level assigned to this cell (e.g., 'MILD', 'M', 'NA'). Used for IRT/RTSM system configuration and programmatic identification of the level within the randomization engine.
reference: IRT/RTSM system configuration

description: The specific level value assigned to this stratification factor within this cell (e.g., 'Mild', 'Male', 'North America'). This is the core data element that defines the cell's position on this factor's axis and is required for SDTM STRATM variable mapping.
reference: CDISC SDTM STRATM variable

description: Geographic region stratification factor level associated with this cell (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific), when geography is used as a stratification factor. Supports regional enrollment balance monitoring.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Multi-Regional Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on Stratification

description: Indicates whether this stratification cell is currently open and accepting new subject randomizations. Controlled by the RTSM system and updated when enrollment caps or protocol amendments are applied.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment within this stratification cell is blinded to investigators and subjects. True for double-blind studies; False for open-label designs.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Blinding

description: Indicates whether this stratification factor is the primary axis used to define and label this cell within the randomization scheme. Used by biostatisticians to identify the dominant stratification dimension for reporting and SAP documentation purposes.
reference: Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP)

description: Date of the most recent subject randomization event recorded within this stratification cell. Used to monitor cell activity and detect stalled enrollment.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM RAND domain; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: The ordinal position of this factor's contribution within the cell's composite label definition. Determines the display order of factor levels in the cell label (e.g., factor 1 = Disease Severity, factor 2 = Gender, factor 3 = Region) and is required for consistent RTSM cell label generation.
reference: RTSM cell label specification

description: Maximum number of subjects permitted in this stratification cell per treatment arm. Enrollment is capped at this value to prevent over-representation of a stratum.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; SAP

description: Minimum number of subjects required in this stratification cell to maintain statistical validity of the study, as specified in the SAP. Triggers an alert if actual enrollment falls below this threshold.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; SAP

description: Free-text operational notes or comments regarding this stratification cell, such as enrollment challenges, protocol deviation impacts, or DSMB/DMC recommendations affecting cell management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Version of the study protocol under which this stratification cell definition was established or last amended. Ensures traceability of cell definitions to specific protocol versions for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Randomization algorithm applied within this stratification cell (e.g., permuted block, minimization, dynamic allocation). Determines how subjects are allocated to treatment arms within the cell.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Randomization; CDISC SDTM RAND domain

description: Number of subjects who have received a randomization code assignment within this stratification cell, which may differ from enrolled count if subjects are randomized but not yet dosed.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM RAND domain; Medidata Rave RTSM

business_glossary_term: Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) Cell Reference
description: External cell identifier as configured in the RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One IRT). Used for reconciliation between the data lakehouse and the operational RTSM system.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Clinical One

description: Version of the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that defines the stratification factors, cell structure, and enrollment targets for this cell. Critical for regulatory audit traceability.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on SAP; CDISC ADaM

description: Number of subjects who have been screened and assessed for eligibility within this stratification cell, used to monitor the screening-to-enrollment funnel per stratum.

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Stratum Value
description: CDISC SDTM-compliant stratum value code for this cell, mapped to the STRATCD variable in the RAND domain. Required for regulatory submission datasets and SDTM-to-ADaM derivations.

description: Operational system of record from which this stratification cell record originated (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Protocol-specified target number of subjects to be enrolled in this stratification cell per treatment arm, as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and randomization schedule.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; CDISC SDTM

description: Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this stratification cell record. Required for electronic records compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ attributability.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this stratification cell record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.

description: Number of subjects who were enrolled in this stratification cell but subsequently withdrew or were discontinued, used to assess cell-level dropout impact on balance.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM DS domain; FDA Guidance on Missing Data

description: Username or system identifier of the user or process that created this stratification cell record. Required for electronic records compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ attributability.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the stratification factor record within the randomization scheme. Primary key for this entity.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent randomization scheme to which this stratification factor belongs. Each factor is owned by exactly one scheme.

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this stratification factor is defined. Enables direct study-level filtering without joining through the scheme.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Clinical One

description: The CDISC ADaM variable name in the ADSL dataset corresponding to this stratification factor (e.g., 'STRAT1', 'BSEVSEV'). Used in primary and sensitivity analyses per the Statistical Analysis Plan.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA eCTD; SAS Drug Development

description: The date on which this stratification factor configuration was formally approved by the biostatistics or RTSM team. Required for regulatory audit trail and TMF documentation.

description: Statistical method used to adjust for this stratification factor in the primary analysis: 'ancova' for Analysis of Covariance, 'cmh' for Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test, 'logistic_regression' for binary endpoints, 'none' if not used as a covariate in analysis.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Covariate Adjustment (2023); CDISC ADaM; SAP

description: The date and time when this stratification factor record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.

description: Identifies the system or method from which the stratification factor value is captured at randomization: 'ecrf' from the EDC eCRF, 'ivrs' from Interactive Voice Response System, 'irt' from IRT/RTSM, 'central_lab' from laboratory results, 'manual_entry' for site-entered values.

reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Short, system-level alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the stratification factor within the scheme (e.g., REGION, DISEASE_STAGE, GENDER). Used as the programmatic key in RTSM/IRT configuration and SDTM mapping.

description: Detailed narrative description of the stratification factor, including its clinical rationale, how it is assessed, and its role in balancing treatment arms as documented in the Statistical Analysis Plan.

description: Human-readable name of the stratification factor as displayed in the RTSM/IRT interface and statistical analysis plan (e.g., 'Geographic Region', 'Baseline Disease Severity', 'Prior Treatment Status').
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM; SAP (Statistical Analysis Plan)

description: Lifecycle status of the stratification factor within the scheme design workflow: 'draft' during initial configuration, 'approved' after biostatistics/RTSM sign-off, 'active' when live in the IRT system, 'suspended' during protocol amendment review, 'retired' when permanently decommissioned.

description: Classification of the factor's measurement scale: 'categorical' for nominal groupings (e.g., region), 'continuous_binned' for numeric ranges converted to categories (e.g., age groups), 'ordinal' for ranked categories (e.g., disease severity), 'binary' for two-level factors (e.g., yes/no prior treatment).
reference: ICH E9; CDISC SDTM; FDA Guidance on Stratified Randomization

description: The exact parameter name as configured in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One) for this stratification factor. Used for system integration and UAT validation.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Clinical One; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether this stratification factor is currently active and in use for subject randomization (True) or has been deactivated via protocol amendment (False). Inactive factors are retained for historical traceability.

description: Indicates whether the stratification factor value is masked from unblinded personnel (True) or visible to all study team members (False). Relevant for double-blind studies where certain stratification criteria could reveal treatment assignment.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Blinding

description: Indicates whether this factor is designated as a primary stratification factor (True) versus a secondary or exploratory factor (False) in the Statistical Analysis Plan. Primary factors are used in the primary efficacy analysis.
reference: ICH E9; SAP; FDA Guidance on Stratified Randomization

description: Indicates whether this stratification factor is included as a covariate in the primary statistical analysis model (True) or is used only for randomization balance without inclusion in the primary analysis (False). Relevant for ANCOVA and CMH test specifications in the SAP.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Covariate Adjustment; CDISC ADaM; SAP

description: Total number of enumerated levels (strata) defined for this stratification factor. Drives the dimensionality of the stratification matrix and the number of randomization lists required. For example, a 3-region factor has level_count = 3.

description: The maximum allowable imbalance (as a percentage or absolute count, per unit_of_imbalance) between treatment arms within any stratum defined by this factor before a balancing action is triggered in the minimization algorithm.

description: Specific variant of the minimization algorithm applied for this factor: 'pocock_simon' for the Pocock-Simon method, 'weighted_minimization' for factor-weighted minimization, 'biased_coin' for biased-coin minimization, 'deterministic' for deterministic minimization, 'none' if minimization is not applicable.
reference: ICH E9; Pocock-Simon (1975); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Numeric weight assigned to this stratification factor in a minimization or weighted minimization algorithm (e.g., Pocock-Simon). Values typically range from 0.0 to 1.0, with higher weights indicating greater influence on treatment allocation balance. Null if minimization is not used.
reference: ICH E9; Pocock-Simon Minimization Algorithm; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Defines the RTSM system behavior when the stratification factor value is missing or unknown at the time of randomization: 'block_randomization' to prevent randomization, 'assign_default_level' to use a pre-specified default level, 'flag_and_hold' to alert the site, 'exclude_from_minimization' to proceed without this factor.
reference: ICH E9; Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Clinical One; SAP

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, biostatistician comments, or RTSM configuration remarks related to this stratification factor that do not fit into structured fields. Not used for regulatory submissions.

description: Reference to the specific section of the study protocol document that defines this stratification factor (e.g., 'Section 8.2.1 Stratification Factors'). Enables traceability from the data model to the governing protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA IND Regulations 21 CFR 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The randomization algorithm applied in conjunction with this stratification factor: 'permuted_block' for block randomization within strata, 'minimization' for adaptive minimization, 'stratified_block' for stratified permuted block, 'dynamic_allocation' for covariate-adaptive methods.
reference: ICH E9; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; CDISC SDTM

description: The native identifier assigned to this stratification factor within the source RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM internal factor ID or Oracle Clinical One factor GUID). Used for system integration, reconciliation, and audit trail linkage.

description: Reference to the specific section of the Statistical Analysis Plan that describes the use of this stratification factor in primary and sensitivity analyses (e.g., 'SAP Section 5.3 Stratification'). Supports regulatory audit traceability.

description: The CDISC SDTM variable name that maps to this stratification factor for regulatory submission datasets (e.g., 'STRAT1', 'REGION1'). Used in SDTM TR/SUPPFA domains and ADaM ADSL derivations.

description: Integer defining the display and processing sequence of this stratification factor within the randomization scheme. Lower values appear first in RTSM screens, SAP tables, and stratification matrix axes.

description: Specifies whether the max_imbalance_threshold is expressed as a 'percentage' of subjects within the stratum or as an absolute 'subject_count'. Determines how the RTSM system evaluates balance during randomization.

description: The date and time when this stratification factor record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks configuration changes and supports change control audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11.

description: Business rule or logical expression applied to validate the stratification factor value at the time of randomization (e.g., 'BMIBL >= 18 AND BMIBL < 30'). Prevents invalid factor assignments and ensures data integrity per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Version identifier of this stratification factor definition, incremented with each protocol amendment or RTSM configuration change (e.g., '1.0', '2.0'). Supports change control and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
