description: Treatment arm code as configured in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave EDC or Oracle Clinical One). Used for eCRF field population, data validation rules, and SDTM mapping. Ensures consistency between EDC and IRT arm definitions.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; Oracle Clinical One; CDISC SDTM IG ARM domain

description: Treatment arm code as configured in the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) / Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS) / Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) system (Medidata Rave RTSM). Used for kit assignment, supply reconciliation, and randomization code generation. May differ from the SDTM arm code.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM Configuration; CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment is masked from the Principal Investigator (PI) and site staff. Critical for double-blind study integrity and GCP compliance documentation.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment is masked from the study subject. Supports blinding integrity documentation required by ICH E9 and GCP. Distinct from investigator or sponsor blinding.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; FDA Guidance on Blinding

description: Indicates whether this treatment arm serves as the control arm (placebo or active comparator) in the study. Used in statistical analysis to define the reference group for primary and secondary endpoint comparisons.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM ADSL ACTARM; FDA Guidance on Control Groups

description: Maximum number of subjects permitted in this treatment arm. Enforced by IRT/RTSM to prevent over-enrollment. May be set for adaptive designs or safety-driven enrollment caps.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; RTSM Configuration

description: Minimum number of subjects required in this treatment arm to maintain statistical validity and meet protocol-defined endpoints. Used by DSMB/DMC for interim analysis decisions and by biostatistics for power calculations.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; DSMB Charter; SAP

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this treatment arm record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Updated on every change to the record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, clarifications, or sponsor-specific instructions related to this treatment arm definition. Not used for regulatory submissions; intended for internal data management and CTMS operational use.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Target number of subjects planned for enrollment and randomization into this treatment arm as defined in the study protocol and sample size calculation. Used for enrollment tracking, site feasibility, and supply planning.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the specific section(s) of the study protocol document that defines this treatment arm (e.g., 'Section 5.2 - Treatment Arms and Dosing Regimen'). Supports traceability between the data model and the governing protocol document stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6; Veeva Vault eTMF; TMF Reference Model

description: Statistical randomization method used to allocate subjects to this treatment arm. Drives IRT/RTSM algorithm configuration in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One. Documented in the SAP and protocol.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Guidance on Randomization in Clinical Trials

description: Indicates whether this treatment arm definition has been included in a regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or CTD). Used for submission readiness tracking and eCTD package assembly in Veeva Vault.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD; eCTD Submission Standards

description: Route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects in this arm. Mapped to CDISC Controlled Terminology for the EX domain EXROUTE variable. Drives supply kit configuration and nursing/pharmacy instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology ROUTE; CDISC SDTM EX domain; ICH M4 CTD

description: Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) section that defines the analysis methodology for this treatment arm. Ensures traceability between arm definitions and the pre-specified statistical analysis approach used in SAS Drug Development.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; SAP; CDISC ADaM IG

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) ARM Value
description: The exact ARM variable value as it will appear in the CDISC SDTM DM (Demographics) and ARM domains for regulatory submission. Must conform to CDISC Controlled Terminology and be consistent across all SDTM datasets submitted in the eCTD package.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 DM domain ARM variable; CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA eCTD Submission Standards

description: Operational system of record from which this treatment arm definition was sourced or is mastered (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and Silver layer ETL audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Plan (DMP)

description: Comma-separated list of stratification factors applied during randomization to this arm (e.g., 'region, disease severity, prior treatment'). Defined in the protocol and SAP. Used to ensure balanced allocation across subgroups and configure IRT/RTSM stratification layers.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; SAP; CDISC SDTM STRATCD variable

description: Reason for temporary suspension of randomization to this treatment arm (e.g., safety signal, supply shortage, protocol amendment). Documented for regulatory compliance and DSMB/DMC review. Populated only when arm_status is 'suspended'.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.17; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; DSMB Charter

description: Target percentage of randomized subjects to be allocated to this treatment arm as specified in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and protocol. Used for enrollment monitoring, supply forecasting, and IRT/RTSM configuration. Example: 33.33 for a 1:1:1 three-arm study.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; SAP; CDISC SDTM IG ARM domain

description: Detailed narrative description of the investigational treatment, comparator, or placebo administered in this arm, including drug name, formulation, dose, route of administration, and dosing schedule as specified in the protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6; ICH E3 CSR; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: Description of the emergency unblinding procedure applicable to this treatment arm, including authorized personnel, system access method (e.g., IRT/IVRS emergency unblinding), and documentation requirements. Classified confidential to protect blinding integrity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; FDA Guidance on Blinding; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Number of days required for washout between treatment periods for this arm in crossover or multi-period study designs. Defined in the protocol based on pharmacokinetic (PK) half-life data. Used for visit scheduling and IRT/RTSM period management.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; DMPK Guidelines; FDA Guidance on Crossover Studies

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse event record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the adverse_event data product and is the SSOT reference for all AE occurrences across studies and subjects.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM AE Domain; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: AEs must be attributed to the treatment arm for SDTM ADAE dataset generation, blinded/unblinded safety reporting, and dose-response analysis. A clinical-research expert expects every AE record to iden
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU trials require AEs to be reported under the CTA. The CTA governs safety reporting obligations in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects every AE to reference the governing CTA for EU
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: adverse_event stores ctcae_grade as an INT (the grade number). Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table establishes referential integrity and enables navigation to the full CTCAE gr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: The dosing regimen active at AE onset is essential for dose-response analysis and causality assessment. Pharmacovigilance teams and DSMB require AE-to-regimen linkage to evaluate whether dose level, f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Source data verification (SDV) and ALCOA audit trail require tracing each adverse event back to the specific eCRF AE form it was collected on. Regulatory inspectors expect this traceability link betwe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: AEs are classified by study epoch (treatment vs. follow-up) for SDTM mapping and safety analysis. Regulatory submissions require epoch-level AE breakdowns; epoch context determines whether an AE is tr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance causality assessment and DSUR/PBRER generation require direct linkage from AE to the IMP product master. Regulatory submissions (ICSRs, expedited reports) must identify the exact IMP
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: AEs must be reported to the FDA under the IND. The IND number is required on expedited safety reports (7-day/15-day). A clinical research expert expects every AE to reference the governing IND for US
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the investigational site where the adverse event was reported. Used for site-level safety monitoring and CRA oversight.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: GxP regulations (21 CFR 312, EMA GMP) require batch-level traceability from adverse events for product quality investigations and potential recalls. Direct FK enables immediate batch identification du
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: adverse_event stores MedDRA codes (meddra_llt_code, meddra_pt_code, meddra_soc_code) as denormalized strings. Adding meddra_term_id FK to the meddra_term reference master establishes referential integ
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to study.study.study_id — Critical for study-level safety analysis — AEs must join to study for DSUR preparation, aggregate safety tables, and cross-study signal detection.

Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: AE records require the subject's authoritative randomization record for causality assessment, ICSR narrative population, and expedited reporting obligation determination. treatment_arm on adverse_even
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: AEs are reported at specific subject visits. Linking adverse_event to subject_visit_data enables visit-level data completeness review, SDV tracking, and lock/freeze status checks before safety databas
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: AEs are collected at specific study visits; SDTM AE domain requires AEVISIT mapping. Safety reviewers and data managers trace AE onset to the reporting visit for source data verification and visit-win
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: AE source documents (CRF pages, follow-up reports) must be filed in the eTMF per ICH E6 GCP requirements. Safety monitors and data managers need direct traceability from each AE record to its filed TM
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event. Core party reference linking the AE to the enrolled participant.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM AE Domain; HIPAA; GDPR Article 4

description: Action taken with the investigational product in response to the adverse event, as recorded by the investigator. Drives dose modification tracking and safety signal analysis.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AEACN; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Current lifecycle status of the adverse event record, reflecting whether the event is still active, has resolved, or resulted in death. Drives workflow routing in Oracle Argus Safety and expedited reporting decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AEOUT; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The exact verbatim text of the adverse event as reported by the investigator or subject on the eCRF, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail integrity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; CDISC SDTM AETERM; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Externally-known case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety upon initial AE entry. This is the business-facing identifier used in regulatory submissions, safety narratives, and cross-system reconciliation with the source system of record.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety; ICH E2B(R3); FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) first became aware of the adverse event. This is the clock-start date for regulatory expedited reporting timelines per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014; Oracle Argus Safety; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Investigator's assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Determines whether the event qualifies as a suspected adverse reaction and drives SUSAR classification and expedited reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment; CDISC SDTM AEREL; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether the adverse event may be related to concomitant medications taken by the subject, as assessed by the investigator. Supports differential causality analysis and confounding factor assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AERELCON; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The timestamp when this adverse event record was first created in the source system (Oracle Argus Safety). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; Oracle Argus Safety; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade
description: Severity grade assigned per the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE), ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). The primary severity measure used in oncology and other therapeutic areas for safety reporting and DSMB/DMC reviews.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM AETOXGR; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on CTCAE

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Reported Flag
description: Indicates whether this adverse event has been reported to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety review. Supports DSMB/DMC meeting preparation and safety oversight documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on DSMB; EMA Guideline on DMC; 21 CFR Part 312

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Capture (EDC) Subject Sequence Number
description: The AE sequence number assigned within the EDC system (Medidata Rave eCRF) for the subject, used to reconcile the safety database record with the source eCRF entry during SDV and data review.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC SDTM AESEQ; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether this record is the most current version of the adverse event. Used to filter the latest state of each AE without requiring a MAX(version_number) query, supporting efficient reporting and analytics.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Indicates whether the adverse event is listed in the current Investigator's Brochure (IB) or product labeling as an expected/anticipated reaction. Unexpected serious adverse reactions trigger SUSAR reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AEEXP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether the adverse event meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition). Triggers expedited regulatory reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance; CDISC SDTM AESER

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting to health authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) within defined timelines (7 or 15 calendar days).
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014; EMA EudraVigilance; MHRA

description: Indicates whether the adverse event is treatment-emergent, defined as an event that first occurred or worsened in severity after the first dose of investigational product. Used in TEAE incidence tables in the CSR and SAP-defined safety analyses.
reference: ICH E9; CDISC ADaM ADAE; SAP; SAS Drug Development; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Lowest Level Term (LLT) Code
description: MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) numeric code assigned during medical coding, representing the most granular level of the MedDRA hierarchy closest to the verbatim term.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AELLTCD; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Lowest Level Term (LLT) Name
description: MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) text description corresponding to the LLT code. Used in safety listings, narratives, and regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AELLT; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Code
description: MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) numeric code, the primary level used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEPTCD; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Name
description: MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) text description. The standardized medical term used in all regulatory submissions, DSMB/DMC reports, and safety narratives.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEDECOD; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) numeric code representing the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for aggregate safety tabulations by body system in CSRs and regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AESOCCD; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Name
description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) text description. Used in safety tabulations, listings, and figures in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AESOC; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Version
description: Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding this adverse event (e.g., '26.0'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coded terms across dictionary versions.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether the adverse event is considered related to or a continuation of a pre-existing medical history condition, as assessed by the investigator. Supports pre-existing condition confounding analysis.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AERELMH; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Free-text medical narrative describing the clinical course of the adverse event, including relevant medical history, treatment, and outcome. Required for SAE/SUSAR regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC safety reviews.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The date on which the adverse event first began, as reported by the investigator. This is the principal real-world event date used for duration calculations, expedited reporting timelines, and TEAE determination.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AESTDTC; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Final or current outcome of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator. Used in safety listings, aggregate safety tables, and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AEOUT; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The deadline by which an expedited safety report (e.g., SUSAR, IND Safety Report) must be submitted to the relevant health authority. Calculated from the date of first awareness per ICH E2A timelines (7-day for fatal/life-threatening, 15-day for other SUSARs).
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014; EMA EudraVigilance; MHRA; PMDA

description: The actual date on which the expedited safety report was submitted to the health authority. Used to calculate compliance with regulatory reporting timelines and track submission performance KPIs.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014; EMA EudraVigilance; MHRA; PMDA

description: Indicates whether this record represents the initial AE report, a follow-up update with new clinical information, or the final report upon event resolution. Supports version history tracking as the clinical picture evolves.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); Oracle Argus Safety; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The date on which the adverse event resolved or returned to baseline. Null if the event is ongoing. Used to calculate AE duration and determine outcome status.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM AEENDTC; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this SAE (e.g., death, life-threatening, hospitalization required, persistent disability, congenital anomaly, medically significant). Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; CDISC SDTM AESDTH/AESHOSP/AESLIFE/AESDISAB/AESCONG/AESMIE; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Descriptive severity classification of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator (mild, moderate, severe, life-threatening, fatal). Distinct from CTCAE grade; used in non-oncology studies and as a complementary severity descriptor per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AESEV; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this adverse event record was sourced (Oracle Argus Safety, Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Clinical One, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; CDISC Data Management

business_glossary_term: Study Day of Adverse Event (AE) Onset
description: The study day (relative to first dose date) on which the adverse event began. Calculated per CDISC convention (Day 1 = first dose date). Used in time-to-event analyses and safety exposure-adjusted incidence calculations.
reference: CDISC SDTM AESTDY; CDISC ADaM; ICH E9; SAS Drug Development

description: The timestamp when this adverse event record was most recently modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; Oracle Argus Safety; GDPR Article 5

description: Sequential version number of this AE record, incremented with each follow-up update. Version 1 is the initial report; subsequent versions capture evolving clinical information. Enables temporal evolution tracking across the AE lifecycle.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each MedDRA coding record assigned to an adverse event. Primary key for the ae_meddra_coding data product in the safety domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Reference to the parent adverse event record for which this MedDRA coding assignment was made. Links the coding record back to the originating AE in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE Domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Foreign key linking to data.coding_assignment. Business justification: MedDRA coding reconciliation process requires matching safety-side ae_meddra_coding records with EDC-side coding_assignment records. Regulatory submissions (SDTM AE domain) depend on reconciled coding
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: ae_meddra_coding stores ctcae_grade (INT) and ctcae_version (STRING). Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table normalizes the grade reference and enables navigation to the full CTCA
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.discrepancy_query. Business justification: MedDRA coding discrepancies generate EDC queries that must be resolved before database lock. Linking ae_meddra_coding to discrepancy_query enables data managers to track open coding queries against sa
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: MedDRA coding traceability requires identifying which specific eCRF field contained the verbatim AE term that was coded. This supports SDTM AE domain mapping and regulatory submission audit trails whe
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: ae_meddra_coding is the MedDRA coding assignment table and should reference the authoritative meddra_term reference master. Adding meddra_term_id (pointing to the PT-level term) establishes referentia
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which the adverse event and its MedDRA coding were recorded. Supports study-level aggregate safety reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM TS Domain; FDA IND regulations 21 CFR 312

description: Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: AE MedDRA coding is performed on verbatim terms captured in subject visit data records. Medical coders need to trace each coding assignment back to the source visit data for coding quality review, rec
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: MedDRA coding of AEs requires visit context for SDTM AE domain mapping (VISITNUM, VISIT) and for reconciliation with EDC source data at the visit level. Data managers performing coding reconciliation
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event being coded. Enables subject-level safety queries and MedDRA-coded AE listings.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The case identifier from Oracle Argus Safety that corresponds to this MedDRA coding record. Serves as the source system business key for traceability back to the originating safety case in the system of record.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety data model; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The event-level identifier from Oracle Argus Safety for the specific adverse event record within a case that this MedDRA coding applies to. Enables precise traceability to the source system event record.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety data model; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Calendar date on which the MedDRA code was assigned to the verbatim adverse event term. Used for coding timeliness metrics and regulatory submission audit trails.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the MedDRA code was assigned. Provides granular audit trail for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety system timestamp.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Free-text comment entered by the coder to document coding rationale, ambiguities, or special considerations for this MedDRA assignment. Supports coding consistency reviews and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DM Best Practices; ALCOA+ principles

description: Indicates how the MedDRA code was assigned: 'auto' for system-generated auto-coding by Oracle Argus Safety, 'manual' for coder-assigned coding, 'auto_confirmed' for auto-coded and confirmed by a coder, 'recoded' for a previously coded term that was revised. Drives QC workflows and audit requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DM Best Practices; Oracle Argus Safety coding workflow; ALCOA+ principles

description: Current workflow status of the MedDRA coding assignment. 'pending' indicates awaiting coding, 'coded' indicates a code has been assigned, 'confirmed' indicates QC-approved, 'rejected' indicates coding was rejected and requires rework, 'under_review' indicates currently in QC review. Drives coding workflow in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DM Best Practices; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles

description: Date on which the MedDRA coding assignment was confirmed (QC-approved) by a second reviewer or coding supervisor. Null if coding has not yet been confirmed. Supports dual-coder QC workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DM Best Practices; ALCOA+ principles

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade
description: Severity grade assigned to the adverse event using the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) scale (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Used in oncology and other therapeutic areas for standardized severity grading alongside MedDRA coding.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM AETOXGR; FDA Oncology guidance; ICH E3

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Version
description: Version of the NCI CTCAE grading scale used for severity assessment (e.g., '5.0', '4.03'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure consistent severity grading interpretation across study sites and time periods.
reference: NCI CTCAE; FDA Oncology guidance; CDISC SDTM

description: Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding (e.g., 'ENG' for English, 'JPN' for Japanese). Relevant for multi-regional studies where local language MedDRA versions may be used (e.g., PMDA submissions in Japan).
reference: ICH M1; PMDA MedDRA/J requirements; ISO 639-2

description: Date from which this MedDRA coding record version is considered effective. Used in conjunction with is_current_version and effective_to_date to implement SCD Type 2 versioning for coding history tracking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; CDISC DM Best Practices

description: Date until which this MedDRA coding record version is considered effective. Null for the current active record. Used with effective_from_date and is_current_version for SCD Type 2 coding history. Populated when a recoding event supersedes this record.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; CDISC DM Best Practices

business_glossary_term: MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) Code
description: Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the High Level Group Term (HLGT), which sits between the HLT and SOC in the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for broad safety grouping in regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEHLGTCD

business_glossary_term: MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) Name
description: Descriptive text of the MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) corresponding to hlgt_code. Supports broad-level safety grouping in aggregate analyses and CSR appendices.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEHLGT

description: Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the High Level Term (HLT) in the MedDRA hierarchy above the PT. Used for grouping related PTs in aggregate safety analyses and signal detection.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEHLTCD

description: Descriptive text of the MedDRA High Level Term (HLT) corresponding to hlt_code. Supports mid-level grouping in safety tabulations and DSMB/DMC review packages.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEHLT

description: Indicates whether this record represents the current (active) MedDRA coding assignment for the associated adverse event. Set to false for superseded coding records retained for historical audit trail. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD Type 2) versioning in the Silver layer.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; CDISC DM Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this MedDRA coding record meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition). Drives expedited reporting timelines.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; CDISC SDTM AESER

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting (7-day or 15-day) to health authorities. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety case classification.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014; CDISC SDTM

description: Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the Lowest Level Term (LLT) assigned to the verbatim adverse event term. The LLT is the most granular level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is the primary coding target. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety MedDRA dictionary.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AELLTCD

description: Descriptive text of the MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) corresponding to llt_code. Provides the human-readable label for the most granular MedDRA coding level. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety MedDRA dictionary.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AELLT

description: Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for this coding assignment (e.g., '26.0', '26.1'). Critical for regulatory submissions as coding must reference the specific MedDRA version in use at the time of the submission. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety dictionary configuration.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; FDA eCTD submission requirements; EMA MedDRA versioning policy

description: The MedDRA PT code that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Populated only when coding_method = 'recoded'. Enables before/after comparison for coding change impact analysis and regulatory audit.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: Previous MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: The MedDRA SOC code that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Populated only when coding_method = 'recoded'. Enables assessment of whether a recoding changed the SOC-level classification, which may affect regulatory safety tables.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this SOC assignment is the primary (true) or secondary (false) SOC for the PT. MedDRA PTs may be linked to multiple SOCs; the primary SOC is used for standard regulatory safety tables. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AESOCCD primary SOC convention

description: Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the Preferred Term (PT) to which the LLT maps. The PT is the primary level used for regulatory reporting, safety queries, and signal detection. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AEPTCD; FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: Descriptive text of the MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) corresponding to pt_code. Used as the standard term in regulatory submissions, CIOMS forms, and safety narratives.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AEDECOD

description: Free-text justification provided by the coder when a previously assigned MedDRA code is revised (coding_method = 'recoded'). Required for audit trail integrity and regulatory inspection readiness. Null when coding_method is not 'recoded'.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; CDISC DM Best Practices

description: Timestamp at which this MedDRA coding record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Timestamp at which this MedDRA coding record was last modified in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Tracks the most recent update for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the System Organ Class (SOC), the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. SOC is the primary axis for organizing adverse events in regulatory safety tables (e.g., Table 14.3.1 in ICH E3 CSR).
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1; ICH E3; CDISC SDTM AESOCCD; FDA NDA/BLA safety tables

description: Descriptive text of the MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) corresponding to soc_code. Used as the primary grouping header in all regulatory safety tabulations, PSUR/PBRER narratives, and DSMB reports.

description: Identifies the operational system from which this MedDRA coding record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer. Typically 'argus_safety' for records originating from Oracle Argus Safety.

description: The exact, unmodified adverse event term as reported by the investigator or subject in the source document or eCRF prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety verbatim field.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

Supports reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|closed|submitted|archived|pending_review|locked — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: The calendar date on which the causality assessment was performed or last confirmed by the assessor. Used to track compliance with expedited reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day/15-day clock).
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Externally-known business identifier for this causality assessment record, typically generated by Oracle Argus Safety (e.g., CA-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence.
reference: ICH E2A; Oracle Argus Safety case numbering conventions; 21 CFR Part 11

reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11; Oracle Argus Safety workflow states

description: The professional role of the individual performing the causality assessment. Distinguishes between the Principal Investigator (PI) site-level assessment and the Sponsor Medical Monitor's independent assessment, as required by ICH E2A and GCP guidelines.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; ICH E6(R2) Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: The assessor's determination of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Standard WHO/ICH causality categories used in Oracle Argus Safety and regulatory submissions. 'Not assessable' applies when insufficient information is available.
reference: WHO Causality Assessment Criteria; ICH E2A; CIOMS; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Standardized short code for the causality category used in CDISC SDTM AE domain submissions and Oracle Argus Safety exports (UN=Unrelated, UL=Unlikely, PO=Possible, PR=Probable, DE=Definite, NA=Not Assessable).
reference: CDISC SDTM AE domain; ICH E2A; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Indicates whether this causality assessment pertains to a concomitant medication (True) rather than the primary investigational product (False). Enables differentiation of IP-related versus concomitant drug-related assessments within the same AE case.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM CM domain; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Name of the concomitant medication being assessed for causal relationship when concomitant_medication_flag is True. Populated from WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) preferred drug name.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM CM domain

description: The date and time when this causality assessment record was first created in the source system (Oracle Argus Safety). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Outcome observed when the investigational product was withdrawn (dechallenged) following the adverse event. A positive dechallenge (AE resolved or improved upon withdrawal) supports a causal relationship. Captured per ICH E2A causality criteria.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment Criteria; CIOMS

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Review Date
description: The date on which the DSMB/DMC formally reviewed this causality assessment during a scheduled or ad hoc safety review meeting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on DMC

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Reviewed Flag
description: Indicates whether this causality assessment was reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) as part of a formal safety review. Supports DSMB/DMC meeting documentation and regulatory oversight.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on DMC; EMA Reflection Paper on DMC

description: Determination of whether the adverse event is listed in the current Investigator's Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI) as a known effect of the investigational product. Drives SUSAR classification and expedited reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether this causality assessment triggers an obligation for expedited regulatory reporting (7-day or 15-day report) to health authorities such as FDA, EMA, or PMDA based on SUSAR classification.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether this causality assessment is a revision of a previously submitted assessment. True when the assessor has changed their causality determination after initial submission, triggering regulatory follow-up reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Free-text clinical narrative provided by the assessor explaining the reasoning behind the causality determination. Includes consideration of temporal relationship, biological plausibility, dechallenge/rechallenge results, and alternative explanations. Required for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Outcome observed when the investigational product was re-administered (rechallenged) after the adverse event resolved. A positive rechallenge (AE recurred) provides strong evidence of causal relationship. Captured per ICH E2A causality criteria.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment Criteria; CIOMS

description: Calculated deadline date by which the expedited regulatory report (SUSAR/IND safety report) must be submitted to health authorities. Derived from the date of first awareness plus the applicable reporting timeline.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The applicable expedited reporting timeline in calendar days from the date of first awareness of the SUSAR (typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening or 15 days for other serious unexpected reactions). Drives compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Sequential revision version number for this causality assessment. Starts at 1 for the original assessment and increments with each revision. Enables audit trail tracking of assessment changes over the case lifecycle.
reference: ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; Oracle Argus Safety versioning

description: Indicates whether this causality assessment has been flagged for inclusion in pharmacovigilance signal detection analysis within Oracle Argus Safety. Supports aggregate safety review and cumulative causality trend monitoring.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this causality assessment record was extracted. Primarily Oracle Argus Safety; may include Medidata Rave for EDC-sourced assessments or 'manual' for paper-based entries.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles

description: The Sponsor Medical Monitor's independent causality determination, captured separately from the investigator's assessment. When sponsor_causality_override is True, this field holds the sponsor's differing conclusion used for regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the Sponsor Medical Monitor's causality determination differs from (overrides) the Investigator's assessment. When True, both assessments are retained and the sponsor's determination governs regulatory reporting obligations per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Assessment of whether the timing between investigational product administration and adverse event onset is consistent with a causal relationship. A key criterion in WHO and ICH causality assessment frameworks.

description: The date and time when this causality assessment record was most recently modified in the source system. Tracks the latest change for audit trail compliance and Silver layer incremental load processing.

description: Version of the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events used for severity grading in this narrative (e.g., '5.0'). Ensures consistency in grading across study sites and regulatory submissions.
reference: NCI CTCAE; CDISC SDTM; FDA Guidance on Adverse Event Reporting

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Review Date
description: Date on which the DSMB or DMC reviewed this safety case. Populated only when dsmb_reviewed is True. Used to track safety oversight compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Reviewed Flag
description: Indicates whether this safety case and its narrative have been reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). True if reviewed; False if not yet presented to the DSMB/DMC.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees; EMA Reflection Paper on DMCs

description: Unique message identifier assigned to the ICSR transmission in E2B(R3) XML format as submitted via eCTD to health authorities. Enables traceability between the narrative record and the regulatory submission artifact.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA Electronic Submissions Gateway; EMA EVWEB; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Detailed narrative describing the onset, nature, severity, and clinical course of the adverse event or serious adverse event, including signs, symptoms, and diagnostic findings. Core narrative section for ICSR submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section C.2; ICH E2A; FDA MedWatch 3500A; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Regulatory deadline by which the expedited safety report (ICSR) must be submitted to health authorities. Calculated from the date of first awareness per ICH E2A (7-day for fatal/life-threatening; 15-day for other serious unexpected).
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Actual date on which the expedited safety report (ICSR) was submitted to the relevant health authority via eCTD/E2B(R3) format. Used to verify compliance with regulatory reporting timelines.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the narrative was authored under blinded conditions (True) or after unblinding of treatment assignment (False). Critical for maintaining study integrity in double-blind trials; blinded narratives must not reveal treatment allocation.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Blinding; EMA Reflection Paper on Blinding

description: Clinical commentary and causality assessment provided by the medical monitor, including their opinion on the relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event, and any recommendations for protocol amendments or safety actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Full name of the medical monitor or safety physician who reviewed and approved the safety narrative. Required for regulatory audit trail and ICSR submission accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; ALCOA+ principles

description: Current workflow status of the safety narrative document. Tracks progression from initial authoring through medical monitor review and approval to regulatory submission. 'superseded' indicates a newer version has replaced this record.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Argus Safety workflow; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Narrative section describing the resolution or current status of the adverse event, including recovery details, sequelae, or death information. Corresponds to the outcome field in E2B(R3) regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section C.2.r.8; ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE.AEOUT

description: Pipe-delimited list of regulatory authorities to which this safety report was submitted (e.g., FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014; PMDA regulations

description: Indicates whether this narrative supports an initial expedited report, a follow-up report with updated information, or a final report closing the case. Drives regulatory submission timelines under ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the medical monitor or safety physician completed their clinical review of the narrative. Distinct from the authoring date; marks the transition from draft to reviewed status.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Argus Safety workflow; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Pipe-delimited list of ICH E2A seriousness criteria met by the adverse event (e.g., death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant). Determines expedited reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) Section C.2.r.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Reference identifier or path to the source documents (e.g., hospital records, lab reports, investigator notes) used to author this narrative, as stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. Supports SDV and audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ principles; Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Structured narrative section describing the patient's relevant medical history, demographics (age, sex, weight), concomitant medications, and pre-existing conditions relevant to the adverse event. Contains protected health information (PHI).
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section C.1; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; GDPR Article 9; CDISC SDTM DM/MH domains

description: Narrative section describing the investigational product(s) and concomitant medications administered, including dose, route, frequency, start and stop dates, and any dose modifications or interruptions relevant to the adverse event.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section B.4; CDISC SDTM EX/CM domains; ICH E2A

description: Date on which the treatment assignment was unblinded for this subject in the context of this safety case. Populated only when is_blinded transitions to False. Relevant for causality re-assessment post-unblinding.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Blinding; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Timestamp when this safety narrative record was most recently modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail, version control, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Sequential version number of the safety narrative document, incremented each time the narrative is revised. Version 1 is the initial draft; subsequent versions reflect amendments or follow-up updates.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Serious Adverse Event record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.32; Oracle Argus Safety

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the study subject (patient/participant) who experienced the SAE. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header per canonical attribute rules.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312.32; CDISC SDTM DM Domain

description: The action taken with the investigational product in response to the SAE (e.g., dose reduced, drug withdrawn, dose interrupted). This is a key regulatory field in ICSR submissions and clinical study reports.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Element G.k.8; CDISC SDTM AE.AEACN; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Externally-known unique case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety upon SAE intake. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER used for cross-system traceability, regulatory submissions, and sponsor communications.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Indicates whether the SAE case is currently blinded (treatment assignment unknown) or unblinded (treatment assignment revealed for safety reporting purposes). Unblinding may be required for SUSAR reporting to regulatory authorities.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312.32; Oracle Argus Safety

description: The investigator's assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the SAE. This is a critical regulatory field used to determine whether the event qualifies as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR).
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; CDISC SDTM AE.AEREL

description: The timestamp when the SAE record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field per canonical transaction header requirements. Conforms to 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles; Oracle Argus Safety

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Severity Grade
description: The CTCAE severity grade assigned to the SAE, ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). Grade 3 = severe, Grade 4 = life-threatening, Grade 5 = death. Used for oncology and other therapeutic area safety assessments.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM AE.AETOXGR; FDA Guidance on Adverse Event Reporting

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Reported Flag
description: Indicates whether this SAE was reported to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety review. Required for studies with an active DSMB/DMC charter.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees (2006); EMA Reflection Paper on DMCs

description: Indicates whether the SAE is expected (listed in the Investigator's Brochure or product labeling) or unexpected (not listed or differing in nature/severity). Unexpected + serious + related = SUSAR, triggering expedited regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Sequential number indicating which follow-up report this record represents (0 = initial report, 1 = first follow-up, 2 = second follow-up, etc.). Used to track the evolution of the SAE case over time as new information becomes available.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The date on which the SAE was first reported to the sponsor or CRO by the investigational site. This is Day 0 for calculating regulatory reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day or 15-day expedited reporting clock).
reference: ICH E2A Section 5; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI; Oracle Argus Safety

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Notification Date
description: The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of the SAE. Required under GCP and local regulations to ensure ongoing ethical oversight of the study.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3; 21 CFR Part 312.66; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this SAE has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting to health authorities (e.g., FDA 7/15-day reporting, EMA reporting). Derived from causality, expectedness, and seriousness criteria but stored as a business flag.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42; EMA GVP Module VI

description: The MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) code that most closely matches the verbatim SAE term. The LLT is the most granular level of MedDRA coding and is used to preserve the original reported term in a coded format.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE.AELLT

description: The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code assigned to the SAE. The PT is the primary level of MedDRA used for regulatory reporting and is the standard coded term submitted in ICSRs and clinical study reports.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE.AEDECOD; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code assigned to the SAE during medical coding. Represents the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy, used for regulatory submissions and safety signal detection.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE.AESOC

description: The version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this SAE (e.g., '26.0', '26.1'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding and compliance with current dictionary standards.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM

description: The full medical narrative describing the SAE, including clinical course, relevant medical history, concomitant medications, investigational product details, and the investigator's assessment. This is the primary free-text field in the Argus Safety case and is included in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2A Section 6; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32; Oracle Argus Safety

description: The date on which the serious adverse event first began or was first observed by the subject or investigator. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for the SAE transaction.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; CDISC SDTM AE.AESTDTC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The clinical outcome of the serious adverse event at the time of the most recent report. 'recovered_with_sequelae' indicates the subject recovered but with lasting effects. 'fatal' must align with seriousness_death = true.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) Element C.1.7; CDISC SDTM AE.AEOUT; Oracle Argus Safety

description: The calculated deadline by which the expedited regulatory report (e.g., IND Safety Report, SUSAR notification) must be submitted to the relevant health authority. Typically 7 calendar days for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs and 15 calendar days for other SUSARs.
reference: ICH E2A Section 5; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Classifies the type of safety report generated for this SAE. 'expedited' = 7/15-day SUSAR or IND safety report; 'periodic' = included in DSUR/PSUR; 'spontaneous' = post-marketing spontaneous report; 'literature' = identified from published literature.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2C(R2) DSUR; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The health authority or regulatory body to which the SAE/SUSAR report was submitted (e.g., 'FDA', 'EMA', 'MHRA', 'PMDA', 'TGA', 'Health Canada'). May contain multiple authorities if reported to more than one. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|ANVISA|CDSCO — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014; EMA GVP Module VI; MHRA; PMDA

description: The date on which the serious adverse event resolved, stabilized, or the subject died. Null if the event is ongoing at the time of reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE.AEENDTC; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Current lifecycle status of the SAE case in Oracle Argus Safety. 'initial' = first report received; 'follow_up' = additional information received; 'closed' = case fully resolved and closed; 'nullified' = case determined not to meet SAE criteria and voided.
reference: ICH E2A; Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The verbatim (as-reported) description of the serious adverse event as recorded by the investigator or site staff in the source document or eCRF. This is the raw, uncoded term prior to MedDRA coding.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE.AETERM; Oracle Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 312.32

business_glossary_term: Seriousness Criterion: Congenital Anomaly or Birth Defect
description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in a congenital anomaly or birth defect in the offspring of the subject.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in death of the subject. One of the six ICH E2A seriousness criteria that must be captured individually.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in persistent or significant disability or incapacity.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of requiring inpatient hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of being immediately life-threatening (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death at the time of the event).
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Seriousness Criterion: Other Medically Important Condition
description: Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of being an other medically important condition — events that may not be immediately life-threatening but may jeopardize the subject or require intervention to prevent one of the other serious outcomes.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA GVP Module VI

description: The date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) received the complete minimum information required to process the SAE. This date starts the regulatory reporting clock per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 5; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI; Oracle Argus Safety

description: The protocol-assigned subject identifier (e.g., site-subject number) for the patient who experienced the SAE. Restricted as it can be used to identify an individual participant in combination with study data.
reference: ICH E6(R2); HIPAA 45 CFR §164; GDPR Article 4; CDISC SDTM DM.SUBJID

description: The timestamp when the SAE record was most recently modified in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field per canonical transaction header requirements. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles; Oracle Argus Safety
