description: Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this SUSAR occurred. Links the SUSAR to the sponsoring study for regulatory reporting and safety surveillance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: SUSARs require investigator and IRB/IEC notification at the study-site level. Linking susar to study_site drives per-site SUSAR notification workflows and enables study-site-level SUSAR aggregate repo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: SUSAR causality and expectedness determination requires the subject's authoritative treatment assignment from the randomization system. treatment_assignment on susar is denormalized from subject_rando
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: SUSARs are compiled from subject visit eCRF data. Regulatory traceability for 7/15-day expedited reports requires linking the SUSAR to the specific visit data record from which the case was identified
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: The visit at which a SUSAR-triggering event was identified is required for SDTM AE domain traceability and source document verification during regulatory inspections. Pharmacovigilance teams need visi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SUSARs result in expedited regulatory submissions (IND safety reports, EudraVigilance submissions). The submission record tracks the regulatory filing. A clinical research expert expects SUSARs to ref
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: SUSAR expedited reports are essential TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 1, Section 11). Regulatory inspectors verify that each SUSAR submission is filed in the TMF; this FK enables that trac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the study subject (patient) who experienced the SUSAR. Links to the subject master record for patient-level safety surveillance and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9

description: Verbatim description of the adverse event as reported by the investigator or subject, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for source data verification (SDV) and coding audit trail per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: CDISC SDTM AE domain; ICH E2A; MedDRA; ALCOA+

description: System-generated case identifier from Oracle Argus Safety, the operational system of record for adverse event and SUSAR management. Used to trace the SUSAR back to its source case in Argus.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety; ICH E2A

description: Date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) first became aware of the SUSAR. This date starts the regulatory reporting clock for 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting obligations under ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the treatment blind was broken for this subject. Populated only when blind_broken is True. Required for audit trail and regulatory documentation of unblinding events.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.3; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the treatment blind was broken for this subject in order to perform the causality assessment for this SUSAR. A blind break is a significant event requiring documentation and regulatory notification.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Blinding status of the study at the time of SUSAR reporting. Indicates whether the study was blinded, and if so, whether the blind was broken for this subject to determine treatment assignment for causality assessment.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; ICH E9

description: Externally-known alphanumeric case number assigned by the sponsor or CRO for this SUSAR, used in all regulatory submissions and correspondence. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Investigator's or sponsor's assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the serious adverse reaction. A SUSAR requires a positive causality assessment (related or probably/possibly related) to the investigational product.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; WHO Causality Assessment; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp when the SUSAR record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction header, supporting ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Severity Grade
description: CTCAE severity grade assigned to the adverse reaction (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Used for safety signal detection, DSMB/DMC reviews, and regulatory reporting.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE domain

description: Calculated deadline for 15-day expedited reporting to regulatory authorities for non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSARs. Derived from the reporting clock start date plus 15 calendar days per ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c)(2); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Calculated deadline for 7-day expedited reporting to regulatory authorities for fatal or life-threatening SUSARs. Derived from the reporting clock start date plus 7 calendar days per ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c)(1); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Dose of the investigational product administered to the subject at the time of the adverse reaction onset, including amount and unit (e.g., '100 mg'). Required for causality assessment and regulatory case narratives.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM EX domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Date on which the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this SUSAR. Populated only when dsmb_notified is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on DSMB

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Notification Indicator
description: Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this SUSAR. Required for studies with an active DSMB/DMC per ICH E6(R2) and study protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on DSMB; ICH E9

description: Unique case identifier assigned by the EudraVigilance system upon submission of the SUSAR to the EMA. Used for tracking and referencing the case in EU regulatory correspondence and EudraVigilance database.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42; EMA GVP Module VI; EudraVigilance

description: Assessment of whether the serious adverse reaction is unexpected — i.e., not listed or not consistent with the nature, severity, or frequency described in the current Investigator's Brochure (IB). A SUSAR must be assessed as unexpected to qualify for expedited reporting.
reference: ICH E2A Section 2; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(a)(2); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date of subject death if the outcome of the SUSAR is fatal. Triggers the 7-day expedited reporting clock and is required for death narratives in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c)(1); CDISC SDTM DS domain

description: Sequential number of the follow-up report for this SUSAR case (e.g., 1 for first follow-up, 2 for second). Zero or null for initial reports. Tracks the version history of regulatory submissions for this case.
reference: ICH E2A Section 5; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of this SUSAR. Populated only when irb_iec_notified is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR 312.66; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Notification Indicator
description: Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of this SUSAR as required by GCP and local regulations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR 312.66; EU CTR 536/2014

description: MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) code that most closely matches the verbatim adverse event term. Used for granular coding traceability and verbatim-to-PT mapping audit.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE domain

description: MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code assigned to this SUSAR. The PT is the primary level of MedDRA used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis in IND/NDA/BLA submissions.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code assigned to this SUSAR during medical coding. Represents the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is required for regulatory submissions and aggregate safety reporting.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding this SUSAR (e.g., 26.0, 26.1). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding and compliance with current MedDRA release requirements.
reference: MedDRA; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM AE domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32

description: Medical narrative summarizing the SUSAR case, including patient demographics, event description, clinical course, causality assessment, and outcome. Required component of all expedited regulatory reports per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 6; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; CIOMS I Form

description: Date on which the subject first experienced the serious adverse reaction. This is the principal real-world event time (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP) for the SUSAR and is the anchor for all regulatory reporting clocks per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; CDISC SDTM AE domain

description: Clinical outcome of the serious adverse reaction at the time of reporting. Required for regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC safety reviews. Tracks resolution status of the event.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: The date from which the regulatory reporting clock begins for this SUSAR. Typically the sponsor awareness date, but may be adjusted based on the date of unblinding or receipt of minimum reportable information per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 4; FDA 21 CFR 312.32(c); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Current lifecycle state of the SUSAR regulatory reporting workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through submission to regulatory authority acknowledgement and case closure. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Route by which the investigational product was administered (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous). Required for regulatory case narratives and pharmacovigilance signal assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM EX domain; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; WHO-DD

description: ICH E2A seriousness criterion met by this adverse reaction that qualifies it as a Serious Adverse Event (SAE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A Section 1; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether this SUSAR submission is an initial report, a follow-up report with additional information, or a final report closing the case. Regulatory authorities require tracking of report versions per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 5; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SUSAR record in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header, supporting audit trail and data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each CTCAE grade definition record in the reference table. This is the primary key for the ctcae_grade product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a controlled NCI-published code list of severity grades; per-role minimum categories are exempt.]
reference: NCI CTCAE; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: ctcae_grade stores meddra_pt_code, meddra_pt_name, meddra_soc_code, meddra_version as strings representing the MedDRA mapping for each CTCAE grade. Adding meddra_term_id FK to the meddra_term referenc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The official CTCAE adverse event term name as published by NCI (e.g., 'Atrial fibrillation', 'Nausea', 'Peripheral neuropathy'). This is the standardized clinical term used to classify and report adverse events across all clinical trials. Corresponds to the AETERM or AEDECOD field in CDISC SDTM AE domain and is the primary human-readable label for this grading record.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 AE Domain; ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management

description: Specifies the patient population for which this CTCAE grade definition is applicable (e.g., 'All', 'Pediatric', 'Adult', 'Oncology'). Some CTCAE terms and grade criteria differ by population (e.g., pediatric-specific grading criteria). Used to filter applicable grade definitions when assigning severity in studies with specific population constraints.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E11 Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Pediatric Population; FDA Pediatric Study Requirements

business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Codelist Code
description: The CDISC controlled terminology codelist code associated with this CTCAE grade entry (e.g., the AETOXGR codelist code). Enables direct mapping to CDISC SDTM submission datasets and ADaM analysis datasets produced in SAS Drug Development. Required for eCTD regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, and PMDA.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3; CDISC ADaM IG; FDA eCTD Technical Specifications

description: A brief description of what changed in this grade definition relative to the prior CTCAE version (e.g., 'Grade 3 criteria updated to include specific lab threshold', 'New term added in v5.0', 'Grade description clarified'). Supports data managers and regulatory affairs teams in assessing the impact of CTCAE version upgrades on ongoing studies and in preparing regulatory submission change justifications.
reference: NCI CTCAE Version History; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on Protocol Amendments; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Supplementary clinical criteria, laboratory thresholds, or objective measurement parameters that define the boundary conditions for assigning this specific grade to the AE term (e.g., specific lab value ranges, vital sign thresholds, functional impairment descriptors). Distinct from the grade_description in that it captures the measurable/objective criteria rather than the narrative description. Used by clinical staff for precise grade assignment and by biostatisticians for consistency validation.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CTCAE Grading Guidelines; ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM IG

description: The timestamp when this CTCAE grade reference record was first loaded into the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, ALCOA+ principles, and GDPR Article 5 data integrity obligations. Used by data management teams to track when new CTCAE version data was ingested.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Term Code
description: Official NCI-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the CTCAE adverse event term within a given version. Used as the primary business identifier for cross-system referencing and CDISC SDTM mapping (AETOXGR domain). Enables unambiguous linkage between AE records and the reference grading criteria.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM IG v3.3 AE Domain; NCI Thesaurus (NCIt)

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Version
description: Version number of the CTCAE publication from which this grade definition originates (e.g., 'v5.0', 'v4.03'). Critical for ensuring AE severity grading is applied against the correct version mandated by the study protocol. New CTCAE versions are released by the National Cancer Institute (NCI) and may alter grade descriptions or add/remove terms.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on AE Reporting

description: The date on which this CTCAE grade definition was superseded, retired, or replaced by a newer version (nullable for currently active records). Used in conjunction with effective_start_date to implement bi-temporal versioning of the reference table, ensuring that AE records can always be validated against the grade criteria that were in effect at the time of the adverse event.
reference: NCI CTCAE Publication History; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5 (data accuracy)

description: The date from which this CTCAE grade definition became effective and available for use in clinical trial AE grading (typically the official NCI publication date of the CTCAE version). Used to determine which version of a grade definition was applicable at the time of an AE occurrence, supporting retrospective audit and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: NCI CTCAE Publication History; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Numeric severity grade (1 through 5) as defined by CTCAE. Grade 1 = Mild; Grade 2 = Moderate; Grade 3 = Severe; Grade 4 = Life-threatening or disabling; Grade 5 = Death related to AE. This is the core grading dimension of the reference table and is used to populate the AETOXGR field in CDISC SDTM submissions and to trigger SAE/SUSAR reporting workflows in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; CDISC SDTM IG AE Domain

description: Full verbatim clinical description of the severity grade for the specific AE term as published in the CTCAE by NCI (e.g., for 'Nausea' Grade 2: 'Moderate nausea; oral intake decreased without significant weight loss, dehydration or malnutrition'). This is the authoritative clinical criteria text used by investigators and CRAs to assign the correct grade during AE assessment. Stored verbatim to preserve regulatory integrity.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Standardized textual label corresponding to the numeric CTCAE grade (e.g., 'Mild' for Grade 1, 'Moderate' for Grade 2, 'Severe' for Grade 3, 'Life-Threatening' for Grade 4, 'Death' for Grade 5). Provides a human-readable severity descriptor for use in clinical reports, DSMB/DMC safety narratives, and regulatory submissions without requiring a lookup of the numeric grade.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E2A; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: Additional clinical notes, caveats, or guidance provided by NCI for applying this specific grade to the AE term (e.g., 'Also consider Anemia', 'See also Cardiac disorders: Left ventricular systolic dysfunction'). Captures the 'Also consider' and 'Navigational Note' annotations present in the CTCAE publication that guide investigators in differential grading decisions.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on AE Assessment

description: Indicates whether this CTCAE grade definition record is currently active and available for use in new AE grading assignments. Set to False when a term or grade is retired, superseded by a newer CTCAE version, or deprecated. Enables version-controlled filtering so that active studies use only current, approved grading criteria while historical records remain accessible for audit.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this CTCAE adverse event term and grade definition is specific to oncology/cancer treatment contexts. CTCAE was originally developed for oncology trials; some terms are exclusively relevant to chemotherapy, radiation, or immunotherapy adverse effects. Used to filter applicable grading criteria for oncology versus non-oncology study protocols.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; NCI Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP); FDA Oncology Guidance

business_glossary_term: Serious Adverse Event (SAE) Flag by Grade
description: Indicates whether this CTCAE grade level is inherently classified as a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) based on regulatory definitions (typically Grade 3 and above may trigger SAE criteria, Grade 4–5 are always serious). Used to automate SAE flagging logic in Oracle Argus Safety and EDC systems (Medidata Rave) when a grade is recorded, supporting expedited reporting timelines under ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; NCI CTCAE v5.0

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Code
description: The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code that most closely corresponds to this CTCAE adverse event term, enabling cross-coding between CTCAE and MedDRA terminologies. Used for regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) that require MedDRA coding, and for signal detection in Oracle Argus Safety. Alignment maintained per current MedDRA version used in the study.
reference: ICH M1 MedDRA Terminology; MedDRA Introductory Guide; CDISC SDTM IG AE Domain; FDA MedDRA Requirements

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) numeric code corresponding to the primary SOC classification of this CTCAE term's MedDRA PT cross-reference. Enables integrated safety tabulations that span both CTCAE-graded AE data and MedDRA-coded data in regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC reports.
reference: ICH M1 MedDRA Terminology; CDISC SDTM IG AE Domain; FDA MedDRA Requirements; EMA MedDRA Requirements

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Version
description: Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for the cross-reference mapping between this CTCAE term and its corresponding MedDRA PT/SOC codes (e.g., '26.0', '25.1'). MedDRA is updated biannually; version tracking ensures regulatory submissions reference the correct dictionary version as required by FDA, EMA, and PMDA.
reference: ICH M1 MedDRA Terminology; MedDRA Release Notes; FDA MedDRA Requirements; EMA MedDRA Requirements

business_glossary_term: National Cancer Institute (NCI) Thesaurus Concept ID
description: The NCI Thesaurus (NCIt) concept identifier (e.g., 'C54767') for this CTCAE adverse event term. Provides a stable, version-independent identifier from the NCI controlled vocabulary that persists across CTCAE versions. Used for ontology-based data integration, CDISC terminology alignment, and cross-study analytics in SAS Drug Development and AI/ML safety signal detection pipelines.
reference: NCI Thesaurus (NCIt); CDISC Controlled Terminology; NCI CTCAE v5.0; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The official publication date of the CTCAE version from which this grade definition originates, as released by the National Cancer Institute (NCI) Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP). Provides the authoritative reference date for regulatory submissions and audit trails, distinct from the effective_start_date which may reflect internal adoption timing.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; NCI Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP); FDA Guidance on AE Reporting

description: The CTCAE term code from the immediately preceding CTCAE version that this record supersedes or corresponds to, enabling version-to-version lineage tracking. Used by data managers and biostatisticians to reconcile AE grading data collected under different CTCAE versions within the same study or across studies in integrated safety analyses.
reference: NCI CTCAE Version History; CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: The maximum number of calendar days within which an AE at this grade must be reported to regulatory authorities upon sponsor awareness (e.g., 7 days for fatal/life-threatening unexpected SUSARs, 15 days for other serious unexpected reactions, null for non-expedited grades). Drives automated due-date calculation in Oracle Argus Safety and regulatory_report_due_date population on the adverse_event product.
reference: FDA 21 CFR 312.32; ICH E2A; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014; PMDA Safety Reporting Guidelines

description: Indicates whether an AE recorded at this CTCAE grade level triggers mandatory expedited regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., 7-day or 15-day IND safety reports to FDA, EMA, PMDA). Derived from regulatory guidance but stored as a reference attribute to drive automated reporting workflow triggers in Oracle Argus Safety and CTMS. Supports compliance with FDA 21 CFR 312.32, EMA GVP Module VI, and ICH E2A timelines.
reference: FDA 21 CFR 312.32; ICH E2A; EMA GVP Module VI; PMDA Safety Reporting Guidelines; ICH E6(R2)

description: The two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code to which this CTCAE grade reference applies (e.g., 'AE' for Adverse Events domain). Supports SDTM dataset construction in SAS Drug Development and ensures correct domain-level mapping during regulatory submission preparation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA CDISC Requirements

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Variable Name
description: The CDISC SDTM variable name to which this CTCAE grade value maps in regulatory submission datasets (e.g., 'AETOXGR' for the AE domain toxicity grade). Used by SAS Drug Development programmers and CDISC data managers to ensure correct SDTM dataset population and FDA/EMA submission compliance.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.3; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide AE Domain; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA CDISC Requirements

description: NCI-assigned code for the System Organ Class (SOC) category within the CTCAE hierarchy. Enables programmatic grouping and filtering of adverse event terms by organ system for safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC reports, and regulatory submissions. Distinct from MedDRA SOC codes but may be cross-referenced.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; NCI Thesaurus (NCIt); CDISC SDTM IG AE Domain

description: The System Organ Class (SOC) category name as defined in the CTCAE, representing the highest-level anatomical or physiological grouping of adverse event terms (e.g., 'Cardiac disorders', 'Gastrointestinal disorders', 'Nervous system disorders'). Aligns with MedDRA SOC hierarchy for cross-coding purposes. Used for safety signal detection and aggregate reporting by organ system.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH M1 MedDRA Terminology

description: Reference to the specific section, page, or table in the official NCI CTCAE publication document from which this grade definition was extracted (e.g., 'CTCAE v5.0, Section 2, Table: Cardiac Disorders, p.12'). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and audit trail requirements by providing a direct link to the authoritative source document stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

value_regex: Oracle Argus Safety|SAS Drug Development|NCI CTCAE|Manual
description: The operational system or authoritative source from which this CTCAE grade reference record was loaded (e.g., 'Oracle Argus Safety' where CTCAE codes are maintained, 'SAS Drug Development' for CDISC-aligned reference data, 'NCI CTCAE' for direct NCI publication ingestion). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver layer provenance requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC Data Management Standards

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to this CTCAE grade reference record in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Tracks corrections, enrichments, or metadata updates applied after initial load. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

description: The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF for the official NCI CTCAE source document associated with this version. Enables direct navigation from the reference record to the controlled source document in the electronic Trial Master File, supporting audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness per 21 CFR Part 11 and EU CTR 536/2014.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF System

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Development Safety Update Report record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E2F Guideline on Development Safety Update Reports

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: DSUR cumulative subject exposure counts and SAE incidence rates are reported per SAP-defined analysis population (typically the safety population). Regulatory DSUR templates require population-stratif
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: DSURs in EU trials are filed under specific CTAs per EMA requirements. Linking dsur to clinical_trial_authorization replaces the denormalized cta_number and enables CTA-level DSUR compliance tracking
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: DSUR data lock date must be traceable to the formal database lock event for regulatory audit trail. ICH E2F requires DSURs to specify the data lock point; linking to database_lock_event provides the f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.dsmb_review. Business justification: A DSUR (Development Safety Update Report) references the DSMB review conducted during the reporting period. dsur has dsmb_review_conducted (BOOLEAN) and dsmb_recommendation (STRING) but no FK to the d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: DSURs are submitted to specific primary health authorities. Linking to health_authority normalizes the primary_health_authority field and enables DSUR submission tracking by authority, supporting annu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: DSUR is fundamentally organized around the investigational product per ICH E2F. dsur.investigational_product_name and investigational_product_code are denormalized; FK to imp_master enables automated
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

The CTA defines the reporting obligation and authority for ICSR submissions in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects ICSRs to r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: ICSRs are filed per study investigational product — the E2B(R3) message requires study-specific IP details (IND number, formulation, dose form) from study.investigational_product, which is distinct fr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site. Business justification: ICSRs submitted to FDA (MedWatch), EMA (EudraVigilance), and other authorities must identify the reporting site per ICH E2B(R3). icsr currently has no site FK despite containing site-level regulatory
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions require inclusion of relevant laboratory test results (Section C.3.1 lab data fields). Pharmacovigilance systems (Argus, Veeva Vault Safety) must link the specific lab res
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: ICSRs submitted to regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) are included in IND/CTA regulatory binders. Regulatory affairs teams need to link each ICSR to its submission binder for eCTD compilation, agency corr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.safety_reporting_obligation. Business justification: Each ICSR is filed to fulfill a specific regulatory reporting obligation. icsr.reporting_obligation_type is a STRING that should be normalized to a FK. Adding safety_reporting_obligation_id links the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.serious_adverse_event. Business justification: ICSRs are the formal regulatory submission records for expedited safety reporting, primarily triggered by SAEs and SUSARs. While icsr.adverse_event_id links to the base AE, a direct FK to serious_adve
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study associated with this ICSR, linking the safety report to the sponsoring trial.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: ICSRs must be tracked against the study_site for site-level regulatory reporting metrics, IRB/IEC notification tracking, and CRO monitoring dashboards. icsr already has investigational_site_id but lac
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: ICSR (E2B/MedWatch) preparation requires the subject's randomization assignment — treatment arm and blinding status — to complete causality and drug exposure sections. Pharmacovigilance systems like A
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: ICSRs are compiled from subject visit eCRF data. E2B traceability requirements and health authority inspections require linking each ICSR to the specific visit data record from which the case informat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: ICSRs require visit-level traceability for complete case narrative construction and SDTM AE domain mapping. The visit at which the reportable event was observed is a standard data element in E2B(R3) c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: ICSRs are expedited regulatory submissions (7/15-day reports) that must be filed as essential documents in the TMF per ICH E6(R2) and GVP Module VI. TMF managers and regulatory affairs teams track the
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event triggering this ICSR.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA

description: The date on which the regulatory agency formally acknowledged receipt of the ICSR submission. For electronic submissions via EudraVigilance or FDA gateway, this is the date of the automated acknowledgement message (ACK).
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA EudraVigilance; FDA ESG (Electronic Submissions Gateway)

description: The unique reference number or message ID returned by the regulatory agency in the acknowledgement (ACK) message confirming receipt of the ICSR. Used for reconciliation and proof of submission.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA EudraVigilance ACK; FDA ESG

description: The operative regulatory deadline date for this specific ICSR, reflecting the applicable reporting window (7-day or 15-day) based on the seriousness criteria and expectedness of the adverse event. Used for compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The unique case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety to the source safety case from which this ICSR was generated. Used for traceability back to the system of record.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety case submission module; ICH E2B(R3)

business_glossary_term: Clock Start Date (Date of First Awareness)
description: The date on which the sponsor or CRO first became aware of the adverse event that triggered this ICSR. This is the regulatory clock start date (Day 0) from which all expedited reporting deadlines (7-day and 15-day) are calculated per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EMA EudraVigilance

description: The date and time at which this ICSR record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8

description: The number of calendar days elapsed between the awareness_date (clock start) and the actual submission_date. Used to assess compliance with 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting requirements. Negative values indicate early submission.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The calculated calendar date by which a serious unexpected adverse reaction (non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSAR) must be submitted to the regulatory agency, computed as awareness_date + 15 calendar days per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(ii); EMA EudraVigilance

description: The calculated calendar date by which a fatal or life-threatening unexpected SUSAR must be submitted to the regulatory agency, computed as awareness_date + 7 calendar days per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(i); EMA EudraVigilance

description: Free-text explanation documenting the reason why the ICSR was not submitted within the applicable regulatory deadline. Required for late submissions to support CAPA processes and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E2A; GCP ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; CAPA requirements

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this ICSR. Required for studies with an active DSMB charter.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on DSMB; EMA DSMB guidance

description: The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of this ICSR. Used for compliance tracking against GCP notification requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.66; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study was notified of this ICSR, as required by GCP and applicable regulations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.66; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP

description: Indicates whether this record represents the most current version of the ICSR. True for the latest version; False for superseded versions retained for audit history.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); ALCOA+ data integrity principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this ICSR resulted in the death of the subject. Fatal cases trigger the 7-day expedited reporting clock per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(i); EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this ICSR was life-threatening (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death). Life-threatening cases trigger the 7-day expedited reporting clock per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section 3.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(i); EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether the ICSR was submitted to the regulatory agency on or before the applicable regulatory deadline date. True = compliant submission; False = late submission. Key KPI for regulatory compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance; ISO 14155

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this ICSR relates to a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR). SUSARs require expedited reporting to regulatory authorities and ethics committees under ICH E2A and EU CTR 536/2014.
reference: ICH E2A; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42; EMA EudraVigilance; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The structured free-text clinical narrative summarizing the adverse event, patient information, treatment details, and outcome as included in the ICSR submission. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) H.1 narrative section.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) H.1; FDA MedWatch 3500A Section G; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The reason provided by the regulatory agency when an ICSR submission is rejected (e.g., data validation failure, duplicate case, missing mandatory fields). Populated only when submission_status = 'rejected'.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA EudraVigilance; FDA ESG validation rules

description: The externally-known, unique regulatory report identifier assigned to this ICSR submission (e.g., the E2B message identifier or MedWatch report number). Used by health authorities to track and acknowledge the submission.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.0.1; FDA MedWatch 3500A; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Indicates whether this ICSR is an initial submission, a follow-up update with new information, or a nullification (withdrawal) of a previously submitted report. Aligns with ICH E2B(R3) A.1.6.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.6; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Sequential version number of this ICSR record. Version 1 is the initial report; subsequent versions represent follow-up or amended submissions for the same case.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); Oracle Argus Safety case versioning

description: The unique identifier of the organization or individual sending the ICSR to the regulatory agency, as defined in ICH E2B(R3) A.3.1. Typically the sponsor or CRO EudraVigilance/FDA sender ID.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.3.1; EMA EudraVigilance; FDA ESG

description: The operational system of record from which this ICSR record was sourced. Primarily Oracle Argus Safety case submission module; 'Manual' for records entered outside the system.
reference: ALCOA+ data integrity principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: The actual calendar date on which the ICSR was transmitted to the regulatory agency. Used to calculate days-to-submission and determine on-time compliance against the applicable deadline.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance; ALCOA+

description: The technical format or channel used to transmit the ICSR to the regulatory agency. E2B XML is the standard electronic format per ICH E2B(R3); MedWatch and CIOMS I are paper/PDF formats used for specific agencies or fallback scenarios.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA MedWatch 3500A; CIOMS I Form; EMA EudraVigilance gateway

description: Current workflow state of the ICSR submission lifecycle. Tracks progression from internal drafting through regulatory acknowledgement or rejection.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: The precise date and time (with timezone) at which the ICSR was electronically transmitted to the regulatory agency gateway. Provides audit-grade precision beyond the submission date for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 11; EMA EudraVigilance gateway

description: The date and time at which this ICSR record was most recently modified in Oracle Argus Safety or the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports change tracking and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each MedDRA term record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — _canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a standardized regulatory reference code list (MedDRA hierarchy) maintained by MSSO/ICH; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt.]
reference: MedDRA Maintenance and Support Services Organization (MSSO); ICH M1 Guideline

business_glossary_term: Oracle Argus Safety Dictionary Record ID
description: The internal dictionary record identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety when this MedDRA term is loaded into the Argus dictionary module. Enables direct cross-reference between the lakehouse silver layer and the Oracle Argus Safety system of record for adverse event coding reconciliation and audit.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety Dictionary Management Guide; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Describes the nature of the change applied to this term in the current MedDRA version: 'new' (first introduced), 'modified' (name or hierarchy changed), 'retired' (removed from active use), 'split' (one term divided into multiple), 'merged' (multiple terms combined), or 'reinstated' (previously retired term reactivated). Critical for managing dictionary upgrade impacts in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Change Documentation; ICH E2B(R3); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: The timestamp when this MedDRA term record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA+ data integrity principles for the clinical data management platform.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade Mapping
description: The CTCAE v5.0 term name that corresponds to this MedDRA PT, where a direct mapping exists. Enables cross-referencing between MedDRA coding and CTCAE severity grading used in oncology and other therapeutic area studies. Null where no CTCAE mapping exists for this term.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; MedDRA-CTCAE Mapping Table; CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Oncology Guidance

description: The date from which this MedDRA term is officially effective and available for use in adverse event coding and regulatory submissions, corresponding to the MSSO release date of the version in which it was introduced. Supports version-gating logic in Oracle Argus Safety and study-level coding version controls.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Release Schedule; ICH E2B(R3); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: The level within the five-tier MedDRA hierarchy to which this term belongs: LLT (Lowest Level Term), PT (Preferred Term), HLT (High Level Term), HLGT (High Level Group Term), or SOC (System Organ Class). Determines how the term is used in adverse event coding, CTCAE mapping, and regulatory aggregate reporting.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1 Guideline; CDISC SDTM IG (AEDECOD, AEBODSYS); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code for the High Level Group Term (HLGT) in the primary SOC path for this term. Populated for LLT, PT, HLT, and HLGT level terms to support aggregate safety analysis and regulatory tabulations at the HLGT level.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The official MedDRA name of the High Level Group Term (HLGT) in the primary SOC path. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations and signal detection analytics without requiring a hierarchy traversal.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code for the High Level Term (HLT) in the primary SOC path for this term. Populated for LLT, PT, and HLT level terms to support aggregate safety analysis at the HLT level in regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC reports.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The official MedDRA name of the High Level Term (HLT) in the primary SOC path. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations and signal detection analytics.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The MedDRA version in which this term was first introduced into the dictionary. Supports historical analysis of term availability across study timelines and ensures correct version-gating when coding adverse events from legacy studies.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term History Documentation

description: Applicable to LLT-level terms only. Indicates whether the LLT is a 'current' LLT (true) — meaning it maps to a PT with the same name — or a 'non-current' LLT (false) that maps to a PT with a different name. Current LLTs are preferred for new coding; non-current LLTs are retained for historical traceability per MSSO policy.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO LLT Currency Policy; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Indicates whether this term's association to a System Organ Class (SOC) is the primary (true) or secondary (false) SOC linkage. MedDRA allows a PT to be linked to multiple SOCs; the primary SOC is used for regulatory aggregate safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC safety reviews, and CTCAE-aligned reporting.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG (AEBODSYS); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the language of this term record (e.g., 'en' for English, 'ja' for Japanese, 'de' for German). MedDRA is published in multiple languages; this field supports multi-language implementations required for submissions to PMDA (Japanese), EMA (EU languages), and Health Canada (French/English).
reference: ISO 639-1; MedDRA Multi-Language Guide; PMDA Submission Requirements; EMA MedDRA Policy; Health Canada Guidance

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code for the Lowest Level Term (LLT). For LLT-level records this equals term_code. For higher-level terms this field is null. LLTs capture verbatim-level medical concepts and are the entry point for verbatim-to-MedDRA coding in Oracle Argus Safety and EDC systems.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG; FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: The official MedDRA name of the Lowest Level Term (LLT). For LLT-level records this equals term_name. Null for higher-level terms. Used in verbatim coding workflows and auto-coding dictionaries in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG

description: The date on which this MedDRA term record was loaded into the lakehouse silver layer from the MSSO distribution files. Used to track the effective date of each biannual dictionary update and to support audit trails required under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; MSSO Release Schedule; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The biannual MedDRA release version in which this term was introduced or last updated (e.g., '27.0', '26.1'). Critical for regulatory submissions requiring version-specific coding, database lock (DBL) documentation, and CDISC SDTM dataset compliance. MedDRA releases occur twice yearly (March and September).
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Release Schedule; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code for the Preferred Term (PT) to which this term maps. For PT-level records this equals term_code. For LLT-level records this is the parent PT code. Used as the primary coding level in Oracle Argus Safety adverse event records, CDISC SDTM (AEDECOD), and regulatory case submissions (ICH E2B).
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG (AEDECOD); FDA Adverse Event Reporting; EMA MedDRA Policy

description: The official MedDRA name of the Preferred Term (PT) to which this term maps. Denormalized for direct use in adverse event coding displays, safety narratives, CDISC SDTM datasets, and regulatory submissions without requiring a hierarchy join.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH E2B(R3); CDISC SDTM IG (AEDECOD); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

business_glossary_term: Medidata Rave EDC Dictionary Entry ID
description: The dictionary entry identifier assigned by Medidata Rave EDC when this MedDRA term is configured in the Rave coding dictionary for eCRF verbatim-to-MedDRA auto-coding. Supports reconciliation between EDC-coded adverse events and the master MedDRA reference in the lakehouse.
reference: Medidata Rave Coding Module Documentation; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code of the term that replaces this term when it has been retired, split, or merged. Null for active terms and retired terms with no direct replacement. Supports automated re-coding workflows in Oracle Argus Safety during biannual MedDRA version upgrades.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term Retirement and Replacement Policy; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The MedDRA version in which this term was retired, if applicable. Null for active terms. Used to enforce coding rules in Oracle Argus Safety and to flag terms that should not be applied to new adverse event records after the specified version.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term Retirement Policy; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The date on which this MedDRA term was officially retired by MSSO, corresponding to the release date of the version in which it was retired. Null for active terms. Used to enforce coding restrictions in Oracle Argus Safety and to flag terms in legacy study datasets.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term Retirement Policy; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Pipe-delimited list of Standardised MedDRA Query (SMQ) names to which this PT belongs. SMQs are validated, topic-focused groupings of MedDRA terms used for signal detection, aggregate safety analysis, and regulatory queries (e.g., 'Anaphylactic reaction', 'Drug-induced liver injury'). Populated for PT-level terms only.
reference: MedDRA SMQ Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO SMQ Documentation; ICH E2B(R3); EMA Signal Detection Guidance

description: Indicates whether this term's SMQ membership is classified as 'broad' (higher sensitivity, more inclusive) or 'narrow' (higher specificity, more restrictive) within the associated SMQ. Used to configure signal detection algorithms in Oracle Argus Safety and to define the scope of safety queries in regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA SMQ Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO SMQ Documentation; EMA Signal Detection Guidance; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The standardized two-to-four letter abbreviation for the System Organ Class (SOC) as defined by MSSO (e.g., 'CARD' for Cardiac disorders, 'NERV' for Nervous system disorders). Used in safety tabulation headers, DSMB/DMC slide decks, and abbreviated regulatory reports.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO SOC Abbreviation List; ICH M1

description: The 8-digit MedDRA code for the System Organ Class (SOC) to which this term is linked (primary or secondary). Populated for all hierarchy levels to enable direct SOC-level filtering in safety tabulations, signal detection, and regulatory submissions without requiring a hierarchy traversal join.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1; CDISC SDTM IG (AEBODSYS); EMA MedDRA Policy

description: The official MedDRA name of the System Organ Class (SOC) associated with this term. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC reports, and CDISC SDTM datasets (AEBODSYS variable) without requiring a hierarchy join.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E2B(R3); FDA Adverse Event Reporting

description: The name of the MSSO-distributed MedDRA ASCII flat file from which this term record was loaded (e.g., 'llt.asc', 'pt.asc', 'hlt.asc', 'hlgt.asc', 'soc.asc'). Supports data lineage traceability and ETL audit for the biannual MedDRA dictionary update process in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: MSSO MedDRA Distribution File Format Guide; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The official 8-digit numeric MedDRA term code assigned by the Maintenance and Support Services Organization (MSSO) uniquely identifying this term within the MedDRA hierarchy. Used as the primary lookup key in Oracle Argus Safety for adverse event coding and regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1 Guideline; FDA MedDRA Requirements; EMA MedDRA Policy

description: The official MedDRA term label as published by MSSO for this term code. Represents the standardized medical concept name used in adverse event coding, regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA), and safety signal detection across all five hierarchy levels.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; ICH M1 Guideline; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

description: Indicates whether the MedDRA term is currently active and available for coding or has been retired in a subsequent MedDRA version release. Retired terms are retained for historical traceability of previously coded adverse events but must not be used for new coding in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term Retirement Policy; ICH E2B(R3) Guideline

description: Classifies the term as 'standard' (fully endorsed MedDRA term) or 'non-standard' (a term that has been superseded, split, or merged and retained for backward compatibility). Drives coding validation rules in Oracle Argus Safety and Medidata Rave auto-coding configurations.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide v27.0; MSSO Term Classification Policy; ICH E2B(R3)

description: The timestamp when this MedDRA term record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer, typically triggered by a biannual MedDRA version release load. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: The corresponding WHO Adverse Reaction Terminology (WHO-ART) preferred term code or name for this MedDRA term, where a legacy mapping exists. Supports legacy study migration and cross-dictionary reconciliation for studies coded under WHO-ART prior to MedDRA adoption.

business_glossary_term: Oracle Argus Safety Signal Evaluation Number
description: Externally-known business identifier for this signal evaluation as assigned by Oracle Argus Safety. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety Signal Management; ICH E2E; FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: Narrative summary of the benefit-risk assessment conducted during this evaluation cycle, documenting the clinical judgment on the balance between therapeutic benefit and identified safety risk.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER); EMA GVP Module V; FDA Benefit-Risk Framework

description: Formal conclusion of the benefit-risk assessment for this evaluation cycle. Drives downstream regulatory action decisions.

description: Total number of adverse event cases reviewed during this signal evaluation cycle. Provides a quantitative measure of the evidence base examined.

description: Assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the safety signal, using WHO-UMC or investigator causality criteria.

description: Description of the data sources reviewed during this evaluation, such as clinical trial databases, spontaneous report databases (FAERS, EudraVigilance), published literature, and DSMB/DMC reports.

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board / Data Monitoring Committee (DSMB/DMC) Recommendation

reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DSMB; EMA Reflection Paper on DMC

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board / Data Monitoring Committee (DSMB/DMC) Referral Date
description: Date on which the signal evaluation was formally referred to the DSMB or DMC for independent safety review. Populated only when is_dsmb_dmc_referral is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DSMB; EMA Reflection Paper on DMC

description: Date on which the formal signal evaluation was completed and conclusions were finalized. Marks the end of the evaluation lifecycle for this cycle.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; Oracle Argus Safety Signal Management

description: Sequential cycle number for this evaluation of the signal (e.g., 1 for initial evaluation, 2 for re-evaluation after new data). Supports tracking of iterative evaluation rounds for the same signal.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2E

description: The date on which the formal signal evaluation process was initiated. Serves as the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this transaction, marking the start of the regulatory evaluation clock.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; ICH E2E

description: Final outcome determination of the signal evaluation cycle. Indicates whether the signal was confirmed as a new safety finding, refuted by the evidence, or requires ongoing monitoring.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; CIOMS VIII

description: Narrative description of the evaluation plan, including the scope of data to be reviewed, analytical approach, and objectives for this evaluation cycle.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: Current lifecycle state of the signal evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E Pharmacovigilance Planning; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Regulatory deadline by which the expedited safety report (e.g., SUSAR) must be submitted to the relevant authority. Calculated from the awareness date per ICH E2A timelines (7 or 15 calendar days).
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether this signal evaluation triggers an expedited regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., SUSAR 7-day or 15-day report). True = expedited reporting required.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Reference number assigned by a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) for any formal query or request for information related to this signal evaluation. Enables traceability of regulatory correspondence.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GVP Module IX; MHRA; ICH E2E

description: Name of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or drug substance associated with the safety signal under evaluation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GVP Module IX

description: Indicates whether this record represents the current active version of the signal evaluation. True = current version; False = superseded version. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) pattern in the Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; ALCOA+ Principles; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board / Data Monitoring Committee (DSMB/DMC) Referral Flag
description: Indicates whether this signal evaluation has been referred to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent review. True = referred; False = not referred.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Guidance on DSMB; EMA Reflection Paper on DMC

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether the safety signal under evaluation has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), triggering specific expedited reporting obligations under ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Code

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code

description: Indicates whether this signal evaluation has resulted in a recommendation to amend the study protocol (e.g., dose modification, additional safety monitoring, exclusion criteria update).

description: Narrative justification for the recommended regulatory action, documenting the clinical and scientific rationale supporting the recommendation.

description: Formal regulatory action recommended as an outcome of this signal evaluation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_action|label_update|dhpc|study_suspension|protocol_amendment|rmp_update|urgent_safety_measure|additional_pharmacovigilance — promote to reference product]

description: Identifies the primary regulatory authority notified as part of this signal evaluation outcome. description: Origin of the safety signal that triggered this evaluation. description: Classification of the reason driving the site close-out. Distinguishes between normal study completion, early termination for safety or operational reasons, and sponsor-initiated decisions. reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; Veeva Vault eTMF

reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the final payment to the investigational site was processed and confirmed, closing all financial obligations under the site budget agreement.
reference: ICH E6(R2); SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Status of the final financial payment to the investigational site upon close-out, covering all outstanding per-subject fees, procedure fees, and close-out visit fees per the site budget agreement.
reference: ICH E6(R2); SAP S/4HANA Finance; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Overall GCP compliance assessment for the site at close-out, based on findings from the close-out visit and cumulative monitoring history. Informs the site's eligibility for future studies.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ISO 14155

description: Date on which unused investigational medicinal product (IMP) was returned to the sponsor/depot or destroyed on-site per the drug destruction protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.6; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Status of the return or on-site destruction of unused investigational medicinal product (IMP) at the site. Distinct from drug accountability reconciliation — this tracks the physical disposition of remaining IMP stock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.6; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the formal site close-out process was officially initiated in the CTMS, typically following LSLV confirmation and sponsor authorization.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of study completion or early termination at this site.

reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date of the last protocol-required visit for the last subject enrolled at this site. LSLV is a critical clinical milestone that triggers the initiation of the close-out process and database lock activities.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Free-text field for CRA or study manager notes regarding the close-out process, including any outstanding issues, special circumstances, or follow-up actions required after the close-out visit.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Number of unresolved data queries (discrepancies) remaining in the EDC system at the time of close-out visit. A non-zero value indicates data cleaning is incomplete and may delay database lock contribution.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the final site payment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency global trials.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) formally acknowledged the close-out letter and confirmed all site obligations were fulfilled.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) has formally acknowledged receipt of the close-out letter and confirmed completion of all close-out obligations, as required by ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Total number of protocol deviations recorded at this site during the study. Summarized at close-out for site performance assessment and inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM

description: Date on which the applicable regulatory authority was formally notified of the site close-out.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; MHRA

description: Status of notification to applicable regulatory authorities (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA) regarding site close-out, as required under applicable IND/CTA regulations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; MHRA; ICH E6(R2)

description: Percentage of critical and non-critical data fields that have been source data verified (SDV) at the site as of the close-out visit. Reflects data quality and monitoring completeness per the monitoring plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ALCOA+

description: Date on which the site's investigator file and essential documents were successfully transferred to the sponsor or CRO central archive.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Status of the transfer of the site's investigator file and essential documents to the sponsor or CRO archive. Applicable when physical or electronic records must be transferred from the site to a central repository.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Number of subjects who completed all protocol-required visits and procedures at this site. Used to calculate site completion rate and assess protocol adherence.

description: Number of subjects who discontinued from the study prior to completing all protocol-required activities at this site. Includes withdrawals, screen failures post-enrollment, and protocol deviations leading to discontinuation.

description: Total number of subjects who were enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) at this site during the study. Used for final enrollment reconciliation at close-out.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS
