business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Document ID
description: The document identifier assigned by the eTMF system (e.g., Veeva Vault) to the regulatory submission document. Enables direct cross-reference between the CTMS submission record and the corresponding document stored in the eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 11; TMF Reference Model

description: The calendar date on which the regulatory authority or ethics committee granted approval or authorization for the site to conduct the clinical trial. Null if submission has not yet been approved.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.53; EU CTR 536/2014

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the country in which the site is located and to whose regulatory jurisdiction this submission pertains (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, AUS, CAN).
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The timestamp when this regulatory submission record was first created in the CTMS or data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: The formal title of the regulatory submission document as filed with the regulatory authority or ethics committee (e.g., 'FDA Form 1572 - Statement of Investigator', 'IRB Initial Review Application'). Supports document management and eTMF indexing.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date on which the regulatory approval or authorization for this site expires and must be renewed. Particularly relevant for IRB/IEC approvals which typically require annual renewal. Null for approvals with no defined expiry.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3.3; 21 CFR Part 56.109; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates the type of FDA financial disclosure form submitted for the investigator at this site: FDA Form 3454 (certification of no disclosable financial interest) or FDA Form 3455 (disclosure of financial interest). Required under 21 CFR Part 54 for studies supporting marketing applications.
reference: 21 CFR Part 54; FDA Form 3454; FDA Form 3455; FDA Guidance on Financial Disclosure

description: Indicates whether the investigator has disclosed a financial interest or arrangement that could affect the reliability of the clinical data, as required by 21 CFR Part 54. True if a disclosable financial interest exists (FDA Form 3455 filed); False if no disclosable interest (FDA Form 3454 filed).
reference: 21 CFR Part 54; FDA Form 3454; FDA Form 3455

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Compliance Confirmed Flag
description: Indicates whether the investigator and site have confirmed compliance with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines as part of this submission (e.g., as declared on FDA Form 1572). Required for regulatory submissions under ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.53; FDA Form 1572

description: The version of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) that was submitted to and approved by the IRB/IEC as part of this regulatory submission. Tracks which ICF version is authorized for use at the site.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP

business_glossary_term: Local Language Informed Consent Form (ICF) Flag
description: Indicates whether the ICF submitted for IRB/IEC approval is in the local language of the site country, as required for patient comprehension and regulatory compliance in non-English-speaking countries.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.6; EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR; HIPAA

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, internal comments, or operational notes related to this regulatory submission that are not captured in structured fields. Used by CRAs and regulatory affairs staff for tracking follow-up actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; ALCOA+

description: The regulatory body or ethics committee to which this site-level submission was directed (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada, IRB, IEC, or local health authority). Determines applicable regulatory framework and review timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|IRB|IEC|Local_HA|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); MHRA; PMDA; TGA; Health Canada

description: The number of calendar days from submission date to approval date, representing the actual regulatory review cycle time for this submission. Used for site activation performance analytics and benchmarking against regulatory authority standard timelines.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; KPI reporting

description: The actual date on which the site or sponsor submitted a formal response to regulatory authority or ethics committee queries or conditions. Used to track responsiveness and compliance with regulatory timelines.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ALCOA+

description: The deadline by which the site or sponsor must respond to regulatory authority or ethics committee queries, conditions, or requests for additional information arising from the review of this submission.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Narrative comments or conditions provided by the regulatory authority or ethics committee as part of their review decision. Captures required modifications, conditions of approval, or reasons for deferral or rejection.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014; ALCOA+

description: The formal outcome of the regulatory authority's or ethics committee's review of this submission. Distinct from submission_status in that it captures the specific decision rendered (e.g., approved with conditions, deferred pending additional information).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 56.109; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the regulatory submission outcome. Supports sponsor oversight tracking and compliance with ICH E6(R2) notification requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of the regulatory submission outcome (approval, rejection, or conditions). Required under ICH E6(R2) for sponsor oversight of site regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 312

description: High-level category of the submission indicating whether it is an initial submission, a periodic renewal, a protocol amendment, a site closure notification, or a safety report submission to the ethics committee or regulatory authority.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The calendar date on which the site-level regulatory submission was formally submitted to the regulatory authority or ethics committee. Serves as the principal business event date for this transaction.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ALCOA+

description: The method by which the regulatory submission was transmitted to the regulatory authority or ethics committee (e.g., electronic portal, paper, email). Relevant for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and audit trail.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The tracking or confirmation number issued by the regulatory authority's electronic submission portal (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA CTIS, MHRA IRAS) upon receipt of the submission. Provides external confirmation of receipt.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014 (CTIS); FDA ESG; MHRA IRAS

description: Current lifecycle state of the site-level regulatory submission. Tracks progression from draft preparation through regulatory authority review to final disposition (approved, rejected, withdrawn, or superseded by an amendment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Under_Review|Approved|Rejected|Withdrawn|Superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the site-level regulatory submission. Includes FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator), FDA Form 3454/3455 (Financial Disclosure), IRB/IEC applications, local health authority notifications, and country-specific site authorization documents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA_Form_1572|FDA_Form_3454|FDA_Form_3455|IRB_IEC_Application|Local_Health_Authority_Notification|Site_Authorization|Amendment|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53; 21 CFR Part 54; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Version identifier for this submission document, used to track amendments and resubmissions (e.g., v1.0, v2.0, Amendment 1). Supports version control of regulatory documents in the eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault eTMF; ALCOA+

description: The timestamp when this regulatory submission record was last modified in the CTMS or data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Reference to the original site agreement record from which this amendment was derived. Null for original agreements (amendment_number = 0). Enables amendment lineage tracking and supersession chain reconstruction.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; Veeva Vault; 21 CFR Part 11

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

The agreement's financial terms and subject enrollment limits are bounded by the CTA. This link is requir

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Document ID
description: Reference identifier of the executed agreement document as stored in the Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) in Veeva Vault. Enables direct navigation from the CTMS record to the source document for inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; TMF Reference Model; Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the site agreement (e.g., CTA-2024-0042). Used for cross-system tracking, regulatory correspondence, and audit trails. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS or Veeva Vault contract management module.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Current lifecycle state of the site agreement. Tracks progression from initial drafting through negotiation, execution, and eventual expiry or termination. Drives workflow routing in Veeva Vault and Oracle Siebel CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Under Negotiation|Pending Execution|Executed|Expired|Terminated|Superseded — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault Contract Lifecycle Management

value_regex: CTA|CDA|Budget Amendment|Site Budget Agreement|Letter of Intent
description: Classification of the site-level agreement. CTA (Clinical Trial Agreement) is the primary investigational site contract; CDA (Confidential Disclosure Agreement) governs pre-trial information sharing; Budget Amendment captures renegotiated financial terms; Site Budget Agreement formalizes per-patient and overhead fee structures; Letter of Intent signals site commitment prior to full execution.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312.53; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 46

description: Sequential amendment version number for this agreement record. A value of 0 indicates the original agreement; values ≥1 indicate successive amendments. Enables version tracking of negotiated changes over the study lifecycle.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; Veeva Vault Document Management; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Fixed fee payable to the investigational site upon study close-out to cover archival, final monitoring visit support, and regulatory document finalization activities. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Duration in years for which confidentiality obligations under the agreement remain in force after the agreement's expiration or termination. Particularly relevant for CDA (Confidential Disclosure Agreement) components.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; GDPR Article 5; HIPAA; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Timestamp indicating when this site agreement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Name of the Contract Research Organization (CRO) acting as the contracting party on behalf of the sponsor, if applicable. Captures the legal entity name when the CRO executes the agreement under delegated authority.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.2; FDA 21 CFR 312.52

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all financial terms within this site agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-country trials.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Finance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Indicates whether a data privacy addendum (DPA) or data processing agreement is included as part of or annexed to this site agreement, addressing GDPR, HIPAA, or other applicable data protection obligations for patient data.
reference: GDPR Article 28; HIPAA 45 CFR 164; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the site agreement becomes legally binding and operative. Marks the start of the contractual relationship between the sponsor/CRO and the investigational site. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 46

description: Date on which all required parties signed and fully executed the site agreement. May differ from the effective date when agreements are backdated or have a future effective date. Critical for regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the site agreement is scheduled to expire or cease to be operative. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow alerts in Veeva Vault. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; Veeva Vault Contract Lifecycle Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this site agreement. Critical for multi-national trial legal compliance and dispute resolution.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR; HIPAA; ICH E6(R2); ISO 3166-1

description: Indicates whether the site agreement includes an indemnification clause protecting the investigational site and PI from third-party claims arising from the sponsor's negligence. Standard in CTA templates but may be absent in certain jurisdictions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the agreement mandates that the sponsor or CRO maintain clinical trial insurance coverage for the duration of the study at this site. Regulatory requirement in many jurisdictions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.8; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 76; ISO 14155

description: Reference number of the clinical trial insurance policy covering this site agreement. Populated when insurance_coverage_required is True. Used for regulatory inspection documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.8; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 76

description: Indicates whether the site agreement contains an intellectual property ownership clause specifying that all study data, inventions, and results generated at the site are owned by the sponsor. Standard in CTA templates.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Approval Required
description: Indicates whether IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval is a condition precedent to the agreement becoming operative. Relevant for agreements at sites in jurisdictions requiring ethics committee review prior to contract execution.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3; FDA 21 CFR 56; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 4; OHRP

description: Indicates whether the sponsor's or CRO's legal team has completed their review and approved the final agreement text prior to execution. Part of the quality control workflow tracked in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; Veeva Vault Quality Management; SOX

description: Date on which the legal review of the site agreement was completed and sign-off was granted. Populated when legal_review_completed is True. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault

description: Date on which formal contract negotiation commenced between the sponsor/CRO and the investigational site. Used to calculate cycle time KPIs for site activation performance reporting in CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Free-text field for capturing supplementary notes, special conditions, deviations from standard templates, or negotiation context relevant to this site agreement. Supports audit trail and institutional knowledge retention.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; ALCOA+; Veeva Vault

description: Institutional overhead or indirect cost rate (expressed as a percentage) applied on top of direct site costs as negotiated in the site budget agreement. Typically charged by academic medical centers or hospital institutions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; SAP S/4HANA Finance; OMB Circular A-21 (US academic institutions)

description: Defines the payment trigger mechanism agreed upon in the site budget agreement. Determines how and when invoices are generated and processed in SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6

description: Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment must be remitted to the investigational site, as stipulated in the site agreement (e.g., Net 30, Net 45, Net 60). Drives accounts payable processing in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6

description: Agreed fee payable to the investigational site for each evaluable patient who completes the study per protocol. Core financial term in the site budget agreement. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; SAP S/4HANA Project Accounting; FDA 21 CFR 312.53

description: Indicates whether the site agreement includes a publication rights clause defining the investigator's right to publish study results and the sponsor's right to review manuscripts prior to submission. Relevant for academic site agreements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; ICMJE Publication Guidelines; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 37

description: Date on which the authorized representative of the investigational site (e.g., PI or institutional official) signed the agreement. May differ from the sponsor/CRO signature date in multi-party execution workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the authorized representative of the sponsor or CRO signed the agreement. The later of site_signature_date and sponsor_signature_date typically defines the execution_date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: One-time fixed fee payable to the investigational site upon activation to cover site initiation activities such as staff training, IRB/IEC submission preparation, and regulatory document collection. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Date on which the site agreement was early-terminated prior to its scheduled expiration. Populated only when agreement_status is 'Terminated'. Supports regulatory close-out documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312.64; EU CTR 536/2014

value_regex: Mutual Consent|Sponsor Decision|Site Decision|Regulatory Action|Protocol Termination|For Cause
description: Categorized reason for early termination of the site agreement. Required for regulatory reporting and site performance analytics. Populated only when agreement_status is 'Terminated'.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; FDA 21 CFR 312.64

description: Total contracted budget value for this site agreement, inclusive of all per-patient fees, procedures, overhead, and any fixed fees. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Used for financial forecasting and accrual management in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Accounting; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; SOX

description: Timestamp indicating when this site agreement record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each investigational site record in the clinical research enterprise. Primary key for the master site record referenced by all study-site associations, feasibility assessments, and capacity records.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Sites with local labs must reference the qualified lab_facility for specimen processing, local safety lab results, and site activation checklists. investigational_site.has_local_laboratory is a boolea
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to site.principal_investigator

Date on which the investigational site was formally activated and authorized to begin enrolling subjects into clinical trials. Marks the completion of all site initiation activities including IRB/IEC approval, regulatory submissions, and staff training.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4; Oracle Siebel CTMS; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Primary street address line of the investigational site facility. Required for regulatory submissions, site visit logistics, and TMF documentation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53; ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR 164

description: Secondary address line for the investigational site (suite, floor, building, department). Supplements the primary address for precise facility location identification.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53; ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; GDPR Article 4

description: City in which the investigational site is located. Used for geographic distribution analysis, patient recruitment planning, and regulatory submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the investigational site was formally closed out, completing all study-related activities, resolving outstanding data queries, and returning or destroying unused investigational product. Triggers archival obligations per GCP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 37; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the investigational site is located. Determines applicable regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada), GCP guidelines, and data privacy regulations (GDPR, HIPAA).
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR; HIPAA

description: Timestamp when the investigational site master record was first created in the system of record (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) nine-digit identifier for the investigational site organization. Used for sponsor contracting, grant management, and financial due diligence.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS Standard; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master; ICH E6(R2)

description: Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system deployed at the investigational site for eCRF data entry and clinical data management. Determines site training requirements, data integration pathways, and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance approach.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; CDISC; Medidata Rave; Oracle Clinical One

description: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) site-level identifier assigned for EU-regulated clinical trials. Required for EMA regulatory submissions and EU CTR 536/2014 compliance reporting.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; EMA EudraCT Database; EMA Guidance on Clinical Trials

description: Classification of the investigational site by facility category, indicating the type of healthcare or research institution. Drives feasibility scoring, site selection criteria, and therapeutic area alignment assessments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA Guidance on Site Selection

description: Fax number for the investigational site, used for transmission of regulatory documents, IRB/IEC correspondence, and sponsor communications where electronic submission is not available.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.53

description: U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Facility Establishment Identifier (FEI) number assigned to the investigational site. Required for FDA-regulated clinical trials and referenced in IND applications and NDA/BLA submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 207; FDA FEI Registry; 21 CFR Part 312

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Certification Expiry Date
description: Date on which the investigational site's current GCP certification expires. Triggers renewal workflow and site eligibility review. Sites with expired GCP certification are ineligible for new study activations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; MasterControl Training Management

description: Current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) certification status of the investigational site, reflecting compliance with ICH E6(R2) training and audit requirements. Sites must maintain active GCP certification to participate in sponsor-funded clinical trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC; MasterControl

description: Current GCP compliance status of the investigational site based on the most recent monitoring visit, audit, or inspection findings. Drives risk-based monitoring intensity, CAPA requirements, and site eligibility for new study assignments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC; MasterControl CAPA

description: Indicates whether the investigational site employs dedicated clinical research staff (e.g., Clinical Research Coordinators (CRCs), research nurses) exclusively for clinical trial conduct, as opposed to relying solely on general clinical staff.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.2; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA Guidance on Site Selection

description: Indicates whether the investigational site has an on-site clinical laboratory certified to process and analyze clinical trial specimens. Relevant for protocol feasibility, sample logistics planning, and central vs. local lab decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; CLIA (42 CFR Part 493); CAP Accreditation; ISO 15189

description: Indicates whether the investigational site has an on-site pharmacy capable of receiving, storing, dispensing, and accounting for investigational medicinal products (IMPs) per GCP and regulatory requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.6; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU GMP Annex 13; Oracle Clinical One RTSM

description: UK Integrated Research Application System (IRAS) project identifier assigned to the site for studies conducted under MHRA oversight. Required for UK clinical trial regulatory submissions.
reference: MHRA Clinical Trials Regulation; UK SI 2004/1031; IRAS System

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Name
description: Name of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) responsible for ethical oversight of clinical research conducted at this investigational site. Required for regulatory submissions and TMF documentation.

description: Indicates whether the sponsor must provide explicit written approval before this site selection decision is finalized. Relevant for CRO-managed studies where sponsor sign-off is a contractual requirement.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CRO sponsor agreements

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the site within the context of this specific study, used in EDC, RTSM, and regulatory submissions. Distinct from the internal site_id.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave; Oracle Clinical One

description: The planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site as agreed at the time of selection. Used for enrollment planning, site performance benchmarking, and resource allocation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Indicates whether a formal site selection visit (also known as a pre-study site visit or PSSV) was conducted at this site prior to the selection decision, as recommended by ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.6; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the site selection visit (pre-study site visit / PSSV) was conducted. Null if no visit was conducted or if the visit is pending.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.6; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the study-site association record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: study_site tracks regulatory_approval_date as a denormalized field but lacks a FK to the formal approval record. Linking to approval enables regulatory status rollup across sites, supports inspection
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Each study_site is assigned a central laboratory for specimen analysis. This drives specimen shipment routing, lab kit ordering, result reporting, and eTMF documentation. Role-prefixed 'central_' beca
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Each study_site in EU member states is governed by a specific CTA for that country. Regulatory affairs and site management teams track the CTA covering each study_site for compliance monitoring, enrol
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the investigational site master record. Links to the site entity representing the physical or organizational research location.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) assigned to lead clinical research activities at this site for this study. The PI is responsible for the conduct of the trial per ICH E6(R2) GCP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.53; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Study sites are activated under a specific protocol version; regulatory submissions, IRB approvals, and site agreements all reference the governing protocol. protocol_number on study_site is a denorma
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to study.study.study_id — Critical for study-site relationship — study_site is the association entity linking studies to sites, must have direct FK to study for enrollment tracking and site activation workflows.

Total agreed budget amount for this site's participation in the study, as specified in the Clinical Trial Agreement. Covers per-patient fees, overhead, and procedure costs. Managed in SAP S/4HANA project accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Accounting; ICH E6(R2) GCP; SOX Financial Controls

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the agreed site budget amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA; SOX Financial Controls

description: Maximum number of subjects the site has declared capacity to enroll and manage concurrently for this study, based on staff resources, facilities, and patient population. Used in feasibility assessment and enrollment planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.19; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate Feasibility Standards

description: Date on which the Confidential Disclosure Agreement (CDA) between the sponsor/CRO and the site was fully executed. A prerequisite milestone in the site activation workflow before protocol sharing.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS Site Activation Workflow; TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics

description: Date on which the Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) or Clinical Site Agreement between the sponsor/CRO and the site institution was fully executed. A mandatory activation milestone before site initiation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management

description: Timestamp when this study-site association record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which authorization was granted to ship investigational medicinal product (IMP) to this site. A critical activation milestone managed via RTSM/IRT (e.g., Medidata RTSM). Shipment cannot proceed without IRB approval and regulatory clearance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Date on which the site was provisioned and granted access to the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) for this study. Required before site staff can enter subject data into eCRFs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC

description: Actual number of subjects who have been enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) at this site to date. Updated from EDC/RTSM data feeds. Used for real-time enrollment performance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave EDC; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM

description: Date on which enrollment of new subjects at this site was formally closed, either due to target achievement, sponsor decision, or protocol amendment. Marks transition to 'enrollment_complete' status.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM

description: Date on which the site was authorized to begin enrolling subjects into the study. Marks the transition to 'enrolling' status. Used for site performance analytics and FPFV (First Patient First Visit) tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate KPIs

description: Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site for this study, as agreed between the sponsor/CRO and the site during feasibility and site selection. Used for site performance tracking and enrollment forecasting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate Site Performance KPIs

description: Date of the first subject's first protocol-specified visit at this site. A critical KPI milestone in clinical trial management used to measure site activation speed and study startup performance.
reference: TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM

description: Current GCP compliance status of the site for this study based on monitoring visit findings, audit results, and CAPA resolution. Drives risk-based monitoring intensity and escalation decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliant|minor_findings|major_findings|critical_findings|suspended|not_assessed — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC; MasterControl CAPA; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the IRB or IEC granted initial approval for the study protocol at this site. Milestone tracked in CTMS and eTMF for regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014; Veeva Vault eTMF

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Approval Number
description: Official approval reference number issued by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) authorizing the conduct of this study at this site. Required before any subject enrollment can begin.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP regulations; 45 CFR Part 46

description: Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval expires. Sites must obtain renewal before this date to continue enrollment. Triggers automated alerts in CTMS for renewal tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date of the most recent monitoring visit (on-site or remote) conducted at this site for this study. Used to calculate time since last visit and trigger overdue monitoring alerts in CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance

description: Date of the last subject's last protocol-specified visit at this site. Marks the end of active subject participation and triggers site close-out activities. Key milestone for study timeline management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM; TransCelerate KPIs

description: Total number of monitoring visits (on-site and remote) conducted at this site for this study to date. Used for site oversight reporting and resource planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance

description: Planned date for the next monitoring visit at this site for this study. Scheduled in CTMS based on risk tier, enrollment activity, and monitoring plan requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Number of currently open data queries (discrepancies or clarification requests) raised against eCRF data submitted by this site in the EDC system. A key data quality KPI for site performance management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC; Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+ principles; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Cumulative number of protocol deviations recorded at this site for this study. Includes both minor and major deviations. Used for site quality oversight, regulatory reporting, and CAPA management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP; MasterControl CAPA; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the required regulatory submission (e.g., IND amendment, national competent authority notification) was made for this site to participate in the study. Applicable for sites in jurisdictions requiring site-level regulatory approval.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Documented reason why a candidate site was not selected or was subsequently withdrawn from the study. Populated only when site_status transitions to a rejected or withdrawn state. Supports feasibility learning and sponsor reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Risk-based monitoring tier assigned to this site for this study, reflecting overall site risk profile based on enrollment performance, GCP compliance history, data quality, and PI experience. Determines monitoring visit frequency and SDV scope.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Addendum R2; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance (2013); EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Total number of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) reported from subjects enrolled at this site for this study. Used for safety signal monitoring and site-level safety oversight. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; Oracle Argus Safety

description: Number of subjects who were screened but did not meet eligibility criteria and were not enrolled. Used to calculate screen failure rate, a key site performance metric for feasibility planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate KPIs

description: Total number of subjects who have undergone screening procedures at this site, regardless of eligibility outcome. Used to calculate screen failure rate and assess site recruitment efficiency.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the site was formally selected by the sponsor or CRO for participation in the study, following feasibility assessment and committee review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.19; TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Narrative justification for selecting this site for the study, including patient population access, PI expertise, infrastructure capability, and prior performance. Captured during the site selection committee review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.19; FDA Guidance on Site Selection

description: Date on which the formal site close-out visit was completed and the site was officially closed for this study. Triggers archival of site documents in eTMF and final reconciliation activities.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric site number unique within the study protocol. Used in EDC, RTSM, and regulatory submissions to identify the site within the trial context. Typically formatted as a 3–10 character code (e.g., 'US001', 'DE002').
reference: CDISC SDTM SITEID; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the site within this study. Drives operational workflows in CTMS: candidate (under feasibility review), selected (approved for activation), initiated (Site Initiation Visit completed), enrolling (actively recruiting subjects), enrollment_complete (target met or enrollment closed), closed (site formally closed out).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics

description: Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the CRA. The SIV confirms site readiness, trains site staff on the protocol and EDC system, and is a key activation milestone before first subject enrollment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Oracle Siebel CTMS; TransCelerate Site Activation Metrics

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this study-site association record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Number of enrolled subjects who withdrew consent or were discontinued from the study at this site. Tracked for safety reporting, ITT/PP population analysis, and site performance assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the study-country record. Primary key for the study_country data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Linking study_country to the CTA record enables site activation workflows, enrollment gati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Each country's CTA submission (tracked via cta_submission_date, cta_approval_date on study_country) is filed in a country-specific regulatory binder. Country regulatory leads need direct traceability
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the governing regulatory authority responsible for clinical trial oversight in this country (e.g., FDA for US, EMA/national competent authority for EU member states, PMDA for Japan). Links to the health_authority reference product.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); PMDA

description: Reference to the parent clinical study for which this country-level record is maintained. Links the country configuration to the study protocol.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Country-level regulatory submissions generate country-specific TMF documents (translated ICF, country protocol synopsis, local ethics approval). eTMF country-level document management requires direct
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual number of investigative sites that have been activated and authorized to enroll subjects in this country. Compared against planned_site_count for country-level site activation KPI reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Overall operational activation status of the country within the study lifecycle, distinct from enrollment_status. Reflects the country's readiness and operational state as tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Actual number of subjects enrolled (consented and randomized or screened) in this country to date. Compared against planned_enrollment_target for enrollment performance reporting in Medidata Rave and Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM DM domain; Medidata Rave

description: Actual number of subjects screened at sites in this country to date. Used to calculate the realized screen failure rate and to assess enrollment efficiency.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM DM domain; Medidata Rave

description: Total approved budget allocated for all study activities in this country, expressed in the country's local currency (see currency_code). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA project accounting and used for country-level financial performance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; ICH E6(R2); SOX

description: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code identifying the country where the clinical study is conducted (e.g., US, DE, JP). Used as the primary geographic identifier for regulatory and enrollment reporting.

description: Full English name of the country as defined by ISO 3166-1 (e.g., United States, Germany, Japan). Used for display in regulatory submissions, reports, and dashboards.
reference: ISO 3166-1; FDA; EMA; PMDA

description: Timestamp when this study-country record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which the regulatory authority granted approval for the clinical trial to proceed in this country. Milestone used for site activation planning and regulatory timeline KPI reporting.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2); MHRA; Health Canada

description: Current approval status of the CTA or IND application with the national regulatory authority. Drives regulatory hold flags and country activation readiness assessments.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2); EMA

description: Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization (CTA) or Investigational New Drug (IND) application was submitted to the national regulatory authority for this country. Tracked in Veeva Vault for regulatory timeline management.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the country (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Used for country-level budget planning, site payment processing, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA; ICH E6(R2)

description: Primary data privacy regulatory framework applicable to subject data collected in this country (e.g., GDPR for EU, HIPAA for US, PIPEDA for Canada, APPI for Japan). Drives data handling, consent, and transfer requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|HIPAA|PIPEDA|PDPA|APPI|LGPD|other|not_applicable — promote to reference product]
reference: GDPR; HIPAA; PIPEDA; APPI; LGPD; ICH E6(R2); ISO 14155

description: Assumed proportion of enrolled subjects expected to withdraw or be lost to follow-up in this country, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 = 10%). Used in sample size and enrollment buffer calculations.
reference: ICH E8(R1); ICH E9; SAS Drug Development; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which enrollment was formally closed in this country, either due to target achievement, regulatory hold, or study termination. Distinct from last_subject_enrolled_date as it reflects the administrative closure decision.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Enrollment Rate Assumption (Subjects per Site per Month)
description: Assumed average number of subjects enrolled per active site per month in this country, used in enrollment forecasting and timeline modeling. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.5000 subjects/site/month).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; SAS Drug Development

description: Current enrollment lifecycle status for this country within the study. Drives enrollment forecasting, site activation planning, and country-level feasibility reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM

business_glossary_term: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) Number
description: EudraCT number assigned by the European Medicines Agency (EMA) for clinical trials conducted in EU/EEA member states. Required for EU CTR compliance and EudraCT/CTIS registration. Applicable only for EU countries.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; EMA EudraCT; Directive 2001/20/EC

description: Date on which the country-level feasibility assessment was completed and the feasibility_score was assigned. Used to track the currency of feasibility data for enrollment planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Composite numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the overall feasibility assessment rating for conducting the study in this country, based on site availability, patient population, regulatory environment, and infrastructure. Derived during the Site Selection and Feasibility Assessment process.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Date on which the first investigative site in this country was activated and authorized to enroll subjects. Key milestone for country-level startup timeline KPI reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CDISC SDTM

description: Date on which the first subject was enrolled (consented and randomized or screened) at any site within this country. Marks the start of active enrollment for country-level timeline tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM DM domain; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) approved by the national IRB/IEC and regulatory authority for use in this country. Ensures sites use the correct country-specific ICF version as tracked in Clinpal and Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; EU CTR 536/2014; Clinpal

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Approval Date
description: Date on which the national or central IRB/IEC granted ethical approval for the study to proceed in this country. Required before any subject enrollment can commence.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP; GDPR Article 9

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Submission Date
description: Date on which the study protocol and informed consent materials were submitted to the national or central Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for ethical review in this country.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the last subject was enrolled in this country. Used to calculate country-level enrollment duration and to trigger enrollment completion milestones.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Primary local language code for the country using IETF BCP 47 format (e.g., de-DE, ja-JP, fr-FR). Drives Informed Consent Form (ICF) translation requirements and eCRF localization in Clinpal and Medidata Rave.
reference: IETF BCP 47; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinpal; Medidata Rave

description: Country-specific regulatory reference or dossier number assigned by the national competent authority upon CTA/IND submission or approval (e.g., EudraCT number for EU, IND number for US, CTN for Australia). Stored in Veeva Vault.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; TGA CTN; Health Canada; Veeva Vault

description: Country-specific clinical trial registry number assigned by the national registry (e.g., ISRCTN, UMIN for Japan, CTRI for India). Distinct from EudraCT and ClinicalTrials.gov identifiers. Required for WHO ICTRP compliance.
reference: WHO ICTRP; ICMJE; ISO 14155; Health Canada

description: Target number of subjects planned to be enrolled in this country as defined in the study protocol and country feasibility assessment. Drives enrollment forecasting and country-level resource allocation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Planned number of subjects to be screened in this country, derived from the planned enrollment target and the assumed screen failure rate. Used for site capacity planning and patient recruitment forecasting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Number of investigative sites planned to be activated in this country as defined in the study feasibility assessment and country-level enrollment plan. Used for resource planning and site activation tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: Indicates whether a regulatory hold (e.g., FDA Clinical Hold under 21 CFR 312.42, or equivalent national authority hold) is currently in effect for this country, preventing or suspending subject enrollment.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.42; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 35; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the regulatory hold was lifted by the national authority, allowing enrollment to resume. Null if the hold is still in effect.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.42; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Narrative description of the reason for the regulatory hold in this country, as communicated by the national regulatory authority. Populated only when regulatory_hold_flag is True.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.42; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the regulatory hold was imposed by the national authority in this country. Used for regulatory timeline impact analysis and sponsor reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.42; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Assumed proportion of screened subjects expected to fail screening criteria in this country, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.3000 = 30%). Used in enrollment forecasting models to derive planned_screen_count from planned_enrollment_target.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; SAS Drug Development

business_glossary_term: Subject Data Cross-Border Transfer Allowed Flag
description: Indicates whether subject-level clinical data collected in this country may be transferred cross-border to the sponsor or CRO data systems under applicable data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR Chapter V adequacy decisions, SCCs). Critical for EDC data flow configuration in Medidata Rave.
reference: GDPR Chapter V; HIPAA; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Timestamp when this study-country record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and change audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the dosing regimen record within the Clinical Trials data platform. Primary key for the dosing_regimen product in the study domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Regulatory approvals specify the approved dosing regimen on the product label. Post-approval change management and label reconciliation require tracing which dosing_regimen record was approved, enabli
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the study arm (treatment group) to which this dosing regimen applies. A study may have multiple arms each with distinct regimens (e.g., active drug arm vs. placebo arm).
reference: CDISC SDTM ARM; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: A dosing regimen is active during a specific study epoch (e.g., a loading dose regimen during the Run-in epoch, a maintenance dose regimen during the Treatment epoch). dosing_regimen already has effec
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the investigational product (IP) being administered under this dosing regimen. Supports traceability from regimen to supply chain and accountability logs.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX.EXTRT; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the parent clinical study for which this dosing regimen is defined. Links the regimen to the study protocol and all associated study-level metadata.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: dosing_regimen has amendment_number as a STRING but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Dosing regimens are introduced or modified by protocol amendments — this FK formalizes that traceability. No bidi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: CDISC ADaM Exposure (ADEX) Domain Mapping Notes
description: Notes describing how this dosing regimen maps to the CDISC ADaM ADEX (Analysis Dataset for Exposure) domain, including derivation rules for cumulative dose, dose intensity, and relative dose intensity calculations used in biostatistical analysis.
reference: CDISC ADaM ADEX Implementation Guide; CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA Statistical Review Guidance; SAP

description: Blinding level applied to this dosing regimen as defined in the study protocol (e.g., 'double-blind' where neither subject nor investigator knows the treatment assignment). Impacts RTSM/IRT dispensing logic and unblinding procedures.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM TI Domain; FDA Guidance on Blinding

description: Indicates whether this regimen represents an active investigational product, placebo, vehicle control, sham procedure, or no-treatment arm. Critical for ITT and PP population definitions and regulatory benefit-risk assessment.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E10 Choice of Control Group; CDISC SDTM TA Domain; FDA Guidance on Placebo Controls

description: Timestamp when this dosing regimen record was first created in the data platform, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Sequential step number within the dose escalation scheme (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables ordering of dose cohorts in escalation studies and supports dose-response curve construction in PK/PD modeling.

reference: CDISC SDTM TV (Trial Visits) Domain; ICH E6(R2)

description: CDISC ADaM dataset name to which this procedure's data contributes for statistical analysis (e.g., ADLB, ADVS, ADEG, ADPC). Used by SAS Drug Development for biostatistical analysis and CSR generation.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; SAS Drug Development

business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization (CDASH) Domain
description: CDISC CDASH domain code that defines the data collection standard for this procedure's eCRF (e.g., VS, LB, EG, PE). Ensures the eCRF design in Medidata Rave aligns with CDASH standards for downstream SDTM mapping.
reference: CDISC CDASH Implementation Guide v2.0; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Indicates whether specimens collected by this procedure must be processed by the sponsor-designated central laboratory (as opposed to a local site laboratory). Drives sample kit supply chain and central lab requisition workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; Central Laboratory Agreement; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

description: Timestamp when this visit procedure record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grading Applicable Indicator
description: Indicates whether CTCAE grading criteria apply to results collected by this procedure (e.g., laboratory abnormalities graded per CTCAE v5.0). Drives safety data review workflows and adverse event reporting linkage.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E2A; FDA Safety Reporting Guidance; CDISC SDTM

description: Date on which this procedure definition was retired or superseded by a protocol amendment. Null for currently active procedures. Used to determine which procedure version applies to a given subject visit date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date from which this procedure definition became effective in the study protocol. For amended procedures, reflects the date the amendment was approved and implemented at sites.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Type of clinical equipment or device required to perform this procedure at the site (e.g., 'Holter Monitor', 'Spirometer', 'Dual-Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DXA) Scanner', 'Electrocardiograph'). Used for site feasibility assessment and equipment qualification tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; ISO 14155; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Estimated time in minutes required to complete this procedure at the site. Used for visit duration planning, site burden assessment, and subject scheduling optimization.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS Visit Planning; Protocol Feasibility Assessment

description: Indicates whether this procedure is required (mandatory) or optional at the associated visit per the study protocol. Mandatory procedures must be completed for the visit to be considered evaluable; optional procedures may be performed at investigator discretion.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; CDISC SDTM TV Domain

description: Indicates whether this procedure involves pharmacokinetic (PK) sample collection or assessment. PK procedures require precise timing relative to dosing and specialized sample handling per the bioanalytical plan.
reference: FDA Guidance on Bioanalytical Method Validation; ICH M10; CDISC SDTM PC/PP Domains

description: Indicates whether this procedure collects data that directly contributes to the study's primary efficacy or safety endpoint. Used to prioritize monitoring, SDV, and data cleaning activities.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Indicates whether this procedure is designated as a safety assessment per the protocol (e.g., safety labs, ECG for QTc monitoring, vital signs for blood pressure monitoring). Drives safety data review workflows and DSMB/DMC reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; ICH E2A; FDA Safety Reporting Guidance

description: Indicates whether local site laboratory processing is permitted for this procedure (e.g., for safety monitoring labs that require rapid turnaround). May coexist with central_lab_required for procedures requiring both central and local processing.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Protocol Laboratory Manual; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

description: Free-text field for protocol-specific instructions, special handling requirements, or clarifications related to this procedure that are not captured in structured fields (e.g., 'Collect sample within 2 hours of dosing', 'Perform in triplicate').
reference: ICH E6(R2); Protocol Schedule of Events Footnotes

description: Identifier of the corresponding task or activity in Oracle Clinical One study design module. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse visit procedure record and the Oracle Clinical One study build configuration.
reference: Oracle Clinical One Study Design Module; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Categorical classification of the clinical procedure type used to group procedures in the Schedule of Events matrix and CDISC SDTM domain mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vital_signs|lab_draw|ecg|imaging|questionnaire|dosing|pk_sampling|physical_exam|biopsy|ecog_assessment|other — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2)

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the procedure within the study protocol (e.g., VITALS-01, LBDRAW-02, ECG-01). Used as the business identifier for the procedure in the Schedule of Events matrix and eCRF mapping.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; Medidata Rave EDC Configuration

description: Detailed narrative description of the clinical procedure, including instructions, conditions, and any protocol-specific requirements (e.g., fasting requirements, timing relative to dosing, specimen handling instructions).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: Human-readable name of the clinical procedure or assessment (e.g., 'Vital Signs', '12-Lead ECG', 'Fasting Blood Draw', 'ECOG Performance Status Assessment'). Used in Schedule of Events displays and site training materials.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; ICH E6(R2)

description: Current lifecycle status of the visit procedure definition within the study protocol. Draft procedures are under development; active procedures are in use; amended procedures have been modified by protocol amendment; retired procedures are no longer required.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Specifies the qualification or role required to perform this procedure (e.g., 'Phlebotomist', 'Radiologist', 'Cardiologist', 'Trained Study Nurse'). Used for site staff delegation log and training requirement planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Delegation of Authority Log

description: Version identifier of the Medidata Rave eCRF form associated with this procedure (e.g., '1.0', '2.1'). Tracks form versioning to ensure data collected aligns with the correct protocol version and supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC Study Build Standards; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5

description: Indicates whether the subject must be in a fasting state prior to this procedure (e.g., fasting lipid panel, fasting glucose). Drives subject instruction materials and visit scheduling constraints.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Protocol-specific laboratory manual; CDISC CDASH

description: Indicates whether this procedure definition applies to unscheduled visits (e.g., safety follow-up, early termination). Distinguishes procedures that are only applicable at unscheduled visits from those in the planned Schedule of Events.
reference: CDISC SDTM TV Domain; ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical One Study Design

description: CDISC SDTM domain code to which this procedure's collected data maps (e.g., VS=Vital Signs, LB=Laboratory Test Results, EG=ECG Test Results, EX=Exposure, PE=Physical Examination). Drives SDTM dataset construction for regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VS|LB|EG|IE|DS|AE|CM|EX|MH|PE|PR|SC|TU|RS|FA|other — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: Specifies the Source Data Verification (SDV) requirement level for data collected by this procedure. Drives risk-based monitoring plans and CRA visit scope. 100_percent requires full SDV; risk_based applies statistical sampling; critical_only limits SDV to primary endpoint data.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance (2013); EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: Numeric ordering of the procedure within the visit, defining the recommended execution sequence (e.g., 1=vital signs, 2=blood draw, 3=ECG). Used to generate the ordered Schedule of Events and site procedure checklists.
reference: CDISC SDTM TV Domain; ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4

description: Type of biological specimen collected during this procedure, if applicable (e.g., blood, urine, tissue biopsy, CSF). Drives laboratory supply chain planning, sample handling instructions, and CDISC SDTM LBSPEC mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|urine|csf|tissue|saliva|stool|plasma|serum|whole_blood|not_applicable — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM LB Domain LBSPEC variable; WHO Laboratory Manual; ICH E6(R2)

description: Volume of biological specimen required for this procedure in millilitres (mL). Used for blood volume tracking per subject visit, laboratory supply planning, and protocol feasibility assessment (total blood volume per subject).
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Blood Volume in Clinical Trials; CDISC SDTM LB Domain

description: Specifies the timing reference point for procedure execution relative to a clinical anchor event (e.g., pre_dose, post_dose, fasting). Critical for PK sampling, vital signs, and lab draws where timing relative to dosing affects data interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_dose|post_dose|any_time|fasting|pre_meal|post_meal|bedtime|morning|evening — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM EPOCH/TAETORD; ICH E6(R2); FDA Bioanalytical Guidance

description: Allowable time window in minutes after the nominal procedure time point (positive offset). Defines the acceptable late execution window for protocol compliance assessment (e.g., +30 minutes for a post-dose PK sample).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4; CDISC SDTM SUPPQUAL; FDA Protocol Deviation Guidance

description: Allowable time window in minutes before the nominal procedure time point (negative offset). Defines the acceptable early execution window for protocol compliance assessment (e.g., -30 minutes for a pre-dose PK sample).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4; CDISC SDTM SUPPQUAL; FDA Protocol Deviation Guidance

description: Timestamp when this visit procedure record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each study arm record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the study_arm data product.
reference: CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: FK to study.investigational_product
Reference to the parent clinical study to which this arm belongs. Links the arm to its governing study protocol.
reference: CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Treatment arms can be added, modified, or removed by protocol amendments (e.g., adding a new dose arm in a dose-escalation study). Linking arm to study_protocol_amendment enables full amendment tracea
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Indicates whether this arm is part of an adaptive trial design where arm allocation, sample size, or continuation may be modified based on interim analysis results per a pre-specified adaptation rule.
reference: FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs (2019); EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs; ICH E9(R1)

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the arm within the study. Maps directly to the ARMCD variable in the CDISC SDTM ARM domain and is used in randomization and data tabulation.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 ARM Domain; CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: Full descriptive name of the study arm as defined in the protocol (e.g., 'Drug A 10mg QD', 'Placebo'). Maps to the ARM variable in the CDISC SDTM ARM domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 ARM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6

description: Current lifecycle status of the study arm, reflecting whether the arm is planned, actively enrolling, suspended, closed to enrollment, or discontinued per protocol amendment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the arm per ClinicalTrials.gov and CDISC controlled terminology, indicating the nature of the intervention assigned to subjects in this arm. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Experimental|Active Comparator|Placebo Comparator|Sham Comparator|No Intervention|Other — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; CDISC SDTM Controlled Terminology; ICH E8(R1)

description: Specifies the level of blinding applied to this arm: Open Label (no blinding), Single Blind (subject blinded), Double Blind (subject and investigator blinded), or Triple Blind (subject, investigator, and sponsor blinded).
reference: ICH E9(R1); ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; CDISC SDTM ARM Domain

description: The native arm identifier assigned by Oracle Clinical One (the source system of record for study design and patient randomization). Used for system-of-record traceability and data lineage from source to lakehouse.
reference: Oracle Clinical One Study Design Module; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The date and time when this study arm record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Arm Reference
description: The arm reference identifier as recorded in Oracle Siebel CTMS for site monitoring, visit tracking, and enrollment management. Supports cross-system reconciliation.
reference: Oracle Siebel CTMS; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reason for early discontinuation of the study arm, if applicable (e.g., safety signal, futility, sponsor decision, regulatory hold). Required for regulatory reporting when an arm is terminated prior to planned completion.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

description: Textual description of the dose level and regimen for the investigational product in this arm (e.g., '10 mg once daily', '200 mg BID'). Supports dose-response analysis and protocol compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6.4; CDISC SDTM EX Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Frequency of investigational product administration for subjects in this arm (e.g., QD - once daily, BID - twice daily, QW - once weekly). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXDOSFRQ variable.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; CDISC Controlled Terminology; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The date on which the study arm was closed to enrollment or treatment, either per protocol schedule or due to early termination. Null if the arm is still active.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014; Oracle Clinical One

description: Indicates whether this arm is subject to one or more pre-specified interim analyses by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) during the study.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; DSMB Charter Requirements

description: Detailed narrative description of the intervention assigned to subjects in this arm, including drug name, dose, route of administration, and dosing schedule as specified in the study protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6.4; CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Category of the therapeutic intervention assigned to subjects in this arm. Aligns with ClinicalTrials.gov intervention type classifications and supports regulatory submission categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Drug|Biologic|Device|Procedure|Behavioral|Dietary Supplement|Radiation|Genetic|Combination|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the treatment assignment for this arm is blinded to subjects, investigators, or both (double-blind). Supports blinding integrity tracking and unblinding event management.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.7; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Indicates whether this arm serves as the control arm (comparator) in the study design. True for placebo, active comparator, or sham comparator arms used as the reference group in statistical analyses.
reference: ICH E8(R1); ICH E9(R1); CDISC SDTM ARM Domain

description: Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM ADSL; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The maximum allowable number of subjects that may be enrolled in this arm, including any over-enrollment buffer defined in the protocol. Distinct from planned_subjects_count which reflects the statistical target.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6.7; Oracle Clinical One; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: The minimum number of subjects required in this arm to maintain statistical validity of the study, as defined in the SAP. Used for futility analysis and early stopping rule evaluation.
reference: ICH E9(R1); SAP Requirements; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, clarifications, or protocol-specific instructions related to this arm that are not captured in structured fields. For internal use by study team.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Clinical One

description: The planned (target) number of subjects to be enrolled and randomized into this arm as specified in the study protocol and statistical analysis plan (SAP).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6.7; CDISC SDTM ARM Domain
