description: Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Subjects with major protocol deviations may be excluded.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM ADSL; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The allocation ratio for randomization of subjects into this arm relative to other arms (e.g., '1:1', '2:1:1'). Used by the RTSM/IRT system (Medidata Rave RTSM) to control subject assignment.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Numeric representation of the allocation weight for this arm relative to total allocation (e.g., 0.50 for a 1:1 ratio arm). Supports quantitative analysis and reporting of enrollment balance.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC SDTM ARM Domain

description: The route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects in this arm (e.g., Oral, Intravenous, Subcutaneous, Intramuscular). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXROUTE variable and WHO-DD terminology.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; WHO-DD; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; NCI Thesaurus

description: Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the safety analysis population (all subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment), as defined in the SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM ADSL; FDA Statistical Guidance; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Arm Domain
description: CDISC SDTM domain identifier confirming this arm maps to the ARM domain in the SDTM submission dataset. Always 'ARM' per CDISC standard; included for regulatory submission traceability.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 ARM Domain; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA CDISC Requirements

description: Ordinal sequence number defining the display and processing order of arms within the study. Used for consistent ordering in reports, eCRFs, and SDTM datasets.
reference: CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; Oracle Clinical One Study Design

description: The date on which the study arm became active and eligible for subject enrollment, as defined in the approved protocol or protocol amendment. Supports enrollment timeline tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014; Oracle Clinical One

description: Planned duration of treatment for subjects in this arm, expressed in days. Used for supply planning, visit schedule design, and protocol compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 6.4; CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; Oracle Clinical One

description: The username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this study arm record. Required for audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable) principle.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The date and time when this study arm record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The username or system identifier of the user or process that created this study arm record. Required for audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable) principle.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each eligibility criterion record in the clinical study. Primary key for the eligibility_criterion data product.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: eligibility_criterion has meddra_pt_code and meddra_pt_name as plain attributes — inclusion/exclusion criteria referencing medical conditions are MedDRA-coded for SDTM IE domain mapping and regulatory
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the prior version of this criterion that was superseded by the current record. Enables full version history chain for audit trail and regulatory traceability of criterion evolution across protocol amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Reference to the specific protocol version under which this criterion was introduced or last modified. Supports amendment-driven criterion tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM IE Domain

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this eligibility criterion is defined. Links the criterion to its parent study protocol.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: eligibility_criterion has amendment_number as a STRING and is_amendment_driven BOOLEAN flag but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Eligibility criteria are frequently introduced or modified by protoco
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Specifies the sub-population to which this criterion applies when it is not universally applicable to all subjects (e.g., reproductive status criteria apply to FEMALE_ONLY, pediatric dosing criteria apply to PEDIATRIC). Supports conditional screening logic.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E11 (Pediatric Studies); FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The documented scientific or operational rationale for introducing or modifying this criterion, particularly when driven by a protocol amendment. Required for regulatory submissions and IRB/IEC amendment packages.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The date and time when this eligibility criterion record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: The page or section reference in the electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) where this eligibility criterion is assessed and documented. Used for site training, monitoring, and Source Data Verification (SDV) activities.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Clinical or operational category classifying the nature of the criterion. Supports structured analysis and site training. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEMOGRAPHIC|DIAGNOSIS|LAB_VALUE|PRIOR_THERAPY|CONCOMITANT_MEDICATION|REPRODUCTIVE_STATUS|WASHOUT_PERIOD|CONSENT|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain (IESCAT); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Sequential number assigned to the criterion within its type (inclusion or exclusion) as it appears in the study protocol. Used for ordering and referencing in site screening checklists and EDC eligibility modules.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain (IESEQ); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Abbreviated or condensed label for the criterion used in EDC display, site training materials, and operational screening tools where full text is impractical. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IETESTCD.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain (IETESTCD); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Current lifecycle status of the eligibility criterion. DRAFT indicates under review; ACTIVE indicates approved and in use; SUPERSEDED indicates replaced by an amended version; RETIRED indicates removed from the protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Full verbatim text of the eligibility criterion as written in the approved study protocol. This is the authoritative, protocol-exact wording used in site screening checklists, EDC eligibility modules, and regulatory submissions. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IETEST.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain (IETEST); ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the criterion is an inclusion criterion (subject must meet) or an exclusion criterion (subject must not meet) for enrollment eligibility. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IECAT.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain (IECAT); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The display sequence order for this criterion within its type group (inclusion or exclusion) as it should appear in site screening checklists, EDC eligibility modules, and protocol documents. May differ from criterion_number when criteria are reordered by amendment.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Capture (EDC) Field Object Identifier (OID)
description: The Object Identifier (OID) of the corresponding field in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) eligibility module. Enables direct mapping between the protocol criterion and the EDC data collection form for data lineage and validation.
reference: CDISC ODM (Operational Data Model); Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date from which this criterion version became operationally effective for subject screening. Aligns with the IRB/IEC approval date of the applicable protocol version.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board (IRB) / Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) Approval Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a change to this criterion requires formal IRB/IEC approval before implementation at sites. Typically True for all criteria changes driven by protocol amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 56

description: Indicates whether this criterion was introduced or modified as a result of a protocol amendment. True when the criterion originated from or was changed by a formal protocol amendment. Critical for regulatory audit trails and amendment impact analysis.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether this criterion is designated as critical, meaning a violation would constitute a major protocol deviation requiring immediate reporting to the sponsor and potentially the IRB/IEC. Critical criteria are typically safety-related or have significant impact on data integrity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM IE Domain

description: The standardized laboratory test code (e.g., LOINC code or sponsor-defined code) for the lab parameter referenced in this criterion. Applicable for lab value criteria such as eGFR, hemoglobin, or platelet count thresholds.
reference: LOINC; CDISC SDTM LB Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Code
description: The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code associated with the diagnosis, condition, or medical history referenced in this criterion. Enables standardized medical coding for regulatory submissions and cross-study analysis.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; CDISC SDTM

description: The date and time when this eligibility criterion record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: The logical comparator defining the relationship between the subject's measured value and the numeric threshold (EQ=equal, NE=not equal, LT=less than, LE=less than or equal, GT=greater than, GE=greater than or equal). Used in conjunction with numeric_threshold_value.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ISO 11179

description: The unit of measure for the numeric threshold value (e.g., years, mL/min/1.73m², kg/m², mg/dL). Follows CDISC controlled terminology for units.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ISO 11240

description: The quantitative threshold value associated with the criterion where applicable (e.g., age ≥ 18, eGFR ≥ 60, BMI ≤ 35). Used for lab value, vital sign, and demographic criteria that have a numeric boundary.
reference: CDISC SDTM IE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The protocol version number (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) in which this criterion first became applicable. Used to determine which subjects enrolled under which protocol version were subject to this criterion.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IE Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The protocol version number in which this criterion was last applicable before being superseded or retired. Null if the criterion is still active in the current protocol version.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IE Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required for this criterion during monitoring visits. When True, the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) must verify the criterion assessment against source documents at the site.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ALCOA+

description: The date on which this criterion was retired or superseded. Null if the criterion remains active. Used to determine the applicable criterion set for subjects screened on a given date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Operational guidance notes for site staff on how to assess and document this criterion during subject screening. May include clarifications, assessment methods, source document requirements, and common pitfalls. Sourced from the study's screening manual or site training materials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM IE Domain

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Inclusion/Exclusion (IE) Test Code
description: CDISC SDTM-compliant short code for the criterion, conforming to the IE domain IETESTCD variable. Maximum 8 uppercase alphanumeric characters. Used for regulatory submission datasets and CDISC compliance validation.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide IE Domain; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: The type of source document required to verify this criterion (e.g., medical records, laboratory report, imaging report, physician attestation, birth certificate). Used to guide site staff and CRAs during screening and monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The operational system of record from which this eligibility criterion record originated (e.g., Oracle Clinical One for study design, Medidata Rave for EDC eligibility module). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; CDISC SDTM

description: The native identifier of this criterion record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One criterion object ID, Medidata Rave field OID). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the source system.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: The unit of time for the time window associated with the criterion (e.g., DAYS, WEEKS, MONTHS, YEARS). Used for washout periods, prior therapy windows, and diagnosis recency requirements.
reference: CDISC Controlled Terminology; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The numeric duration of the time window associated with the criterion (e.g., prior therapy washout period of 4 weeks, prior diagnosis within 6 months). Used in conjunction with time_window_unit.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IE Domain

description: Indicates whether a protocol deviation waiver or exception can be granted for this criterion on a per-subject basis. When True, a formal waiver request process is required and must be documented in the Trial Master File (TMF).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM IE Domain

description: The body or role authorized to grant a waiver for this criterion (e.g., Medical Monitor, Sponsor Medical Director, IRB/IEC). Applicable only when waiver_allowed is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

business_glossary_term: World Health Organization Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) Drug Code
description: The WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) code for the medication referenced in this criterion, applicable for prior therapy or concomitant medication criteria. Enables standardized drug coding for regulatory submissions.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); CDISC SDTM CM Domain

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a study endpoint record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the study_endpoint data product.
reference: CDISC SDTM/ADaM; ICH E9(R1)

description: Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: Safety endpoints reference specific CTCAE grade thresholds (study_endpoint has ctcae_version and ctcae_grade as plain attributes). Linking to ctcae_grade supports SAP authoring, SDTM CTCAE domain mapp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: study_endpoint has meddra_soc_code as a plain attribute — safety endpoints are defined using MedDRA terminology. Linking to meddra_term supports SDTM FATEST/CETERM mapping, signal detection alignment,
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent clinical study to which this endpoint belongs. Links the endpoint to its governing protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Endpoints are formally defined in the protocol and can be modified by amendments (e.g., changing a secondary endpoint to primary, adding exploratory endpoints). Linking endpoint to study_protocol_amen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The CDISC ADaM PARAMCD value (≤8 characters, uppercase) that uniquely identifies the analysis parameter corresponding to this endpoint within the ADaM dataset. Required for CDISC-compliant regulatory submissions to FDA and EMA.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: The CDISC ADaM PARAM value — the full descriptive label for the analysis parameter corresponding to this endpoint (e.g., 'Overall Survival Time (Days)'). Displayed in analysis output tables and listings.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: The pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level) for this endpoint (e.g., 0.05 for two-sided, 0.025 for one-sided). Defined in the SAP and referenced in the protocol.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; CDISC ADaM

description: The analysis population to which this endpoint applies: Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT (mITT), Per Protocol (PP), safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), or biomarker population. Defined in the SAP and referenced in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITT|mITT|PP|safety|PK|biomarker — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; CDISC ADaM

description: The protocol-defined timepoint or window at which the endpoint is assessed (e.g., 'Week 24', 'End of Treatment', 'Month 6 ± 7 days'). Directly maps to the visit schedule and SDTM VISITNUM.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E9(R1)

description: The date and time when this endpoint record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Version
description: The version of the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) used to grade safety endpoints (e.g., '5.0', '4.03'). Applicable for oncology and safety-focused endpoints.
reference: NCI CTCAE; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; ICH E2A

description: Specifies whether the statistical test for this endpoint is one-sided or two-sided. Determines the alpha allocation and critical value used in hypothesis testing.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: The date from which this endpoint definition became effective, typically aligned with the protocol version approval date. Supports temporal versioning across protocol amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The date on which this endpoint definition was superseded or retired, typically aligned with a subsequent protocol amendment. Null for currently active endpoints. Supports temporal versioning.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Scientific domain category of the endpoint: efficacy (treatment effect), safety (harm characterisation), tolerability, Pharmacokinetics/Pharmacodynamics (PK/PD), quality of life (patient-reported outcomes), or biomarker. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|safety|tolerability|PK_PD|quality_of_life|biomarker — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA PDUFA; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this endpoint within the study (e.g., EP-PRI01, EP-SEC03). Used as the externally-known business identifier in SAP cross-references and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM PARAMCD conventions

description: Full narrative description of the endpoint as defined in the study protocol, including the precise measurement, population, and timeframe. Directly referenced in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Short, human-readable name of the endpoint as it appears in the protocol and Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) (e.g., 'Overall Survival', 'Change from Baseline in HbA1c at Week 24').
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: Current lifecycle state of the endpoint definition: draft (under authoring), active (protocol-approved), amended (modified via protocol amendment), retired (removed from protocol), locked (post-Database Lock). Drives SAP version control.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM

description: Classification of the endpoint role within the study hierarchy: primary (confirmatory, drives sample size), secondary (supportive), exploratory, safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), Pharmacodynamic (PD), or biomarker. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|exploratory|safety|PK|PD|biomarker — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: The strategy for handling intercurrent events per ICH E9(R1): treatment_policy (ignore occurrence), hypothetical (counterfactual), composite (incorporate into outcome), while_on_treatment (restrict to on-treatment period), or principal_stratum (condition on intercurrent event status).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands

description: The target population component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand, specifying the patient population to which the treatment effect applies (e.g., 'all randomised patients with baseline HbA1c ≥ 7.5%').
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands

description: The population-level summary measure component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand (e.g., 'difference in means', 'hazard ratio', 'odds ratio'). Specifies how individual outcomes are aggregated to a treatment effect estimate.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands

description: The treatment condition component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework, describing the treatment regimen of interest (e.g., 'randomised treatment regardless of adherence', 'treatment as prescribed').
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands

description: The variable component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand, defining the outcome measure for each patient (e.g., 'change from baseline in HbA1c at Week 24'). Directly corresponds to the ADaM analysis variable.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; CDISC ADaM

description: The validated instrument, scale, or tool used to measure the endpoint (e.g., RECIST 1.1, HAMD-17, SF-36, CTCAE v5.0, EORTC QLQ-C30). Required for regulatory submission and biostatistical analysis planning.
reference: FDA Guidance on Patient-Reported Outcomes; CTCAE v5.0; ICH E9(R1)

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code associated with this endpoint when the endpoint relates to an adverse event or safety outcome. Enables standardised safety reporting per ICH M1.
reference: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities); ICH M1; FDA/EMA MedDRA requirements

description: The pre-specified strategy for handling missing data for this endpoint per the SAP: Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), Baseline Observation Carried Forward (BOCF), Multiple Imputation (MI), Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM), observed cases only, worst-case, or best-case imputation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LOCF|BOCF|MI|MMRM|observed_cases|worst_case|best_case — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); NRC Report on Missing Data in Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance on Missing Data

description: Indicates whether a multiplicity adjustment (e.g., Bonferroni, Holm, Hochberg, gatekeeping procedure) is applied to this endpoint to control the family-wise error rate. Critical for confirmatory trial design and regulatory acceptance.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity

description: The specific statistical procedure used for multiplicity adjustment for this endpoint (e.g., 'Hochberg step-up', 'Bonferroni', 'Fixed-sequence testing', 'Graphical gatekeeping'). Populated only when multiplicity_adjustment_flag is True.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity

description: The pre-specified non-inferiority or equivalence margin (delta) for the endpoint, expressed in the unit of the primary outcome measure. Applicable only when statistical_hypothesis is non_inferiority or equivalence. Regulatory-critical value.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E10; FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials

description: Reference to the study objective that this endpoint operationalises, enabling objective-to-endpoint traceability required by the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM

description: The section number or heading in the study protocol document where this endpoint is formally defined (e.g., 'Section 3.2.1 Primary Endpoint'). Enables direct cross-reference to the Trial Master File (TMF).
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The section number or heading in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) where the analysis method for this endpoint is specified. Provides the scientific rationale chain from objective through endpoint to SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on SAP; CDISC ADaM

description: The CDISC SDTM domain from which the source data for this endpoint is collected (e.g., AE, LB, EX, RS, TU, TR). Establishes the SDTM-to-ADaM data lineage chain required for regulatory submission.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Ordinal position of this endpoint within its objective type group (e.g., 1st primary, 2nd secondary). Used for hierarchical ordering in the SAP and regulatory submission tables.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this endpoint definition originates: Medidata Rave (EDC), Oracle Clinical One (study design), SAS Drug Development (biostatistical analysis), or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: The native identifier of this endpoint record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One endpoint object ID, SAS Drug Development analysis parameter ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ETL lineage.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: The formal statistical hypothesis framework applied to this endpoint: superiority (treatment better than control), non-inferiority (treatment not worse by a defined margin), equivalence (treatment within a symmetric margin), or descriptive (no formal hypothesis). Drives SAP design.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E10; FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials

description: The unit in which the endpoint outcome is expressed (e.g., 'months', 'mg/dL', 'mmHg', 'score', '%', 'events/patient-year'). Required for ADaM AVALU mapping and regulatory submission.
reference: CDISC SDTM/ADaM; UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure); FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: The date and time when this endpoint record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and change control requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a study objective record in the clinical trial data platform. Primary key for the study_objective entity.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice

description: Reference to the primary endpoint directly associated with this objective. Establishes the objective-to-endpoint traceability chain required by the SAP and regulatory submissions. Nullable for objectives that map to multiple endpoints.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; CDISC ADaM

description: Reference to the parent clinical study to which this objective belongs. Links the objective to its governing protocol and study master record.
reference: CDISC SDTM OI Domain; ICH E6(R2)

description: Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Study objectives are formally stated in the protocol and can be modified by amendments (e.g., refining the primary objective, adding secondary objectives). Linking objective to study_protocol_amendmen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: CDISC ADaM dataset name(s) used for the primary analysis supporting this objective (e.g., ADEFF, ADTTE, ADAE). Provides direct linkage from objective to analysis-ready datasets in SAS Drug Development.
reference: CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Data Standards Catalog; PMDA Data Standards

description: Pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level) allocated to this objective in the SAP. Typically 0.05 for primary objectives or a fraction thereof when multiplicity adjustments are applied. Drives sample size calculations and hypothesis testing.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E9 Statistical Principles; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Date on which this objective (as part of the protocol or amendment) received formal approval from the sponsor and/or IRB/IEC. Marks the transition from draft to active status and is required for regulatory audit trails.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46

description: Timestamp when this study objective record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Section number within the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where results for this objective are reported. Provides traceability from protocol objective through analysis to regulatory submission document per ICH E3 CSR structure.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; eCTD

description: Module and section reference within the Common Technical Document (CTD) / electronic CTD (eCTD) where this objective's results contribute to the regulatory dossier (e.g., Module 5.3.5.1). Supports regulatory submission traceability.
reference: ICH M4E(R2) CTD Efficacy; eCTD Specification; FDA NDA/BLA Submission Requirements

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether interim results for this objective are to be reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) during planned interim analyses. Drives interim analysis planning and unblinding procedures.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which this version of the objective ceased to be effective, typically due to a protocol amendment superseding it. Null for currently active objectives. Supports temporal versioning of protocol objectives.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date from which this version of the objective became effective in the approved protocol. For original protocol objectives, this is the initial protocol approval date. For amended objectives, this is the amendment approval date.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Statistical hypothesis framework associated with this objective. Superiority objectives aim to demonstrate a treatment effect greater than control; non-inferiority objectives aim to show the treatment is not worse by a defined margin; equivalence objectives test for no meaningful difference; descriptive objectives have no formal hypothesis.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Non-Inferiority Clinical Trials; EMA Guideline on the Choice of Non-Inferiority Margin

description: ICH E9(R1) estimand attribute specifying the strategy for handling intercurrent events (post-randomization events that affect interpretation of the outcome) for this objective. Strategies include while-on-treatment, hypothetical, composite, principal stratum, and treatment policy.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum; EMA Reflection Paper on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Estimands

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Review Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a change to this objective requires IRB/IEC review and approval (e.g., substantial protocol amendment). Drives the amendment workflow and site notification process.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46

description: Indicates whether statistical multiplicity adjustment (e.g., Bonferroni, Hochberg, hierarchical testing) is required for this objective due to multiple comparisons. Informs the SAP multiplicity control strategy.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or internal commentary related to this objective that does not fit structured fields. Used by data managers and biostatisticians during protocol development and SAP authoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Structured alphanumeric identifier for the objective within the protocol (e.g., P-1 for Primary Objective 1, S-2 for Secondary Objective 2). Used for cross-referencing in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; CDISC Protocol Representation Model

description: Current lifecycle state of the study objective. Draft objectives are under authoring review; active objectives are in the approved protocol; amended objectives have been modified by a protocol amendment; superseded objectives have been replaced; retired objectives have been removed from the protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC Protocol Representation Model

description: Full verbatim text of the study objective as stated in the approved protocol. This is the authoritative scientific statement of what the study aims to demonstrate or evaluate, used verbatim in regulatory submissions and the CSR.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the study objective by its scientific hierarchy and regulatory weight. Primary objectives drive the primary endpoint and sample size calculation; secondary objectives provide supportive evidence; exploratory objectives generate hypotheses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|exploratory|safety|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E8(R1) General Considerations for Clinical Studies; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: Description of the analysis population to which this objective applies (e.g., Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety Population). Aligns with the estimand population attribute per ICH E9(R1).
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; CDISC ADaM

description: Standardized code for the analysis population associated with this objective. Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT (mITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), or Pharmacodynamic (PD) population. Drives dataset selection in SAS Drug Development CDISC ADaM programming.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Section number within the study protocol document where this objective is formally stated (e.g., Section 2.1, Section 3.2). Enables direct navigation to the source text in the protocol and eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23; CDISC Protocol Representation Model

description: Indicates whether this objective is a formal regulatory commitment (e.g., a post-marketing commitment to FDA or EMA, or a condition of approval). Regulatory commitment objectives require heightened change control and reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA Post-Authorization Commitments; ICH E6(R2)

description: Section number or identifier within the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that addresses the analysis corresponding to this objective. Provides direct traceability from protocol objective to statistical methodology, as required for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Statistical Guidance on Reporting Results; CDISC ADaM

description: Narrative justification for why this objective is scientifically important and how it contributes to the overall study goals. Supports regulatory review, protocol synopsis, and investigator brochure alignment.
reference: ICH E8(R1) General Considerations for Clinical Studies; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Design; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Domain Reference
description: CDISC SDTM domain(s) that contain the data supporting this objective (e.g., ADAE, ADEFF, ADTTE). Enables automated linkage between protocol objectives and SDTM/ADaM datasets in SAS Drug Development.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Ordinal position of this objective within its objective type group (e.g., first primary objective = 1, second primary objective = 2). Governs display order in the protocol, SAP, and CSR sections.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC Protocol Representation Model

description: Concise human-readable label for the objective used in dashboards, reports, and CTMS displays where the full objective text is too lengthy. Typically 10–15 words summarizing the scientific intent.
reference: CDISC Protocol Representation Model; Oracle Clinical One Study Design Module

description: Operational system of record from which this objective record was sourced or authored (e.g., Oracle Clinical One study design module, Veeva Vault protocol document). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Native identifier of this objective record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One objective GUID, Veeva Vault document section ID). Enables back-referencing and reconciliation with the system of record.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: Statistical summary measure used to quantify the treatment effect for this objective (e.g., difference in means, odds ratio, hazard ratio, response rate difference). Corresponds to the population-level summary attribute of the ICH E9(R1) estimand.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum; ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM

description: Numeric level in the hierarchical testing procedure for this objective. Level 1 is tested first; subsequent levels are tested only if the preceding level achieves statistical significance. Implements the gatekeeping strategy defined in the SAP.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Multiple Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Multiplicity Issues

description: Therapeutic area or disease indication to which this objective is aligned (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Supports cross-study analytics and portfolio-level objective benchmarking.
reference: MedDRA; CDISC Therapeutic Area Data Standards; ICH E8(R1)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this study objective record in the data platform. Required for change tracking, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Description of the variable (outcome measure) used to address this objective, as defined in the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework. Specifies what is measured on each subject to evaluate the treatment effect.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum; CDISC Protocol Representation Model

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the clinical study protocol record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the protocol data product.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Every protocol is conducted under an IND. This link is required for annual IND report generation, clinical hold management, and safety reporting obligation tracking. ind_cta_number is a denormalized t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Method by which participants are assigned to study arms. 'Randomized' indicates use of RTSM/IRT for random assignment; 'non-randomized' indicates assignment by investigator or other non-random method. Drives RTSM configuration in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG ARM domain; ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ClinicalTrials.gov

description: Date on which the protocol amendment was formally issued or approved. Null for the original protocol version. Used to establish the amendment timeline and regulatory notification deadlines.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Sequential identifier for the protocol amendment (e.g., Amendment 1, Amendment 2). Null for the original protocol version. Used to track the amendment history and link to regulatory submission records in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EMA Variation Regulation

description: Formal justification for the protocol amendment describing the scientific, safety, or operational reasons for the change. Required for regulatory submissions and IRB/IEC notifications. Stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Classification of the protocol amendment per regulatory definitions. 'Substantial' amendments require prior regulatory authority and IRB/IEC approval before implementation; 'non-substantial' require notification only; 'administrative' are clerical corrections with no impact on study conduct or subject safety.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; MHRA Clinical Trials Regulations

description: Date on which the protocol version received final sponsor and/or regulatory/IRB/IEC approval, making it the operative protocol for study conduct. Tracked in Veeva Vault quality management workflow. Establishes the effective date for GCP compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Timestamp when this protocol record was first created in the source system or ingested into the lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ALCOA+ Data Integrity; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

business_glossary_term: Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the protocol mandates the establishment of a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety oversight and interim analysis review. Typically required for Phase III trials and studies with significant safety risks.
reference: FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees (2006); ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; EMA Guideline on DMC

description: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) number or Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) number assigned under EU CTR 536/2014. Required for all clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA. Used for EMA regulatory tracking and public registry disclosure.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; EudraCT/CTIS Registration; EMA Transparency Policy

description: Indicates whether the protocol includes a formal declaration of compliance with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines and applicable regulatory requirements. Required for all interventional clinical trials. Tracked in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; MHRA GCP Regulations

business_glossary_term: International Council for Harmonisation (ICH) Compliance Declaration
description: Indicates whether the protocol formally declares compliance with applicable ICH guidelines (E6, E8, E9, E10, etc.). Distinct from GCP declaration; covers the broader ICH guideline framework including statistical and design standards.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E8(R1); ICH E9(R1); ICH E10; FDA/EMA/PMDA ICH Implementation

description: Describes the strategy for assigning interventions to participants. Determines the statistical analysis plan (SAP) structure, number of arms, and eCRF design in Medidata Rave. Aligns with CDISC SDTM ARM domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG ARM domain; ICH E9(R1) Addendum; ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration

description: Status of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) notification or approval for this protocol version or amendment. Tracks compliance with ethics review requirements across jurisdictions. Sourced from Veeva Vault regulatory module.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 4; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46

description: Indicates whether the study employs an adaptive design allowing pre-specified modifications to the trial or statistical procedures based on interim data analysis. Triggers additional regulatory review requirements and DSMB/DMC oversight obligations.
reference: FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials (2019); EMA Reflection Paper on Adaptive Designs; ICH E9(R1)

description: Indicates whether the study includes a pediatric population (subjects under 18 years). Triggers Pediatric Investigation Plan (PIP) requirements under EMA and Pediatric Study Plan (PSP) under FDA PREA/BPCA, and specific ICF/assent requirements.
reference: FDA PREA/BPCA; EMA Pediatric Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006; ICH E11(R1)

description: Level of blinding applied to the study to minimize bias. Specifies which parties (participant, investigator, outcomes assessor, data analyst) are blinded to treatment assignment. Critical for RTSM/IRT configuration and unblinding procedures.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; CDISC SDTM IG; ClinicalTrials.gov; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Total count of treatment arms (groups) defined in the protocol. Drives RTSM randomization configuration, eCRF arm assignment, and statistical analysis plan (SAP) structure. Must be consistent with the intervention model.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG ARM domain; ICH E9 Statistical Principles; Oracle Clinical One Study Design

description: Total planned duration of the study in months from first subject first visit (FSFV) to last subject last visit (LSLV) or database lock (DBL), as specified in the protocol. Used for timeline planning, resource forecasting, and milestone tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; Oracle Siebel CTMS Study Timeline

description: Anticipated date of last subject last visit (LSLV) or database lock (DBL) as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for resource planning, regulatory submission timelines, and study close-out activities.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; WHO ICTRP Registration

description: Total number of subjects planned for enrollment across all study sites and arms as specified in the protocol. Derived from the statistical power calculation in the SAP. Used for site feasibility, patient recruitment planning, and IP supply forecasting.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs; CDISC SDTM

description: Anticipated date of first subject first visit (FSFV) or study initiation as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for milestone tracking, site activation planning, and regulatory submission timelines in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; WHO ICTRP Registration

description: The principal outcome measure used to evaluate the primary objective of the study. Defines the variable, measurement method, timepoint, and population. Drives eCRF design in Medidata Rave and the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; CDISC ADaM

description: Formal statement of the primary scientific objective of the study as defined in the protocol. Describes the main question the study is designed to answer. Directly linked to the primary endpoint and powers the sample size calculation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.2; ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: The primary reason for conducting the clinical study. Determines regulatory pathway, endpoint requirements, and IRB/IEC review criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: treatment|prevention|diagnostic|supportive_care|screening|health_services_research|basic_science|device_feasibility — promote to reference product]
reference: ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; ICH E8(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Externally-known, sponsor-assigned unique alphanumeric identifier for the clinical study protocol (e.g., ABC-2024-001). Used across all regulatory submissions, site documents, and operational systems as the primary business key. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23; CDISC SDTM STUDYID

description: Current lifecycle state of the protocol version. 'draft' indicates under development; 'approved' indicates IRB/IEC and sponsor approval obtained; 'amended' indicates a formal amendment is in effect; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version; 'final' indicates study completed and protocol finalized; 'retired' indicates withdrawn. Sourced from Veeva Vault quality management workflow.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Public clinical trial registry identifier such as ClinicalTrials.gov NCT number (e.g., NCT12345678) or WHO ICTRP identifier. Required by ICMJE for publication eligibility and by FDA/EMA for transparency. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: WHO ICTRP; ClinicalTrials.gov (42 CFR Part 11); ICMJE Clinical Trial Registration Policy; FDA FDAAA 801

description: Status of the regulatory authority submission for this protocol version or amendment (e.g., IND amendment to FDA, CTA variation to EMA/MHRA). Tracks compliance with regulatory notification and approval requirements. Sourced from Veeva Vault regulatory submissions module.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; ICH E6(R2); MHRA Clinical Trials Regulations

description: Abbreviated or lay-language title of the clinical study used in operational communications, CTMS displays, patient-facing materials, and internal reporting. Distinct from the full regulatory title.
reference: WHO ICTRP Registration Data Set; ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration

description: Operational system of record from which this protocol record was sourced. Primary sources are Oracle Clinical One (study design and protocol configuration) and Veeva Vault (eTMF and regulatory document management). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles; 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; EU Annex 11

description: Native record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Clinical One study ID or Veeva Vault document ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: ICH-defined phase of clinical development for the investigational product. Determines regulatory requirements, sample size expectations, and study design complexity. 'Not Applicable' used for non-IND studies such as device or observational studies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I|Phase I/II|Phase II|Phase II/III|Phase III|Phase IV|Not Applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E8(R1) General Considerations for Clinical Studies; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.21; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Narrative description of the target patient population for the study, including disease characteristics, demographic parameters, and key eligibility criteria summary. Used in IRB/IEC submissions, regulatory filings, and patient recruitment materials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Broad classification of the study design indicating whether participants receive an intervention or are observed. Determines applicable regulatory framework, IRB review requirements, and reporting obligations.
reference: ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; ICH E8(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Full official title of the clinical study protocol as it appears on the protocol document and regulatory submissions. Used in IRB/IEC submissions, clinical trial registries, and the Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; WHO ICTRP Registration Data Set; ClinicalTrials.gov

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this protocol record in the source system or lakehouse. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; ALCOA+ Data Integrity; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Version identifier of the protocol document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 3.1). Combined with protocol_number, uniquely identifies a specific iteration of the protocol. Incremented with each formal amendment. Sourced from Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the clinical study record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the study_study entity. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: study_study is the top-level master entity for a clinical trial; protocol is the master record for the study protocol. Adding protocol_id FK to study_study formalizes the authoritative link between th
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The short alphanumeric acronym or abbreviated name assigned to the clinical study for operational use across CTMS, EDC, and internal communications (e.g., APEX-HF, BRIGHT-2). Facilitates quick identification in dashboards, reports, and cross-system references.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS Study Object

description: The actual date on which the last patient completed the last study visit (Last Patient Last Visit - LPLV). Recorded upon confirmation in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Triggers database lock preparation, CSR authoring, and TMF archival workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: The actual cumulative number of subjects enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) across all study sites at the current point in time. Updated from Medidata Rave EDC enrollment data. Used for enrollment tracking KPIs and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ClinicalTrials.gov Reporting Requirements; FDAAA 801

description: The actual date on which the first patient was enrolled or the first study procedure was performed (First Patient First Visit - FPFV). Recorded upon confirmation in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for regulatory reporting, timeline variance analysis, and KPI measurement.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; WHO ICTRP Registration; ClinicalTrials.gov

description: The sequential count of protocol amendments issued since the original protocol approval. A value of 0 indicates the original protocol with no amendments. Increments with each approved protocol amendment. Used to track protocol evolution and regulatory submission history.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: The blinding (masking) classification applied to the clinical study indicating which parties are unaware of treatment assignment. Drives operational procedures for unblinding, DSMB/DMC oversight, and safety reporting. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) Section 1.11; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The timestamp when the study record was first created in the source system (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: The date on which the Clinical Study Report (CSR) was submitted to the regulatory authority or sponsor. The CSR is the comprehensive document summarizing study methods, results, and conclusions per ICH E3 guidelines. Tracked in Veeva Vault regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Study Identifier
description: The native study identifier assigned by Oracle Siebel CTMS, the system of record for this entity. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit, reconciliation, and data lineage purposes per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The date used as the data cutoff for interim or final analyses, defining the observation window for subject data included in the analysis dataset. Specified in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and used to generate SDTM/ADaM datasets in SAS Drug Development.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC ADaM IG; SAS Drug Development; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The date on which the clinical database was formally locked following completion of all data cleaning, query resolution, and medical coding activities. Database Lock (DBL) is a critical regulatory milestone that freezes the dataset for statistical analysis. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC SDTM IG; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The methodological design classification of the clinical study (e.g., Parallel Group, Crossover, Single Arm, Factorial, Adaptive). Informs statistical analysis plan design, randomization schema, and protocol complexity. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One study design configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Parallel Group|Crossover|Single Arm|Factorial|Adaptive|Basket|Umbrella|Platform — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E8(R1); CDISC SDTM IG

value_regex: Phase I|Phase I/II|Phase II|Phase II/III|Phase III|Phase IV
description: The regulatory development phase of the clinical study per ICH and FDA/EMA classification (Phase I through Phase IV). Drives regulatory requirements, statistical analysis plan complexity, safety reporting obligations, and resource planning. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS Protocol Phase field.
reference: ICH E8(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The study identifier assigned within the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave) used for clinical data collection. Enables cross-system traceability between the CTMS master record and the EDC operational system. Required for data lineage and audit trail purposes per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+ Principles

description: The European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) registration number assigned by the EMA for clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA (e.g., 2023-001234-56). Required for all interventional trials in EU member states under EU CTR 536/2014.
reference: EMA EU CTR 536/2014; EudraCT Regulation (EC) No 536/2014

description: The date on which the first investigational site was formally initiated (Site Initiation Visit completed) and authorized to enroll subjects. A key operational milestone tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS for timeline KPI reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19; Oracle Siebel CTMS Milestone Management

description: The date of initial approval granted by the lead Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for the study protocol. This is a prerequisite for study initiation and subject enrollment. Tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP 45 CFR Part 46; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the clinical study employs randomization to assign participants to treatment arms (True) or uses a non-randomized assignment method (False). Determines RTSM/IRT configuration requirements in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM IG

description: The ClinicalTrials.gov registry identifier assigned by the U.S. National Library of Medicine upon study registration (e.g., NCT12345678). Mandatory for studies subject to FDAAA 801 and ICMJE registration requirements. Used for public disclosure and regulatory cross-referencing.
reference: WHO ICTRP; FDAAA 801; ICMJE Registration Policy; 42 CFR Part 11

description: The total number of treatment arms or groups defined in the study protocol (e.g., 2 for a two-arm parallel study, 1 for a single-arm study). Drives RTSM/IRT configuration in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One randomization setup.
reference: ICH E9(R1); CDISC SDTM ARM Domain; Oracle Clinical One Study Design

description: The planned number of countries in which the clinical study will be conducted. Drives regulatory strategy (multi-national submissions), country-level feasibility, and operational complexity assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2); EMA EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The originally planned date for last patient last visit (LPLV) or study completion as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for resource planning, site contract management, and regulatory timeline commitments.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS Milestone Management

description: The planned number of investigational sites to be activated for the clinical study as defined in the feasibility assessment and study protocol. Used for site selection planning, budget forecasting, and resource allocation in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19; Oracle Siebel CTMS Site Management

description: The originally planned date for first patient first visit (FPFV) or study initiation as defined in the study protocol and project plan. Used for timeline tracking, milestone management, and KPI reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Siebel CTMS Milestone Management; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The primary regulatory jurisdiction governing the clinical study, determining the lead regulatory authority (e.g., US for FDA, EU for EMA, JP for PMDA). Drives applicable GCP guidelines, submission requirements, and safety reporting timelines.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; PMDA; TGA; Health Canada

description: The sponsor-assigned unique alphanumeric identifier for the clinical study (e.g., ABC-1234-001). This is the primary business identifier used across all sponsor systems, regulatory submissions, and inter-organizational communications. Corresponds to the Protocol Number in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: The current operational lifecycle status of the clinical study. Drives workflow gating, resource allocation, regulatory reporting obligations, and system access controls across CTMS, EDC, and eTMF. This is the authoritative status field sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS. Transitions are tracked in the study status history product.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; Oracle Siebel CTMS Study Status

description: The fundamental classification of the clinical study design indicating whether participants receive an investigational intervention (Interventional), are observed without intervention (Observational), or receive investigational product outside a trial (Expanded Access). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E8(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; WHO ICTRP; ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration

description: The planned total number of subjects to be enrolled across all study sites as specified in the approved protocol and statistical analysis plan (SAP). Drives site allocation, recruitment planning, and power calculations. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23; CDISC SDTM IG

description: The documented reason for early termination of the clinical study when study_status is Terminated. Captures the primary rationale (e.g., Safety Concerns, Lack of Efficacy, Business Decision, Regulatory Hold, Enrollment Failure). Required for regulatory notifications and final CSR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Safety Concerns|Lack of Efficacy|Business Decision|Regulatory Hold|Enrollment Failure|Sponsor Decision|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.44; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 37

description: The complete official title of the clinical study as stated in the approved protocol document. Used in regulatory submissions, IRB/IEC applications, informed consent forms, and the Trial Master File. Corresponds to the Protocol Title field in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; CDISC SDTM IG; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: The date on which the Trial Master File (TMF) was formally archived following study close-out. The TMF must be retained for a minimum of 15 years (or longer per regional requirements) after study completion. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 58; EU GMP Annex 11

description: The timestamp when the study record was most recently modified in the source system (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each protocol amendment record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Protocol amendments are submitted as IND amendments or CTA variations filed in regulatory binders. study_protocol_amendment tracks fda_submission_date, ema_submission_date, pmda_submission_date. Linki
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each protocol amendment generates a TMF essential document (DIA TMF Zone 4). Amendment tracking and TMF completeness assessments require linking the amendment record to its filed TMF document. Inspect
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.version. Business justification: Each protocol amendment produces a new document version in the eTMF. Regulatory binders and IRB/IEC submissions require amendment-to-document-version traceability. Role prefix 'amendment_' reflects th
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: A study protocol amendment is by definition an amendment TO a specific protocol. While study_protocol_amendment already has study_id → study_study, it lacks a direct FK to the protocol it amends. This
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Regulatory affairs teams must link each internal study amendment record to its corresponding regulatory submission record in the regulatory domain. This is required for amendment implementation tracki
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent clinical study to which this protocol amendment belongs. Links the amendment to the study master record.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM IG

description: Comma-delimited list or free-text enumeration of protocol section numbers and titles modified by this amendment (e.g., '5.1 Inclusion Criteria, 8.3 Visit Schedule, 10.2 Safety Monitoring'). Enables targeted review by site staff.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6; Veeva Vault eTMF Document Metadata

description: The date on which the amendment was formally authored or finalized by the sponsor. This is the document date, distinct from submission or approval dates.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Detailed narrative describing all changes introduced by this amendment, including modified sections, added or removed procedures, and updated criteria. Serves as the primary change log for regulatory and audit purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Sequential identifier for the amendment within a given protocol (e.g., AM-001, AM-002). Used by sponsors, health authorities, and IRB/IEC to uniquely reference a specific amendment version. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Current lifecycle state of the protocol amendment. Tracks progression from internal drafting through regulatory approval and implementation. 'Superseded' indicates a later amendment has replaced this one.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault Lifecycle States; Oracle Clinical One Amendment Workflow

description: Short descriptive title summarizing the primary purpose or scope of the amendment (e.g., 'Addition of New Safety Monitoring Criteria'). Used in regulatory submissions and internal tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Classification of the amendment per regulatory definitions. 'Substantial' amendments require prior health authority and ethics committee approval before implementation. 'Non-substantial' amendments may be implemented with notification only. 'Administrative' covers clerical corrections with no scientific impact. Aligns with EU CTR 536/2014 and FDA guidance.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; EMA Guidance on Substantial Modifications

description: Name or identifier of the sponsor representative (e.g., Chief Medical Officer, Head of Regulatory Affairs) who provided final internal approval for the amendment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.2; GCP SOP Requirements; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Primary category of change introduced by the amendment. Used for analytics, regulatory reporting, and impact triage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eligibility_criteria|dosing_regimen|visit_schedule|safety_monitoring|endpoint|statistical|icf|administrative|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC Protocol Representation Model; FDA Amendment Classification Guidance

description: Timestamp when this amendment record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Indicates whether this amendment was triggered by a formal recommendation from the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). Requires documentation of the DSMB/DMC meeting minutes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.17; FDA Guidance on Data Monitoring Committees; DSMB Charter

description: Indicates whether the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave) has been updated to reflect the protocol changes introduced by this amendment. Critical for data integrity and eCRF alignment.
reference: CDISC CDASH; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5

description: The date from which the amended protocol becomes operative at clinical sites. Sites must implement protocol changes on or after this date. May differ from approval date if a transition period is granted.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15

description: Date the amendment was submitted to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) or relevant national competent authority under EU CTR 536/2014. Null if no EU sites are involved.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; EMA Guidance on Substantial Modifications

description: Current regulatory submission status with the European Medicines Agency (EMA) or relevant EU national competent authority for this amendment.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 15; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation

description: Date the amendment was submitted to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as an IND amendment. Null if the study is not conducted under a U.S. IND.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; IND Amendment Requirements

description: Current regulatory submission status with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). 'On_clinical_hold' indicates FDA has placed a hold pending resolution of safety concerns.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; FDA IND Regulations

business_glossary_term: Informed Consent Form (ICF) Version Updated Flag
description: Indicates whether this amendment necessitates a new version of the Informed Consent Form (ICF). When true, a new ICF document version must be created and approved before re-consent activities begin.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; Clinpal eConsent System

description: Structured narrative summarizing the assessed impact of the amendment on study operations, subject safety, data integrity, site procedures, and timelines. Produced during the amendment review process.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Protocol Amendments; GCP SOP Requirements

description: The date by which all active clinical sites must have fully implemented the amendment. Used for monitoring follow-up and escalation planning in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; GCP Monitoring Requirements; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the lead or central IRB/IEC granted approval for the amendment. For substantial amendments, implementation cannot proceed until this approval is received.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP Regulations; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of this amendment. Required for GCP compliance at all participating sites.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP Regulations

description: Current review and approval status from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). 'Approved_with_conditions' requires sponsor response before full approval.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date the amendment was submitted to Japan's Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA). Null if no Japan sites are involved.
reference: PMDA Clinical Trial Notification Requirements; ICH E6(R2)

description: Current regulatory submission status with Japan's Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA) for this amendment.
reference: PMDA Clinical Trial Notification Requirements; ICH E6(R2)

description: The protocol version number that was in effect immediately before this amendment was applied. Enables version chain traceability for audit and regulatory inspection purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Scientific, safety, or operational justification for the amendment. Required by health authorities to assess the necessity and appropriateness of protocol changes. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.30; EMA Guidance on Substantial Modifications

description: Indicates whether enrolled subjects must provide updated Informed Consent Form (ICF) signatures due to material changes introduced by this amendment. Drives re-consent workflow in Clinpal eConsent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 50.25; OHRP; GDPR Article 7

description: Indicates whether this amendment was initiated primarily due to a safety signal, Serious Adverse Event (SAE), SUSAR, or DSMB/DMC recommendation. Safety-driven amendments may require expedited regulatory review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.17; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Safety Reporting Guidelines; DSMB Charter Requirements

description: Indicates whether this amendment requires an update to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Changes to primary/secondary endpoints, analysis populations (ITT, PP), or statistical methods trigger SAP revision.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E9(R1) Estimands; CDISC ADaM; SAS Drug Development

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this amendment record was sourced (Oracle Clinical One for study design, Veeva Vault for document management, or manual entry). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Date on which the sponsor's internal governance (e.g., medical, regulatory, and legal sign-off) formally approved the amendment prior to external submission.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.2; GCP SOP Requirements

description: Indicates whether this amendment has a direct impact on currently enrolled subjects (e.g., changes to dosing, visit schedule, eligibility criteria, or safety monitoring). True triggers mandatory re-consent assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; OHRP Guidance on Re-consent

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this amendment record in the lakehouse silver layer. Required for change data capture, audit trail, and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: The document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF to the amendment document. Enables direct cross-reference to the source document management system for audit trail and TMF completeness tracking.
