reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; TMF Reference Model

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each concomitant medication record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; 21 CFR Part 11

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the specific eCRF form instance in Medidata Rave from which this concomitant medication record was sourced. Supports source data verification (SDV) and audit trail traceability per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study under which this concomitant medication record was collected.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reference to the investigational site where the concomitant medication was reported or collected.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM CM Domain

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) for whom this concomitant medication is recorded.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; GDPR Article 4

description: Indicates whether this concomitant medication was initiated or modified in response to an adverse event (AE). Supports causality assessment and safety narrative construction in Oracle Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM CM Domain; Oracle Argus Safety; FDA Safety Reporting

description: Sequential number identifying each concomitant medication record for a given subject within a study, used for SDTM CM domain sequencing (CMSEQ). Enables ordering of multiple medication records per subject.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain v3.3; FDA SDTM Submission Guidelines

description: Free-text comments or notes entered by the investigator, CRC, or data manager regarding this concomitant medication record. May include clarifications on dose changes, interruptions, or special circumstances not captured in structured fields.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Indicates whether the medication was taken concomitantly (during the study treatment period) as opposed to only in the pre-study period. Supports classification of medications as prior vs. concomitant per SDTM CM domain conventions.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Concomitant Medications

description: Sponsor-defined category grouping for the concomitant medication (e.g., PRIOR, CONCOMITANT, PROHIBITED, RESCUE). Corresponds to SDTM CMCAT. Used for protocol-specific medication classification and reporting.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMCAT); ICH E6(R2); Study Protocol

description: The date and time when this concomitant medication record was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC

description: The numeric dose amount of the concomitant medication administered per dose event. Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSE. Must be interpreted in conjunction with dose_unit.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDOSE); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The pharmaceutical form of the concomitant medication (e.g., TABLET, CAPSULE, SOLUTION, PATCH, INJECTION). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSFRM. Supports pharmacokinetic and bioavailability analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDOSFRM); CDISC Controlled Terminology; WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD)

description: The frequency at which the concomitant medication is administered (e.g., QD, BID, TID, QID, PRN, weekly). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSFRQ. Uses CDISC controlled terminology for standardized reporting.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDOSFRQ); CDISC Controlled Terminology

description: The route by which the concomitant medication is administered (e.g., ORAL, INTRAVENOUS, SUBCUTANEOUS, TOPICAL, INHALATION). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSROUTE. Uses CDISC controlled terminology.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDOSROUTE); CDISC Controlled Terminology; FDA Labeling Requirements

description: Unit of measurement for the concomitant medication dose (e.g., mg, mcg, IU, mL). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSU. Required for accurate dose interpretation and cross-study comparisons.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDOSU); CDISC Controlled Terminology; ICH E6(R2)

description: The date on which the subject stopped taking the concomitant medication. Corresponds to SDTM CMENDTC (date portion). Null if medication is ongoing at time of data collection. Used for duration calculations and temporal overlap analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMENDTC); ICH E6(R2); FDA Safety Reporting

description: The clinical reason or medical condition for which the concomitant medication is being taken, as reported by the subject or investigator. Corresponds to SDTM CMINDC. Used for safety and drug-drug interaction analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMINDC); ICH E6(R2); MedDRA

description: The date and time when this concomitant medication record was most recently modified in the source EDC system. Required for audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC

description: Standardized or sponsor-modified version of the verbatim drug name after data cleaning, prior to WHO-DD coding. Corresponds to SDTM CMMODIFY. Used when the verbatim term is modified for coding purposes.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMMODIFY); WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD)

description: Indicates whether the concomitant medication was still ongoing at the time of the last assessment or data collection. Corresponds to SDTM CMONGO. True when end_date is not yet known or medication continues beyond the observation window.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMONGO); ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the medication was taken prior to the start of the study (pre-existing medication) as opposed to being initiated during the study. Corresponds to SDTM CMPRESP/CMOCCUR context. Supports prior therapy analysis in efficacy and safety assessments.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; ICH E3 Clinical Study Report Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this concomitant medication is classified as a prohibited medication per the study protocol. A True value may trigger a protocol deviation review. Critical for protocol compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Study Protocol; CDISC SDTM CM Domain; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: Indicates whether there is at least one open data discrepancy query against this concomitant medication record in the EDC system. Supports data management workflow tracking and database lock (DBL) readiness assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DM Best Practices; Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+

description: Current data management status of the concomitant medication record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Indicates whether the record is active, has been deleted, amended after lock, or locked for database lock (DBL).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Indicates whether this medication was administered as a rescue therapy per the study protocol (e.g., for breakthrough pain, hypoglycemia rescue). Rescue medication use is a key efficacy endpoint in many trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Study Protocol; CDISC SDTM CM Domain; FDA Efficacy Endpoint Guidance

description: The CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. Always 'CM' (Concomitant and Prior Medications) for records in this product. Used for SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission via eCTD.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.3; FDA SDTM Submission Guidelines; EMA Data Standards

description: Current source data verification (SDV) status for this concomitant medication record, as tracked by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) in the CTMS. Indicates whether the data has been verified against source documents.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+; Oracle Siebel CTMS; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: The native record identifier from the source EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave internal record key) for this concomitant medication entry. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC

description: The operational system of record from which this concomitant medication record was extracted (e.g., MEDIDATA_RAVE, ORACLE_CLINICAL_ONE). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Best Practices

description: The date on which the subject began taking the concomitant medication. Corresponds to SDTM CMSTDTC (date portion). Used to determine temporal overlap with investigational product exposure and adverse event onset.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMSTDTC); ICH E6(R2); FDA Safety Reporting

description: The study day on which the concomitant medication was stopped, calculated relative to the reference start date. Corresponds to SDTM CMENDY. Null if medication is ongoing. Used for duration and overlap calculations.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMENDY); CDISC Study Day Calculation Rules; ICH E3

description: The study day on which the concomitant medication was started, calculated relative to the reference start date (Day 1 = first dose of investigational product). Corresponds to SDTM CMSTDY. Enables temporal analysis relative to IP exposure.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMSTDY); CDISC Study Day Calculation Rules; ICH E3

description: Sponsor-defined subcategory for further classification of the concomitant medication within its category (e.g., ANALGESIC, ANTIBIOTIC, ANTIHYPERTENSIVE). Corresponds to SDTM CMSCAT. Enables granular therapeutic class reporting.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMSCAT); Study Protocol; ICH E3

description: The medication name exactly as reported by the subject or investigator on the electronic Case Report Form (eCRF), prior to WHO-DD coding. Corresponds to SDTM CMTRT field. Preserved for source data traceability per ALCOA+ principles.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMTRT); ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) Classification Code
description: The Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code assigned by the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) for the concomitant medication. Enables therapeutic class-level analysis and drug-drug interaction assessment.
reference: WHO ATC Classification System; WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); CDISC SDTM CM Domain

description: The preferred drug name as coded against the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD). Corresponds to SDTM CMDECOD. Enables standardized cross-study drug identification and safety signal detection.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); CDISC SDTM CM Domain (CMDECOD); ICH E2B; FDA Pharmacovigilance

description: Version of the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) used for coding this concomitant medication record. Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coded data.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); CDISC SDTM CM Domain; FDA eCTD Submission Guidelines

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each subject demographic record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the demographic data product.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM Domain

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Baseline demographic data collection and SDV is PI-delegated per GCP. SDTM DM domain submissions and regulatory inspection readiness require identifying the PI responsible for demographic data collect
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: SDTM DM domain requires ARM and ACTARM per subject derived from the canonical treatment_arm definition. arm_code, arm_name, actual_arm_code, actual_arm_name on demographic are denormalized; FK enables
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study for which this demographic record was collected. Links the subject demographics to the parent study context.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.STUDYID

description: Reference to the investigational site where the subject was enrolled and demographic data was collected.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.SITEID; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Demographic data (age, sex, race, ethnicity, height, weight, BMI) is collected at a specific study visit — typically the screening visit or baseline visit. Linking the demographic record to the specif
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the trial subject (participant) whose demographic data is captured in this record. Core linkage to the trial_subject master entity.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.USUBJID

description: Subject's age in whole years at the time of the screening visit, derived from date of birth and screening date. Used for eligibility verification, stratification, and CDISC SDTM DM domain submission.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.AGE; ICH E6(R2)

description: Unit of measure for the age_at_screening field. Typically YEARS for adult studies; MONTHS or WEEKS may be used for pediatric or neonatal studies per CDISC SDTM convention.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.AGEU; ICH E11 Pediatric Studies

description: Subject's year of birth as a four-digit integer. Used when full date of birth is masked for privacy compliance but year-level granularity is required for age-cohort analyses and CDISC partial date handling.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.BRTHDTC (partial date); GDPR Article 9; HIPAA Safe Harbor

description: Subject's body mass index calculated as weight (kg) divided by height squared (m²) at baseline. Stored as a baseline characteristic per CDISC ADaM ADSL convention; used in eligibility assessment and subgroup analyses.
reference: CDISC ADaM ADSL BMIBL; WHO BMI Classification

description: Free-text field for additional notes or clarifications related to the subject's demographic data, such as explanations for partial dates, data corrections, or special circumstances noted by the site or data manager.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; Medidata Rave EDC

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country where the subject was enrolled. Used for geographic subgroup analyses, regulatory reporting by region, and CDISC SDTM DM domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.COUNTRY; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3

description: Timestamp when the demographic record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Current data entry and verification status of the demographic eCRF form in the EDC system. Tracks completeness and readiness for database lock (DBL) and SDTM submission.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave EDC; ICH E6(R2) GCP 5.18

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the demographic data was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements across the lakehouse pipeline.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Plan (DMP)

description: Subject's date of birth in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd). Used to calculate age at key study milestones and to verify age-based eligibility criteria. May be partially masked (year only) per privacy regulations.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.BRTHDTC; GDPR Article 9; HIPAA 45 CFR 164.514

description: Sequential record number within the DM domain for a given subject, per CDISC SDTM convention. Used to uniquely identify each demographic record when multiple records exist per subject (e.g., re-screening scenarios).
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.DMSEQ

description: Source system record key for the subject's demographic record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables traceability from the silver layer back to the source EDC record for SDV and audit purposes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; Medidata Rave EDC

description: The study epoch (e.g., SCREENING, TREATMENT, FOLLOW-UP) during which the demographic data was collected, per CDISC SDTM Trial Epochs dataset. Used for temporal context in multi-epoch study designs.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.EPOCH; CDISC Trial Epochs Dataset

value_regex: HISPANIC OR LATINO|NOT HISPANIC OR LATINO|NOT REPORTED|UNKNOWN
description: Subject's self-reported ethnic origin, coded per FDA and CDISC controlled terminology. Distinguishes Hispanic/Latino origin from non-Hispanic/Latino. Required for FDA demographic subgroup reporting in NDA/BLA submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.ETHNIC; FDA Guidance on Collection of Race and Ethnicity Data; ICH E5

description: Date of the subject's first exposure to the investigational product (IP). Defines the start of the treatment period and is the reference date for study day calculations in CDISC SDTM DM domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RFSTDTC; ICH E6(R2)

description: Subject's self-reported gender identity, distinct from biological sex. Collected per evolving FDA guidance on inclusion of gender identity in clinical trials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MALE|FEMALE|TRANSGENDER MALE|TRANSGENDER FEMALE|NON-BINARY|PREFER NOT TO ANSWER|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Draft Guidance on Diversity Action Plans (2024); CDISC SDTM DM.GENDER

description: Subject's height in centimeters measured at baseline/screening visit. Used for body surface area (BSA) calculations, dose determination, and baseline characteristic tables in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).
reference: CDISC SDTM VS Domain (VSTEST=HEIGHT); CDISC ADaM ADSL HEIGHTBL

description: Date on which the subject signed the informed consent form (ICF) prior to any study-related procedures. Anchors the CDISC SDTM DM.RFICDTC reference date and is required for regulatory compliance and ALCOA+ data integrity.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RFICDTC; ICH E6(R2) GCP 4.8; 21 CFR 50.25; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date of the subject's last exposure to the investigational product (IP). Used to define the end of the treatment period and calculate treatment duration for safety and efficacy analyses.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RFENDTC; ICH E6(R2)

description: Lock status of the demographic eCRF record in the EDC system. LOCKED records cannot be modified without an audit-trailed unlock. FROZEN records are pending final database lock (DBL).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP 5.18; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA DBL Guidance

description: Version of the study protocol in effect at the time the demographic data was collected. Ensures traceability of demographic assessments to the applicable protocol amendment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP 8.2; CDISC SDTM; FDA 21 CFR 312.62

description: Indicates whether one or more open data queries (discrepancies) exist against this demographic record in the EDC system. True = at least one unresolved query; False = no open queries. Used for data cleaning tracking.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP 5.18; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Subject's self-reported racial category, coded per FDA and CDISC controlled terminology (e.g., WHITE, BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN, ASIAN, AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE, NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER, MULTIPLE, UNKNOWN, NOT REPORTED). Required for FDA demographic subgroup analyses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WHITE|BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN|ASIAN|AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE|NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER|MULTIPLE|UNKNOWN|NOT REPORTED — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RACE; FDA Guidance on Collection of Race and Ethnicity Data; 21 CFR 312.33

description: Free-text description of race when the subject selects 'OTHER' or 'MULTIPLE' as their racial category. Captures verbatim self-reported race for completeness in regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RACEOTH; FDA Guidance on Collection of Race and Ethnicity Data

description: The protocol-defined reference start date for the subject, used as the anchor for study day (STUDYDAY) calculations across all SDTM domains. May differ from first_exposure_date in some study designs (e.g., observational studies).
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.RFSTDTC; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

business_glossary_term: CDISC Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Domain
description: CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. Always 'DM' for demographic records. Included to support automated SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission pipelines.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM Domain; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Status of source data verification (SDV) for the demographic record, indicating whether the EDC data has been verified against source documents by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) during site monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; ALCOA+

description: Biological sex of the subject as recorded at screening, coded per CDISC controlled terminology. M=Male, F=Female, U=Unknown, UNDIFFERENTIATED=Intersex or not classifiable. Used in safety analyses, PK/PD modeling, and regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.SEX; CDISC CT SDTM SEX; FDA Guidance on Sex as Biological Variable

description: Site-assigned subject number used to identify the participant within the study site. Typically formatted as a numeric or alphanumeric code assigned at screening or enrollment.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.SUBJID; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Globally unique identifier for the subject across the study, constructed per CDISC SDTM convention (typically STUDYID-SITEID-SUBJID). Used as the primary cross-domain linkage key in SDTM submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM DM.USUBJID; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the demographic record in the silver layer lakehouse. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Subject's body weight in kilograms measured at baseline/screening visit. Used for dose calculations, PK/PD modeling, BMI derivation, and baseline characteristic tables in the CSR.
reference: CDISC SDTM VS Domain (VSTEST=WEIGHT); CDISC ADaM ADSL WEIGHTBL

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each medical history record in the clinical trial system. Primary key for the medical_history data product.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; 21 CFR Part 11

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Medical history is collected during the screening and enrollment process as part of eligibility assessment. Linking medical_history to the specific enrollment record provides the enrollment-episode co
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Medical history collection and SDV is a PI-delegated activity per GCP. Delegation log audits and regulatory inspections require traceability of which PI or sub-investigator collected and verified base
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study under which this medical history record was collected. Links the record to the sponsoring protocol.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reference to the investigational site where the medical history was collected during screening or baseline assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM MH Domain

description: Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Medical history is collected during screening visits. medical_history carries a denormalized screening_visit_number (STRING) that should be normalized via FK to subject_visit. Adding subject_visit_id
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose medical history is recorded. Core linkage to the trial_subject master record.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA

description: Indicates whether this medical history condition has been associated with or contributed to an adverse event (AE) reported during the study. Supports integrated safety analysis.
reference: ICH E2A; CDISC SDTM AE Domain; FDA Safety Reporting; EMA Pharmacovigilance

description: The date on which the medical history was collected or assessed by the investigator or CRC during the screening visit. Corresponds to the eCRF data entry date for this record.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: The date on which the verbatim medical history condition was coded to a MedDRA term. Supports audit trail requirements for medical coding activities.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; MedDRA; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Current status of the MedDRA coding process for this medical history condition. Tracks whether the verbatim term has been coded, auto-coded, reviewed, or approved by the medical coder.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; MedDRA; FDA Data Standards

description: Free-text comments or additional clinical context provided by the investigator regarding this medical history condition. May include relevant clinical details not captured in structured fields.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM MHCOMM; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether the subject is receiving concomitant medication for this medical history condition at the time of screening. Supports concomitant medication reconciliation and safety analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Concomitant Medications

description: High-level category classifying the type of medical history entry. Corresponds to SDTM MHCAT variable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MEDICAL HISTORY|SURGICAL HISTORY|FAMILY HISTORY|PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY|ALLERGY|OBSTETRIC HISTORY — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM MHCAT; ICH E6(R2)

description: Current clinical status of the pre-existing medical condition at the time of data collection. Supports safety profiling and eligibility determination.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Optional secondary classification of the medical history condition providing further granularity within the category (e.g., CARDIOVASCULAR within MEDICAL HISTORY). Corresponds to SDTM MHSCAT.
reference: CDISC SDTM MHSCAT; ICH E6(R2)

description: The verbatim (as-reported) text of the pre-existing medical condition or medical history event as entered by the investigator or CRC in the eCRF. This is the raw, uncoded term prior to MedDRA coding.
reference: CDISC SDTM MHTERM; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: The timestamp when this medical history record was first created in the EDC system or lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: The unique record identifier assigned by the EDC system (Medidata Rave) for this medical history entry. Used for source data verification (SDV) and data reconciliation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; Medidata Rave

description: Indicates whether this medical history condition is relevant to the study's inclusion/exclusion eligibility criteria. Used by the medical monitor and data management team to assess protocol compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR 312; CDISC SDTM MH Domain

description: Data lock status of this medical history record in the EDC system. Locked records cannot be modified without an authorized unlock procedure, supporting database lock (DBL) integrity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM; ALCOA+

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Lowest Level Term
description: The MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) that most closely maps to the verbatim condition text. Provides the most granular level of MedDRA coding hierarchy.
reference: MedDRA; CDISC SDTM; ICH M1

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term
description: The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) assigned to the verbatim medical history condition during medical coding. Used for standardized regulatory reporting and safety analysis.
reference: MedDRA; CDISC SDTM MHDECOD; ICH M1; FDA; EMA

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class
description: The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) associated with the coded medical history condition. Used for aggregate safety reporting and tabulation by body system.
reference: MedDRA; CDISC SDTM MHSOC; ICH M1; FDA; EMA

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Version
description: The version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this medical history condition (e.g., 26.0). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding.
reference: MedDRA; CDISC SDTM MHVERSION; ICH M1; FDA eCTD

description: Sequential number identifying each medical history record for a given subject within a study. Corresponds to the SDTM MHSEQ variable used in regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain v3.3; FDA eCTD

description: Indicates whether the medical history condition was ongoing at the time of the subject's screening or baseline assessment. True = condition is still active; False = condition has resolved. Corresponds to SDTM MHENRF or MHONGO.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E6(R2)

description: The date on which the pre-existing medical condition first began or was first diagnosed. May be partial (year only or year-month) per CDISC partial date conventions. Corresponds to SDTM MHSTDTC.
reference: CDISC SDTM MHSTDTC; ICH E6(R2); HIPAA; GDPR

description: Indicates whether the condition was present prior to the subject's first dose of investigational product. Distinguishes pre-existing conditions from treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) in safety analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM MH Domain; ICH E2A; FDA Safety Reporting Guidance; CTCAE

description: The version of the study protocol in effect at the time this medical history record was collected. Ensures traceability of data to the applicable protocol amendment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR 312; CDISC SDTM

description: Indicates whether there is at least one open data query (discrepancy) against this medical history record in the EDC system. Supports data cleaning and database lock readiness tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: The date on which the pre-existing medical condition resolved or ended. Null if the condition is ongoing at the time of assessment. Corresponds to SDTM MHENDTC.
reference: CDISC SDTM MHENDTC; ICH E6(R2)

description: The CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. For medical history records this is always 'MH'. Used during SDTM dataset generation for regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Data Standards Catalog; EMA Data Standards

description: The date on which source data verification was completed for this medical history record by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance; ALCOA+

description: Status of source data verification for this medical history record. Indicates whether the CRA has verified the eCRF data against source documents at the investigational site.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; ALCOA+

description: Clinical severity grading of the pre-existing medical condition as assessed by the investigator. Supports safety profiling and eligibility assessment.
reference: CTCAE; CDISC SDTM MHSEV; ICH E6(R2)

description: The operational system of record from which this medical history record was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: The native primary key or record identifier of this medical history entry in the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave record OID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse and the EDC.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: The timestamp when this medical history record was last modified in the EDC system or lakehouse. Required for audit trail compliance and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the subject contact record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reference to the investigational site where the subject is enrolled. Used to scope contact data to the correct site for monitoring and follow-up.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this subject is enrolled. Scopes the contact record to a specific trial.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose contact information is stored in this record. Links to the subject master entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG

description: First line of the subject's home address (street number and street name). Used for postal communications, visit logistics, and safety follow-up mailings.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Second line of the subject's home address (apartment, suite, unit, floor, or building name). Supplements address_line1 for complete postal address.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the subject's home address was last verified by site staff. Supports ALCOA+ data quality requirements and ensures contact information is current for safety follow-up.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Indicates whether the subject's home address has been verified by site staff or through an address validation service. True = address confirmed; False = address unverified or pending confirmation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; ALCOA+ principles

description: City or municipality of the subject's home address. Used for geographic analysis of subject distribution and postal communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the subject granted consent for contact communications. Required for GDPR and HIPAA audit trails to demonstrate lawful basis for processing contact data.
reference: GDPR Article 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinpal

description: Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit consent to be contacted for study-related communications (visit reminders, follow-up calls). True = consent granted; False = consent not granted or withdrawn. Governed by GDPR and HIPAA privacy requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 6 & 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinpal

description: Date on which the subject withdrew consent for contact communications. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Once populated, all non-safety-critical outreach must cease per GDPR right to withdraw.
reference: GDPR Article 7 & 17; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Current lifecycle status of the subject contact record. 'active' indicates the contact details are current and in use; 'inactive' indicates superseded or no longer valid; 'withdrawn' indicates the subject has withdrawn consent for contact; 'pending_verification' indicates details awaiting confirmation; 'deceased' indicates the subject is deceased and contact is no longer applicable.
reference: GDPR Article 17; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.526; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the subject's home address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines applicable data privacy regulations (GDPR, HIPAA) and communication rules.
reference: ISO 3166-1; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Timestamp when this subject contact record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Hard stop flag indicating the subject must not be contacted under any circumstances (excluding mandatory safety communications). True = do not contact; False = contact permitted per consent settings. Overrides all other contact preferences.
reference: GDPR Article 17 & 21; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.522; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date from which this contact record is considered valid and active. Supports temporal versioning of contact information to maintain a history of address and phone changes over the course of a long-running trial.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5(1)(d) accuracy principle

description: Date on which this contact record ceased to be valid (e.g., subject moved, changed phone number). Null for the currently active record. Enables Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 history tracking of contact information.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5(1)(d) accuracy principle

description: Primary email address for the subject. Used for electronic communications including visit reminders, eConsent notifications, and patient engagement via Clinpal.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; 21 CFR Part 11; Clinpal

description: Indicates whether the subject has specifically consented to receive communications via email. True = email contact permitted; False = email contact not permitted. Distinct from general contact consent as email may carry additional regulatory requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 6 & 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; CAN-SPAM Act; Clinpal

description: Full name of the subject's designated emergency contact person. Used by site staff to reach a responsible party in the event of a serious adverse event (SAE) or medical emergency.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; GDPR Article 9

description: Phone number for the subject's emergency contact. Used exclusively for safety-related communications and serious adverse event (SAE) follow-up.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; GDPR Article 9

description: Relationship of the emergency contact person to the subject (e.g., spouse, parent, legal guardian). Provides context for site staff when initiating emergency communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|parent|child|sibling|friend|caregiver|legal_guardian|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50

description: Date of the most recent successful contact with the subject. Used to track subject engagement, identify subjects at risk of dropout, and support visit compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; CTMS KPI reporting

description: Communication channel used during the most recent successful contact with the subject. Supports analysis of effective outreach strategies and visit compliance improvement.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Free-text notes recorded by site staff regarding the subject's contact preferences, special communication instructions, or relevant context (e.g., best time to call, language interpreter required). Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive subject information.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Postal or ZIP code of the subject's home address. Used for geographic analysis, site feasibility, and postal communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification

description: Subject's stated preference for how they wish to be contacted by the site or sponsor. Governs outreach channel selection for visit reminders and safety follow-up. 'no_contact' indicates the subject has requested no proactive outreach.
reference: GDPR Article 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.522; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinpal

description: Subject's preferred time of day to be contacted. Supports scheduling of outreach calls and reminders to maximize response rates and respect subject preferences.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinpal; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: ISO 639-1/639-2 language code representing the subject's preferred language for communications (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'de', 'zh-CN'). Ensures communications are delivered in the subject's native language to support informed participation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014; GDPR Article 7; Clinpal

description: Primary telephone number for contacting the subject. Used for visit reminders, recruitment follow-up, and safety follow-up communications. Stored in international format where possible.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Classification of the primary phone number (e.g., mobile, home, work). Informs the appropriate time and method for outreach.
reference: Clinpal; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Classification of the contact record indicating the role of the contact in relation to the subject. 'primary' is the subject themselves; 'secondary' is an alternate contact; 'emergency' is for emergency use only; 'caregiver' is a designated caregiver; 'legal_representative' is a legally authorized representative.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether safety-critical communications (e.g., SAE follow-up, urgent safety notifications) are permitted even when do_not_contact_flag is True. True = safety contact allowed regardless of general consent; False = all contact restricted. Supports regulatory obligation to notify subjects of safety findings.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.11; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 41

description: Indicates whether the subject has specifically consented to receive communications via SMS/text message. True = SMS contact permitted; False = SMS contact not permitted.
reference: GDPR Article 6 & 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); Clinpal

description: Operational system of record from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between Clinpal (eConsent/patient engagement) and Oracle Siebel CTMS (clinical trial management).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Natural key or unique identifier of this contact record in the originating source system (Clinpal or Oracle Siebel CTMS). Enables bidirectional traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: State, province, or region of the subject's home address. Supports regulatory reporting and geographic stratification of subject populations.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this subject contact record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Tracks when contact details were last updated by site staff.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a trial subject record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the trial_subject entity. All subject-level transactional records in the subject domain reference this identifier.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

True = subject data locked; False = data still open for entry or query resolution. Critical for CSR preparation and regulatory submission readiness.

description: Date on which the subject was formally enrolled in the study following confirmation of eligibility criteria. Distinct from screening date and randomization date. Used for enrollment tracking, site performance KPIs, and study timeline reporting.

description: Indicates whether the subject is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Subjects may be excluded from PP due to major protocol deviations, insufficient drug exposure, or missing primary endpoint data.

description: Indicates whether the subject is included in the safety analysis population (typically all randomized subjects who received at least one dose of investigational product). Used for adverse event incidence tables and safety reporting.

description: Reason why a screened subject did not proceed to enrollment, coded per protocol-defined eligibility criteria categories (e.g., INCLUSION CRITERIA NOT MET, EXCLUSION CRITERIA MET, SUBJECT WITHDREW CONSENT, INVESTIGATOR DECISION). reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) who performed or missed this visit. Core party reference for the transaction.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; GDPR

description: The actual calendar date on which the subject attended the visit and assessments were performed. Maps to SVSTDTC in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Null if the visit was missed or not yet conducted. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.SVSTDTC; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The actual calendar date on which the visit concluded, applicable for multi-day visits or inpatient stays. Maps to SVENDTC in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Null for single-day visits.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.SVENDTC; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Timestamp when this subject visit record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Status of eCRF data entry for this visit in the EDC system (Medidata Rave), indicating whether data collection has been initiated, is in progress, completed, locked, or frozen. Used for data management tracking and database lock readiness assessment.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC CDASH; Medidata Rave EDC; ALCOA+

description: Signed integer representing the deviation in days between the actual visit date and the planned visit date (actual minus planned). Negative values indicate early visits; positive values indicate late visits. Zero indicates on-schedule. Used for visit compliance analytics and protocol deviation assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM SV Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Total duration of the visit in minutes from check-in to check-out as recorded in the CTMS or EDC. Used for site capacity planning, resource utilization analysis, and subject burden assessment.
reference: Oracle Siebel CTMS; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reason for early termination of the visit or the subject's participation, applicable when visit_type is 'early_termination'. Captures clinical, safety, or administrative reasons per protocol requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM DS Domain

description: Indicates whether a valid, signed Informed Consent Form (ICF) was confirmed on file prior to this visit (True) or not (False). Critical GCP compliance requirement; absence may constitute a major protocol deviation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP

description: Indicates whether this visit was a protocol-scheduled visit (True) or an unscheduled/ad-hoc visit not defined in the protocol schedule of assessments (False). Unscheduled visits may indicate safety events or protocol deviations.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the treatment assignment was unblinded at or prior to this visit (True) or the subject remained blinded (False). Relevant for double-blind studies and impacts data integrity assessments.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Free-text or coded reason explaining why a scheduled visit was missed by the subject (e.g., subject unavailable, adverse event, travel, withdrew consent, site closure). Populated only when visit_status is 'missed'. Required for protocol deviation assessment and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM SV Domain

description: Standardized coded value for the reason a scheduled visit was missed, aligned with sponsor or protocol-defined coding scheme. Complements missed_reason free text for structured analytics and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM SV Domain; MedDRA

description: Free-text clinical or operational notes recorded by the site staff or CRA regarding this visit, including observations about subject condition, procedural issues, or site-specific circumstances. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS or Medidata Rave.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Medidata Rave EDC

description: The target date on which the visit was scheduled to occur per the protocol-defined visit schedule, calculated from the subject's reference date (e.g., randomization date or first dose date). Used to compute visit window compliance.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.SVSTDTC; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether this visit resulted in a protocol deviation (True), such as a visit outside the allowed window, missed required assessments, or procedural non-compliance. Triggers downstream protocol deviation record creation in the compliance domain.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Number of open data discrepancy queries (DCFs) associated with this visit's eCRF data in the EDC system. Used for data quality monitoring and site performance tracking. Non-zero values may block database lock.
reference: ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC CDASH

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this visit record was sourced (Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Siebel CTMS, Oracle Clinical One, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; CDISC SDTM SV Domain

description: The native primary key or unique identifier for this visit record in the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave visit OID, Siebel CTMS visit row ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; CDISC SDTM SV Domain

description: The relative study day on which the visit occurred, calculated from the subject's reference start date per CDISC convention (Day 1 = reference date; Day -1 = day before; no Day 0). Maps to SVSTDY in CDISC SDTM SV domain.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.SVSTDY; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this subject visit record in the source system or Silver layer. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Protocol-defined alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the visit within the study schedule (e.g., SCR, BL, V01, V02, ET, EOS). Maps to VISITNUM/VISIT in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Used as the externally-known business identifier for the visit.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.VISIT; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Sequential numeric identifier for the visit as defined in the study protocol schedule of assessments. Maps directly to VISITNUM in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Used for ordering visits chronologically within a subject's participation.
reference: CDISC SDTM SV.VISITNUM; ICH E6(R2)

description: Current completion status of the visit indicating whether the subject attended and completed all required assessments, missed the visit entirely, partially completed assessments, had the visit rescheduled, or the visit was cancelled. Core lifecycle status field for monitoring trigger logic.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Classification of the visit by its role in the study protocol lifecycle. Drives SDTM SV domain population and visit compliance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|unscheduled|early_termination|end_of_study — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM SV Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: The nominal study week associated with this visit as defined in the protocol schedule of assessments (e.g., Week 1, Week 4, Week 12). Used for time-point labeling in biostatistical analyses and ADaM dataset derivations.
reference: CDISC ADaM; CDISC SDTM SV Domain; ICH E9

description: The latest acceptable date for the visit to occur per the protocol-defined visit window (planned date plus the allowed late deviation in days). Used to determine visit window compliance status.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM SV Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the actual visit date fell within the protocol-defined visit window, occurred early (before window open), occurred late (after window close), was missed entirely, or is not applicable (e.g., unscheduled visits). Critical for protocol deviation identification and monitoring trigger logic.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM SV Domain

description: The earliest acceptable date for the visit to occur per the protocol-defined visit window (planned date minus the allowed early deviation in days). Used to determine visit window compliance status.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM SV Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); ISO 14155

description: Net quantity adjustment applied to the reconciliation (positive or negative) to account for documented corrections such as data entry errors, kit relabeling, or sponsor-approved adjustments. Zero if no adjustments were made.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual notes entered by the CRA, site staff, or data manager regarding this reconciliation. Supplements the discrepancy_detail field for general narrative.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles

description: The date on which the CRA / monitor signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation. Null if cra_signoff_flag is False.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.18.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.68; 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: Clinical Research Associate (CRA) Monitor Sign-Off Flag
description: Indicates whether the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) / monitor has reviewed and signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation record. True = signed; False = pending. Required at every monitoring visit per ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.18.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.68; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date and time when this drug accountability reconciliation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Total quantity of IMP units destroyed at the site or returned to depot for destruction during the reconciliation period. Destruction must be documented per sponsor SOP and applicable regulations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); EU CTR 536/2014

description: Number of individual discrepancy line items identified during this reconciliation (e.g., missing kit, unaccounted return, dispensing record mismatch). Distinct from discrepancy_units which measures total quantity variance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Free-text narrative describing the nature, root cause, and resolution status of identified discrepancies. Captured by the CRA or site staff. Required when discrepancy_count > 0. Supports regulatory inspection readiness and CAPA documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Current resolution status of identified discrepancies. 'not_applicable' = no discrepancy found; 'open' = discrepancy identified, investigation not started; 'under_investigation' = root cause analysis in progress; 'resolved' = discrepancy explained and documented; 'escalated' = referred to sponsor QA or regulatory authority.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; MasterControl CAPA

description: Quantity difference between expected_closing_balance_units and actual_closing_balance_units (actual minus expected). A non-zero value triggers discrepancy investigation. Positive = surplus; negative = shortage.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Total quantity of IMP units dispensed to subjects at the site during the reconciliation period. Sourced from dispensing records in Medidata Rave RTSM and eCRF dispensing logs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Calculated expected closing balance: opening_balance_units + received_units - dispensed_units + returned_units - destroyed_units + adjusted_units. Used as the reference value against which the physical count is compared to identify discrepancies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a)

description: Number of IMP units that had expired as of the reconciliation period end date. Expired units must be quarantined and destroyed per sponsor SOP; their presence in usable inventory constitutes a protocol deviation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.13.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Earliest IMP Expiry Date on Site
description: The earliest expiry date among all IMP units currently on hand at the site as of the reconciliation period end date. Used to flag impending expiry risk and trigger resupply or destruction actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.13.3; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: Indicates whether this reconciliation record is fully compliant with GCP §8.3.13 requirements (both investigator and CRA sign-offs obtained, no unresolved discrepancies, all required fields populated). True = compliant; False = non-compliant or pending.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether this reconciliation record and its supporting documentation are complete and ready for regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.). True = inspection-ready; False = documentation gaps exist. Supports eTMF completeness tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.68; EU CTR 536/2014; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation. Null if investigator_signoff_flag is False.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §4.6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator has reviewed and signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation record. True = signed; False = pending. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §4.6.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The scheduled due date for the next drug accountability reconciliation at this site for this IMP. Derived from the monitoring visit schedule or protocol-defined reconciliation frequency. Used for proactive compliance tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.18.4; Oracle Siebel CTMS Visit Tracking

description: The quantity of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, kits) on hand at the site at the start of the reconciliation period. Carried forward from the closing balance of the prior reconciliation period or from the initial receipt if this is the first period.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: The last calendar date of the reconciliation period covered by this record. Defines the closing boundary for all IMP transactions included in this reconciliation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a)

description: The first calendar date of the reconciliation period covered by this record. Together with period_end_date, defines the window of IMP activity (receipts, dispensing, returns, destruction) being reconciled.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a)

description: Indicates whether a protocol deviation was identified during this reconciliation (e.g., expired IMP dispensed, incorrect kit dispensed, dispensing outside visit window). True = deviation identified; False = no deviation. Triggers deviation reporting workflow.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §4.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; MasterControl CAPA

description: Total quantity of IMP units received at the site from the depot or sponsor during the reconciliation period. Sourced from shipment receipt records in Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: The date on which the drug accountability reconciliation was physically performed (i.e., the date of the physical count and record review). This is the principal business event date for this transaction, distinct from sign-off dates.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this reconciliation record, typically generated by the RTSM or CTMS (e.g., RECON-SITE001-20240315). Used in monitoring reports, TMF filings, and regulatory inspection packages. Maps to the accountability record reference in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Current workflow status of the reconciliation record. 'open' = in progress; 'reconciled' = balances confirmed with no discrepancy; 'discrepancy_flagged' = unresolved quantity mismatch requiring investigation; 'pending_review' = awaiting CRA or investigator sign-off; 'closed' = finalized and archived post-DBL.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Classification of the reconciliation trigger. 'routine_monitoring' = performed at a scheduled CRA monitoring visit; 'pre_dbl' = required prior to Database Lock (DBL); 'site_closeout' = performed at study close-out; 'interim' = ad hoc between visits; 'triggered' = initiated due to a discrepancy or audit finding.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.18.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Total quantity of IMP units returned from subjects to the site (unused or partially used) during the reconciliation period. Includes returns captured in Medidata Rave RTSM return logs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62(a); CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: The reconciliation reference or transaction identifier assigned by the Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) system (Medidata Rave) for this accountability event. Enables traceability back to the source system record.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Reference identifier for the physical or electronic source document (e.g., drug accountability log, dispensing record binder, eTMF artifact ID in Veeva Vault) that supports this reconciliation record. Required for SDV and regulatory inspection.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §1.52; ALCOA+ principles; Veeva Vault eTMF; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Indicates whether any cold-chain temperature excursion was recorded for IMP stored at this site during the reconciliation period. True = at least one excursion event occurred; False = no excursion. Excursions may affect IMP usability and require sponsor assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP §5.13.2; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: The unit in which IMP quantities are counted and reconciled (e.g., kit, tablet, vial). Must be consistent with the dispensing unit defined in the kit definition and RTSM configuration. description: The date and time when this drug accountability reconciliation record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Tracks the last state change in the reconciliation lifecycle.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP §8.3.13; ALCOA+ principles

description: Reference number or document identifier for the sponsor or depot authorization to destroy the returned IMP. Links to the destruction certificate in the eTMF (Veeva Vault). Required before any IMP destruction can proceed.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.14.4; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date on which the returned IMP was physically destroyed per the authorized destruction method. Completes the drug accountability lifecycle for the returned units.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.14.4; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Method used to destroy the returned IMP, as specified in the protocol or sponsor instructions. Must comply with local environmental and pharmaceutical waste regulations.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EPA pharmaceutical waste regulations

description: Free-text explanation provided by the site or CRA to account for any quantity discrepancy between dispensed and returned IMP. Required for regulatory audit trail when discrepancy_quantity is non-zero.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Numeric difference between the expected return quantity (dispensed minus used) and the actual quantity returned. A non-zero value triggers a drug accountability discrepancy investigation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Unit of measure for the returned IMP (e.g., tablet, vial, syringe). Required for accurate drug accountability reconciliation across different dosage forms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|syringe|patch|sachet|ampoule — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Expiration date of the returned IMP as printed on the label. Used to confirm whether the product was within shelf life at the time of dispensing and return.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137; ICH Q1A

description: The unique RTSM-assigned kit number or barcode identifier for the specific IMP kit being returned. Enables unit-level drug accountability traceability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM EX Domain; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Number of dispensing units originally dispensed to the subject or site for the associated kit. Stored on the return record to enable direct accountability reconciliation without joining to dispensing records.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Number of dispensing units (tablets, vials, syringes, etc.) returned. Used in drug accountability reconciliation to verify compliance with dispensing records.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Number of dispensing units confirmed as consumed by the subject, calculated or reported at the time of return. Supports compliance and adherence analysis.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Date on which the returned IMP or kit was physically received at the destination (site or depot). Used to calculate transit time and confirm chain of custody.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13.3; GDP Guidelines

description: Date on which the drug accountability reconciliation for this return was completed and confirmed by the responsible party (CRA or site staff).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Status of the drug accountability reconciliation process for this return, tracking whether dispensed vs. returned quantities have been verified and any discrepancies resolved.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Physical condition of the IMP or kit at the time of return, as assessed by the site or depot receiving staff. Critical for determining disposition (destruction vs. re-use) and identifying cold-chain or handling failures.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; GDP Guidelines; EU GMP Annex 13

description: The actual calendar date on which the IMP or kit was physically returned by the subject to the site, or dispatched from the site to the depot. Principal business event date for drug accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this return transaction, typically generated by the RTSM (Medidata Rave) or CTMS. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory audit.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.3

description: Narrative or coded reason for the return, such as study completion, subject withdrawal, protocol deviation, product expiry, or damaged packaging. Supports root cause analysis and supply planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM EX Domain

description: Current lifecycle state of the return record, tracking progression from initiation through receipt, reconciliation, and final destruction or disposition.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the return event indicating the reason and direction of the return: subject returning unused IMP to site, site returning stock to depot, expired stock return, damaged stock return, or return triggered by a protocol deviation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.6.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

business_glossary_term: Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) Return ID
description: Source system identifier for this return transaction as recorded in the RTSM (Medidata Rave). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational RTSM system.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Courier or logistics provider tracking number for the return shipment from site to depot. Enables cold-chain monitoring and chain-of-custody verification during transit.
reference: GDP Guidelines; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13

description: Indicates whether the sponsor has been formally notified of this return event, particularly relevant for returns involving temperature excursions, damaged product, or significant quantity discrepancies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.13; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether a cold-chain temperature excursion was recorded or reported for the returned IMP during storage or transit (True = excursion occurred). Triggers quality investigation and potential destruction.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; GDP Guidelines; ICH Q1A

description: System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this return record. Required for audit trail integrity and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Date on which the CRA or pharmacist completed Source Data Verification (SDV) of the return record against source documents at the site or depot.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Name or identifier of the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or pharmacist who verified the return quantities and condition during a monitoring visit or depot check. Supports Source Data Verification (SDV).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the clinical supply depot. Primary key for the depot master record.
reference: ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Depots serving as IMP import/distribution nodes must be authorized under the CTA for the relevant country. CTA-to-depot traceability is required for regulatory inspection readiness and to confirm that
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
