reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary street address of the depot facility. Used for logistics routing, regulatory filings, and cold-chain shipment documentation.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Secondary address detail (suite, building, floor) for the depot facility.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ICH Q10

description: Total ambient temperature storage capacity of the depot measured in cubic metres. Ambient storage is typically 15–25°C. Used for supply planning and capacity allocation across studies.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; EU GMP Annex 13; USP <1079>

description: City in which the depot facility is located. Used for geographic distribution planning and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Indicates whether the depot has been formally qualified for cold-chain IMP handling, including validated temperature monitoring, qualified packaging, and trained personnel per WHO and GDP guidelines.
reference: WHO TRS 961 Annex 9; EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; ICH Q1A

description: Indicates whether the depot is licensed to store and handle controlled substances (narcotics, psychotropics) as defined by DEA Schedule (US), Home Office (UK), or equivalent national authority. Required for studies involving opioids, CNS agents, or other scheduled compounds.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; UK Misuse of Drugs Regulations 2001; UN Convention on Psychotropic Substances

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the depot is located. Drives regulatory jurisdiction, import/export rules, and cold-chain routing logic.
reference: ISO 3166-1; ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Timestamp when the depot master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Depot ID
description: Depot identifier as recorded in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the supply chain data product and clinical trial management records.
reference: Oracle Siebel CTMS; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the depot was or is scheduled to be decommissioned and removed from the active clinical supply network. Null for currently active depots.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the depot within the clinical supply network. Used in RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) and CTMS for depot referencing across studies.
reference: CDISC CDASH; ICH Q10

description: Full legal or operational name of the clinical supply depot as registered with regulatory authorities and used in the Trial Master File (TMF).
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the depot. Active depots are approved for IMP storage and distribution. Suspended depots are temporarily halted pending investigation or re-qualification. Decommissioned depots are permanently closed.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: Classification of the depot within the clinical supply logistics network. Central depots serve as primary distribution hubs; regional depots serve geographic clusters; local depots are near investigational sites; satellite depots are temporary; returns depots handle IMP returns and destruction.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Indicates whether the depot is authorized to perform on-site destruction of expired, returned, or unusable IMPs in compliance with GMP and environmental regulations.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EPA Hazardous Waste Regulations

description: Vendor or supplier identifier for the depot as registered in SAP S/4HANA. Used for purchase order processing, invoice matching, and financial reconciliation of depot service fees.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; SOX Section 404; ICH Q10

description: Total frozen storage capacity (typically -20°C or -80°C) of the depot in cubic metres. Required for biologics, gene therapies, and other temperature-sensitive IMPs.
reference: ICH Q1A; EU GMP Annex 13; USP <1079>; WHO TRS 961 Annex 9

business_glossary_term: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Certificate Expiry Date
description: Date on which the depot's GMP certificate expires. Triggers re-qualification workflows and may suspend depot operations if not renewed prior to expiry.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211; EMA

description: Official certificate number issued by the regulatory authority confirming GMP compliance for the depot. Referenced in regulatory submissions and audit documentation.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; EMA; MHRA; TGA

description: Indicates whether the depot holds a current Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification as required for IMP storage and handling under ICH Q7/Q10 and applicable regional regulations.
reference: ICH Q7; ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211; EMA GMP Guidelines

description: Expiry date of the depot's import license for IMP receipt. Expired licenses block inbound international shipments.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA; PMDA

description: Regulatory import license number permitting the depot to receive IMP shipments from international origins. Required for cross-border clinical supply operations.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA IND Regulations; PMDA; Health Canada

description: Date of the most recent GMP/GDP audit conducted at the depot by the sponsor, CRO, or regulatory authority. Audit outcomes are tracked in the quality management system (MasterControl).
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: Date on which the depot last completed a formal qualification or re-qualification exercise (IQ/OQ/PQ). Qualification records are maintained in the eTMF and are subject to regulatory inspection.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 15; 21 CFR Part 211.68; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Scheduled date for the next GMP/GDP audit at the depot. Used for audit planning and compliance calendar management.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Scheduled date for the next depot qualification or re-qualification. Drives preventive maintenance and compliance calendar in the CTMS and quality management system.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 15; 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or depot-specific constraints relevant to clinical supply operations. Not intended for regulatory documentation.
reference: ICH Q10

description: Date on which the depot became operationally active for clinical supply activities. Used for lifecycle tracking and regulatory documentation.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Postal or ZIP code of the depot facility. Used for logistics routing and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.
reference: ICH Q10

description: Email address of the primary operational contact at the depot. Used for shipment alerts, RTSM notifications, and supply chain communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ICH Q10

description: Full name of the primary operational contact person at the depot. Used for logistics coordination, shipment notifications, and regulatory correspondence.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Primary telephone number for the depot operational contact. Used for urgent logistics coordination and cold-chain incident escalation.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ICH Q10

description: Total refrigerated storage capacity (typically 2–8°C) of the depot in cubic metres. Critical for IMPs requiring cold-chain management.
reference: ICH Q1A; EU GMP Annex 13; USP <1079>; WHO TRS 961 Annex 9

description: Indicates whether the depot is authorized to receive returned Investigational Medicinal Products (IMPs) from investigational sites. Required for accountability reconciliation and destruction workflows.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Depot identifier as configured in the RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM). Used to link depot master records to RTSM dispensing, randomization kit assignment, and resupply trigger logic.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: State, province, or administrative region where the depot is located. Relevant for import/export licensing and regional regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Name or model of the continuous temperature monitoring system installed at the depot (e.g., Sensitech TempTale, Berlinger ELPRO). Ensures 21 CFR Part 11 compliant electronic records of storage conditions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU GDP Guidelines; WHO TRS 961 Annex 9; ICH Q1A

description: IANA time zone identifier for the depot location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling shipment windows, expiry checks, and operational hours alignment.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ICH Q10

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the depot master record. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: License number authorizing the depot to distribute investigational medicinal products (IMPs) as a wholesale distributor. Required under EU GDP guidelines and equivalent national regulations.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; MHRA; Health Canada; TGA; 21 CFR Part 205

description: Expiry date of the wholesale distribution license. Depot cannot legally distribute IMPs after this date without renewal.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; MHRA; 21 CFR Part 205

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each depot inventory record. Primary key for the depot_inventory data product in the supply domain Silver layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Depot inventory records are scoped to a CTA for the country. Import permit numbers on depot inventory tie back to CTA-authorized shipments; regulatory inspectors verify depot inventory against the CTA
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the depot (central or regional warehouse) where this inventory is physically held. Links to the depot master record.

reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH Q7; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the kit definition record specifying the kit type, dosage form, and packaging configuration for this inventory. Supports RTSM-driven kit-level tracking.
reference: CDISC SDTM; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave RTSM

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Status of the drug accountability reconciliation process for this inventory record. Reconciliation confirms that all dispensed, returned, and destroyed quantities account for the total received, as required by GCP and regulatory authorities.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62; FDA Guidance on IND Safety Reporting

description: Manufacturer-assigned batch or lot number for the IMP stock held in this inventory record. Critical for GMP traceability, recall management, and regulatory accountability.

description: Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this depot inventory record as assigned by the RTSM or CTMS system (e.g., INV-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.

description: Indicates whether this IMP batch is classified as a controlled substance requiring enhanced security, dual-custody handling, and DEA/national narcotics authority reporting for inventory accountability.

description: Classification of the kit contents held in this inventory record. Distinguishes IMP, comparator, placebo, rescue medication, ancillary supplies, and open-label kits for supply planning and accountability.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM EX domain

description: Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was conducted at the depot for this batch/kit type. Supports GMP compliance scheduling and audit readiness.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.192; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this depot inventory record, whether from an RTSM transaction, physical count, or manual adjustment. Supports audit trail and data lineage per ALCOA+ principles.

description: Date on which the IMP batch was manufactured. Used alongside expiry date to calculate remaining shelf life and support GMP batch traceability.

description: Minimum quantity of available stock that must be maintained at this depot for this IMP/kit type before a replenishment order is triggered. Supports proactive supply planning and prevents stockouts.

description: Date on which the Qualified Person formally released this batch for clinical use. Null if release is pending or the batch has been rejected.

description: Number of kits or units reserved for pending outbound shipments or site replenishment orders that have been confirmed but not yet physically dispatched. Prevents double-allocation in RTSM.

description: Number of kits or units that have passed their expiry date and are no longer eligible for dispensing. Tracked separately to support destruction reconciliation and regulatory accountability reporting.

description: Actual kit or unit count recorded during the most recent physical inventory count at the depot. Used to reconcile system-recorded quantities against physical stock and identify discrepancies.

description: Total number of kits or units of this IMP batch currently physically present at the depot, regardless of allocation status. The gross stock figure before netting allocated, quarantined, or expired quantities.

description: Number of kits or units placed under quarantine status due to quality investigations, temperature excursions, damaged packaging, or pending QP release. Quarantined stock is excluded from available supply.

reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.192; ICH Q10; MasterControl CAPA

description: Date on which this batch/kit stock was physically received at the depot. Used for FIFO/FEFO inventory management, shelf-life calculations, and GMP receipt documentation.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.122; ICH Q10

description: Timestamp when this depot inventory record was first created in the system, typically upon receipt of the batch at the depot. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.

description: Cumulative number of IMP units or kits dispensed to study subjects at this location since the study began or since the last accountability reconciliation period. Sourced from dispensing records in the RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; Medidata Rave RTSM; 21 CFR Part 312.62; CDISC CDASH

description: Number of IMP units or kits that have passed their expiry date at this location and are no longer eligible for dispensing. Tracked separately for regulatory accountability and waste reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Current physical count of IMP units or kits available at this location, excluding quarantined, allocated, and reserved stock. Represents the net usable inventory at the time of the last update. Used as the primary metric for resupply trigger evaluation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; Medidata Rave RTSM; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Number of IMP units or kits currently placed in quarantine at this location due to quality concerns, temperature excursions, damaged packaging, or pending investigation. These units are not available for dispensing.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Cumulative number of IMP units or kits received at this location since the study began or since the last accountability reconciliation period. Sourced from inbound shipment confirmations in the RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; Medidata Rave RTSM; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Cumulative number of IMP units or kits returned from subjects or from this site to the depot or sponsor for destruction or reconciliation. Used in accountability reconciliation calculations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; Medidata Rave RTSM; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date on which the most recent IMP accountability reconciliation was completed and approved for this inventory position. Required for regulatory inspection readiness and study close-out documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Timestamp when this inventory record was first created in the system, conforming to ALCOA+ principles of contemporaneous data capture. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.
reference: ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5

description: Timestamp when this inventory record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements under ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11, and enables change detection in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5

description: Minimum quantity threshold at which a resupply request should be triggered for this IMP or kit type at this location. When qty_on_hand falls at or below this value, the RTSM or supply planner initiates a replenishment shipment.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; GCP supply planning best practices

description: Standard replenishment quantity to be shipped when a resupply is triggered for this location and IMP/kit type. Defined in the supply plan and used by the RTSM to generate resupply orders automatically.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13

description: Date on which the most recent resupply request was initiated for this inventory position. Used to track supply chain responsiveness and expected delivery timelines.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13

description: Indicates whether an active resupply request has been triggered for this inventory position. Set to True when qty_on_hand falls at or below the reorder_point and a resupply order has been initiated in the RTSM or supply management system.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; GCP supply planning

description: Date by which the IMP batch must be retested to confirm continued stability and suitability for use. Applicable for products with defined retest periods rather than fixed expiry dates. Triggers stability testing workflows.

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|infusion|patch|oral_solution|powder|cream|inhaler|suppository — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; FDA Dosage Form Terminology; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Date from which this IMP master record version is considered authoritative and approved for use in supply planning and dispensing. Supports version control and regulatory compliance for product specification changes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline; ALCOA+

description: Narrative description of the IMP formulation including excipients, concentration, and any special formulation characteristics (e.g., modified-release, lyophilised). Sourced from sponsor-provided specifications and IMPD. Supports pharmacist review and site dispensing guidance.
reference: EMA IMPD Guideline; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ICH Q8

description: Reference number of the GMP certificate issued by the relevant regulatory authority for the manufacturing site producing this IMP. Required for regulatory submissions, batch release, and inspection readiness.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; EMA GMP Guidelines (EudraLex Vol. 4); ICH Q7

description: Version identifier of the IMPD or IND module that governs the current specifications for this IMP. Ensures traceability between the product master record and the regulatory submission version in effect. Supports inspection readiness and TMF completeness.
reference: EMA IMPD Guideline; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the IMP is a biological product (e.g., monoclonal antibody, vaccine, gene therapy, cell therapy) as opposed to a small-molecule chemical entity. Drives regulatory pathway selection (BLA vs NDA/NDA), cold-chain requirements, and special handling protocols.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 600 (Biologics); EMA Guideline on Biological Medicinal Products; ICH Q5A-Q5E

description: Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport, typically 2–8°C or below). Drives cold-chain packaging selection, qualified shipper requirements, and temperature monitoring obligations.
reference: ICH Q1A; WHO Technical Report Series 961 (Cold Chain); GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 343/01)

description: Indicates whether the IMP is classified as a controlled substance under applicable national regulations (e.g., DEA Schedule in the US, Misuse of Drugs Act in the UK). Triggers enhanced accountability, storage security, and regulatory reporting requirements.
reference: US DEA Controlled Substances Act (21 USC 812); UK Misuse of Drugs Act 1971; EMA Guideline on Controlled Substances

description: Indicates whether the IMP has received orphan drug designation from FDA (ODD) or EMA (OMP), conferring special regulatory incentives and obligations. Relevant for regulatory strategy, supply planning, and sponsor reporting.
reference: FDA Orphan Drug Act (21 CFR Part 316); EMA Regulation (EC) No 141/2000; ICH E6(R2)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the IMP is manufactured. Used for import/export regulatory compliance, customs documentation, and supply chain risk assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Name of the GMP-certified manufacturer responsible for producing the IMP. Required for regulatory submissions (IND/IMPD), batch release documentation, and supply chain traceability.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; EMA IMPD Guideline; ICH Q7 GMP

description: Maximum permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP. Used to define temperature excursion thresholds, configure temperature monitoring devices, and assess cold-chain compliance at depots and sites.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 343/01); FDA 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: Minimum permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP. Used to define temperature excursion thresholds, configure temperature monitoring devices, and assess cold-chain compliance at depots and sites.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 343/01); FDA 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: FDA-assigned National Drug Code (NDC) for commercially procured comparators or approved products used in the trial. Applicable for US-sourced products. Used for procurement, accountability, and regulatory reporting.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; FDA 21 CFR Part 207

description: Description of the IMP packaging unit configuration as used in the trial (e.g., 'blister pack of 28 tablets', 'vial 10 mL', 'pre-filled syringe 1 mL'). Drives kit assembly, labelling design, and RTSM dispensing unit definitions.
reference: EMA IMPD Guideline; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.130; ICH Q6A

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the IMP within the sponsor's product catalogue. Used as the externally-known business identifier across RTSM, EDC, and regulatory submissions. Maps to the product code field in Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Full human-readable name of the investigational medicinal product as defined by the sponsor. May be a code name, development compound name, or commercial name for comparators. Used in labelling, supply documentation, and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Current lifecycle status of the IMP master record within the supply domain. Active records are eligible for supply planning and dispensing. Inactive or discontinued records are retained for audit and historical accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Classification of the product role within the clinical trial: IMP (the investigational compound under study), comparator (active reference product), placebo (inactive control), or auxiliary medicinal product (supportive agent not under investigation). Drives supply planning, labelling requirements, and regulatory classification.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; EMA IMPD Guideline; ICH E6(R2)

description: Regulatory filing category under which the IMP is authorised for use in the clinical trial. Determines the applicable regulatory framework, submission requirements, and oversight obligations across jurisdictions (FDA IND, EMA IMPD/CTA, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|IMPD|CTA|NDA|BLA|MAA|exempt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); EMA EU CTR 536/2014 (IMPD/CTA); ICH E6(R2)

description: Period in months after which the IMP must be retested before continued use, applicable where a formal expiry date is not assigned. Relevant for bulk drug substances and certain investigational compounds. Supports expiry and retest date calculations in RTSM.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: The path by which the IMP is delivered into the body (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Required for eCRF design, SDTM CM domain mapping, and regulatory labelling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM CM Domain; FDA Route of Administration Terminology; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Approved shelf life of the IMP in months from the date of manufacture, as established by stability studies and documented in the IMPD/IND. Used for expiry management, resupply planning, and depot/site inventory accountability.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Indicates how the IMP is obtained for the clinical trial: manufactured (custom GMP production), commercially procured (purchased from market), over-the-counter, or imported. Drives procurement workflows, GMP documentation requirements, and supply chain planning.
reference: EMA IMPD Guideline; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ICH Q7

description: The sponsor's internal product code or compound number for the IMP (e.g., development compound identifier such as 'ABC-123'). Distinct from the CRO's product_code; used for sponsor-facing reporting, cross-study portfolio analytics, and regulatory correspondence.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Required storage conditions for the IMP as specified in the product dossier and labelling (e.g., 'Store at 2–8°C', 'Store below 25°C, protect from light'). Drives cold-chain logistics, depot and site storage compliance, and temperature excursion management.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.142; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Unit of measure for the active substance strength (e.g., mg, g, mcg, mg/mL, IU). description: Numeric quantity of the active substance per dosage unit (e.g., 10, 500, 1.5). Must be interpreted in conjunction with strength_unit. Represents the principal quantitative fact about the IMP formulation. Used in dispensing records, RTSM kit assignment, and regulatory labelling.
reference: CDISC SDTM EX Domain; FDA 21 CFR Part 201; EMA IMPD Guideline

description: Number of individual dosage units (tablets, vials, syringes, etc.) contained in one RTSM dispensing kit. Used for supply planning, kit assembly, and accountability reconciliation in Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the IMP master record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the regulatory authority that issued the permit (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, AUS, CAN, DEU). Identifies the jurisdiction of the issuing body.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q10

description: Name of the national regulatory authority or government body that issued the import permit (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada). Used for regulatory correspondence and compliance reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA; MHRA; PMDA; TGA; Health Canada; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Free-text field for additional regulatory or operational notes related to the import permit, such as special conditions imposed by the authority, customs broker instructions, or escalation details.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Reference identifier for the scanned or electronic copy of the import permit document stored in the eTMF (Veeva Vault). Enables direct retrieval of the official permit document for customs and audit purposes.

description: Official permit number assigned by the issuing regulatory authority. This is the externally-known identifier used in regulatory correspondence, customs declarations, and shipment documentation.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH Q10; WHO ICTRP

description: Current lifecycle status of the import permit. Drives shipment release decisions and regulatory compliance tracking. Draft = in preparation; Submitted = application filed with authority; Approved = permit granted; Rejected = application denied; Expired = validity period elapsed; Revoked = permit withdrawn by authority.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2)

description: Classification of the import permit by scope of authorization. Single_shipment covers one consignment; multi_shipment covers a defined number of shipments; blanket covers all shipments within a validity period; emergency is expedited authorization; re_export covers onward shipment to another country.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q10; WHO ICTRP

description: Unit of measure for the permitted_quantity field (e.g., units, vials, tablets, capsules, kg, g, mg, L, mL). Must align with the IMP dosage form and packaging configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: units|vials|tablets|capsules|kg|g|mg|L|mL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q10; CDISC SDTM; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Maximum number of individual shipments authorized under this permit. Applicable for multi_shipment permit types. Null for single_shipment or blanket permits where shipment count is not restricted.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q10

description: Date on which the regulatory authority formally rejected the import permit application. Populated only when permit_status is 'rejected'. Used to track regulatory response timelines and initiate resubmission.

description: Reason provided by the regulatory authority for rejecting the import permit application. Populated when permit_status is 'rejected'. Used to guide resubmission and CAPA activities.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2)

description: Number of calendar days before permit expiry that the renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority. Used to trigger automated renewal reminders and prevent supply disruption.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether this import permit requires periodic renewal before the study completes. Triggers proactive renewal workflow to prevent supply chain disruption due to permit expiry.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics management during import (refrigerated, frozen, or ultra-frozen transport). Drives logistics planning and customs clearance prioritization.
reference: ICH Q10; EU GDP guidelines; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Date on which the regulatory authority revoked a previously approved import permit. Populated only when permit_status is 'revoked'. Triggers immediate shipment hold and regulatory notification.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2)

description: Reason provided by the regulatory authority for revoking a previously approved import permit. Populated when permit_status is 'revoked'. Triggers regulatory notification and supply chain hold.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2)

description: Required storage condition for the IMP as specified on the import permit and in the IMP dossier. Drives cold-chain logistics requirements during import and customs clearance. Must align with kit_definition storage conditions.
reference: ICH Q10; EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; GDP guidelines

description: Timestamp when the import permit record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

description: Cumulative quantity of IMP actually imported against this permit to date. Used to track permit utilization, remaining authorized quantity, and accountability reconciliation. Expressed in the same unit as permitted_quantity_unit.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q10; ALCOA+

description: Number of shipments that have been made against this permit to date. Used to track permit utilization and determine remaining shipment capacity for multi_shipment permits.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q10; ALCOA+

description: Date from which the import permit is legally effective and shipments may be made under its authority. Typically coincides with or follows the approval date.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH Q10

description: Date on which the import permit expires and is no longer valid for authorizing IMP shipments. Critical for preventing shipment delays due to expired permits. Null for open-ended permits.

description: Blinding configuration of the study as it affects supply planning. Double-blind studies require matched placebo kits; open-label studies have simpler kit structures. Impacts kit design and labeling requirements.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Target buffer stock expressed in weeks of supply to be maintained at depot and/or site level. Ensures continuity of supply during manufacturing lead times and logistics delays.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport) throughout the supply chain. Drives depot selection, packaging specifications, and logistics provider requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; WHO Technical Report Series 961; GDP guidelines

description: Timestamp when this supply plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this supply plan version includes a returns and destruction plan for expired, unused, or damaged IMP. Required for GMP compliance and accountability reconciliation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62; FDA IND regulations

description: Assumed subject dropout/discontinuation rate (as a percentage) applied in demand forecasting calculations. Accounts for subjects who withdraw consent, are lost to follow-up, or are discontinued per protocol.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; SAP guidelines

description: Strategy applied for managing IMP expiry at depot and site level. FIFO (First In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out), manual review, or RTSM-managed automated expiry tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; Medidata Rave RTSM; 21 CFR Part 211.137

description: Total number of subjects projected to be enrolled over the planning horizon, derived from enrollment projections and site feasibility assessments. Primary driver of IMP demand forecasting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Oracle Clinical One

description: End date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Typically extends to study completion or database lock (DBL) plus a buffer for returns and destruction.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the beginning of the forecasted demand and manufacturing schedule window.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Free-text field for supply chain planners to document assumptions, constraints, protocol amendment impacts, or sponsor-specific instructions relevant to this supply plan version.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Planned overage percentage applied on top of net demand to account for wastage, damaged kits, protocol amendments, and supply chain uncertainties. Agreed with sponsor and documented in the supply agreement.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; EU GMP Annex 13

description: The principal business date on which this supply plan was formally prepared or issued. Represents the real-world event date distinct from system audit timestamps.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the supply plan, typically formatted as SP-{StudyCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}. Used in sponsor communications, RTSM configuration, and regulatory documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Current lifecycle status of the supply plan. Draft indicates initial creation; under_review indicates sponsor/CRO review; approved indicates sign-off; active indicates currently governing supply operations; superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; cancelled indicates plan was voided.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Clinical One

description: Total number of manufacturing batches planned to fulfill the forecasted IMP demand over the planning horizon, including buffer and overage quantities.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211; FDA IND regulations

description: Frequency or trigger basis for this supply plan update cycle. Typically monthly or per enrollment milestone as defined in the supply agreement with the sponsor.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Randomization methodology used in the RTSM for kit assignment to subjects. Drives kit numbering, stratification factors, and supply allocation logic within Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; Oracle Clinical One

description: Standard quantity of kits or units to be shipped per resupply order when the resupply trigger threshold is reached. Defined per site tier and country in the supply plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Minimum kit or unit quantity at depot or site level that triggers an automatic resupply order via RTSM (Interactive Response Technology). When inventory falls to or below this threshold, a resupply shipment is initiated.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave RTSM; CDISC CDASH

business_glossary_term: Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) Plan ID
description: Identifier of the corresponding supply plan configuration record within the Medidata Rave RTSM system. Enables traceability between the lakehouse supply plan record and the operational RTSM configuration.
reference: Medidata Rave RTSM; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Approved shelf life of the IMP in months from date of manufacture, as defined in the product specification and stability data. Critical for expiry management and resupply timing calculations.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Testing; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.137; FDA IND regulations

description: Site tier classification covered by this supply plan. Allows differentiated supply planning for high-enrolling versus low-enrolling sites, supporting optimized kit allocation and resupply strategies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP as specified in the product dossier. Defines upper bound for cold-chain and ambient storage qualification.

description: Minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP as specified in the product dossier. Used to define cold-chain logistics requirements and depot qualification criteria.

description: Total number of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, kits) planned for manufacture and distribution over the planning horizon, inclusive of overage and buffer stock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; Medidata Rave RTSM

description: Number of treatment arms (including placebo/comparator) in the study protocol that require separate IMP supply planning. Drives kit type differentiation in RTSM randomization configuration.

description: The unit in which IMP supply quantities are expressed and tracked throughout the supply plan (e.g., kit, vial, tablet). Aligns with RTSM kit configuration and dispensing records. description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this supply plan record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every planning cycle revision or status change.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Version identifier of the supply plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3), incremented at each planning cycle or upon significant enrollment milestone updates. Supports version history and audit trail.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each IMP/kit shipment record in the clinical supply chain. Primary key for the shipment data product.

Supply operations teams verify CTA authorization status as a mandatory gate in the shipment release p
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Shipment handling, cold-chain receipt, and temperature monitoring procedures are governed by SOPs (ICH E6 R2). Shipment records must reference the applicable handling SOP for GCP audit readiness and r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the destination clinical site receiving the IMP shipment. Used for site-level inventory accountability and regulatory reconciliation.

description: The confirmed date on which the IMP shipment was received at the destination node. Used for delivery performance measurement, accountability reconciliation, and regulatory documentation.

description: Precise date and time the IMP shipment was confirmed received at the destination. Supports cold-chain total transit time calculations and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.

description: Investigational Medicinal Product (IMP) manufacturing batch or lot number included in this shipment. Critical for product traceability, recall management, and regulatory accountability.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.192; ICH Q7; ISO 14155

description: Name of the logistics carrier or courier responsible for transporting the IMP shipment (e.g., World Courier, Marken, DHL). Required for cold-chain accountability and deviation investigation.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Unique identifier of the temperature data logger device placed in the shipment. Used to retrieve detailed temperature profiles from the logistics provider system for excursion investigation and regulatory documentation.

description: Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the source RTSM system or lakehouse. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.

description: Date on which customs clearance was obtained for the international IMP shipment. Used for transit time analysis and regulatory import documentation.

description: Indicates whether the shipment requires customs clearance due to international cross-border transit. Drives import/export documentation requirements and regulatory import permits for IMP.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Health Canada

description: Human-readable name of the supply chain destination node (e.g., site name, depot name). Supports logistics documentation and regulatory accountability records.

description: Type of supply chain node to which the shipment is directed (e.g., clinical site, central depot, manufacturer). Supports supply chain network mapping and inventory accountability.

description: Certificate number issued upon confirmed destruction of returned or expired IMP. Required for regulatory accountability reconciliation and GMP compliance documentation.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates the directional flow of the shipment: outbound (manufacturer/depot to site), inbound (site to depot/returns), or lateral (site to site). Supports supply chain node mapping and accountability.

description: The date on which the IMP shipment was physically dispatched from the origin node. Represents the principal business event timestamp for outbound shipments. Used for lead-time calculations and regulatory documentation.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Precise date and time the IMP shipment was dispatched from the origin node. Supports cold-chain elapsed time calculations and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.

description: Total cumulative duration in minutes during which the shipment temperature was outside the specified storage condition range. Used for product impact assessment and quality disposition decisions.

description: The planned date on which the IMP shipment is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for site inventory planning, expiry management, and supply chain KPI monitoring.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Expiration date of the IMP batch included in this shipment. Used for expiry management, re-test date tracking, and prevention of expired product administration to subjects.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.137; ICH Q1A

description: Description of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or comparator included in the shipment (e.g., drug name, formulation, strength). Supports accountability documentation and regulatory submissions.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Regulatory import permit or authorization number issued by the receiving country's health authority for the IMP shipment. Required for cross-border clinical supply and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; Health Canada; PMDA; TGA

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between shipper and receiver for the IMP shipment. description: Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the cold-chain data logger during transit. Used to assess whether the IMP was exposed to excessive heat or out-of-range conditions.

description: Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the cold-chain data logger during transit. Used to assess whether the IMP was exposed to freezing or sub-optimal low temperatures.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH Q1A; WHO TRS 961; 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or comments related to the shipment (e.g., fragile contents, priority handling, deviation observations).

description: Human-readable name of the supply chain origin node (e.g., depot name, manufacturer facility name, or site name). Supports logistics documentation and regulatory accountability records.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: Type of supply chain node from which the shipment originates (e.g., manufacturer, central depot, clinical site, CRO warehouse). Used for supply chain network mapping and accountability.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 211

description: Indicates whether the shipment or its contents have been placed under quarantine pending quality investigation (e.g., following a temperature excursion or damaged packaging). Prevents dispensing of affected kits to subjects.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name of the regulatory inspector or lead inspector who identified and documented this finding. Recorded for correspondence and response addressing purposes. Classified as confidential as it identifies a government official in a regulatory context.
reference: FDA GCP Inspection Program; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether this inspection finding is directly related to a protocol deviation or series of protocol deviations. Enables cross-domain linkage between compliance findings and protocol deviation management.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA Protocol Deviation Guidance; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Name or identifier of the individual (typically a QA manager or Regulatory Affairs lead) responsible for managing the response and resolution of this inspection finding within the sponsor or CRO organization. Supports accountability tracking in MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl Quality Management; FDA GCP Inspection Program

description: Specific regulatory reference cited by the inspecting authority as the basis for the finding (e.g., '21 CFR 312.62(b)', 'ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.0', 'EU CTR 536/2014 Article 47'). Critical for formal response drafting and legal review.
reference: FDA 21 CFR; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ISO 14155

description: Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified deficiency at the same site or sponsor level. Repeat findings signal systemic quality failures and may trigger escalated regulatory enforcement action.
reference: FDA Warning Letter Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The regulatory authority's assessment of whether the sponsor's formal response to the finding is adequate. 'Adequate' results in finding closure. 'Inadequate' or 'Partially_adequate' requires a supplemental response. 'Pending_review' indicates the authority has not yet issued its assessment.
reference: FDA Form 483 Response Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Formal deadline by which the sponsor or CRO must submit a written response to the regulatory authority. For FDA Form 483 observations, this is typically 15 business days from the last day of inspection. For EMA GCP findings, deadlines vary by classification. Drives executive-level regulatory commitment management.
reference: FDA Form 483 Response Guidance (15 business days); EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Actual date on which the formal written response to the inspection finding was submitted to the regulatory authority. Used to calculate response timeliness and compliance with regulatory deadlines.
reference: FDA Form 483 Response Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Categorical classification of the root cause to enable trend analysis across findings. 'Process' indicates a procedural gap; 'Training' indicates inadequate staff training; 'System' indicates an EDC, CTMS, or IT system failure; 'Communication' indicates a breakdown in information flow; 'Resource' indicates insufficient staffing or budget; 'Oversight' indicates inadequate monitoring or QA oversight.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl; ISO 9001

description: Documented root cause of the deficiency identified in the inspection finding, as determined through the CAPA investigation process. Supports systemic quality improvement and recurrence prevention.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl CAPA Module; ISO 9001

description: Executive summary of the sponsor's or CRO's formal written response to the inspection finding, including the root cause identified, immediate corrective actions taken, and preventive measures committed. Stored in MasterControl and linked to the eTMF in Veeva Vault.
reference: FDA Form 483 Response Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Assessment of whether the finding has a direct or potential impact on subject safety. 'None' indicates no subject safety risk. 'Potential' indicates a theoretical risk that has not materialized. 'Confirmed' indicates documented harm or risk to enrolled subjects.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 2; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; OHRP Guidance

description: Deadline for submission of a supplemental or follow-up response when the initial response was assessed as inadequate or partially adequate by the regulatory authority. Null if no supplemental response is required.
reference: FDA Form 483 Response Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this inspection finding record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Official reference number of the FDA Warning Letter or equivalent regulatory enforcement communication issued in connection with this finding. Populated only when escalation_status is 'warning_letter'. Used for regulatory commitment tracking and legal response management.
reference: FDA Warning Letter Program; FDA Enforcement Actions Database

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the CAPA record. Primary key for the CAPA lifecycle master record sourced from MasterControl CAPA module.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Identifier of the internal or external audit that generated the finding triggering this CAPA. Null if the CAPA was not initiated from an audit. Enables audit-to-CAPA traceability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl Audit Module

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: A CAPA can be initiated directly from a regulatory inspection (source_type='inspection'). While inspection_finding->compliance_capa links findings to CAPAs, a CAPA may also reference the inspection it
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: capa.sop_reference is a free-text string identifying the SOP associated with the CAPA. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id enables structured linkage between CAPAs and the SOPs they address or update
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: CAPAs arising from statistical audit or inspection findings require SAP amendments as corrective actions. Linking the CAPA to the SAP it corrects enables tracking of authority-mandated or QA-driven st
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the clinical study associated with this CAPA. Links the CAPA to the relevant study protocol for study-level quality tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM; FDA IND Regulations 21 CFR Part 312

description: Identifier of the investigational site associated with this CAPA, if site-specific. Null for sponsor-level or cross-site CAPAs. Supports site-level quality performance analytics.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: CAPAs produce corrective action documents (SOP revisions, training records, investigation reports) that must be filed in the TMF per GCP. A QA expert expects this link to verify CAPA closure documenta
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Actual calendar date on which all corrective and preventive actions were completed and verified. Null until CAPA is closed. Used to calculate cycle time and on-time closure rate KPIs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Date on which the CAPA plan was formally approved by the authorized QA approver in MasterControl. Marks the transition from initiation to active execution phase.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether the action is corrective (addressing an existing non-conformance), preventive (addressing a potential non-conformance), or both. Drives workflow routing in MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155

description: Narrative justification provided by the responsible owner and QA approver at the time of CAPA closure, summarizing evidence that all actions were completed and the root cause was resolved. Required for regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module; ALCOA+

description: Current lifecycle state of the CAPA record. Drives workflow routing, escalation triggers, and regulatory reporting. 'Overdue' is system-set when target completion date is passed without closure.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Detailed description of the corrective actions planned or implemented to address the identified root cause and prevent recurrence of the quality issue. Core CAPA deliverable per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: System timestamp recording when the CAPA record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the quality issue underlying this CAPA had or could have had an impact on clinical data integrity. Triggers data review, potential database query, and regulatory notification workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Data Integrity Guidance; EMA Data Integrity Guidance; ALCOA+; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Planned or actual date on which the effectiveness of the implemented corrective and preventive actions will be or was verified. Required by GCP to confirm the CAPA resolved the root cause.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Narrative description of the methodology and findings from the effectiveness check, including evidence reviewed and conclusions drawn. Supports regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module; ALCOA+

description: Outcome of the effectiveness verification assessment confirming whether the implemented CAPA actions successfully resolved the root cause and prevented recurrence. Drives CAPA closure or re-opening decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Assessment of the impact of the underlying quality issue on GCP compliance and data integrity. Drives CAPA priority, escalation, and regulatory reporting decisions. Aligned with FDA and EMA GCP inspection classification standards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA GCP Inspections; EMA GCP Inspections; MHRA GCP Inspections

description: Description of any immediate containment or interim actions taken at the time of CAPA initiation to mitigate the impact of the quality issue before the full corrective action is implemented.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Calendar date on which the CAPA was formally initiated and entered into MasterControl. Serves as the start of the CAPA lifecycle clock for overdue tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module; ALCOA+

description: Native record identifier assigned by the MasterControl CAPA module. Used for source system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Detailed description of the preventive actions planned to eliminate the potential for recurrence of similar quality issues across the organization or study portfolio.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Risk-based priority level assigned to the CAPA, reflecting the severity and urgency of the underlying quality issue. Drives escalation timelines and resource allocation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Detailed description of the quality issue, non-conformance, or finding that triggered the CAPA. Captures the 'what happened' narrative for audit trail and regulatory inspection purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module; ALCOA+

description: Indicates whether this CAPA addresses a recurring quality issue that has been the subject of a prior CAPA. True signals a systemic quality problem requiring escalated management attention.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
description: Externally-known, human-readable CAPA reference number assigned by MasterControl upon initiation (e.g., CAPA-2024-00123). Used in audit trails, regulatory submissions, and sponsor communications.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Indicates whether this CAPA is associated with a finding that requires regulatory reporting to FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other health authority. Drives regulatory submission workflow in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Inspections; EU CTR 536/2014; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Organizational department or functional area accountable for CAPA execution (e.g., Clinical Operations, Data Management, Regulatory Affairs, Quality Assurance). Used for departmental quality KPI reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Standardized category classifying the root cause of the quality issue (e.g., training deficiency, process gap, system failure, communication breakdown, resource constraint). Used for trend analysis and systemic quality improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_deficiency|process_gap|system_failure|communication_breakdown|resource_constraint|documentation_error|oversight_failure — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 14155; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Detailed narrative describing the identified root cause of the quality issue, as determined through root cause analysis (RCA). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and CAPA justification.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; EMA GCP Inspections; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Identifier of the originating record that triggered this CAPA (e.g., audit finding ID, inspection finding ID, protocol deviation ID, or QC failure ID). Enables traceability back to the root triggering event.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Category of the triggering event that initiated the CAPA. Classifies whether the CAPA originated from an internal audit, regulatory inspection, protocol deviation, QC failure, self-identified quality issue, complaint, or safety signal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: audit_finding|inspection_finding|protocol_deviation|qc_failure|self_identified|complaint|safety_signal — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; EMA GCP Inspections; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Date on which the study sponsor was formally notified of this CAPA. Null if sponsor_notified_flag is False. Supports sponsor oversight audit trail.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this CAPA. Required for CRO-managed studies where sponsor oversight obligations apply per ICH E6(R2) GCP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether the quality issue underlying this CAPA had or could have had a direct impact on subject safety. Triggers expedited review and potential safety reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ISO 14155

description: Planned date by which all corrective and preventive actions must be completed and the CAPA closed. Used for overdue status calculation, escalation triggers, and sponsor/regulatory reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the quality issue or finding that triggered the CAPA. Used for identification in dashboards, reports, and audit-readiness reviews.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether the corrective or preventive action includes a mandatory training component. When True, triggers training assignment workflow in MasterControl training management module.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl Training Management Module

description: System timestamp recording when the CAPA record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports change tracking, audit trail integrity, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Action Item ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual action item within a CAPA plan. Primary key for this transactional line record. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — each action item is a discrete line within a parent CAPA plan header, with its own owner, due date, and completion lifecycle.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Reference to the parent CAPA plan to which this action item belongs. A single CAPA plan may contain multiple discrete action items tracked independently. Serves as the HEADER_REFERENCE for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: capa_action_item.related_sop_number is a free-text string referencing the SOP relevant to the action item. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enabling structured trackin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this action item within MasterControl (e.g., CAPA-AI-2024-0042). Used in audit trails, regulatory inspection responses, and cross-system references. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this line record.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Current workflow state of the action item within the CAPA execution lifecycle. Drives CAPA closure eligibility — all action items must reach 'completed' or 'cancelled' before the parent CAPA can be closed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|completed|overdue|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Classification of the action item by its nature within the CAPA framework: corrective (addresses the identified nonconformance), preventive (prevents recurrence), effectiveness check (verifies the CAPA resolved the issue), root cause analysis, training, process change, or documentation update. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrective|preventive|effectiveness_check|root_cause_analysis|training|process_change|documentation_update — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Date on which the action item was actually completed and evidence was submitted. Compared against planned_completion_date to measure CAPA execution timeliness. Null until the action item reaches 'completed' status.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ALCOA+ principles

description: Organizational department or functional unit of the assigned owner (e.g., Quality Assurance, Data Management, Clinical Operations). Used for workload analysis, departmental CAPA metrics, and escalation routing.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Detailed narrative describing the specific action to be taken, including scope, methodology, and expected outcome. Provides the full context required for the assigned owner to execute the action and for auditors to assess adequacy of the response.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ALCOA+ principles

description: Timestamp when this action item was formally closed in MasterControl following successful completion and verification. Null until the item reaches a terminal state (completed or cancelled). Used to calculate cycle time metrics.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Free-text field for additional notes, progress updates, or contextual information entered by the action item owner or QA reviewer during the execution lifecycle. Supports ongoing communication and audit trail within MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Timestamp when this action item record was first created in MasterControl. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity. Supports ALCOA+ auditability and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Number of calendar days this action item has been open, calculated from opened_timestamp to either actual_completion_date (if closed) or the current date (if still open). Used as a KPI for CAPA execution timeliness and audit readiness dashboards. Note: this is a business-captured operational metric from MasterControl, not a derived aggregate.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Number of times the planned completion date has been extended for this action item. A high extension count is a quality risk indicator and may trigger escalation or QA review. Tracked per MasterControl audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Target date by which the effectiveness check for this action item must be completed. Null if effectiveness_check_required is False. Used to schedule and track post-implementation reviews.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether a post-completion effectiveness check is required for this action item to confirm the corrective or preventive action achieved its intended outcome. Typically mandated for critical or repeat findings per GCP quality standards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Current status of the post-completion effectiveness check for this action item. 'Effective' confirms the action resolved the root cause; 'ineffective' triggers re-opening of the CAPA or creation of a new CAPA.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Date on which this action item was formally escalated. Null if escalation_flag is False. Used to measure escalation response time and management accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether this action item has been escalated to senior management or QA leadership due to overdue status, repeated extensions, or high risk. Triggers escalation notifications and management review workflows in MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Narrative description of the evidence provided to demonstrate completion of the action item (e.g., 'Updated SOP-QA-012 v3.0 attached; training records for 12 staff members uploaded'). Supports QA verification and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ALCOA+ principles; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Reference identifier or URL to the supporting evidence document stored in Veeva Vault or MasterControl (e.g., document ID, eTMF reference, SOP version number). Links the action item completion to the formal document management system.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault; MasterControl

description: Narrative explanation provided by the action item owner or QA approver when the planned completion date is extended. Required for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness. Null if no extension has been granted.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; ALCOA+ principles

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this action item record in MasterControl. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this entity. Required for change tracking and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles

description: Native record identifier assigned by MasterControl to this action item in the source system. Used for cross-system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and the MasterControl operational system of record.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Timestamp when this action item was formally opened and assigned to an owner. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity. Distinct from created_timestamp, which reflects system record creation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Target date by which the action item is expected to be completed, as agreed upon during CAPA planning. Used to calculate overdue status, track CAPA execution timeliness, and report on compliance with committed timelines to sponsors and regulatory bodies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Business priority level assigned to this action item, reflecting urgency and risk impact. Critical and high-priority items are subject to expedited review and escalation procedures. Drives resource allocation and monitoring frequency.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: The regulatory authority to which this action item represents a formal commitment, if regulatory_commitment is True (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA). Null if this is an internal CAPA action item without a regulatory commitment dimension. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; EMA GCP Inspections; ICH E6(R2); MHRA GCP Inspections

description: Indicates whether this action item represents a formal commitment made to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) during an inspection or in a regulatory submission response. Regulatory commitment items are subject to heightened monitoring and escalation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; EMA GCP Inspections; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014

description: Ordinal position of this action item within the parent CAPA plan (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables ordered display and reporting of action items within a CAPA. Serves as the LINE_SEQUENCE for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity.
reference: MasterControl CAPA Module; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reference identifier of the specific audit finding, inspection observation, protocol deviation, or nonconformance that this action item directly addresses (e.g., audit finding number, deviation ID). Provides traceability from action item back to the originating quality event.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the action item (e.g., 'Revise SOP-QA-012 to include double-check step'). Used in dashboards, reports, and inspection-ready summaries within MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Date on which all required training associated with this action item was completed and documented in MasterControl. Null if training_required is False or training has not yet been completed. Required as part of completion evidence.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl Training Management Module

description: Indicates whether completion of this action item requires associated staff training (e.g., training on a revised SOP). When True, training completion records in MasterControl must be linked as evidence before the action item can be verified.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl Training Management Module

description: Free-text comments entered by the QA verifier during the evidence review, including rationale for rejection or conditions for acceptance. Provides the audit trail narrative required for regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date on which the QA verifier confirmed the completion evidence was adequate and the action item was satisfactorily executed. Null until verification is completed. Required for regulatory inspection audit trail.

description: Result of the QA verification review of the action item completion evidence. 'Accepted' closes the verification loop; 'rejected' returns the item to the owner for rework; 'pending_revision' indicates partial acceptance with required amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Indicates whether independent QA verification of completion evidence is required before this action item can be marked as completed. True for high-risk or critical action items per the CAPA procedure. Drives the QA review workflow in MasterControl.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.100; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Primary key for deviation_classification
