description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been determined to be required for this quality event. True: CAPA must be initiated in MasterControl. False: event can be resolved without formal CAPA. This is the primary escalation decision field in the quality event triage workflow.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.1; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: System timestamp when the quality event record was first created in MasterControl. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements and ALCOA+ attributability. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Indicates whether the quality event has compromised or potentially compromised the integrity of clinical trial data in the EDC or source documents. True: data integrity affected. False: no data integrity impact. Drives data review, query management, and potential database lock delay decisions.
reference: FDA Data Integrity Guidance 2018; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Number of errors, discrepancies, or non-conformances identified within the QC sample reviewed. Used in conjunction with sample_size to compute error rate for quality trending and threshold monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DMP Guidelines; MasterControl QMS

description: Proportion of errors identified relative to the total sample size (error_count / sample_size). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%). Used for quality threshold monitoring, KPI reporting, and CAPA escalation triggers. Null if sample_size is zero or not applicable.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Data Integrity Guidance; MasterControl QMS

description: Detailed narrative description of the quality event, including what was observed, where it occurred, and the potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. Captured at time of identification per ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation standards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Human-readable, externally-visible reference number assigned to the quality event upon creation in MasterControl. Used in audit trails, regulatory correspondence, and CAPA linkage. Format: QE-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl QMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the quality event within the MasterControl workflow. Open: newly identified, awaiting triage. Under Review: QA/QC team actively investigating. Pending CAPA: CAPA determination made, awaiting CAPA initiation. Closed: event resolved and verified. Cancelled: event determined to be invalid or duplicate.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl QMS

description: Classification of the quality event by its nature. Drives routing, escalation rules, and CAPA determination logic. Values: data_quality_issue (EDC/eCRF data errors), process_non_compliance (GCP/SOP deviation), system_failure (EDC, CTMS, or IRT system outage/error), vendor_quality_issue (CRO, lab, or third-party vendor failure), qc_review_finding (finding from a formal QC review activity).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; MasterControl QMS

description: The activity or mechanism through which the quality event was detected. Drives root cause analysis and process improvement analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qc_review|self_inspection|routine_monitoring|sdv|audit|regulatory_inspection|proactive_surveillance|system_alert — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.1; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance; MasterControl QMS

description: Calendar date on which the quality event was first identified or detected. This is the principal real-world event date per ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements. Distinct from the record creation timestamp.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Description of any immediate containment or corrective actions taken at the time of quality event identification, prior to formal CAPA initiation. Examples: data query raised in EDC, site notified, process halted. Supports ALCOA+ and GCP audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.1; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance; ALCOA+ Principles

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether this quality event must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study. True: IRB/IEC notification required. False: not required. Driven by protocol deviation severity and subject safety impact.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; OHRP Regulations; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Classification of the quality event as a protocol deviation per ICH E6(R2) and sponsor requirements. Major: deviation that may affect subject safety or data integrity. Minor: deviation with minimal impact. Not Applicable: event is not a protocol deviation (e.g., system failure, vendor issue). Required for regulatory submission deviation summaries.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Overall outcome of the QC review activity that generated this quality event. Pass: quality threshold met, no significant issues. Fail: quality threshold exceeded, formal action required. Conditional Pass: threshold borderline, enhanced monitoring required. Applicable when identification_source is qc_review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DMP Guidelines; MasterControl QMS

description: Indicates whether this quality event represents a recurrence of a previously identified and closed quality event of the same type at the same site or study. True: recurrence identified. False: first occurrence. Recurrences trigger mandatory CAPA escalation and may indicate CAPA ineffectiveness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl QMS

description: Name of the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada) notified of this quality event, if applicable. Null if regulatory_reportability is not_reportable. Supports regulatory submission tracking and inspection readiness.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GCP Guideline; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Assessment of whether this quality event requires reporting to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) or to the sponsor. Reportable: must be submitted to regulatory body. Not Reportable: no regulatory notification required. Under Assessment: reportability determination pending QA review.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GCP Guideline; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date on which the quality event was formally resolved and closed in MasterControl. Populated when event_status transitions to closed. Used to calculate time-to-resolution KPIs and assess compliance with quality SLA targets.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl QMS; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: Narrative description of how the quality event was resolved, including actions taken, verification of effectiveness, and confirmation that the issue has been addressed. Required for event closure in MasterControl and supports regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl QMS; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Date on which the QA reviewer formally assessed and triaged the quality event in MasterControl. Used to measure triage cycle time and compliance with internal quality SLA targets for event review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.1; MasterControl QMS; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: Name or user identifier of the Quality Assurance (QA) professional who reviewed and assessed the quality event in MasterControl. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail and GCP oversight accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.1; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl QMS

description: High-level categorization of the root cause identified during quality event investigation. Used for systemic quality trending, SOP improvement prioritization, and CAPA effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_gap|process_gap|system_error|communication_failure|resource_constraint|vendor_failure|protocol_complexity|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA CAPA Guidance; ISO 9001 Root Cause Analysis

description: Detailed narrative of the root cause identified through investigation of the quality event. Captured by the QA investigator following root cause analysis. Supports CAPA development and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA CAPA Guidance; MasterControl QMS

description: Total number of records, data points, or items reviewed during the QC activity that identified this quality event. Used to calculate error rate and assess the statistical significance of the finding. Applicable when the event originates from a QC review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC DMP Guidelines; MasterControl QMS

description: Risk-based severity classification of the quality event per GCP quality standards. Critical: may impact subject safety or data integrity requiring immediate action. Major: significant GCP deviation with potential regulatory impact. Minor: low-risk deviation with limited impact. Observation: informational finding with no immediate compliance risk.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EMA GCP Inspection Findings Classification; FDA Guidance on GCP Inspections

description: The operational system of record from which this quality event was originated or imported into the data lakehouse. Supports data lineage tracking and cross-system quality event reconciliation. Primary source is MasterControl quality management module.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl QMS

description: Date on which the study sponsor was formally notified of this quality event. Populated when sponsor_notified is True. Used to verify compliance with contractual and regulatory notification timelines.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; FDA GCP Guidance; Sponsor Quality Agreement

description: Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this quality event. True: sponsor notification sent. False: sponsor not yet notified. Required for CRO-sponsor quality agreement compliance and sponsor oversight obligations under ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.2; FDA GCP Guidance; Sponsor Quality Agreement

description: Indicates whether the quality event has a direct or potential impact on the safety of enrolled study subjects. True: subject safety may be or has been affected. False: no subject safety impact identified. Critical field for regulatory reportability determination and DSMB/DMC notification decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GCP Guideline

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to the quality event record in MasterControl. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The date on which the risk assessment was formally conducted and documented. Represents the principal business event date for this record. Used for trend analysis and review frequency tracking.

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record must be initiated as a result of this risk assessment. Typically set to True when residual risk rating is critical or high, or when identified gaps meet CAPA threshold criteria.
reference: ICH Q9; ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Narrative description of the existing or planned quality control and risk mitigation measures applied to reduce the identified risk (e.g., enhanced SDV, targeted monitoring, additional training, SOP updates, EDC edit checks). Sourced from MasterControl.
reference: ICH Q9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the source system (MasterControl). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) for electronic records.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the identified risk has a potential or actual impact on clinical data integrity (ALCOA+ principles: Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate). Triggers enhanced data review and potential database lock delay.
reference: FDA Data Integrity Guidance; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2); MHRA GXP Data Integrity Guidance

description: Indicates whether the identified risk has a direct impact on Good Clinical Practice (GCP) compliance. Drives escalation to QA leadership and potential regulatory notification obligations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Narrative description of quality gaps, process deficiencies, or compliance vulnerabilities identified during the risk assessment (e.g., missing SDV documentation, incomplete ICF versions, delayed SAE reporting). Drives CAPA initiation and remediation planning.
reference: ICH Q9; ICH E6(R2); FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the ability to detect the risk before it causes harm, prior to control measures. Used in FMEA-style risk scoring (Risk Priority Number = Likelihood × Impact × Detectability).
reference: ICH Q9 FMEA Methodology; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the severity of impact on patient safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance if the risk materializes before controls are applied. Component of the inherent risk score calculation.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the probability of the risk occurring before any control measures are applied. Component of the inherent risk score calculation per ICH Q9 risk estimation methodology.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Qualitative risk rating derived from the inherent risk score before controls: critical, high, medium, or low. Used for risk prioritization, escalation thresholds, and sponsor/QA reporting dashboards.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Composite numeric risk score calculated before control measures are applied, typically derived from likelihood, impact, and detectability scores (e.g., product or weighted sum). Establishes the baseline risk level for prioritization.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on RBQM

description: Indicates whether this risk assessment was conducted specifically in preparation for a regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or sponsor audit). Enables filtering of inspection-readiness assessments for pre-inspection reporting.
reference: FDA Inspection Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2)

description: The native record identifier assigned by MasterControl (the system of record for this data product) to the risk assessment document. Enables direct traceability back to the source system for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: MasterControl Document Control; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this risk assessment, based on the defined review frequency. Supports proactive risk monitoring and inspection readiness. Calculated from assessment_date and review_frequency_days.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on RBQM

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the risk assessment record in MasterControl (e.g., RA-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability, audit trails, and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: MasterControl Document Control; ICH Q9; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The primary regulatory authority whose standards and guidelines govern this risk assessment (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Determines applicable GCP framework and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guideline; ICH E6(R2); MHRA GCP Guidance

description: Documented plan for addressing identified gaps and reducing residual risk, including specific actions, responsible parties, and target timelines. Distinct from CAPA (which is a formal corrective/preventive action record); this is the risk-level remediation narrative.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Current status of remediation activities for identified gaps: not_started, in_progress, completed, or overdue. Supports inspection readiness dashboards and QA oversight reporting.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; MasterControl Workflow

description: Target completion date for the remediation actions identified in the risk assessment. Used for tracking remediation progress and escalation when overdue.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; MasterControl CAPA Tracking

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the ability to detect the risk after control measures are in place. Component of the residual risk score calculation.
reference: ICH Q9 FMEA Methodology; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the severity of impact after control measures have been applied. Used to calculate the residual risk score.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the probability of the risk occurring after control measures have been applied. Used to calculate the residual risk score.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Qualitative risk rating after control measures are applied: critical, high, medium, or low. Drives risk acceptance decisions, escalation to DSMB/DMC, and CAPA initiation. A residual rating of critical or high typically triggers mandatory CAPA.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Composite numeric risk score calculated after control measures are applied. Represents the remaining risk exposure. Compared against risk acceptance thresholds to determine if further mitigation is required.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA Reflection Paper on RBQM

description: Defined interval in calendar days between periodic re-assessments of this risk (e.g., 30, 60, 90, 180 days). Drives scheduling of next_review_date and ensures ongoing risk monitoring per ICH Q9 principles.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance

description: Formal decision on whether the residual risk level is acceptable: accepted (risk within tolerance), not_accepted (requires further mitigation or CAPA), or conditionally_accepted (accepted with defined monitoring conditions). Documented per ICH Q9 risk acceptance principles.
reference: ICH Q9; FDA RBQM Guidance; EMA GCP Guideline

description: High-level classification of the risk domain being assessed: data_integrity (ALCOA+ violations, EDC issues), patient_safety (adverse event under-reporting, protocol deviations), regulatory_compliance (GCP, ICH, FDA/EMA non-compliance), or operational (site performance, resource, supply chain). Drives escalation and reporting pathways.

description: Detailed narrative describing the identified risk, including the potential failure mode, root cause hypothesis, and potential impact on study quality, patient safety, or regulatory compliance. Sourced from MasterControl risk assessment form.

description: Organizational department of the risk owner (e.g., Quality Assurance, Data Management, Clinical Operations, Regulatory Affairs). Used for departmental risk reporting and workload analysis.

description: More granular classification within the risk category (e.g., 'Source Data Verification', 'Informed Consent Process', 'SAE Reporting Timeliness', 'Protocol Deviation Management'). Enables detailed risk trending and root cause analysis.

description: The functional role this document plays within this specific regulatory submission binder (e.g., primary_evidence, supporting_reference, administrative). The same document may serve different roles in different binders.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: The eCTD specification version used to structure and validate this submission (e.g., 3.2.2 for legacy eCTD, 4.0 for next-generation eCTD). Determines the XML backbone schema and validation rules applied during submission compilation.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD v4.0; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide v4.5; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: The date on which the regulatory authorization or IND/CTA clearance expires and requires renewal. Applicable for conditional approvals, time-limited authorizations, and annual IND renewals. Null for permanent approvals.
reference: EMA Conditional MA Guideline; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.54 (IND Annual Report)

description: The date on which this TMF document was formally added to the regulatory binder. Belongs to the relationship, not to either entity — the same document may have been added to different binders on different dates.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Format Flag
description: Indicates whether this regulatory binder is assembled and submitted in eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) format as required by ICH M8. True = eCTD format; False = non-eCTD (e.g., paper or NeeS format for legacy submissions).
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Indicates whether this regulatory binder has been reviewed and certified as inspection-ready per GCP and DIA TMF Reference Model standards. True = inspection-ready; False = not yet certified. Critical for regulatory inspection preparedness programs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, regulatory strategy notes, agency feedback summaries, or operational comments related to this regulatory binder. Not intended for structured data; used for qualitative annotations by regulatory affairs staff.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault Regulatory

business_glossary_term: Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) Target Action Date
description: The FDA PDUFA goal date by which the agency commits to completing its review and taking action on the submission. Applicable to NDA/BLA submissions to FDA. Used for regulatory timeline management and commercial launch planning.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; FDA 21 CFR Part 314.101; FDA MDUFA (for devices)

description: The internally planned or target date for submitting the regulatory binder to the health authority. Used for project planning, resource allocation, and regulatory milestone tracking. Compared against actual submission_date to measure submission timeliness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA PDUFA Milestone Tracking; EMA Procedural Guidance

description: The date on which the mandatory regulatory retention period for this binder expires, after which the documents may be destroyed per applicable regulations. Calculated based on archival_date plus jurisdiction-specific retention requirements (e.g., 15 years post-approval for EU, 2 years post-marketing authorization for ICH).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 58; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: The regulatory review designation or pathway applicable to this submission (e.g., standard, priority review, accelerated approval, breakthrough therapy, orphan drug, conditional marketing authorization). Determines review timelines and agency commitments.
reference: FDA PDUFA; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.80 (Accelerated); FDA Breakthrough Therapy Guidance; EMA Conditional MA Guideline; FDA Orphan Drug Act

description: The ordering or sequence position of this document within its CTD section in the binder. Used to control document ordering during eCTD compilation and health authority review. Specific to this binder-document filing.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Categorizes the submission as an original filing, amendment, supplement, annual report, or response to agency queries. Determines the review pathway and applicable regulatory timelines.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.70; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.31; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008

description: The date on which the regulatory binder was formally submitted to the target health authority. This is the principal business event date for the submission lifecycle and is used for regulatory timeline tracking and PDUFA/review clock calculations.
reference: FDA PDUFA; FDA 21 CFR Part 314.101; EMA Procedural Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: The physical or electronic medium used to deliver the submission to the health authority (electronic = eCTD gateway/portal, paper = physical binders, hybrid = combination). FDA mandates electronic submission for most NDA/BLA/IND filings.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.50(l) (Electronic Submissions); FDA ESG (Electronic Submissions Gateway); EMA eSubmission Gateway

description: The electronic gateway or portal used to transmit the submission to the health authority (e.g., FDA ESG - Electronic Submissions Gateway, EMA eSubmission Gateway, PMDA eCTD Gateway). Used for submission tracking and technical troubleshooting.
reference: FDA ESG Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eSubmission Gateway Guidance; PMDA eCTD Submission Guidance

description: Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to this submission package (e.g., '0000' for initial submission, '0001' for first amendment). Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD specification and is used by health authorities to track submission history.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: The regulatory health authority to which this binder is submitted (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA). Determines applicable regulatory requirements, submission format, and review timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|ANVISA|NMPA|COFEPRIS — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; PMDA Pharmaceutical Affairs Law; MHRA Guidance; ICH M4 CTD

description: The clinical therapeutic area or disease indication covered by this regulatory submission (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio management, regulatory strategy, and agency routing.
reference: MedDRA SOC Classification; ICH E6(R2); FDA CDER/CBER Division Alignment

description: The timestamp when this regulatory binder record was last modified. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Used to detect stale records and track binder lifecycle changes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Veeva Vault Regulatory Binder Reference ID
description: The unique document or binder object identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/Regulatory module) that corresponds to this regulatory binder record. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: Veeva Vault Regulatory; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The date on which the sponsor formally withdrew the regulatory submission from health authority review. Null if not withdrawn. Used for regulatory portfolio tracking and lessons-learned analysis.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.65; EMA Withdrawal Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Free-text description of the reason for withdrawing the regulatory submission (e.g., safety concerns, commercial decision, insufficient efficacy data). Populated only when withdrawal_date is set. Required for regulatory portfolio governance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.65; EMA Withdrawal Guidance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the document inspection readiness record. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Reference to the formal CAPA record created in MasterControl to address inspection findings. Links this inspection readiness record to the quality management CAPA workflow.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; MasterControl CAPA; FDA BIMO

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Vault Study ID
description: The Veeva Vault eTMF system identifier for the study's TMF vault, enabling direct cross-reference to the source system of record for document completeness metrics and readiness scores.
reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Document inspection readiness assessments are conducted in preparation for specific regulatory inspections. Linking the document readiness record to the regulatory inspection event enables cross-domai
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are conducted at the site file level — the ISF/regulatory binder maintained at each investigator site is the primary artifact reviewed during GCP inspections. Linking
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the investigational site when the TMF inspection readiness record is scoped to a specific site file (tmf_scope = 'site-specific'). Null for full-study or sponsor-section scoped records.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are conducted pre-submission (NDA/BLA/MAA) to ensure TMF completeness. Linking inspection readiness records to the target submission enables submission-gated readiness
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_completeness. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are directly informed by TMF completeness assessments. The tmf_completeness_percentage DECIMAL in document_inspection_readiness is a denormalized value from tmf_comple
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection concluded. Used to calculate inspection duration and trigger post-inspection response workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

description: The actual date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection commenced. Populated once the inspection begins; used to measure planning accuracy against target inspection date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required as a result of inspection findings. True when critical or major findings necessitate a structured CAPA response per GCP quality management requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; MasterControl CAPA; ISO 9001

description: The timestamp when this inspection readiness record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Number of critical-severity document gaps or deficiencies that could result in a regulatory finding or inspection observation if not remediated prior to inspection. Subset of open_findings_count.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

business_glossary_term: DIA TMF Reference Model Zone Coverage
description: Comma-separated list of DIA TMF Reference Model zones (e.g., Zone 1-Trial Management, Zone 2-Central Trial Documents, Zone 3-Regulatory, Zone 4-IRB/IEC, Zone 5-Site Management, Zone 6-IP, Zone 7-Safety Reporting, Zone 8-Statistics) included in the scope of this readiness assessment.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: The regulatory authority or organization conducting or expected to conduct the inspection (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Drives jurisdiction-specific readiness requirements and document standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|ANVISA|CFDA|sponsor|internal — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; MHRA GCP Inspectorate; PMDA; ICH E6(R2)

description: The date on which the regulatory authority formally notified the sponsor or CRO of the upcoming inspection. Triggers the formal inspection preparation timeline and escalation procedures.
reference: FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; MHRA GCP Inspectorate; ICH E6(R2)

description: The formal outcome classification issued by the regulatory authority following inspection closure. FDA classifications: No Action Indicated (NAI), Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), Official Action Indicated (OAI). Populated post-inspection; null or 'pending' during active inspection.
reference: FDA BIMO Classification; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2)

description: Red-Amber-Green (RAG) traffic-light classification of overall inspection readiness. 'green' indicates ready for inspection; 'amber' indicates preparation in progress with manageable gaps; 'red' indicates significant deficiencies requiring urgent remediation before inspection.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The date on which the regulatory authority issued the formal inspection report or Form FDA 483 (for FDA inspections). Triggers the response timeline for addressing inspection observations.
reference: FDA Form 483; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; MHRA GCP Inspectorate

description: Classification of the regulatory or sponsor inspection for which readiness is being tracked. GCP inspections are conducted by regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA); TMF inspections focus on document completeness; sponsor inspections are internal quality audits; for-cause inspections are triggered by specific concerns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gcp|tmf|sponsor|regulatory-authority|for-cause|routine — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Bioresearch Monitoring (BIMO); EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; MHRA GCP Inspectorate

description: The date on which the most recent internal TMF readiness review was conducted. Used to assess currency of the readiness assessment and trigger periodic re-reviews per SOP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Number of major-severity document gaps identified during TMF readiness review. Major gaps are significant deficiencies that require remediation but are less severe than critical gaps.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Number of minor-severity document gaps or observations identified during TMF readiness review. Minor gaps are low-risk deficiencies that should be addressed but do not pose immediate inspection risk.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Number of expected TMF artifacts per the DIA TMF Reference Model that are entirely absent from the eTMF system. Distinct from overdue documents which are expected but not yet filed.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The date on which the next scheduled internal TMF readiness review is planned. Supports proactive inspection readiness management and escalation when overdue.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Free-text field for the TMF Manager to document key observations, risk factors, remediation priorities, or contextual information relevant to the inspection readiness status of this study's TMF.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Total number of unresolved inspection readiness findings (gaps, deficiencies, or observations) identified during internal TMF reviews. A key KPI for inspection readiness dashboards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; FDA BIMO

description: Number of TMF documents that are past their expected filing deadline per the TMF filing timeline or DIA TMF Reference Model expectations. Indicates timeliness risk for inspection readiness.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Date of the most recent prior regulatory inspection for this study, if applicable. Used to assess inspection history, recurrence risk, and whether prior findings have been adequately remediated.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

description: The formal outcome of the most recent prior regulatory inspection for this study. Provides historical context for current readiness planning and risk assessment.
reference: FDA BIMO Classification; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2)

description: Externally-known business identifier for this inspection readiness record, used in communications with sponsors, regulatory authorities, and internal tracking systems (e.g., Veeva Vault eTMF). Format: IR-{StudyCode}-{Sequence}.
reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2)

description: Quantitative score (0.00–100.00) representing the overall inspection readiness of the study's TMF, calculated based on document completeness, quality metrics, and open findings. Sourced from Veeva Vault eTMF completeness metrics or internal scoring methodology.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; Veeva Vault eTMF; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8

description: Current lifecycle state of the TMF inspection readiness process for this study. 'not-started' indicates no preparation has begun; 'in-preparation' indicates active remediation; 'ready' indicates the TMF has passed internal review; 'under-inspection' indicates the regulatory authority is actively inspecting; 'closed' indicates the inspection cycle is complete.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The deadline by which the sponsor or CRO must submit a formal written response to the regulatory authority's inspection observations or findings. Typically 15 business days for FDA Form 483 responses.
reference: FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; MHRA GCP Inspectorate; ICH E6(R2)

description: The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the upcoming regulatory inspection. Populated when sponsor_notified is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of the upcoming regulatory inspection. Required for sponsor oversight and co-inspection preparation per GCP obligations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR 312

description: The sponsor-assigned protocol number for the clinical study associated with this TMF inspection readiness record. Enables cross-referencing with regulatory submissions and sponsor systems.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: The anticipated or scheduled date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection is expected to commence. Used for readiness planning and milestone tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA BIMO; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

description: Defines the scope of the TMF covered by this inspection readiness record. 'full-study' covers the entire TMF; 'site-specific' covers a single investigator site file; 'sponsor-section' covers sponsor-held documents only; 'investigator-section' covers investigator-held documents only.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The timestamp when this inspection readiness record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: Version number of this inspection readiness record, incremented each time a formal readiness review is conducted and the record is updated. Supports audit trail and version history tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Investigator Site File (ISF) or Regulatory Binder record in the lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Site files must contain the CTA approval for the country where the site operates — a TMF essential document requirement. Linking site files to their governing CTA enables verification that the correct
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study with which this site file is associated. Links the ISF to its parent protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; CDISC SDTM

description: Reference to the investigational site at which this site file is maintained. Ties the ISF to a specific site entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Date on which the site file was formally activated and made available for document filing, coinciding with or following site initiation. Marks the start of the file's active lifecycle.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

value_regex: ICH E6(R2)|ICH E6(R3)|21 CFR Part 312|EU CTR 536/2014|ISO 14155
description: The specific GCP guideline version or regulation that governs the conduct of this site file. Determines essential document requirements, retention obligations, and inspection readiness criteria.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E6(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ISO 14155

description: Date on which the site file was transferred to long-term archival storage per ICH GCP and sponsor retention requirements. Typically 15 years post-study completion for IND studies.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 58

description: Physical or electronic location where the site file has been archived post-study closure (e.g., sponsor archive facility name, Veeva Vault archive vault path, or third-party archive vendor name).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date on which the site file was formally closed following site close-out activities. After this date, no new documents should be filed except for archival or regulatory response purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.4; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Numeric percentage (0.00–100.00) representing the proportion of expected essential documents that are present and current in the site file. Calculated at the time of the last completeness review. Used for KPI reporting and inspection readiness dashboards.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0

description: Overall completeness assessment of the site file relative to the expected essential documents per ICH GCP Section 8 and the DIA TMF Reference Model. Drives inspection readiness scoring and CRA monitoring activities.

description: Timestamp when the site file record was first created in the system of record (Veeva Vault or CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; ALCOA+

description: Role of the designated custodian responsible for the site file. PI (Principal Investigator) is the accountable party per GCP; CRC (Clinical Research Coordinator) typically manages day-to-day file maintenance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.9; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

business_glossary_term: Delegation of Authority Log On File Indicator
description: Indicates whether a current, signed Delegation of Authority (DoA) log is present in the site file, documenting all site staff authorized to perform study-related tasks and their qualifications.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.1.5; FDA Inspection Guidance

description: Folder path or document library location within Veeva Vault eTMF where the electronic site file is stored. Used for direct navigation and audit trail purposes.
reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the site file is maintained electronically (eTMF via Veeva Vault), in paper form at the site, or as a hybrid of both. Drives inspection readiness procedures and retrieval workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this site file, used in regulatory correspondence, inspection readiness tracking, and eTMF cross-referencing. Typically generated by the CTMS or Veeva Vault.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Current lifecycle state of the site file. Active indicates the file is in use during the study; Under Review indicates a quality or completeness review is in progress; Archived indicates post-study archival; Closed indicates the site has been closed out; Pending Activation indicates the file is being set up prior to site initiation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Classification of the site file by its regulatory and operational purpose. ISF (Investigator Site File) is the primary site-level essential document collection; Regulatory Binder holds IRB/IEC submissions and approvals; Pharmacy Binder tracks investigational product accountability; Laboratory Binder holds lab certifications and normal ranges; Investigator Binder holds PI-specific documents.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether signed financial disclosure forms for the investigator and sub-investigators are present in the site file, as required by FDA regulations to identify potential conflicts of interest.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 54; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2.11

business_glossary_term: Informed Consent Form (ICF) On File Indicator
description: Indicates whether the current, IRB/IEC-approved Informed Consent Form (ICF) is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 50; OHRP Guidance; GDPR Article 7

description: Assessment of the site file's readiness for regulatory inspection by authorities such as FDA, EMA, or MHRA. Ready indicates the file meets all GCP and regulatory requirements; Needs Remediation indicates minor gaps requiring correction; Critical Gaps indicates significant deficiencies that must be resolved before inspection.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; FDA Inspection Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; MHRA GCP Guidance

business_glossary_term: Investigator Curriculum Vitae (CV) On File Indicator
description: Indicates whether a current, signed curriculum vitae (CV) for the Principal Investigator and all sub-investigators is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2.10; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.53

description: Indicates whether the current, valid IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval letter is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 56; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether current laboratory certifications (e.g., CLIA, CAP) and normal reference ranges for the site's clinical laboratory are present in the site file, as required by ICH GCP Section 8.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8.2.15; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Date of the most recent regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other authority) at which this site file was reviewed. Used for inspection history tracking and readiness planning.

description: Date on which the most recent completeness review or quality check of the site file was performed, typically by a CRA (Clinical Research Associate) during a monitoring visit or central review.

description: Number of essential documents identified as missing or outstanding from the site file at the time of the last review. Drives follow-up actions and inspection readiness remediation.

description: Number of outstanding action items or findings related to the site file that have not yet been resolved. Derived from monitoring visit findings and quality reviews. Used for inspection readiness and site risk assessment.

description: Description of the physical storage location of the site file at the investigational site (e.g., room number, cabinet, shelf). Applicable for paper or hybrid files. Required for inspection readiness and retrieval.

description: Indicates whether the signed and dated study protocol (and all applicable amendments) is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.

description: Primary regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the investigational site and its site file requirements. description: Date on which the sponsor or CRO completed the most recent formal review of the site file. Supports audit trail and oversight documentation requirements.

description: DIA TMF Reference Model section code within the assigned zone (e.g., 06.01 for Site Initiation). Provides granular classification for document filing and completeness tracking.

description: Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF to the site file container or root document object. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the lakehouse record and the source eTMF system.

description: The tab, section, or folder position of this document within the physical or electronic site binder (e.g., 'Section 3, Tab 2'). This positional reference belongs to the filing relationship, not to the document or the binder independently.

description: The date on which this TMF document was physically or electronically filed into the site file. Belongs to the filing event, not to the document or the binder independently.

description: The date on which a Clinical Research Associate (CRA) last verified this specific document at this specific site during a monitoring visit. Belongs to the site-document filing record, not to the document globally or the site file as a whole.

description: Short-form abbreviation or acronym for the artifact type as used in the DIA TMF Reference Model and industry practice (e.g., 'IB' for Investigator Brochure, 'ICF' for Informed Consent Form, 'SAP' for Statistical Analysis Plan). Used in compact reporting views and eTMF system labels.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH GCP E6(R2)

description: Standardized DIA TMF Reference Model artifact code in Zone.Section.Artifact format (e.g., 01.01.01 for Zone 1, Section 1, Artifact 1 — Protocol). Serves as the externally-recognized business identifier for the artifact type across sponsors, CROs, and regulatory agencies.

description: Detailed narrative description of the artifact type, its purpose within the TMF, and the business context in which it is generated or collected. description: Official DIA TMF Reference Model name for the artifact type (e.g., 'Protocol', 'Investigator Brochure', 'Informed Consent Form'). This is the human-readable identity label used in TMF completeness reports, inspection readiness dashboards, and eTMF system configuration.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Sequential numeric identifier of the artifact within its section per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., Artifact 01 within Section 1.1). Provides the third and most granular level of the TMF hierarchy. Used in combination with zone_number and section_number to uniquely locate a document type in the TMF structure.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Current lifecycle status of this artifact taxonomy record within the DIA TMF Reference Model (Active = currently in use, Deprecated = removed from the model, Superseded = replaced by a newer artifact definition, Draft = under review for inclusion). business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Controlled Terminology

description: Numeric weight assigned to this artifact type for TMF completeness index calculation (expressed as a percentage or score contribution). Required artifacts typically carry higher weights than Recommended or Optional. Used in the TMF completeness KPI dashboard to produce a weighted completeness score for inspection readiness reporting.

description: The date and time when this artifact taxonomy record was first created in the data platform. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp per ALCOA+ principles and supports data lineage tracking for the TMF reference taxonomy.

business_glossary_term: Document Information Association (DIA) TMF Reference Model Version
description: Version of the DIA TMF Reference Model from which this artifact definition is sourced (e.g., 'v3.2', 'v3.1'). Enables version-controlled TMF taxonomy management and supports migration assessments when the DIA releases updated Reference Model versions. Critical for audit trail and regulatory inspection documentation.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Standard organizational role responsible for generating, maintaining, and filing documents of this artifact type (e.g., 'Sponsor', 'Principal Investigator', 'CRA', 'CRC', 'Biostatistician', 'Regulatory Affairs'). reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 5; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The date from which this artifact definition became effective and applicable for use in TMF filing and completeness assessments. Aligns with the release date of the DIA TMF Reference Model version referenced by dia_reference_model_version. Supports temporal validity management of the artifact taxonomy.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Describes the expected timing for filing this artifact relative to trial milestones (e.g., 'Before First Patient First Visit', 'Within 30 days of site initiation', 'At study close-out', 'Ongoing throughout trial'). Used to assess timeliness of TMF filing and flag overdue documents in inspection readiness dashboards.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA TMF Guidance

description: Operational instructions for TMF staff on how, when, and where to file documents of this artifact type within the eTMF system (Veeva Vault). Includes guidance on acceptable document formats, naming conventions, version control expectations, and timing relative to trial milestones. Supports ALCOA+ compliance and inspection readiness.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ Principles; Veeva Vault eTMF

business_glossary_term: International Council for Harmonisation Good Clinical Practice (ICH GCP) Reference
description: Specific ICH E6(R2) GCP guideline section(s) that mandate or reference this artifact type (e.g., 'ICH E6(R2) Section 8.2.1', 'ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.6'). Provides the regulatory basis for the artifact's required status and supports inspection readiness documentation and regulatory query responses.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice Guideline; ICH E6(R3) Draft

description: Indicates whether this artifact type requires a separate instance per country in which the trial is conducted (True) or is filed at the global trial level (False). Drives country-level TMF completeness calculations and supports multi-jurisdictional regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether this artifact type is classified as an Essential Document per ICH E6(R2) Section 8 (True). Essential documents are those that individually and collectively permit evaluation of the conduct of a trial and the quality of the data produced. This flag drives the core TMF completeness index calculation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Indicates whether this artifact type is considered critical during regulatory inspections by FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or other health authorities (True). Inspection-critical artifacts are prioritized in pre-inspection TMF readiness reviews and gap remediation activities. Absence of inspection-critical artifacts typically results in a major GCP finding.
reference: FDA GCP Inspection Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; MHRA GCP Guidance

business_glossary_term: TMF Artifact Investigator Site File (ISF) Held Indicator
description: Indicates whether this artifact type is held in the Investigator Site File (ISF) at the investigational site (True). Supports site file completeness assessments during monitoring visits (SDV/SIV/COV) and regulatory inspections. CRAs use this flag to verify site file completeness against the DIA TMF Reference Model.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3; FDA GCP Inspection Guidance

description: Indicates whether this artifact type requires a separate instance per investigational site (True) or is filed once at the trial or country level (False). Drives the expected document count calculation in TMF completeness metrics — site-specific artifacts multiply by the number of active sites.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Indicates whether this artifact type is held in the Sponsor's TMF (True) as opposed to the Investigator Site File (ISF) or a shared location. Supports TMF structure design, responsibility allocation between sponsor and investigator, and completeness tracking across the distributed TMF.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA TMF Guidance

description: Free-text field for additional clarifications, implementation guidance, or sponsor-specific customizations applied to this artifact definition. May include notes on jurisdictional variations, therapeutic area-specific applicability, or deviations from the standard DIA TMF Reference Model definition as agreed with the sponsor.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Additional regulatory citations beyond ICH GCP that reference or mandate this artifact type, including FDA 21 CFR Part 312, EU CTR 536/2014, EMA GCP Guideline, MHRA GCP Guidance, or PMDA requirements. Supports multi-jurisdictional TMF compliance and inspection readiness for global trials.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; EMA GCP Guideline; MHRA GCP Guidance; PMDA GCP Ordinance

description: DIA TMF Reference Model classification indicating whether this artifact is Required (mandatory per ICH GCP), Recommended (best practice), or Optional (situational) for a compliant TMF. Drives TMF completeness scoring, gap analysis, and inspection readiness assessments. Required artifacts missing from the TMF constitute a GCP finding.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guideline

business_glossary_term: TMF Artifact Wet Signature Required Indicator

description: Minimum regulatory retention period in years for documents of this artifact type after trial completion or product approval/discontinuation. description: The calendar date on which the TMF completeness assessment was performed. Represents the point-in-time snapshot date for this record.

description: The end date of the period covered by this TMF completeness assessment. For periodic assessments, this defines the end of the reporting window.

description: The start date of the period covered by this TMF completeness assessment. For periodic assessments, this defines the beginning of the reporting window.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2)

description: Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this completeness assessment, used for tracking and cross-referencing in Veeva Vault eTMF and inspection-readiness reports.

description: Current workflow state of the completeness assessment record within Veeva Vault: draft (initiated but not yet submitted), in_progress (actively being assessed), completed (assessment data entry finished), reviewed (QA review completed), approved (formally approved by TMF manager or QA lead).

description: Classification of the assessment trigger: scheduled (routine periodic), ad_hoc (unplanned), pre_inspection (triggered by regulatory inspection notice), milestone (triggered by a study milestone such as first patient in or database lock), or close_out (triggered by study close-out activities).

description: The minimum completeness percentage threshold required for this study at this point in time, as defined in the TMF plan or sponsor SOP. Used to determine whether the study is inspection-ready.

description: Directional trend of TMF completeness compared to the prior assessment: improving (percentage increased), stable (no significant change), declining (percentage decreased), or not_applicable (first assessment for this study).

description: The date and time when this TMF completeness assessment record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.

description: Number of missing or overdue artifacts classified as 'critical' per the DIA TMF Reference Model or sponsor TMF plan (e.g., protocol, ethics approval, signed ICF). Critical gaps pose the highest inspection risk.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) System Snapshot ID
description: The unique identifier of the eTMF system snapshot or report generated in Veeva Vault at the time of this assessment. Enables traceability back to the source system record for audit purposes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Total number of TMF artifacts (documents) expected to be filed for this study at the time of assessment, based on the applicable DIA TMF Reference Model zones and study-specific TMF plan.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Total number of TMF artifacts that have been filed (uploaded and indexed) in the eTMF system (Veeva Vault) at the time of assessment.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Free-text narrative summarizing the key findings, gaps, and observations identified during this TMF completeness assessment. Supports inspection-readiness reporting and CAPA initiation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Qualitative risk rating for inspection readiness based on completeness gaps, overdue artifacts, and zone-level deficiencies. Supports risk-based TMF monitoring and escalation decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1

description: Indicates whether the TMF is deemed inspection-ready based on the overall completeness percentage meeting or exceeding the completeness target. True = inspection-ready; False = not inspection-ready.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Number of expected TMF artifacts that have not yet been filed at the time of assessment. Calculated as expected_artifact_count minus filed_artifact_count, excluding overdue artifacts.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

business_glossary_term: Next TMF Completeness Assessment Due Date
description: The scheduled due date for the next TMF completeness assessment for this study, based on the TMF plan frequency (e.g., monthly, quarterly). Supports proactive inspection readiness monitoring.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2); Sponsor SOP

description: The overall TMF completeness percentage calculated as (number of filed artifacts / number of expected artifacts) × 100. Principal quantitative KPI for inspection readiness monitoring. Expressed as a percentage with two decimal places.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance

description: Number of TMF artifacts that are past their required filing deadline (per the TMF plan or DIA TMF Reference Model timelines) and have not yet been filed. A key inspection-readiness risk indicator.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance

description: Number of TMF artifacts that have been filed but are awaiting QC review or approval in Veeva Vault. These are filed but not yet finalized, and may affect the effective completeness rate.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; Veeva Vault eTMF; ICH E6(R2)

description: Number of TMF artifacts that were filed but subsequently rejected during QC review (e.g., wrong version, illegible, incorrect metadata). Rejected artifacts are not counted as filed for completeness purposes.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Indicates whether this assessment identified gaps requiring formal remediation actions (e.g., CAPA initiation, escalation to study team). True = remediation required; False = no remediation required.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; MasterControl CAPA

description: The calendar date on which the QA reviewer completed their review of this completeness assessment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1

description: Full name of the QA reviewer or TMF lead who reviewed and approved the completeness assessment. Required for inspection-readiness documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The clinical development phase of the study at the time of assessment (Phase 1, 2, 3, 4, or combined phases). Contextualizes completeness expectations against phase-specific TMF requirements.
reference: ICH E8(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation EU CTR 536/2014

description: The operational status of the clinical study at the time this TMF completeness assessment was conducted. Used to contextualize completeness expectations — e.g., a study in close-out has higher completeness requirements than one in start-up.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1

description: Defines the scope of the TMF assessed: full_study (entire sponsor TMF), sponsor_tmf (sponsor-held documents only), site_tmf (site-held documents only), or investigator_site_file (ISF at a specific site). Determines which DIA TMF Reference Model zones and artifacts are in scope.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: The date and time when this TMF completeness assessment record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 1 (Trial Management), covering documents related to trial management, oversight, and administration.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1 Zone 1

business_glossary_term: TMF Zone 2 (Central Trial Documents) Completeness Percentage
description: Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 2 (Central Trial Documents), covering the protocol, amendments, investigator brochure, and other central study documents.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.1 Zone 2

description: Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3 (Regulatory), covering regulatory submissions, approvals, and correspondence with health authorities such as FDA, EMA, and PMDA.

business_glossary_term: TMF Zone 4 (IRB/IEC and Other Approvals) Completeness Percentage
description: Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4 (IRB/IEC and Other Approvals), covering ethics committee approvals, informed consent forms (ICF), and other site-level approvals.

description: Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 5 (Site Management), covering site selection, initiation, monitoring, and close-out documents.

description: Indicates whether the required response has been submitted to the health authority. Used in conjunction with response_required_flag and response_due_date for compliance monitoring and inspection readiness.
