description: The eCTD submission sequence number associated with this correspondence, when the correspondence is linked to a specific eCTD submission sequence. Enables traceability between correspondence and dossier submission history.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this correspondence record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., Veeva Vault eTMF, Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles

description: Name of the sponsor or CRO regulatory affairs representative responsible for this correspondence. Used for accountability, audit trail, and inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5; 21 CFR Part 312.23; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Free-text or structured description of the primary subject or topic of the correspondence (e.g., 'Protocol Amendment Review', 'Clinical Hold Notification', 'Safety Data Request'). Used for regulatory intelligence and search.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Structured executive summary of the correspondence content, key regulatory questions raised, commitments made, or decisions communicated. Used for regulatory intelligence, briefing documents, and inspection preparation.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Veeva Vault eTMF; EU CTR 536/2014

description: The TMF reference model section under which this correspondence is filed in the eTMF (e.g., Zone 1.2 Regulatory, Zone 1.2.1 Health Authority Correspondence). Ensures correct eTMF filing and completeness tracking.
reference: DIA TMF Reference Model v3.0; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU CTR 536/2014; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The method by which the correspondence was transmitted to or received from the health authority (eCTD electronic submission gateway, email, fax, postal mail, hand delivery). Required for audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG (Electronic Submissions Gateway); EMA eSubmission Gateway; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The timestamp when this correspondence record was most recently modified in the system. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and data lineage in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF where the correspondence document is stored. Enables direct linkage between the correspondence metadata record and the source document in the eTMF system of record.
reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014 TMF Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the health authority record in the regulatory reference master. Primary key for all downstream FK references across submission, application, correspondence, and authorization records.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Widely recognized abbreviation for the health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, HC). Used in reporting dashboards, regulatory tracking systems, and internal communications.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; WHO ICTRP

description: Short, standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, HC, TGA, ANVISA, NMPA). Used as a business key in regulatory workflows, submission routing, and eCTD gateway configuration.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG; EMA eSubmission Gateway

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the health authority record. Active indicates the agency is currently accepting submissions; inactive indicates it is no longer operational; merged indicates it has been absorbed into another agency; superseded indicates it has been replaced by a successor body.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Type of periodic safety report required by this health authority for ongoing clinical trials (e.g., DSUR - Development Safety Update Report per ICH E2F for EMA/MHRA, IND Annual Report per 21 CFR 312.33 for FDA, PBRER for post-marketing). Drives safety reporting schedule configuration.
reference: ICH E2F (DSUR); 21 CFR Part 312.33 (IND Annual Report); ICH E2C(R2) (PBRER)

description: Classification of the health authority by its jurisdictional scope: national (single country, e.g., FDA, PMDA), supranational (multi-country, e.g., EMA), regional (sub-national or regional body), or reference_agency (recognized reference authority used by other agencies for reliance procedures).
reference: WHO ICTRP; ICH E6(R2); EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the health authority requires mandatory registration of clinical trials in a public registry (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov for FDA, EU Clinical Trials Register for EMA, JAPIC for PMDA) as a condition of authorization or publication. Drives trial registration workflow in CTMS.
reference: WHO ICTRP; ICMJE; 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 81; FDAAA 801

description: Name of the primary regulatory division or office within the health authority responsible for receiving submissions and correspondence for the sponsor's therapeutic area (e.g., FDA CDER Office of New Drugs, EMA Human Medicines Division, PMDA New Drug Division). Used to address regulatory correspondence and meeting requests.
reference: FDA CDER; EMA Human Medicines; ICH E6(R2)

description: Official email address for regulatory correspondence with the health authority's primary contact division. Used for submission cover letter addressing, meeting request submissions, and regulatory query responses.
reference: ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 4; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Official telephone number for the health authority's primary regulatory contact division. Used for urgent regulatory communications, inspection notifications, and meeting coordination.
reference: ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 4

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction in which the health authority operates (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, AUS, CAN, BRA, CHN). Drives country-level regulatory strategy and submission planning.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ICH M8 eCTD

description: Timestamp when this health authority record was first created in the regulatory reference master. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles for regulatory data.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the health authority mandates submission of dossiers in the ICH Common Technical Document (CTD) format. True for ICH member agencies (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada); may be false for non-ICH jurisdictions with proprietary submission formats.
reference: ICH M4 CTD; ICH M8 eCTD

description: Primary data privacy legislation or framework applicable to clinical trial data collected under this health authority's jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR for EMA/MHRA, HIPAA for FDA, PIPEDA for Health Canada, APPI for PMDA). Governs patient data handling, consent requirements, and cross-border data transfer restrictions.
reference: GDPR (EU 2016/679); HIPAA 45 CFR Parts 160/164; PIPEDA; APPI (Japan); OHRP 45 CFR Part 46

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Gateway Identifier
description: Unique identifier assigned to the health authority's eCTD submission gateway endpoint (e.g., FDA ESG sender ID, EMA DUNS number for eSubmission). Required for configuring automated eCTD transmission via Veeva Vault and validating submission acknowledgement receipts.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eSubmission Gateway Guidance

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Specification Version
description: Version of the eCTD technical specification required by this health authority for electronic submissions (e.g., 3.2.2, 4.0). Governs the XML backbone structure, validation criteria, and submission package format for IND, NDA, BLA, and MAA applications.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Specification v3.2.2 / v4.0; EMA eCTD Specification

business_glossary_term: Health Authority Record Effective From Date
description: Date from which this health authority record is considered valid and active for use in regulatory submissions and correspondence. Supports temporal validity tracking for agencies that have been established, reorganized, or renamed.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault Regulatory; ISO 8601

business_glossary_term: Health Authority Record Effective To Date
description: Date after which this health authority record is no longer valid (e.g., due to agency merger, dissolution, or supersession). Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date. Supports historical regulatory record integrity.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault Regulatory; ISO 8601

description: Indicates whether the health authority mandates electronic submission (eCTD or equivalent) for regulatory applications. True for FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA; may be false for agencies still accepting paper or hybrid submissions. Governs Veeva Vault submission package configuration.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG; EMA eSubmission Gateway; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date from which the current regulatory fee schedule for this health authority is effective. Fee schedules are typically updated annually (e.g., FDA PDUFA fees effective October 1 each fiscal year). Used to ensure correct fee amounts are applied in SAP S/4HANA budget planning.
reference: FDA PDUFA Fee Schedule; EMA Fee Regulation; PMDA Fee Schedule; ISO 8601

description: Complete official name of the health authority organization (e.g., U.S. Food and Drug Administration, European Medicines Agency, Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency). Used in regulatory correspondence, submission cover letters, and CTD/eCTD dossier headers.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA 21 CFR; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Version of the GCP guideline mandated or recognized by this health authority (e.g., ICH E6(R2), ICH E6(R3), EU CTR GCP Annex). Governs clinical trial conduct standards, monitoring requirements, and inspection readiness criteria applicable to studies submitted to this agency.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E6(R3); EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 312

business_glossary_term: International Council for Harmonisation (ICH) Member Status
description: Indicates whether the health authority is a full member or observer of the International Council for Harmonisation of Technical Requirements for Pharmaceuticals for Human Use (ICH). ICH membership determines applicability of harmonized guidelines (CTD, eCTD, GCP, safety reporting) and mutual recognition procedures.
reference: ICH Membership List; ICH M4 CTD; ICH M8 eCTD

description: Local equivalent application type for initiating clinical trials under this health authority's jurisdiction (e.g., IND for FDA, CTA for EMA/MHRA/Health Canada, JIND for PMDA, CTN for TGA). Drives submission template selection and regulatory timeline planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|CTA|JIND|CTN|ANVISA_CTA|NMPA_IND — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (FDA IND); EU CTR 536/2014 (CTA); ICH E6(R2); TGA CTN Scheme

business_glossary_term: Investigational New Drug (IND) / Clinical Trial Authorization (CTA) Review Clock (Days)
description: Number of calendar days the health authority has to review and respond to a clinical trial authorization application before the sponsor may proceed (e.g., 30 days for FDA IND, 30 days for EMA CTA under EU CTR). Critical for study start-up timeline planning.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.40 (FDA); EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 8; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Investigational New Drug (IND) / Clinical Trial Authorization (CTA) Submission Fee Amount
description: Standard regulatory fee amount charged by this health authority for an IND or CTA application in the currency specified by submission_fee_currency. May be zero for agencies that do not charge IND/CTA fees (e.g., FDA does not charge IND fees). Used for regulatory budget planning.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (FDA - no IND fee); EU CTR 536/2014; EMA Fee Regulation; PMDA Fee Schedule

description: Indicates whether this health authority has the mandate and capability to conduct Good Clinical Practice (GCP) inspections of clinical trial sites, sponsors, and CROs. True for FDA, EMA (via national competent authorities), MHRA, PMDA. Drives inspection readiness program scope.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 78; MHRA GCP Inspection

description: Indicates whether the health authority requires submission documents (or portions thereof) to be translated into the local national language in addition to English. True for agencies such as PMDA (Japanese), NMPA (Chinese), ANVISA (Portuguese). Drives translation cost and timeline planning.
reference: PMDA Submission Guidelines; NMPA Regulations; ANVISA RDC; ICH M4 CTD

description: Official mailing address of the health authority's primary contact division for paper-based regulatory correspondence, original document submissions, and formal notifications where electronic submission is not accepted.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Comma-separated list of mutual recognition agreement frameworks in which this health authority participates (e.g., FDA-EMA Parallel Scientific Advice, MHRA-EMA MRA, ACSS - Australia-Canada-Singapore-Switzerland). Informs regulatory reliance strategy and submission optimization.
reference: FDA-EMA Parallel Scientific Advice Program; MHRA MRA; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: New Drug Application (NDA) / Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) Type
description: Local equivalent marketing authorization application type for this health authority (e.g., NDA/BLA for FDA, MAA for EMA, J-NDA for PMDA, ANDS for Health Canada). Used to configure submission workflows, fee schedules, and review clock tracking in Veeva Vault. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NDA|BLA|MAA|J-NDA|ANDS|ARTG — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA); 21 CFR Part 601 (BLA); EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD

business_glossary_term: New Drug Application (NDA) / Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) Submission Fee Amount
description: Standard regulatory submission fee amount charged by this health authority for a full marketing authorization application (NDA/BLA/MAA) in the currency specified by submission_fee_currency. Used for regulatory budget planning and SAP S/4HANA accounts payable. Updated annually per agency fee schedules.
reference: FDA PDUFA Fee Schedule (21 USC 379h); EMA Fee Regulation (EC) No 297/95; PMDA Fee Schedule

business_glossary_term: Non-Fatal Serious Adverse Drug Reaction (SADR) Expedited Reporting Clock (Hours)
description: Mandatory expedited safety reporting timeline in hours for non-fatal, non-life-threatening SUSARs to this health authority (e.g., 15 calendar days / 360 hours for FDA and EMA). Used alongside the fatal SUSAR clock to configure Oracle Argus Safety reporting deadlines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.32 (FDA); EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 42; ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2)

description: Free-text field for regulatory affairs teams to capture additional context, special submission requirements, known agency preferences, or recent regulatory guidance updates specific to this health authority that are not captured in structured fields.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault Regulatory

description: Indicates whether the health authority still accepts paper-based regulatory submissions as an alternative or supplement to electronic submissions. Relevant for agencies in transition to full eCTD mandates or for specific submission types (e.g., original INDs, amendments).
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eSubmission Policy

description: Number of calendar days in the expedited or priority review clock for marketing authorization applications under this health authority (e.g., 180 days for FDA priority NDA review, 150 days for EMA accelerated assessment). Supports regulatory strategy planning for breakthrough and priority designations.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; EMA Accelerated Assessment Guideline; ICH E6(R2)

description: Geopolitical or regulatory region associated with the health authority (e.g., North America, European Union, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, Middle East & Africa). Supports regional regulatory strategy grouping and multi-regional submission planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH M4 CTD

description: Primary regulatory framework or legislation governing the health authority's operations (e.g., 21 CFR Parts 312/314/601 for FDA, EU CTR 536/2014 for EMA, PMDA Act for Japan, Medicines Act for MHRA). Critical for determining applicable submission requirements and compliance obligations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); ISO 14155

description: Primary review procedure applicable under this health authority (e.g., Standard Review, Priority Review, Accelerated Assessment, Breakthrough Therapy, PRIME, Conditional Approval, Orphan Drug). Determines review timelines and regulatory strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority|accelerated|breakthrough|prime|conditional|orphan — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA PDUFA; EMA PRIME; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 314

business_glossary_term: Serious Adverse Drug Reaction (SADR) / Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Expedited Reporting Clock (Hours)
description: Mandatory expedited safety reporting timeline in hours for fatal or life-threatening SUSARs to this health authority (e.g., 7 calendar days / 168 hours for FDA, 7 days for EMA). Drives automated deadline calculation in Oracle Argus Safety for SUSAR and ICSR submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.32 (FDA); EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 42; ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2)

description: Number of calendar days in the standard regulatory review clock for marketing authorization applications under this health authority (e.g., 365 days for FDA standard NDA review, 210 days for EMA MAA). Used for regulatory milestone planning and submission timeline modeling.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; ICH E6(R2)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for regulatory submission fees charged by this health authority (e.g., USD for FDA PDUFA fees, EUR for EMA fees, JPY for PMDA fees). Used in SAP S/4HANA for regulatory fee budget planning and accounts payable processing.
reference: ISO 4217; FDA PDUFA Fee Schedule; EMA Fee Regulation (EC) No 297/95; PMDA Fee Schedule

description: ISO 639 language code(s) required for regulatory submissions to this health authority (e.g., en for English, ja for Japanese, zh for Chinese, de/fr/it for Switzerland). Governs translation requirements for CTD modules, labeling, and patient-facing documents.
reference: ISO 639; ICH M4 CTD; EU CTR 536/2014; PMDA Submission Guidelines

description: Official URL of the health authority's electronic submission portal (e.g., FDA ESG gateway, EMA eSubmission portal, PMDA eCTD gateway). Used by regulatory affairs teams to route eCTD dossier submissions and track submission acknowledgements.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA ESG Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eSubmission Gateway

description: Name of the primary public clinical trial registry mandated by this health authority for trial registration (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov for FDA, EU Clinical Trials Register for EMA, JAPIC CTI for PMDA, ANZCTR for TGA). Used to configure trial registration workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.
reference: WHO ICTRP; FDAAA 801; EU CTR 536/2014 Art. 81; ICMJE

description: Timestamp when this health authority record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory action item record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.agency_meeting. Business justification: Action items frequently arise from agency meetings (FDA Type A/B/C meetings, EMA scientific advice). The source_event_type field on regulatory_action_item can be 'agency_meeting', and action_items_cou
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA) under which this action item was generated. Provides dossier-level context for the task.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Regulatory action items are frequently generated from audit findings with regulatory implications. Linking action items to audit findings supports the audit-to-regulatory-action traceability chain req
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the associated CAPA record in MasterControl when this action item is linked to a quality event, inspection finding, or deviation. Nullable when no formal CAPA is required.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ISO 9001; MasterControl CAPA Module

description: Reference to the health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, etc.) whose interaction generated this action item. Drives jurisdiction-specific reporting and escalation rules.
reference: FDA; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2); PMDA; MHRA

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Action Item ID
description: Cross-reference identifier for this action item in Oracle Siebel CTMS, enabling bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CTMS system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS

description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the previous action item from which this one was derived (e.g., a re-opened or recurring item). Enables lineage tracking across action item generations. Nullable for original items.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Protocol deviations with regulatory reporting requirements generate regulatory action items. Linking action items to protocol deviations supports the deviation-to-regulatory-response workflow required
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.correspondence. Business justification: Action items can arise from regulatory correspondence (Complete Response Letters, Information Requests, deficiency letters). The source_event_type field on regulatory_action_item can be 'correspondenc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Action items frequently arise from regulatory inspection findings (critical, major, and minor findings). Linking regulatory_action_item to regulatory_inspection enables tracking all action items gener
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study (protocol) to which this regulatory action item belongs. Links the action item to the relevant trial context for reporting and filtering.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Reference to the specific regulatory submission (e.g., a response sequence, amendment, or safety report) that triggered or is associated with this action item. Nullable when the action item originates from a meeting or inspection rather than a submission.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314; ICH M4 CTD; Veeva Vault Submissions Module

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory action item responses (agency information requests, inspection findings responses) are filed as TMF documents. Linking action items to their response TMF documents enables inspection-ready
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to randomization.unblinding_request. Business justification: An unblinding event (especially SAE-triggered) generates regulatory action items: agency notification, protocol deviation reporting, or IND safety report submission. regulatory_action_item already tra
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Detailed narrative describing the specific regulatory task, finding, or commitment that must be addressed. Captures the full context of what is required, including any agency-specified language from meeting minutes or inspection reports.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the action item (e.g., RAI-2024-00123). Used in regulatory correspondence, meeting minutes, and inspection response packages to uniquely reference the task.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Veeva Vault Action Item Module

description: Current lifecycle state of the regulatory action item. Drives operational dashboards, escalation logic, and compliance reporting. 'overdue' is system-set when due_date has passed without closure.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Categorical classification of the nature of the action item. Drives workflow templates and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commitment|information_request|capa|protocol_amendment|label_update|response_to_agency|internal_task|other — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: The date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the response or deliverable. Used to confirm the regulatory clock was stopped and to document agency receipt for the eTMF.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: The specific response deadline formally communicated by the health authority (e.g., FDA 30-day information request clock, EMA Day 120 response). Distinct from the internal due_date which may be set earlier to allow review time. Nullable when no formal agency deadline exists.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; PMDA; MHRA

description: The date on which the completed response or deliverable was formally submitted to the health authority. Distinct from completion_date (internal closure) — captures the actual agency-facing delivery date for regulatory clock tracking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD

description: Email address of the individual responsible for completing this action item. Used for automated notifications, escalation alerts, and audit trail. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR and HIPAA.
reference: GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Full name of the individual (typically a Regulatory Affairs professional or CRA) responsible for completing this action item. Captured as a denormalized field for operational task management and audit trail purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 4

description: Name of the regulatory affairs team or functional group responsible for this action item (e.g., 'Global Regulatory Strategy', 'Submissions Team', 'Safety Reporting'). Supports team-level workload reporting and capacity planning.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; Oracle Siebel CTMS; Veeva Vault

description: The date on which the action item was fully completed and closed. Nullable until the item reaches 'closed' status. Used to calculate cycle time and measure regulatory responsiveness KPIs.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; 21 CFR Part 11

description: System timestamp recording when this regulatory action item record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+

description: The date by which the action item must be completed. May be agency-mandated (e.g., 30-day response clock from FDA information request) or internally set. Drives overdue status calculation and escalation triggers.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: The date on which this action item was escalated. Nullable when escalation_flag is False. Used to track escalation response time and management intervention patterns.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether this action item has been escalated to senior regulatory leadership or executive management due to risk, overdue status, or strategic importance. True = escalated; False = not escalated.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Narrative explanation of why this action item was escalated (e.g., 'Approaching agency deadline with incomplete data package', 'Potential clinical hold risk identified'). Provides context for management review.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: GCP/GMP inspection finding classification when the action item originates from an inspection finding. Aligns with FDA and EMA inspection classification standards. Nullable for non-inspection-sourced action items.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Free-text field for additional context, interim progress updates, or commentary on the action item that does not fit structured fields. Supports operational communication within the regulatory affairs team.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date on which this action item was formally opened and entered into the regulatory task register. Represents the business event timestamp for the creation of the obligation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault Action Item Module

description: Business priority assigned to the action item, reflecting urgency and regulatory risk. 'critical' typically indicates a potential clinical hold, safety signal, or imminent agency deadline. Used for resource allocation and escalation decisions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; CTCAE; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether this action item represents a recurrence of a previously identified issue or finding. True = recurrence of a prior finding; False = new issue. Used in quality trend analysis and CAPA effectiveness assessments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; ISO 9001 CAPA

description: Functional regulatory domain to which this action item belongs (e.g., clinical, safety, CMC). Enables domain-specific filtering and assignment routing within the regulatory affairs team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical|safety|cmc|regulatory_affairs|data_management|biostatistics|quality|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH M4 CTD; FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Reference to the specific regulatory procedure, guideline, or regulation that this action item must satisfy (e.g., '21 CFR 312.32', 'EMA/CHMP/ICH/295148/2004', 'ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18'). Provides the regulatory basis for the required action.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; ICH E6(R2); EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD

description: Title of the formal response document prepared to address this action item (e.g., 'Response to FDA Information Request Letter dated 2024-03-15'). Supports eTMF filing and regulatory correspondence tracking.
reference: ICH M4 CTD; FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Indicates the review cycle stage of this action item within an ongoing agency interaction (e.g., initial response, follow-up clarification, final closure). Supports multi-round agency dialogue tracking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Free-text or structured reference identifier for the originating event (e.g., meeting number, inspection report number, correspondence reference number, or internal review ID). Provides traceability back to the source document or event record.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Classification of the business event that originated this action item. Drives workflow routing and reporting dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agency_meeting|inspection_finding|regulatory_correspondence|internal_review|dsmb_recommendation|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory application record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for all downstream joins to submissions, correspondence, approvals, and commitments under this dossier.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether this application is approved or being reviewed under the FDA Accelerated Approval pathway, based on a surrogate or intermediate clinical endpoint reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit. Requires post-approval confirmatory trials.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314 Subpart H; 21 CFR Part 601 Subpart E; FDA Accelerated Approval guidance

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the health authority jurisdiction (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, CAN, AUS). Supports multi-regional dossier tracking and jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ICH M4 CTD; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Date the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the application. May differ from initial_filing_date if the agency issues a receipt acknowledgment letter after a validation check.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23; FDA Acknowledgment Letter guidance; EMA validation procedures under EU CTR 536/2014

business_glossary_term: IND Annual Report Last Submitted Date
description: Date the most recent IND Annual Report was submitted to FDA. Used to calculate the next due date and to confirm compliance with 21 CFR 312.33 annual reporting obligations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.33 (IND Annual Report); FDA IND Annual Report guidance

description: Externally assigned application number issued by the health authority (e.g., IND 123456, NDA 210001, BLA 761234, EMA/H/C/004321, CTA-2024-001). This is the official dossier identifier used in all agency correspondence and is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this entity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23 (IND); 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA); 21 CFR Part 601 (BLA); EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD

description: Current lifecycle status of the regulatory application. 'active' = application is open and under review or in use; 'approved' = marketing authorization or IND in good standing with approval; 'withdrawn' = sponsor voluntarily withdrew; 'refused' = agency issued a refuse-to-file or complete response letter with no resubmission; 'on_clinical_hold' = FDA-issued clinical hold per 21 CFR 312.42; 'inactive' = IND placed in inactive status.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42 (clinical hold); 21 CFR Part 314.110 (complete response); EU CTR 536/2014 Article 14; ICH E6(R2)

description: Classification of the regulatory application by submission type. IND = Investigational New Drug; NDA = New Drug Application; BLA = Biologics License Application; MAA = Marketing Authorization Application; CTA = Clinical Trial Authorization; IMPD = Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA); 21 CFR Part 601 (BLA); EU CTR 536/2014 (CTA/IMPD); ICH M4 CTD (MAA)

description: Date on which the health authority granted marketing authorization or IND approval. Null for applications that have not yet been approved. Critical milestone for commercial launch planning and post-approval commitment tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.105 (NDA approval); 21 CFR Part 601.4 (BLA approval); EMA Decision date; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Indicates whether FDA has granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) for this application, enabling intensive FDA guidance and rolling review. Granted when preliminary clinical evidence shows substantial improvement over available therapy for serious conditions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.300; FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation guidance; FDASIA Section 902

description: Date on which the FDA issued the clinical hold order for this application. Null if no clinical hold has been imposed. Used to track hold duration and response timelines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42; FDA Clinical Hold guidance

description: Current clinical hold status issued by FDA under 21 CFR 312.42. 'none' = no hold; 'partial' = hold on specific studies or patient populations; 'full' = all clinical investigations halted; 'lifted' = hold previously issued but now removed.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42 (Clinical Hold); FDA Clinical Hold guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Timestamp when this regulatory application record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ (Attributable Legible Contemporaneous Original Accurate); ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Indicates which CTD modules are included in this application. M1=Regional Administrative; M2=Summaries; M3=Quality; M4=Nonclinical; M5=Clinical. 'full' indicates all modules are present. Supports dossier completeness tracking.
reference: ICH M4 CTD; ICH M8 eCTD; FDA CTD guidance; EMA CTD guidance

description: ISO 639-2 three-letter language code of the primary language in which the regulatory dossier is submitted (e.g., ENG for English, JPN for Japanese, DEU for German). Relevant for multi-regional submissions requiring translation.
reference: ICH M4 CTD; EMA language requirements; PMDA submission guidelines; ISO 639-2

description: Date the application became legally effective and binding. For INDs, this is 30 days after FDA receipt unless a clinical hold is issued. For approved applications, this is the date the approval letter takes effect.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.40 (IND 30-day rule); 21 CFR Part 314.105; EU CTR 536/2014 Article 8

description: Date on which the application or authorization expires, if applicable. Relevant for CTAs with fixed authorization periods and some national approvals. Null for open-ended authorizations such as US NDA/BLA approvals.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014 Article 14; ICH E6(R2); Health Canada CTA regulations

description: Indicates whether FDA has granted Fast Track Designation for this application, facilitating development and expediting review of drugs to treat serious conditions and fill unmet medical needs. Enables rolling review and more frequent FDA interactions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.300; FDA Fast Track guidance; FDAMA Section 112

description: Next due date for the IND Annual Report submission to FDA, calculated as 60 days before the anniversary of the IND effective date. Tracks compliance with mandatory annual reporting obligations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.33 (IND Annual Report); FDA IND Annual Report guidance

description: FDA-assigned IND serial number for investigational applications (e.g., IND 123456). Populated only for IND-type applications. Used in all FDA correspondence, annual reports, and safety reporting.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23; FDA IND numbering system

description: Subclassification of the IND by purpose. Commercial IND is sponsor-held for drug development; Research IND is investigator-initiated; Treatment IND allows expanded access; Emergency IND is for single-patient emergency use.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.310 (Treatment IND); 21 CFR Part 312.310(d) (Emergency IND); FDA IND guidance

description: Date the application was first submitted to the health authority. For INDs, this is the date the IND package was received by FDA. For NDAs/BLAs, this is the date of original submission. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this entity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23 (IND receipt); 21 CFR Part 314.101 (NDA filing); EU CTR 536/2014 Article 5

description: Primary regulatory health authority to which this application is filed. Determines the regulatory pathway, submission format, and review timelines applicable to this dossier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|Health Canada|TGA|ANVISA|NMPA|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Parts 312/314/601; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M4 CTD; PMDA Pharmaceutical Affairs Law (Japan); MHRA UK Regulations

description: Current development phase associated with this regulatory application. Tracks the application from pre-IND through post-approval lifecycle. Supports portfolio planning and regulatory strategy alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_ind|phase_1|phase_2|phase_3|nda_bla_maa|post_approval|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E8 General Considerations for Clinical Studies; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA PDUFA VI Commitment Letter

description: Free-text field for capturing additional regulatory context, strategic notes, or agency-specific instructions not captured in structured fields. Used by regulatory affairs teams for internal documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault RIM notes field

description: Indicates whether the FDA Office of Orphan Products Development (OOPD) or EMA Committee for Orphan Medicinal Products (COMP) has granted Orphan Drug Designation for this application. Confers 7-year market exclusivity (FDA) or 10-year (EMA) and fee waivers.
reference: 21 CFR Part 316 (Orphan Drug); Orphan Drug Act; EMA Regulation (EC) No 141/2000

business_glossary_term: Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) Goal Date
description: FDA-assigned PDUFA action date by which the agency commits to completing its review of an NDA or BLA. Applicable only to marketing applications under PDUFA. Drives internal milestone planning and commercial readiness timelines.
reference: PDUFA VI Commitment Letter; 21 CFR Part 314.101; FDA PDUFA performance goals

description: Indicates whether the FDA has granted Priority Review designation to this NDA or BLA, reducing the standard review clock from 12 months to 6 months. Applicable to drugs offering major advances in treatment or providing treatment where no adequate therapy exists.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.510; FDA Priority Review guidance; PDUFA VI

description: Name of the drug, biologic, or device covered by this application. May be the INN (International Nonproprietary Name), proprietary brand name, or code name at the investigational stage.
reference: WHO INN Programme; 21 CFR Part 312.23(a)(3); ICH M4Q CTD Quality Module

description: Classification of the investigational or approved product by modality. Determines the applicable regulatory pathway, CMC requirements, and review division assignment at the agency.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (drugs/biologics); 21 CFR Part 820 (devices); EU CTR 536/2014; ICH Q6A/Q6B

description: Indicates whether the health authority has granted rolling review, allowing submission and review of completed sections of the dossier before the full application is submitted. Applicable under FDA Fast Track and EMA PRIME/rolling review procedures.
reference: FDA Fast Track Rolling Review guidance; EMA Rolling Review procedure; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Name of the sponsor's designated regulatory contact as listed on the application cover sheet (e.g., FDA Form 1571 for INDs). This individual is the official point of contact for agency communications.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23(a)(1); FDA Form 1571; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.1

description: Technical format used for dossier submission to the health authority. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA, EMA, PMDA; NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission) is a transitional format; paper submissions are legacy.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD guidance; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Further classification within the application type. Examples for IND: Commercial, Research, Treatment, Emergency; for NDA: 505(b)(1) Full, 505(b)(2), 505(j) ANDA; for BLA: Original, Supplement, Efficacy Supplement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Commercial|Research|Treatment|Emergency|505(b)(1)|505(b)(2)|505(j)|Original|Supplement|Efficacy Supplement — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.310 (Treatment IND); 21 CFR Part 314.50 (NDA subtypes); FDA PDUFA guidance

description: Broad disease or medical specialty area covered by the application (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease, Immunology). Used for portfolio segmentation, resource allocation, and regulatory strategy reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2); MedDRA SOC classification; FDA CDER/CBER therapeutic area taxonomy

description: Timestamp when this regulatory application record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: Cross-reference identifier linking this application record to the corresponding eTMF/regulatory dossier in Veeva Vault (the system of record for regulatory submissions and document management). Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the CTD dossier record. Primary key for this entity.

description: Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) to which this CTD dossier belongs.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the specific regulatory submission sequence this dossier version supports.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The CTD dossier compilation report and validation report are TMF Zone 1 documents. Linking the dossier record to its TMF document enables inspection-ready retrieval of the dossier package and supports
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the CTD dossier was actually submitted to the regulatory agency. Null until submission occurs.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA PDUFA Goal Dates; EMA Submission Timelines

description: Timestamp when the CTD dossier was formally approved internally for submission. Captures the approval event in the dossier lifecycle per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Physical or electronic archival location of the compiled CTD dossier package (e.g., Veeva Vault path, network share, or physical archive reference). Required for inspection readiness and long-term retention.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EU CTR 536/2014; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Free-text field for regulatory affairs team notes, submission strategy remarks, or outstanding action items related to the CTD dossier.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline

description: Timestamp when the CTD dossier record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Submission format of the CTD dossier. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA, EMA, and PMDA. NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission) and paper formats are legacy options.
reference: ICH M2 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Specification; PMDA eCTD Guidance

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the CTD dossier, used in regulatory correspondence and Veeva Vault tracking. Format: CTD-{AppCode}-{Year}-{Seq}.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Specification

description: Current lifecycle status of the CTD dossier. Tracks progression from authoring through regulatory submission and archival.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Human-readable title of the CTD dossier, typically including the drug name, application type, and submission sequence (e.g., 'DrugX NDA Initial Submission CTD Dossier').
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline

description: Classification of the dossier submission type. Initial submissions establish the application; amendments and supplements update specific modules; annual reports fulfill periodic reporting obligations.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; 21 CFR Part 314.70 (NDA Supplements); EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008

description: Version number of the CTD dossier (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented with each major revision or amendment cycle.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Name of the investigational drug or biologic product that is the subject of the CTD dossier (proprietary name, INN, or code name).
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; WHO INN Programme; FDA Drug Naming Guidance

description: Version of the eCTD Document Type Definition (DTD) or XML schema used for the dossier backbone (e.g., 3.2.2 for FDA, 4.0 for EMA). Determines structural compliance requirements.
reference: ICH M2 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Specification

description: Date on which the most recent dossier gap analysis was completed. Used to assess the currency of the gap analysis relative to dossier updates.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA Pre-Submission Meeting Guidance

description: Status of the formal gap analysis conducted to identify missing or incomplete content units within the CTD dossier prior to submission.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA Pre-Submission Meeting Guidance; EMA Pre-Submission Guidance

description: The therapeutic indication or disease area for which the drug is being developed and for which the CTD dossier is submitted.
reference: ICH M4E (R2) CTD Efficacy Guideline; FDA Labeling Guidance; EMA SmPC Guideline

description: Indicates whether the CTD dossier has been assessed and confirmed as ready for regulatory agency inspection. True when all required documents are present, validated, and accessible.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Inspection Readiness Guidance; EMA GCP Inspection Procedures

description: Date on which the CTD dossier was most recently compiled and packaged for submission or review. Tracks the currency of the assembled dossier.
reference: ICH M2 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the CTD dossier record. Required for audit trail and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Completeness status of CTD Module 1 (Regional Administrative Information), which contains region-specific forms, cover letters, and labeling. Content varies by target agency.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Module 1 Specification

description: Completeness status of CTD Module 2 (CTD Summaries), including the Quality Overall Summary (QOS), Nonclinical Overview, Clinical Overview, Nonclinical Written and Tabulated Summaries, and Clinical Summary.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ICH M4Q; ICH M4S; ICH M4E

description: Completeness status of CTD Module 3 (Quality), covering drug substance, drug product, and related quality documentation per ICH M4Q.
reference: ICH M4Q (R1) CTD Quality Guideline

description: Completeness status of CTD Module 4 (Nonclinical Study Reports), including pharmacology, pharmacokinetics, and toxicology study reports per ICH M4S.
reference: ICH M4S (R2) CTD Safety Guideline

description: Completeness status of CTD Module 5 (Clinical Study Reports), including all clinical pharmacology, efficacy, and safety study reports per ICH M4E.
reference: ICH M4E (R2) CTD Efficacy Guideline; ICH E3 Clinical Study Report

description: Number of identified content gaps (missing or incomplete documents/sections) that remain unresolved in the current dossier version. Key metric for submission readiness.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA Pre-Submission Meeting Guidance

description: Aggregate readiness assessment of the entire CTD dossier across all five modules. Used for dossier gap analysis and submission readiness reviews.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA Pre-Submission Meeting Guidance; EMA Pre-Submission Guidance

description: Target date on which the CTD dossier is planned to be submitted to the regulatory agency. Used for milestone tracking and resource planning.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA PDUFA Goal Dates; EMA Submission Timelines

description: Reference number of the prior submission or dossier version that this submission amends or supplements. Enables lineage tracking across submission cycles.
reference: ICH M2 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Name and version of the publishing software used to compile and validate the eCTD dossier (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager, Veeva Vault RIM Publisher). Critical for reproducibility and troubleshooting.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Specification

description: Version number of the publishing tool used to compile this dossier. Required for audit trail and reproducibility of the eCTD package.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Name of the pharmaceutical or biotech sponsor organization that owns the regulatory application and CTD dossier.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.23; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Total number of individual content units (documents, datasets, study reports) included in this CTD dossier version. Used for completeness tracking and submission package verification.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Date on which the most recent eCTD technical validation report was generated for this dossier.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria

description: Status of the eCTD technical validation report generated by the publishing tool. Agencies require a passing validation report before accepting eCTD submissions.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria; ICH M2 eCTD Specification

description: Identifier of the Veeva Vault submission binder that organizes all CTD documents for this dossier. Enables navigation of the dossier structure within Veeva Vault.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; ICH M4 CTD Guideline

description: Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault RIM to the CTD dossier record. Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse and the Veeva Vault eTMF/regulatory submissions system.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the IND application record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent regulatory application record when this IND is a sub-application or cross-references a master NDA/BLA/MAA application. Enables hierarchical navigation of the regulatory application portfolio.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Regulatory Application Hierarchy

description: The cumulative number of IND amendments (protocol amendments, information amendments, safety reports) submitted to the FDA under this IND. Tracks the volume of regulatory activity and dossier complexity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.31; 21 CFR Part 312.30; FDA IND Amendment Regulations

description: The next due date for the IND Annual Report submission to the FDA. Annual reports are required within 60 days of the anniversary of the IND effective date and must summarize clinical progress, safety data, and manufacturing updates.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.33; FDA IND Annual Report Requirements

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which this application is filed (e.g., USA for FDA, GBR for MHRA, JPN for PMDA). Enables multi-country regulatory portfolio analytics.

description: Indicates whether the FDA has granted Breakthrough Therapy designation for the investigational product under this IND. Breakthrough Therapy designation provides intensive FDA guidance and rolling review for drugs showing substantial improvement over existing therapies.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.82; FDA Breakthrough Therapy Guidance; FDASIA Section 902

description: The date on which the FDA issued a clinical hold order on this IND, halting enrollment or dosing of subjects. Nullable for INDs not on clinical hold. Triggers mandatory sponsor response within 30 days.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42; FDA Clinical Hold Regulations

description: The date on which the FDA lifted the clinical hold and authorized resumption of clinical trials under this IND. Nullable for INDs that have never been on clinical hold or where the hold remains active.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42; FDA Clinical Hold Regulations

description: Description of the FDA's stated reason for imposing a clinical hold on this IND. Captures the safety concern, protocol deficiency, or regulatory non-compliance issue cited by the agency. Nullable for INDs not on clinical hold.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.42; FDA Clinical Hold Regulations

description: The timestamp when this IND application record was first created in the lakehouse data platform. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Conforms to 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; ICH E6(R2)

description: The pharmaceutical dosage form of the investigational product (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, infusion, patch). Captured from the IND CMC section and used for supply chain and regulatory tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23(a)(7); ICH Q8

description: International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or chemical name of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) covered under this IND. Used for regulatory cross-referencing and WHO-DD coding.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.23(a)(5); WHO-DD; ICH Q11

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Submission Flag
description: Indicates whether this IND application is submitted in eCTD format via the FDA Electronic Submissions Gateway. True indicates eCTD format; False indicates paper or non-eCTD electronic submission. FDA mandates eCTD for most IND submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; FDA eCTD Guidance; ICH M8

description: The date on which the IND became effective and clinical trials were authorized to commence. Under 21 CFR Part 312.40, an IND becomes effective 30 days after FDA receipt unless FDA places a clinical hold. This is the EFFECTIVE_FROM date for the IND agreement.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.40; FDA IND Regulations

description: The application number assigned by a non-FDA regulatory agency for an equivalent authorization (e.g., EMA CTA number, MHRA CTA reference, PMDA CTN). Used to cross-reference the IND with parallel international submissions.
reference: EU CTR 536/2014; MHRA CTA Regulations; PMDA Regulations

description: Indicates whether the FDA has granted Fast Track designation for the investigational product under this IND. Fast Track facilitates development and expedites review of drugs treating serious conditions with unmet medical need.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.82; FDA Fast Track Guidance; FDAMA Section 112

description: The date on which the FDA officially acknowledged receipt of the IND application. Starts the 30-day review clock per 21 CFR Part 312.40. May differ from the initial submission date if there were transmission delays.

description: The FDA Center and Division responsible for reviewing this IND application (e.g., CDER/Division of Oncology Products 1, CBER/Division of Vaccines). Determines the regulatory review team and applicable FDA guidance documents.

description: Classification of the IND application by its intended purpose. Commercial INDs are filed by pharmaceutical/biotech sponsors for drug development.
