description: Cryptographic algorithm used to generate the checksum value (e.g., MD5, SHA-256). Required for integrity verification during agency validation and archival processes.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Cryptographic hash value (MD5 or SHA-256) of the content unit file, as declared in the eCTD XML backbone. Used to verify file integrity during agency validation, transmission, and archival. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding this content unit, such as rationale for document inclusion, known limitations, pending updates, or agency-specific instructions. Used by regulatory affairs teams for dossier management.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for this content unit within the submission sequence, typically assigned by the publishing tool (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager) or Veeva Vault. Used for cross-referencing in cover letters and agency correspondence.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Date and time when this submission content unit record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Top-level CTD module number (1 through 5) to which this content unit belongs. Module 1 = Administrative/Regional; Module 2 = Summaries; Module 3 = Quality; Module 4 = Nonclinical; Module 5 = Clinical.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline

description: Standardized CTD section reference code indicating the module and subsection where this content unit is placed (e.g., 2.7.3, 5.3.5.1, 3.2.P.1). Drives dossier structure validation and gap analysis against the ICH M4 table of contents.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Official date of the content unit document as stated on the document itself (e.g., report date, approval date of the document). Distinct from submission date; used for document currency assessment and regulatory review.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Current lifecycle status of this content unit within the regulatory dossier workflow. Tracks progression from authoring through agency submission and potential supersession. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|submitted|superseded|deleted|on_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; ICH M8 eCTD Specification; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Full descriptive title of the content unit as it appears in the eCTD leaf title element and the CTD table of contents. Used for dossier navigation and agency review.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Classification of the content unit by document category (e.g., Clinical Study Report, Investigator Brochure, Protocol, Statistical Analysis Plan, Dataset, Labeling, Module 2 Summary). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_study_report|investigator_brochure|protocol|statistical_analysis_plan|dataset|labeling|module_2_summary|nonclinical_study_report|quality_document|correspondence — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; CDISC SDTM/ADaM Standards; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Version of the eCTD Document Type Definition (DTD) or XML schema used for the backbone of the submission sequence containing this content unit (e.g., 3.2.2, 4.0). Determines structural validation rules applied by agency gateway.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Lifecycle Operation
description: eCTD leaf-level lifecycle operation applied to this content unit in the current submission sequence. 'new' = first submission of this document; 'replace' = supersedes a prior version; 'append' = adds to existing content; 'delete' = removes a previously submitted document.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Electronic file format of the content unit (e.g., PDF for narrative documents, XPT for CDISC SDTM/ADaM datasets, XML for eCTD backbone). Must comply with agency-specific technical specifications for electronic submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|xml|sas7bdat|xpt|docx|xlsx|txt|html — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance; ICH M8 eCTD Specification; CDISC SDTM/ADaM Standards

description: Actual file name of the content unit as stored in the eCTD submission package (e.g., csr-study-001-v1.pdf). Must conform to eCTD file naming conventions (lowercase, no spaces, hyphen-delimited).
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Relative file path of the content unit within the eCTD submission folder structure (e.g., m5/53-clin-stud-rep/535-rep-effic-safety-stud/5351-stud-rep-uncontrol/). Used for dossier navigation and publishing validation.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Size of the content unit file in bytes. Used for submission package size validation, agency gateway transmission compliance, and storage management. Agencies impose maximum file size limits for electronic submissions.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Indicates whether this content unit has been identified as a gap (missing or incomplete) during dossier gap analysis. When True, the CTD section requires a new or updated document before submission can proceed.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Date and time when this content unit was formally included in the submission sequence package, as recorded by the publishing tool or Veeva Vault. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction line.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Indicates whether this PDF content unit contains required PDF bookmarks as mandated by agency technical specifications. Bookmark compliance is validated during eCTD technical review.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Indicates whether this content unit contains internal eCTD hyperlinks to other documents within the dossier. Hyperlink integrity is a key validation criterion for FDA and EMA submissions.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Indicates whether this PDF content unit is text-searchable (OCR-processed or natively digital), as required by FDA and EMA for narrative documents submitted in PDF format.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: ISO 639-1 language code of the content unit (e.g., en, ja, de, fr). Required for multi-regional submissions where local language documents are mandated by agencies such as PMDA (Japanese) or Health Canada (French/English).
reference: ISO 639-1; ICH M4 CTD Guideline; PMDA eCTD Guidance; Health Canada Guidance

description: Unique XML leaf element identifier assigned to this content unit within the eCTD backbone XML file. Used for cross-referencing between eCTD sequences and for lifecycle operations (replace/delete) that reference prior leaf IDs.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Leaf ID of the previously submitted content unit that this document replaces, appends, or deletes. Required for eCTD lifecycle operations to maintain document lineage across submission sequences.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Name of the eCTD publishing software used to compile and validate this content unit within the submission package (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager, Certus). Required for technical conformance documentation.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Version of the eCTD publishing software used to compile this content unit. Tracked for technical conformance and reproducibility of the submission package.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Name or user identifier of the regulatory affairs reviewer who performed quality check on this content unit prior to submission. Supports QC accountability and audit trail requirements.
reference: ALCOA+ Principles; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Guideline

description: Code identifying the health authority for which this content unit is specifically prepared or applicable (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA). Some documents are agency-specific (e.g., Module 1 administrative documents differ by region). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|HC|TGA|ANVISA|NMPA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; FDA; EMA; PMDA; MHRA; Health Canada

description: Date and time when this submission content unit record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data integrity auditing.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Descriptive error or warning message returned by the eCTD validation tool or agency gateway when validation_status is 'failed' or 'warning'. Used for remediation and resubmission planning.
reference: FDA eCTD Validation Criteria; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Result of the eCTD technical validation check performed on this content unit prior to or upon agency receipt. Indicates whether the file passed agency gateway validation criteria (e.g., FDA ECTD Validation Criteria, EMA validation rules).
reference: FDA eCTD Validation Criteria; EMA eCTD Guidance; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system, which serves as the system of record for regulatory document management. Enables direct traceability from the dossier content unit back to the source document in Veeva Vault.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; 21 CFR Part 11; EU CTR 536/2014

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each eCTD submission sequence record within the regulatory application dossier.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) to which this submission sequence belongs.
reference: ICH M4 CTD; FDA 21 CFR Part 312/314; EMA EU CTR 536/2014

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence is sent to a specific health authority. The regulatory_authority_code and submission_country_code STRING fields on submission_sequence are denormalized references to heal
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence is packaged as a regulatory binder. Regulatory publishing teams build a binder per sequence; linking submission_sequence to regulatory_binder enables sequence-level binde
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the clinical study associated with this submission sequence, where the sequence relates to a specific study (e.g., a clinical study report submission). Null for application-level sequences not tied to a single study.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2); ICH M4E CTD Module 5; FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence generates a cover letter and eCTD validation report filed as TMF documents. Linking submission sequences to their TMF documents enables sequence-level document tracking a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Veeva Vault Regulatory Information Management (RIM) Submission ID
description: External identifier of this submission sequence record within Veeva Vault RIM Submissions module, the system of record for regulatory dossier management. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational eTMF/RIM system.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM Submissions; 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+

description: Official confirmation or acknowledgement number issued by the regulatory authority upon receipt of the submission sequence (e.g., FDA ESG acknowledgement ID, EMA CESP submission ID). Used for tracking and correspondence reference.
reference: FDA Electronic Submissions Gateway Technical Conformance Guide; EMA CESP User Guide

description: Date on which the regulatory authority issued a formal action or decision on this submission sequence (e.g., approval letter, complete response letter, refuse-to-file letter, Day 120 list of questions). Null if no action has been issued.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.110; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M8

description: Type of formal regulatory action issued by the agency in response to this submission sequence. Drives downstream regulatory strategy and response planning.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.110/314.120; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M8

description: The date on which the regulatory authority officially acknowledged receipt of this submission sequence. Triggers the start of the agency review clock (e.g., PDUFA clock for FDA, Day 0 for EMA).
reference: FDA PDUFA; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 5; ICH M8

description: Data classification level assigned to this submission sequence reflecting the sensitivity of the dossier content. Governs access controls within Veeva Vault and the lakehouse. 'highly_confidential' applies to trade secret CMC data.
reference: GDPR Article 5; 21 CFR Part 11; EMA Policy 0070 (Publication of Clinical Data); HIPAA

description: Timestamp when this submission sequence record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 8

description: Identifies which CTD module(s) are primarily affected by this submission sequence. Module 1: Administrative/Regional; Module 2: Summaries; Module 3: Quality; Module 4: Nonclinical; Module 5: Clinical. 'multiple' indicates cross-module content.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA CTD Guidance

description: Comma-delimited list of specific CTD section identifiers (e.g., '3.2.P.1, 3.2.P.2, 5.3.5.1') included or modified in this submission sequence. Enables granular tracking of which dossier sections evolved with each sequence.
reference: ICH M4 CTD Guideline; ICH M8 eCTD Specification

description: Total number of documents included in this eCTD submission sequence package. Supports completeness verification and dossier audit.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Human-readable name or label assigned to the eCTD submission package for this sequence (e.g., 'NDA 123456 Seq 0012 — CMC Amendment'). Used for identification in Veeva Vault and internal tracking systems.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Total file size in megabytes of the eCTD submission package for this sequence. Used for submission gateway capacity planning and agency file size compliance checks.
reference: FDA ESG Technical Conformance Guide; EMA CESP User Guide

description: Specifies the eCTD XML backbone lifecycle operation applied to documents in this sequence. 'new' adds documents for the first time; 'replace' supersedes a prior document; 'append' adds to an existing document set; 'delete' removes a document from the active dossier view.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Date on which the eCTD technical validation was performed for this submission sequence. Used to confirm validation was completed prior to submission deadline.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria

description: Total number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD validation of this submission sequence. A value of zero is required for a passing validation status.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria

description: Result of the eCTD technical validation check performed against the agency's published validation criteria prior to or upon submission. 'pass' indicates full conformance; 'warning' indicates non-critical issues; 'fail' indicates the sequence was rejected for technical non-conformance.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria; ICH M8

description: Name and version of the eCTD validation software used to validate this submission sequence (e.g., 'Lorenz docuBridge v5.2', 'Extedo eCTD Manager v8.1', 'Veeva Vault RIM Submissions v23.1'). Required for audit trail and reproducibility.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Total number of non-critical technical validation warnings identified during eCTD validation of this submission sequence. Warnings do not block submission but must be reviewed and justified.
reference: FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria

description: Indicates whether this submission sequence qualifies for or was filed under an expedited regulatory pathway (e.g., FDA Fast Track, Breakthrough Therapy, Priority Review; EMA PRIME). True if expedited designation applies.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.310; FDA FDASIA Section 902; EMA PRIME Guideline

description: Indicates whether this submission sequence contains or is primarily driven by safety-related content (e.g., IND Safety Report, SUSAR narrative, labeling safety update, DSUR). True if safety content is the primary driver.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2F; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 42

description: Four-digit sequence number of the immediately preceding submission sequence that this sequence supersedes or builds upon. Enables reconstruction of the dossier evolution chain and supports lifecycle operation traceability.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Deadline by which the sponsor must submit a response to an agency information request or complete response letter associated with this submission sequence. Null if no response is required.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.110; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M8

description: Date on which the regulatory authority's formal review clock begins for this submission sequence (e.g., PDUFA Day 1 for FDA, Day 0 for EMA). Derived from agency acknowledgement and used for PDUFA/review deadline tracking.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; EMA EU CTR 536/2014; ICH M8

description: Target date by which the regulatory authority is expected to complete review of this submission sequence, calculated from the review clock start date per applicable PDUFA or EMA timelines. Critical for regulatory milestone planning.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; EMA EU CTR 536/2014 Article 6; ICH M8

description: Four-digit zero-padded sequence number uniquely identifying this submission within the regulatory application lifecycle (e.g., 0000 for original, 0001 for first amendment). Assigned per ICH M8 eCTD specification and used by agency systems to order dossier evolution.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide v4.5

description: Further classification of the submission type providing regulatory-specific granularity (e.g., Prior Approval Supplement, Changes Being Effected, Complete Response, Major Amendment, Minor Amendment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prior_approval_supplement|changes_being_effected|complete_response|major_amendment|minor_amendment|annual_report — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.70; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; ICH M8

description: The specific regulatory submission unit or grouping within the eCTD backbone that this sequence belongs to (e.g., 'IND Safety Report', 'NDA Module 5 Clinical Study Report', 'BLA CMC Supplement'). Aligns with Veeva Vault RIM submission unit taxonomy.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Specification; Veeva Vault RIM; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this submission sequence record, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+; ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 8
